PAGE: 3D printer
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/3D_printer
======================================================================

For the real-world article, please see
3D printing
.
Adam Soong's 3D printer
A
3D printer
was a type of
machine
that was designed to
printout
three-dimensional
objects
for various uses.
In
2024
,
Q
used
Adam Soong
's 3D printer to printout his
business card
, which had his
phone number
. (
PIC
: "
Fly Me to the Moon
")
External link
3D printing
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: ATM
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/ATM
======================================================================

An ATM in Detroit, 2004.
Archer and T'Pol rob an ATM.
An
ATM
(
A
utomated
T
eller
M
achine) was an
Earth
device
existing during the early
21st century
. It dispensed
currency
to
bank
customers
who had an existing account, with the amount taken from the machine then deducted from their account.
During a visit to the year
2004
,
Captain
Jonathan Archer
used a
scanner
to illegally access an ATM in
Detroit
to acquire money for
gas
for their "borrowed"
Dodge Ram
, while
T'Pol
kept watch to ensure nobody saw the
crime
. After successfully retrieving the money, Archer remarked that people used to go to
jail
for stealing money, to which T'Pol insisted they would return what they didn't use. (
ENT
: "
Carpenter Street
")
External link
Automated teller machine
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Abronath
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Abronath
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
The Abronath
The
Abronath
was an ancient
bioweapon
of mass destruction created by the "
Ancient Ones
" of
Altamid
.
History
Long before the
22nd century
, the
indigenous inhabitants
of Altamid engineered the Abronath as a weapon of war. However, as they could not control it, they split the weapon in two and launched the pieces into
space
, hoping they would never be found. The Ancient Ones later abandoned their
homeworld
, leaving their technology behind.
Krall
, the former
captain
of the
Federation
starship
USS
Franklin
that had
crashed
on Altamid in
2164
, sought the Abronath as the instrument of his vengeance on the Federation. He managed to locate one piece after lifetimes of searching.
A computer scan of a component of the Abronath
The other piece of the Abronath came into the possession of the
Fibonans
. In
2263
, the Fibonans passed the artifact to the Federation to be presented as an offering of peace to the
Teenaxi
. Mistrustful of the Fibonans and suspicious of the inactive weapon, the Teenaxi refused to accept the Abronath piece, and
Captain
James T. Kirk
thereafter had it stored in the
archive vault
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
.
Krall and Manas arm the Abronath
Using captured
Magellan probes
to access the
Starbase
Yorktown
database, Krall discovered the location of the second piece. He dispatched
Kalara
to lure the
Enterprise
to Altamid, where his forces
destroyed
the
Enterprise
and captured most of its crew. Kirk had given the Abronath piece to
Ensign
Syl
for safekeeping, but Krall coerced her into relinquishing it. He then armed the Abronath and demonstrated its power on Syl.
Krall planned to use the Abronath to wipe out the millions of inhabitants on Starbase Yorktown, giving him a powerful base from which to launch further attacks against the Federation. Although his
fleet
of
Swarm ships
was destroyed by Kirk and his crew, Krall reached Yorktown's central atmospheric regulator, where the Abronath's effects would be disseminated throughout the station. Krall activated the Abronath, but was subsequently ejected into space by Kirk and consumed by the weapon. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
Specifications
The Abronath armed
The Abronath consisted of a ring-shaped outer piece and a hexagonal center piece. It was armed by combining the pieces and submerging them in a metallic fluid. Once armed, the Abronath could be activated by turning the center piece clockwise, which caused it to release a cloud of particulates that rapidly disintegrated living beings on contact; if placed in a ventilated area, the cloud could spread to cover an enormous volume. The Abronath could be deactivated by turning the center piece counterclockwise, which caused the cloud to be drawn back inside the device. (
Star Trek Beyond
)
On
26 July
2016
, Pegg News, a Twitter account moderated on behalf of
Star Trek Beyond
actor and co-writer
Simon Pegg
, provided a brief description of Abronath. It read, "
Abronath – Ab 452 is a radioactive transition metal unique to an M-class planet in the southern spiral arm of the Andromeda Galaxy designated, Altamid. When subject to decay, Abronath sub atomically transmutes into the hitherto theoretical, Weber Particle or 'Zombie Mote' (Ab 451). The particle assimilates gas molecules on contact and reduces carbon based organisms to inorganic waste matter. After transmutation, Ab 451 can consume any number of closed ecosystems existing in the same atmosphere in a matter of hours. Altamid has endured several mass extinctions due to the presence of Abronath in its asthenospheric crust.
"
[1]
External link
Abronath
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Aceton assimilator
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Aceton_assimilator
======================================================================

Aceton assimilators
were described as primitive
generators
which could drain
power
from distant sources. They could be easily modified to convert
energy
into
radiation
.
During the war with the
Promellians
in the
14th century
, the
Menthars
hid several hundred thousand Aceton assimilators in the floating remains of
Orelious IX
, creating an instant booby trap. The
Promellian battle cruiser
Cleponji
encountered this booby trap following the destruction of Orelious, where it eventually succumbed to the assimilators.
Over a millennium later, the
Federation
starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
, following the discovery of the
Cleponji
, also fell prey to the effects of the assimilators. They were able to out-maneuver the devices by utilizing the ship's
thrusters
to navigate out of the field. Following their escape, the
Enterprise
destroyed the trap field as well as the
Cleponji
to prevent anyone else from becoming trapped in the future. (
TNG
: "
Booby Trap
")
According to the script, aceton was pronounced as "ASS-ah-tawn".
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Acoustic inverter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Acoustic_inverter
======================================================================

An
acoustic inverter
was a component of the
sonic shower
system.
When
B'Elanna Torres
' acoustic inverter broke in
2374
,
Tom Paris
described the sound of the sonic shower as being sufficient to "
make your
hair
stand on end
". (
VOY
: "
Day of Honor
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Actuator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Actuator
======================================================================

An
actuator
was a part of a
device
or
machine
that helped it to
achieve
physical
movements
by converting
energy
, often
electrical
,
air
, or hydraulic, into mechanical force.
While
contacting
Chakotay
,
B'Elanna Torres
said that the actuators were not working, and
Pelk
believed that it was probably just the
decouplers
, and they were always getting encrusted with
oxides
. (
VOY
: "
Juggernaut
")
External link
Actuator
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Agonizer
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Agonizer
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
"
Ha! Agonizer? Was "Mean Machine" taken?
"
–
Jankom Pog
,
2385
("
Cracked Mirror
")
An agonizer used by the Terran Empire during the 23rd century
An agonizer in use
An
agonizer
was a small
device
worn on the
belts
of
Imperial
personnel in the
mirror universe
, used to inflict pain for minor
transgressions
. More severe offenses called for using the
agony booth
. (
TOS
: "
Mirror, Mirror
")
In the
prime universe
, the
Klingons
also used a similar device in
2268
. (
TOS
: "
Day of the Dove
")
In
2370
,
Arctus Baran
used
neural servos
, which were implanted in each member of his crew, as a means of maintaining
obedience
through
coercive
menace. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
In
2372
, the mirror universe counterparts of
Kira Nerys
and
Elim Garak
were subjected to these devices by their captors to make them more obedient. (
DS9
: "
Shattered Mirror
")
See also
Collar of obedience
Pain band
Painstik
Apocrypha
In the
Star Trek: Mirror Universe
story "Ill Winds" from the
Shards and Shadows
anthology,
Sarah April
's
mirror universe
counterpart (
β
) is said to have developed the agonizer.
In
DS9
: "
Shattered Mirror
", agonizers were seen as still being used in the mirror universe. They were sufficiently improved over the earlier model that they no longer needed physical contact with their victims to be effective.
In the TNG-era novel
Dark Mirror
by
Diane Duane
, a
24th century
Empire had incorporated the agonizer into the
Starfleet
insignia pins worn on the uniform, much as in the primary universe, Starfleet had incorporated communicators. As with those seen in "Shattered Mirror" they were improved technologically, but it was so they could be smaller, and they still needed to touch their victims to work.
Klingon agonizers were also seen in use in the
Star Trek
newspaper comic strip
.
External links
Agonizer
at
StarTrek.com
Agonizer
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Akoonah
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Akoonah
======================================================================

An akoonah
An
akoonah
was a
device
used by some
Native Americans
in the
24th century
to facilitate
vision quests
, taking the place of
psychoactive herbs
used by their
ancestors
. It was used by placing one's
hand
on the surface of the device and
concentrating
on an
object
important
to the
user
.
Commander
Chakotay
's
medicine bundle
consisted of a
blackbird
's
wing
, a
river
stone
bearing a
CHAH-mooz-ee
, and an
akoonah
. In
2371
, he showed
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
how to use the akoonah so that she could seek her
animal guide
. (
VOY
: "
The Cloud
")
In
2372
, believing that
Seska
had
impregnated
herself with his
DNA
and
given birth
to their
son
, Chakotay used the
akoonah
to seek the
spirit
of his
father
,
Kolopak
, for guidance. His father advised him to embrace the child instead of
denying
and
punishing
it for the
crimes
of his mother. (
VOY
: "
Basics, Part I
")
Neelix
asked Chakotay for the use of his
akoonah
in
2374
, following a
near-death experience
after which he found himself in a deep existential and spiritual
crisis
, questioning his long held
beliefs
in the
afterlife
. (
VOY
: "
Mortal Coil
")
The
akoonah
could also be used to induce
lucid dreams
. In
2374
, Chakotay did just that, in an attempt to communicate with aliens that appeared in
dreams
. (
VOY
: "
Waking Moments
")
The device was not mentioned by name in the episode, but Chakotay mentioned his intention to use the same technology as for vision quests, and was then seen holding the device during the procedure.
In
2375
, Chakotay used the
akoonah
to
investigate
the
hallucinations
he experienced after the
USS
Voyager
became trapped in
chaotic space
. The vision quest allowed
lifeforms
native to chaotic space to communicate the means of freeing
Voyager
to him. (
VOY
: "
The Fight
")
The concept of the
akoonah
proved to be highly controversial, which thrilled
Jeri Taylor
. She considered neither the technology nor its uses to be supernatural. Of the device, she remarked, "
It's been extremely controversial, which I love. I'm delighted to stir up controversy. We are positing that maybe American Indians in the twenty-fourth century have a technology that allows them to tap into their subconscious in a safe way so that they no longer have to take drugs,
fast
or go to sweat lodges. Consequently, they navigate their unconscious frequently and are very much more comfortable with it than most of us. I see nothing supernatural about that. This is a man going through his unconscious, tapping into whatever is inside him that can help him navigate through his life.
" (
Captains' Logs Supplemental - The Unauthorized Guide to the New Trek Voyages
)

Akritirian cargo vessel
An
Akritirian cargo vessel
(also called a
Akritirian freighter
) was a type of
freighter
used by the
Akritirians
during the
24th century
. The vessel was small enough in size to be towed into the
shuttlebay
of an
Intrepid
-class
starship
.
One such cargo vessel was operated by
Piri
and
Vel
, members of the
terrorist
group
Open Sky
. In
2373
, they used the ship's
paralithium
, from the
ion-based propulsion system
, to make
trilithium
explosives. (
VOY
: "
The Chute
")
For information on history of this
studio model
, please see:
TNG studio models
.

PAGE: Akritirian patrol ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Akritirian_patrol_ship
======================================================================

A pair of Akritirian patrol ships
The
Akritirian patrol ship
was a type of
starship
used by the
Akritirians
to enforce
laws
in their
space
.
In
2373
, two
patrol ships
attacked the
USS
Voyager
in an attempt to impound the ship, causing
Voyager
to retreat.
Two more patrol ships later attacked
Neelix
's ship
Baxial
during its mission to rescue
Tom Paris
and
Harry Kim
from the
Akritirian maximum security detention facility
. (
VOY
: "
The Chute
")
For more information on the
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

Al-Leyan transport

Transport
Active:

Al-Leyan transport

transport
utilized during the latter half of the
24th century
.
In his search for the
ancient humanoids
'
DNA
puzzle
,
Professor
Galen
wanted to gain passage to
Caere
on an Al-Leyan transport from
Deep Space 4
. (
TNG
: "
The Chase
")
The Al-Leyan were identified as a species by
StarTrek.com
.
[1]
(X)
In the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 15, these transports were of Al-Leyan registry.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ammunition
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ammunition
======================================================================

Ammunition
was a materiel that was shot, dropped, or
detonated
from a
weapon
or weapon system. This included any expendable weapon such as a
bomb
,
missile
,
shell
,
grenade
,
mine
, or other
explosive device
, as well as a component part of a weapon such as a
firearm
cartridge,
bullet
,
arrow
, or
warhead
.
Munition
was a broad
term
that sometimes referred to ammunition, but also any weapon which used ammunition, and any piece of
military
equipment
in general.
In
2375
,
Julian Bashir
described an
1836
event
where
Davy Crockett
was out of ammunition while the
Mexican Army
was swarming the
battlements
of the
Alamo
, and Bashir was convinced that Crockett would have
surrendered
. (
DS9
: "
Once More Unto the Breach
")
External link
Ammunition
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Android duplicator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Android_duplicator
======================================================================

The android duplicator of Exo III
Top view
Control panel in use
"
This is how you make an android.
"
–
Roger Korby
,
2266
("
What Are Little Girls Made Of?
")
The
android duplicator
was a specialized piece of machinery discovered by
Roger Korby
in the underground caverns of
Exo III
around
2261
.
Design
Control of the duplicator was accomplished from a complex control panel, with precision controls that were color-coded but not otherwise labeled. This control center was located in a room adjacent to the duplicator itself.
The duplicator consisted of a large turntable that had slotted positions on either side of the turntable for the "blank" and "template"-being, both separated by a divider, and both with a locking mechanism placed mid-torso. On either side, paralleling the turntable, were two electronic components that were physically responsible for the duplication.
Use
The android duplication involved two major stages in the process.
The initial stage started with a "blank" and a
naked
Human being, serving as the new android's "template", be loaded onto the turntable. The mechanism could create a physically exact duplicate of that being, including
synthetic organs
whose
autonomic rhythms
matched those of the template. While this platform spun at increasing increments of speed, the machine formed the blank into the android duplicate. At the conclusion of this process, the duplicate was only physically identical. No physical harm came to the "template"-being.
In the second stage,
synaptic fusion
, copied the target's thoughts and memories in the android. This stage also did not harm the original, but it was at least somewhat painful.
History
The android duplicator was constructed centuries prior by the
Old Ones
, the race that once lived in the
planet
's caverns, and was tended to for untold years by the
android
Ruk
. With Ruk's help, and with records discovered by Korby, they were able to build
Brown
. According to
Korby's own android
, he was constructed because the original Korby "
had only [his]
brain
between
life
and
death
.
" It was also in this time frame that the android
Andrea
was also constructed, without an apparent source.
In the duplication process performed on
James T. Kirk
in
2266
, he was able to interfere with the outcome of this stage by keeping an atypical thought in the forefront of his mind. Subsequently, the android responded to a comment from
Spock
with this thought, apparently without realizing it was something Kirk would not normally say.
Roger Korby
claimed that if synaptic fusion was carried to conclusion, the original being's consciousness, or
soul
, could be transferred into the android duplicate. Such duplicates could then be programmed not to feel negative
emotions
or express negative traits. They formed the centerpiece of Korby's plan for a programmed utopia. Subsequent events revealed that the duplication process was not exact, and that the duplicates could, in fact, express traits other than those they were programmed to.
It was revealed that during Korby's duplication of himself, he had completely transferred his mind into it. Although the Korby android believed itself to be Korby, outsiders such as Kirk and
Christine Chapel
noted that its behavior was aberrant. Ultimately, Korby and all of the other androids were destroyed, leaving open the questions of when Korby actually died, and whether the Korby android was really Korby, or simply a machine convinced that it was Korby. (
TOS
: "
What Are Little Girls Made Of?
")
By
2401
, the android duplicator was kept in storage among
Section 31
's high-security artifacts at
Daystrom Station
. (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
The "android duplicator" was not identified by name in the episode, other than Korby's roundabout line about Ruk "still tending the machinery", or when the device was simply introduced as "this is how you make an android." Despite the lengthy description of the device in the script notes, emphasizing on the turntable, mention was made of the "
squat "dynamo duplicators" at each end of the table glow and throb with power as Kirk's form and the mold form alternately pass.
"

PAGE: Ankari vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ankari_vessel
======================================================================

An Ankari vessel
The
Ankari vessel
was a class of
starship
used by the
Ankari
in the late-
24th century
. Vessels of this type used a form of propulsion that was difficult for normal
sensors
to detect.
In
2376
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
of the
USS
Voyager
managed to track down one of these vessels in order to gain help from the Ankrari in negotiating peace between them and
nucleogenic lifeforms
that threatened the ship. (
VOY
: "
Equinox, Part II
")
This appears to be a unique
CGI
studio model
. According to Ex Astris Scientia analysis
[1]
, this model was "
rather detailed and share[d] a couple of features with the
Akritirian patrol ship
.
"

Forward view
For the 2377 warship, please see
Annari warship (2377)
.
The
Annari warship
was a vessel designed for
armed conflict
used by the
Annari
in the early
2370s
.
In
2372
, an Annari warship of this type under the command of
General
Valen
was stranded in
the Void
. The vessel later encountered the
USS
Voyager
five years later
. (
VOY
: "
The Void
")
For information on the use of this model, please see
this page
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Annari warship (2377)
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Annari_warship_(2377)
======================================================================

An Annari warship
For the 2372 warship, please see
Annari warship (2372)
.
A class of
Annari warship
was utilized by the
Annari
in the late
2370s
.
In
2377
, vessels of this type intercepted the
Kraylor
vessel "
Nightingale
" after the ship's
cloaking device
malfunctioned. The device was repaired in time before the Annari could destroy the ship.
The Annari also used this ship type to blockade the
Kraylor homeworld
. They used a series of "pulses" to illuminate cloaked vessels. (
VOY
: "
Nightingale
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Anodyne capacitor
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Anodyne_capacitor
======================================================================

An
anodyne capacitor
was a device that could be used in conjunction with a
starship
's
phaser
system. The use of such a device sped up a phaser's recharge time. It did this by accumulating
power
over time and then releasing large amounts of it into the weapons grid to speed up the charging sequence.
In
2372
,
Major
Kira Nerys
suggested that
Gul
Dukat
should use an anodyne capacitor to speed up the recharge time of the
Groumall
's antiquated phaser system. Although Dukat found the information helpful, he did not have such a device aboard his ship to implement such a plan. (
DS9
: "
Return to Grace
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Antares class (Corvallen)
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Antares_class_(Corvallen)
======================================================================

Antares
class (Corvallen)
Affiliation:
Corvallen
Type:
Freighter
Active:
24th century
Crew complement:
18
The
Antares
-class freighter
was an old-style
warp-capable
freighter
utilized by the
Corvallens
during the
24th century
.
These vessels, which could be maintained with a crew of 18, had limited range and
speed
, capable of traveling no more than fifteen
light years
in a single day.
In
2369
, the
Romulan
Warbird
Khazara
was scheduled to rendezvous with a
Corvallen freighter
in the
Kaleb sector
to transfer
cargo
that would be transported to the
Federation
planet
Draken IV
. The
Khazara
later destroyed the freighter, forcing it to find an alternate means of transferring its cargo to
Federation space
. (
TNG
: "
Face Of The Enemy
")
For more information on the history of this
studio model
, please see
TNG studio models
.
External link
Corvallen freighter
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Antarian starship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Antarian_starship
======================================================================

An Antarian starship (2377)
The
Antarian starship
was a type of
warp-capable
starship
utilized by the
Antarians
, who employed the class in the
Delta Quadrant
during the late
24th century
.
In early
2377
, an Antarian starship rendezvoused with
USS
Voyager
to allow
Ambassador
O'Zaal
to review their petition to join the
Antarian Trans-stellar Rally
. (
VOY
: "
Drive
")
For more information on this
studio model
, please see
DS9 studio models
.

Antidean transport
Affiliation:
Antideans
Type:
Transport

24th century
An
Antidean transport

transport

Antideans
.
Such a transport was docked at
Deep Space 9
in mid-
2373
. The ship wanted to depart the
station
earlier than planned and
Odo
asked
Doctor
Julian Bashir
to issue a
health certificate
to bring their
cargo
out of
quarantine
. (
DS9
: "
Doctor Bashir, I Presume
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Antigraviton emitter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Antigraviton_emitter
======================================================================

The
antigraviton emitter
was a technology conceived of by
Damar
in
2374
, in an effort to bring down the field of
self-replicating mines
which had been deployed at the entrance to the
Bajoran wormhole
.
Damar's plan operated by reconfiguring
Deep Space 9
's
deflector field
emitter and refocusing the emitters, essentially transforming the
deflector array
into an antigraviton emitter. The mines would then be isolated in an
antigraviton beam
, preventing them from replacing themselves. Despite an attempt by an underground resistance movement aboard the station to sabotage the plan, the Dominion was successful in destroying the minefield. (
DS9
: "
Behind the Lines
", "
Favor the Bold
", "
Sacrifice of Angels
")
See also
Antigraviton
Graviton emitter

======================================================================
PAGE: Antimatter converter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Antimatter_converter
======================================================================

An antimatter converter
An
antimatter converter
was a piece of technology.
In
2369
an antimatter converter was
stolen
from a
Cardassian
outpost
by the
Bajoran
Kohn-Ma
terrorist
Tahna Los
. The Cardassian
warship
Aldara
tried to stop him but Tahna received
political asylum
aboard
Deep Space 9
.
Tahna later bought
bilitrium
from the
Klingon
Duras sisters
Lursa
and
B'Etor
. Combined, bilitrium and the antimatter converter, were an incredibly powerful source of
energy
like a
bomb
. Tahna planned to destroy the entrance of the
wormhole
with this bomb and transferred energy from the
warp drive
of the
runabout
USS
Yangtzee Kiang
into the storage cells of the converter but was stopped by
Major
Kira Nerys
,
Commander
Benjamin Sisko
, and
Chief
Miles O'Brien
. (
DS9
: "
Past Prologue
")
External link
Antimatter converter
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Anyon emitter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Anyon_emitter
======================================================================

Data uses an anyon emitter
An
anyon emitter
was a handheld device used for the controlled generation of
anyon
particles.
In
2368
,
Lieutenant Commander
Data
used an anyon emitter to eliminate anomalous
chroniton
fields which were appearing at seemingly random locations around the
USS
Enterprise
. It was soon discovered, however, that Lieutenant Commander
Geordi La Forge
and
Ensign
Ro Laren
were producing the chroniton fields every time they passed through a solid object, having been "phased" due to exposure to chronitons from an
interphase generator
. (
TNG
: "
The Next Phase
")

An arcade game on Hauze's ship
For
Star Trek
arcade games, please see
arcade games
.
An
arcade game
was a
game
(e.g.
pinball
or a
video game
) built to be placed in an
arcade
for public enjoyment. Arcade games were generally
coin
-operated, though
tokens
could be used instead. (
LD
: "
Starbase 80?!
")
In
2381
,
Kerner Hauze
had an arcade game among his collection on
his
ship
. (
LD
: "
Kayshon, His Eyes Open
")
Lacking a
holodeck
,
Starbase 80
instead had an arcade, which contained many games for crew recreation. (
LD
: "
Starbase 80?!
")

The orb known as the arcanum
The
Arcanum
was a reliquary of the
Vau N'Akat
's history and knowledge, located on
Solum
.
It appeared as a semi-transparent white orb, and was guarded by the
Lorekeeper
. In
2384
,
Ilthuran
brought
Gwyndala
to the Arcanum to help her establish her legitimacy against
Asencia
in the eyes of the other Vau N'Akat. (
PRO
: "
Into the Breach, Part II
")

stub
.

expanding it
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ardra's ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ardra's_ship
======================================================================

Ardra's ship
was a
starship
active in the
24th century
that was utilized by a
con artist
posing as
Ardra
. The ship functioned as
Ardra
's base of operations during her many ruses throughout the
sector
. It was manned by a crew.
The ship was equipped with a
cloaking device
(described as a "bad copy" of a
Romulan
cloak),
force fields
, and
transporter
and
hologram
technologies
, as well as an energy device that could cause planetary tremors. The ship had prearranged programs in its
computer
which helped the impostor deceive others. Furthermore, the ship was linked with an implant that allowed her
eye
movements to choose and activate each illusion.
In
2367
, the ship entered orbit of
Ventax II
while the impostor began her campaign with the
Ventaxians
. One of her illusions included extending the ship's cloaking shields around the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, and setting up a
subspace
dampening field
. This hid the
Enterprise
from sight, sensors, and communications, making it seem to disappear.
Geordi La Forge
was able to detect the ship's
power
source through a wavelength stretch out, triangulated from the
research station
located on the planet's surface. The ship was subsequently commandeered by the crew of the
Enterprise
, the crew quickly admitting their guilt, and "Ardra" was
arrested
by the Ventaxians, effectively ending the ruse. (
TNG
: "
Devil's Due
")
This cloaked ship was only mentioned in dialogue.
Ardra's vessel was depicted as a three-
nacelle
configuration starship in the
DC Comics
story "
Shore Leave in Shanzibar!
"
In the novel
The Jackal's Trick
, Ardra's ship is named the
Houdini
, and is used by
Tuvok
and La Forge to track another ship belonging to the same
cult
of con artists as Ardra.

======================================================================
PAGE: Areore ship (large)
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Areore_ship_(large)
======================================================================

Areore ship (large)
Owner:
Areore
Armament:
Energy weapon
The
Areore ship
was a type of large
starship
used by the ancient
Areore
in their
interstellar wars
. It was similar in design to the more common
smaller Areore ship
, and armed with a more powerful version of its
energy weapon
. The ship could land on a
planetary
surface.
History
Areore ships depicted in cave art
When the ancient Areore decided to return to a
pre-industrial
lifestyle, they abandoned their ships in underground
caves
on
Areolus
.
In
2381
,
Rawda
showed these ships to
Peanut Hamper
, who secretly invited
Drookmani
scavengers to take them as part of a plan to redeem herself to
Starfleet
. A
Drookmani ship
later arrived and began tearing the ships out of the ground with a
tractor beam
. After Peanut Hamper destroyed the Drookmani ship from within, the enraged Drookmani
beamed
to the small Areore ship they had excavated and attacked the Areore
village
and the
USS
Cerritos
above.
With his village burning and the
Cerritos
outmatched by the Areore ship, Rawda took control of a larger Areore ship and shot down the Drookmani. Landing the ship afterward, he declared to his people that henceforth they would defend their way of life without
fear
. (
LD
: "
A Mathematically Perfect Redemption
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Areore ship (small)
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Areore_ship_(small)
======================================================================

Dorsal view of small Areore ship
Bridge of a small Areore ship
The
Areore ship
was a type of small
starship
used by the ancient
Areore
in their interstellar
wars
. It was equipped with
deflector shields
and potent
energy weapons
of a design unknown to the
Federation
in the
24th century
.
When the ancient Areore decided to return to a
pre-industrial
lifestyle, they abandoned their ships in underground
caves
on
Areolus
. In
2381
,
Rawda
showed these ships to
Peanut Hamper
, who secretly invited
Drookmani
scavengers to take them as part of a plan to redeem herself to
Starfleet
. A
Drookmani ship
later arrived and began tearing the ships out of the ground with a
tractor beam
. After Peanut Hamper destroyed the Drookmani ship from within, the enraged Drookmani
beamed
to the Areore ship they had excavated and launched an attack on the Areore
village
and the
USS
Cerritos
above.
The
Cerritos
crew found that the small Areore ship's weapons were somehow capable of penetrating their shields, and that it was nimble enough to evade the
Cerritos
'
phasers
. With his village burning and the
Cerritos
outmatched, Rawda took control of a
larger Areore ship
and shot down the Drookmani. (
LD
: "
A Mathematically Perfect Redemption
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Arkonian military vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Arkonian_military_vessel
======================================================================

Arkonian military vessel
Operator:
Arkonian
military
Type:
Military vessel
Active:
22nd century
Armament:
Pulse-weapons
An
Arkonian military vessel
was a type of
military vessel
utilized by the
Arkonian
military
during the mid-
22nd century
.
Enterprise
NX-01
encountered one of these vessels after inadvertently traveling into Arkonian space in
2152
. (
ENT
: "
Dawn
")
This vessel was referred to by the production staff (for example, in the script) as an "Arkonian
destroyer
", despite being referred to as an "Arkonian military vessel" on screen.
In the final draft script of "Dawn", this craft was referred to thus; "
Although we've never seen this type of ship before, it should be reminiscent of [an
Arkonian patrol ship
].
"
For more information on this
studio model
, see
ENT studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Arkonian patrol ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Arkonian_patrol_ship
======================================================================

Arkonian patrol ship
Operator:
Arkonian
military
Type:
Patrol ship
Active:
22nd century
Armament:
Pulse-weapons
An
Arkonian patrol ship
was a type of
patrol ship
utilized by the
Arkonian
military
. These one-manned vessels easily outclassed
Earth
Starfleet
shuttlepods
.
One of these vessels, piloted by
Zho'Kaan
, was violently encountered by
Shuttlepod 1
after the latter craft inadvertently traveled into Arkonian space in
2152
.
Weapons
fire from the Arkonian craft caused the
shuttlepod
to
crash land
on a
moon
. The Arkonian vessel also crashed on the same moon, at about the same time. (
ENT
: "
Dawn
")
In the final draft script of "Dawn", this craft was referred to as both a "
shuttlecraft
" and a "
ship
". The script also referred to its weaponry as "
pulse-weapons
blasts" and later noted about the crash-landed depiction of the vessel, "
We will redress an alien ship from storage.
" For more information on the
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Armband
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Armband
======================================================================

Kes and Chakotay wearing armbands
An
armband
was a
device
or a
piece
of material which was worn around the
arm
, over the
sleeve
of other
clothing
if present. Members of the
Nazi Party
, the
Cult of the Pah-wraiths
, and the
Romulan Rebirth movement
all wore armbands as part of their
uniforms
, to show the allegiance of the wearers. (In the case of the Romulan Rebirth movement, their armbands were the only consistent aspect of their attire) (
TOS
: "
Patterns of Force
";
DS9
: "
Image in the Sand
", "
Covenant
";
PIC
: "
Absolute Candor
")
Nyota Uhura
once wore a black fabric armband at her
communications station
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
in
2267
. (
TOS
: "
The City on the Edge of Forever
")
Technological armbands creating an
antipolaric field
were worn by a
landing party
from the
USS
Voyager
, while surveying
Makull's homeworld
in
2371
. (
VOY
: "
Time and Again
")
These
24th century
armbands are not named on screen; their designation as "armbands" comes from the script of "Time and Again".
See also
Emergency transporter armband

======================================================================
PAGE: Arretan vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Arretan_vessel
======================================================================

The
Arretan vessel
was a type of ancient
starship
used by
Arretan
6,000 centuries
before
2268
. With a
size
equivalent to the
USS
Enterprise
, this vessel was powered by an
engine
the
size
of a
walnut
. She, and her
sister ships
, were used for
colonization
and
exploration
. Their travels were documented in records, which were lost in the
war
which destroyed the species' and their
homeworld
. (
TOS
: "
Return to Tomorrow
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Assault class
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Assault_class
======================================================================

Assault
class
Owner:
Hierarchy
Operator:
Overlooker
Type:
Assault vessel
,
survey ship
Active:
24th century
Speed:
Warp-capable
Armament:
Phasers
Defenses:
Cloaking field
,
deflector shields
The
Assault
-class
was a type of
starship
utilized by the
Hierarchy
in the
24th century
, equipped with advanced short and long range
sensors
, including
micro-tunneling sensors
.
The
Assault
-class ship commanded by
Loquar
in
2377
was classified as a
survey ship
not equipped to fight enemy ships. (
VOY
: "
The Void
") Other ships of this class used for raiding enemy ships were classified as
assault vessels
, more heavily armed, with three ships capable of outgunning a
Starfleet
Intrepid
-class
with heavy
phaser
armaments that could be set to fire on rotating frequencies. The assault ships were also equipped with
stealth
technology
.
These ships were used to survey passing ships for future raids, on at least one occasion while hiding in
nebulae
. The Hierarchy used the ships sensors to assess the tactical risk of attacking passing vessels and the available loot. They used the ship's
cloaking field
technology to sneak up on other ships. The Hierarchy applied several attack patterns for use aboard this type of ship, such as a
type 3 stealth assault
and a
type 4 assault
, which was based on energy consumption (see
Hierarchy
for elaboration). A special communication system aboard these ships was used by the crew for consulting with the actual Hierarchy. On these ships every crew member had one duty and work station, hardly any social contacts, and limited access to the ship.
In
2376
, three vessels of this type attacked the
Federation
starship
USS
Voyager
; the ships eventually withdrew in fear of the
photonic cannon
, an imaginary weapon of
The Doctor
's design. (
VOY
: "
Tinker Tenor Doctor Spy
")
In 2377,
Loquar's ship
was pulled into
the Void
. With help from
The Alliance
, the ship escaped the Void. (
VOY
: "
The Void
")
Appendices
See also
Hierarchy survey vessel
Appearances
VOY
:
"
Tinker Tenor Doctor Spy
"
"
The Void
"
Background information
For more information on the
studio model
see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Assembly line
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Assembly_line
======================================================================

Ford Motor Company
Model T
rolling out of a factory as it comes of an early assembly line on
Earth
An
assembly line
was a type of manufacturing set up in which
laborers
or
robots
assembled goods by repeating the same tasks over and over in a set order.
In
2153
,
Travis Mayweather
and
Trip Tucker
marveled over the identical appearances of three
Delphic Expanse spheres
, the former suggesting it was as though they were made on an assembly line. (
ENT
: "
Chosen Realm
")
In
2268
, after
Leonard McCoy
reminded
James T. Kirk
that
Richard Daystrom
's first 4
multitronic
computers
weren't big successes, Kirk explained that
genius
didn't work like an assembly line. (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
External link
Assembly line
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Astro-helmet
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Astro-helmet
======================================================================

An
astro-helmet
was
headgear
of unknown purpose and origin.
There was a
Wadi
booth on
Tulgana IV
in
2381
that had an astro-helmet on display along with other objects. (
LD
: "
Reflections
")
In the real world, the Astro-Helmet was a
children's toy
released in
1967
by Remco Industries. It came with non-shattering bulbous lenses tinted green and an elastic chin strap with chin protector. A decal sticker with the words "Star Trek" was placed above the lenses. There were decorative stickers on either side of the helmet. Other than the decal saying "Star Trek", the Astro-Helmet had absolutely nothing to do with the franchise whatsoever – that is, until its "official" appearance
fifty-five
years
later, in
Star Trek: Lower Decks
.
[1]

Atari
.

Atari 2600

1980s
.
In
2261
,
Pelia
had an Atari 2600 in her
quarters
on the
USS

. (
SNW
: "
The Sehlat Who Ate Its Tail
")

Atari 2600
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Ataxian mood shifter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ataxian_mood_shifter
======================================================================

An Ataxian mood shifter bonding to Tendi
An
Ataxian mood shifter
was a
cube
-shaped artifact that, when held, pushed its user's existing mood to extremes and melded with their body, manifesting an "
emotional
armor
" that physically transformed them.
In
2381
,
Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
found an Ataxian mood shifter during
anomaly consolidation duty
on the
USS
Cerritos
. It responded to her frustration over the events of the day by transforming her into a giant, hostile,
scorpion
-like creature. Ensign
Brad Boimler
, aware of the mood shifter's properties, was able to depower the shifter and turn Tendi back to normal by engaging in a series of pratfalls that made her laugh. The mood shifter then separated from her on its own. (
LD
: "
The Spy Humongous
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Automation
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Automation
======================================================================

A crewless starship
Automation
or an
automated system
referred to a class of
technological
advances that decreased the amount of involvement by
lifeforms
by allowing their tasks to be taken over by
machines
. Advanced
cultures
might use extensive automation.
Automated systems might go on operating long after their builders were gone, sometimes with tragic results. One such example was the
doomsday machine
. (
TOS
: "
The Doomsday Machine
") Beacons and
transmission stations
also tended to be automated, and
distress calls
and other automated signals might continue operating long after those in need of saving had succumbed. (
ENT
: "
Regeneration
", "
Impulse
";
TNG
: "
The Arsenal of Freedom
";
VOY
: "
The 37's
", etc.)
Automation took place on
Earth
as early as the
19th century
, when workers who felt their
livelihood
threatened by automation flung their
sabots
into the machines to stop them. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
Automated probes might be used in space exploration. In particular, species that did not have manned space travel might resort to exploring space using automated probes. Inversely, advanced civilizations might automate previously manned freighters so they no longer required
crew
. Within the Federation, the
Woden
was an example of such a ship. (
ENT
: "
Strange New World
";
TNG
: "
The Price
";
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
The
Constitution
-class
, at least in its refit configuration, critically relied on an
automation center
, without which its engineers could not control anything. According to Montgomery Scott, with sufficient automation a
chimpanzee
and two trainees could run such a ship. Gravitation could also be set on automatic on the
Constitution
-class. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
;
TOS
: "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
")
On the
24th century
Galaxy
-class
, 90% of what happened on the ship was done automatically and completely out of the control of the crew. (
TNG
: "
Contagion
") When the
saucer section
of this class needed to be re-docked after a
separation
, this was usually done through automation; however, manual
docking
was also
possible
. (
TNG
: "
Encounter at Farpoint
") Furthermore, an automated sequence for
departure
could be set in case ships needed to be abandoned. (
TNG
: "
11001001
")
When a
Tarellian starship
approached
Haven
in
2364
, it was speculated that the entire crew might be dead and that the ship might have been brought in solely by automation. (
TNG
: "
Haven
")
Automated security systems and programs might be deployed to protect installations in the absence of crew. (
ENT
: "
Countdown
";
DS9
: "
Civil Defense
", "
To the Death
";
VOY
: "
The Thaw
")
During the
23rd century
, an
unnamed farm planet
utilized automated machines. (
TOS
: "
Mudd's Women
")
An automated transponder sending back false sensor readings was instrumental in the
Battle of the Omarion Nebula
, a pivotal battle in the lead up to the
Dominion War
. (
DS9
: "
The Die is Cast
")
The
Devore Imperium
operated at least one automated
sensor array
. (
VOY
: "
Counterpoint
")
Automated systems
Auto-ascent sequencer
Auto-destruct sequencer
Auto-navigation
Automated distress signal
Automated fire system
Automated Personnel Unit
Automated repair station
Automatic bridge defense system
Automatic locking device
Automatic transmission station
Automaton
Cargo drone
Delta Vega
lithium cracking station
Emergency Medical Holographic program
Exocomp
M-4 robot
M-5 multitronic unit
Planet killer
Probe
Robot
Robot ship
Sensor drone
External link
Automation
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Automaton
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Automaton
======================================================================

An
automaton
was a relatively self-operating
machine
or mechanism designed to
automatically
follow or respond to predetermined
instructions
.
Webster's 24th Century Dictionary
, Fifth
Edition
, defined an
android
as an automaton made to resemble a
Human
being. (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
In
2369
,
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
recalled
when
Hugh
was separated from the
Borg Collective
and began to grow and evolve into something other than an automaton and become a
person
. (
TNG
: "
Descent
")
In
2373
,
Riley Frazier
assured
Chakotay
that she,
Orum
, and the others of the
Cooperative
were
individuals
with distinct
personalities
and were not going to turn Chakotay into some kind of automaton. (
VOY
: "
Unity
")
That same year,
The Doctor
referred to
holograms
inside a
holodeck
version of the
Paxau Resort
as automatons and
mannequin
simulacra
with no
secrets
to reveal. (
VOY
: "
Darkling
")
In
2374
, there was a disagreement between
Seven of Nine
and
B'Elanna Torres
in the
USS
Voyager
's
mess hall
where Torres referred to Seven as an automaton and employed a series of
profane
Klingon
insults
. (
VOY
: "
Concerning Flight
")

External link

======================================================================
PAGE: Axanar cargo ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Axanar_cargo_ship
======================================================================

Axanar cargo ship
Affiliation:
Axanar
Type:
Freighter
Active:
22nd century
Length:
92
meters
Crew complement:
15
The
Axanar cargo ship
was a type of
freighter
used by the
Axanar
during the mid-
22nd century
. Crewed by as many as fifteen, a typical ship of this type was approximately ninety-two
meters
in length, and had a
hull
comprised of
tritanium
and
disilicon polymers
.
In early
2151
,
officers
from the
starship
Enterprise
NX-01
discovered and boarded one such vessel. After some trouble opening the
airlock
door, they discovered that unknown aliens had
murdered
the vessel's crew members and drained their bodies of
triglobulin
. The unknown aliens later returned in
their vessel
and attacked
Enterprise
but were in turn destroyed by
another Axanar vessel
. (
ENT
: "
Fight or Flight
")
In the script of "Fight or Flight", the Axanar cargo vessel was described as "
an extremely alien design: braided conduits run down the length of the hull, and it's impossible to tell the bow from the stern. The ship has a rusted-out, space-worn appearance.
" In the same script, the innards of the craft were described thus; "
The ship's interior looks old and shows signs of wear and tear – corroded metal, dents and scoring on the bulkheads, etc.
"
A specific
deleted scene
from "Fight or Flight" (available in
ENT Season 1 DVD
and
Blu-ray
) is set on the
bridge
of the Axanar craft. A schematic there displays the ship, specifically highlighting the vehicle's power layout.
For more information on this
studio model
, please see
ENT studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Axanar warship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Axanar_warship
======================================================================

Axanar warship
Operator:
Axanar
Type:
Warship
Active:
22nd century
Armament:
Particle beam weapons
The
Axanar warship
was a type of
warship
used by the
Axanar
during the mid-
22nd century
.
Armament and Specifications
This type of vessel was heavily armed, possessing a minimum of three high-yield
particle beam weapons
, as it's weapons were formidable.  (
ENT
: "
Fight or Flight
")
History
In
2151
, a ship of this type responded to a
distress signal
sent from an
Axanar cargo ship
by the crew of
Enterprise
NX-01
. There, the Axanar warship discovered
Enterprise
had been attacked by an
unidentified starship
and was about to be boarded. After learning that the hostile vessel was responsible for killing the cargo ship's crew and not the
Enterprise
, the combat vessel freed
Enterprise
from a
stabilizing beam
and proceeded to destroy the hostile vessel without difficulty. (
ENT
: "
Fight or Flight
")
Assuming the warship's hull was of similar construction to the cargo vessel's, at minimum, it was comprised of
tritanium
and
disilicon
polymers.
In the script for "Fight or Flight", the Axanar warship was described thus; "
It's similar in design to the cargo vessel, but larger, a warship.
" A description slightly later in the script reads, "
We can tell by the interior architecture that the ship is similar, as well.
"
Trivia
The Axanar warship concept was both designed and handled by
John Eaves
.

======================================================================
PAGE: B'omar patrol ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/B'omar_patrol_ship
======================================================================

Three B'omar patrol ships
The
B'omar patrol ship
was a type of small
starship
used by the
B'omar Sovereignty
to guard
B'omar
space. It was armed with
directed energy weapons
and
torpedoes
, and protected by
shields
. Tactically, it was inferior to a
Federation
Type 6 shuttlecraft
.
When the
USS
Voyager
sought to cross
B'omar space
in
2374
, it was met by patrol ships carrying B'omar representatives. During the negotiations,
Seven of Nine
stole a shuttle and entered B'omar space, facing a number of patrol ships while attempting to reach the wreck of the
SS
Raven
. After Seven returned to
Voyager
, a fleet of sixty-eight B'omar ships forced them to withdraw from B'omar space. (
VOY
: "
The Raven
")
On the call sheet for Tuesday
22 July
1997
, the lead vessel is listed as "Dumah's fighter vessel" regarding the scene filmed from inside the
cockpit
.

======================================================================
PAGE: BC 011
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/BC_011
======================================================================

BC 011
Active:
2360s
-
2370
Speed:
Warp 8.7
Armament:
Disruptor array
Deflectors
Energy sheath
"
BC 011
" was the
ship classification
given by
Starfleet Operations
to a
mercenary vessel
operated, for a time in
2369
and
2370
, by
Arctus Baran
. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
Technical data
Defensive systems
Firing disruptors
This
weapon systems
of this class of vessel consisted of a main
disruptor array
that was channeled through dual forward
disruptors
. These disruptors could be configured to fire a significantly more powerful
phase-resonant pulse
.
When faced with the prospect of attacking the
Starfleet
outpost
on
Caldos II
, which was defended with a
type 4 deflector shield
, a minimum of two
phaser banks
, and possibly
photon torpedoes
; by Starfleet's own standards this gave the outpost limited defensive capabilities. To that end, Baran contested their "
Our weapons are more than a match for their defenses. I anticipate we'll be able to destroy the outpost in less than fifteen
minutes
.
"
When faced by a
Galaxy
-class
, during its initial attack on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, the mercenary ship merely scored a minor hit on the port deflector, which caused no damage. During a second engagement, the
Enterprise
's opening barrage scored a direct hit on the aft
deflectors
on Baran's ship, but caused no damage.
The class, which was encased in an
energy sheath
that rendered the ship virtually undetectable to
Federation
long-range sensors
, also prevented
short-range sensors
from penetrating its
hull
.
It also possessed the ability to jam local
com systems
and create an effect similar to that caused by a
transport inhibitor
. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
")
Propulsion systems
Thought capable of a
maximum speed
of
warp 8.7
, the
engines
of this class of vessel were prone to maintenance issues if they were pushed too hard.
After narrowly escaping a raid on
Barradas III
, two of the
power shunts
on Baran's mercenary vessel were on the verge of collapse, which limited the ship to, at best, warp 6.
Repair could be achieved with the realignment of the
warp core
, which was an eleven hour task that required a complete shutdown of the engines. Without a shutdown, the same repairs could be attempted by bypassing the
aft
power couplings
, which, in turn caused a temporary power loss.
During a deliberate sabotage created by
Galen
to mimic a
system failure
, the
antimatter flow regulator
in the engine's
intermix chamber
became stuck open, the ship jumped to warp as the
plasma
pressure simultaneously rose. The source of the malfunction was not immediately determined, as it could have originated in any one of thirty
subsystems
, including the
logic subsystem
and
regulator subsystem
.
At fourteen percent above critical, the
containment fields
began to degrade,  but the overall issue at hand, a
control logic lockout
, could be alleviated through an active bypass through the
plasma flow converter
. This was accomplished by running a delayed
phase-locked feedback
through the malfunctioning regulator. To prevent the recurrence of such a malfunction, it was necessary to re-initiate the
regulator sub-compressors
, followed by a recommend
full diagnostic
of the intermix chamber. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
")
Interior design
Bridge
Interior bridge
Viewscreen
The
bridge
...
One corner of the bridge contained a
transporter pad
, capable of transporting at least five individuals, which operated with a type of high energy
transporter beam
. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
According to the script for "Gambit, Part I", "
the mercenary bridge is sleek and spare with no wasted space, just like everything on this ship. A large
Viewscreen
dominates the forward section and there's a small Transporter pad in one corner. The walls are lined with [manned]
consoles
.  [...] In the center of the bridge is a raised platform encircled by a rail and a console. From here, the Captain commands an unobstructed view of the entire room. It should feel as though this ship and crew are always ready for battle.
"
Cargo hold
The
cargo hold
...  (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
According to the script for "Gambit, Part I", "
The room [cargo bay] looks like a cross between an archaeological museum and a science lab. The walls are jammed with scientific consoles, and the remaining spaces are filled with artifacts scattered around the room in and out of cargo containers.
"
Quarters
Riker's, Baran's, and Galen's
quarters
...  (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
According to the script for "Gambit, Part II", "
In contrast to the other [Riker's] quarters on the mercenary ship, Baran's quarters border on the luxurious...overstuffed furniture, gaudy sculpture -- very decadent. Baran is lounging in a chair while Riker sits on a couch...
" Later, "Galen's" quarters were described as "
A small, cramped room with only the barest of accommodations.
"
Ships of the class
Baran's mercenary ship
Appendices
See also
Theta
-class
Appearances
TNG
:
"
Gambit, Part I
"
"
Gambit, Part II
"
Background information
Ship classification from
okudagram
(lower right corner)
This classification was only mentioned in writing.
This vessel was described in the
script
for "Gambit, Part I" as a "sleek, dangerous-looking mercenary ship".

Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection

.

PAGE: Ba'Neth ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ba'Neth_ship
======================================================================

Ba'Neth ship
Affiliation:
Ba'Neth
Active:
24th century
The
Ba'Neth ship
was a type of
starship
utilized by the
Ba'Neth
, a
xenophobic
species in the
Delta Quadrant
, first discovered in
2376
.
Little was known about this type of starship, as they remain
cloaked
and were very difficult to detect. One of the Ba'Neth's neighboring species, the
Kesat
, believed the Ba'Neth to be a myth, until investigator
Naroq
proved otherwise.
A Ba'Neth ship attacked the
Delta Flyer
in
2376
, but the shuttle became the first vessel to survive an attack by such a ship.
Lieutenant Commander
Tuvok
however, was seriously injured by a Ba'Neth intruder, but with the help of Naroq and a
photolitic converter
, the
USS
Voyager
was able to detect several Ba'Neth ships in close proximity to a space station, where they traded the converter for information which led to Tuvok's recovery. (
VOY
: "
Riddles
")
For more information on the
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Ba'ul fighter
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ba'ul_fighter
======================================================================

Siranna's Ba'ul fighter
The
Ba'ul fighter
was a type of small,
warp-capable
attack fighter
built by the
Ba'ul
. It was flown by a single
pilot
and mounted
directed energy weaponry
.
After receiving a farewell message from
Saru
in
2257
, his sister
Siranna
organized a group of Ba'ul fighters to assist
USS
Discovery
and
USS
Enterprise
in their battle against a
Section 31
armada
. They were contacted by
Ash Tyler
en route, and thus arrived on the battlefield alongside a
Klingon
force. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Ba'ul sentry ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ba'ul_sentry_ship
======================================================================

Ba'ul sentry ships with active weapons
The
Ba'ul sentry ship
was a type of
starship
used by the
Ba'ul
to defend their
homeworld
of
Kaminar
. They were much larger than a
Federation
Crossfield
-class
starship, and featured multiple
weapons
emplacements that extended outward when active.
In
2257
, the
Ba'ul High Council
sent ten sentry ships to surround
USS
Discovery
, to reinforce their demand that
Commander
Saru
be handed over to them. The ships stood down and returned to Kaminar after Saru surrendered himself. (
DIS
: "
The Sound of Thunder
")
This vessel was originally a "Ba'ul culling ship" before the episode story changed. Some of the earliest concept designs for it were inspired by
The War of the Worlds
.
[1]

PAGE: Barolian freighter

Barolian freighter
Affiliation:
Barolians
Type:
Freighter
Active:
24th century
A
Barolian freighter
was a type of
freighter
utilized by the
Barolians
for conducting
trade
during the mid-
24th century
.
In
2368
, a
Ferengi
named
Omag
delivered the
stolen
Vulcan
transport
T'Pau
to a Barolian freighter at
Galorndon Core
, near the
Romulan Neutral Zone
.
A short time later, it was discovered in
Romulan
subspace
logs
that a transmission was made by the
Tal Shiar
from
Romulus
to a Barolian freighter near Galorndon Core revealing a number: "1400." This indicated the time which the Romulan "peace envoy" was set to launch on their trip to
Vulcan
. (
TNG
: "
Unification II
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Batris
CATEGORIES: Starships

Batris
Class:
Freighter
Affiliation:
Talarian
Status:
Destroyed
(
2364
)
The
Batris
was a
Talarian
freighter
that was in service in the mid-
24th century
.
In
2364
, the
Batris
was commandeered by three renegade
Klingons
led by
Commander
Korris
. The
ship
was tracked down in the
Neutral Zone
by the
cruiser
IKS
T'Acog
. After a brief battle, the renegades agreed to surrender, but when the
T'Acog
dropped its
shields
they opened fire with the
merculite rockets
that comprised the
Batris
' armament. The
T'Acog
was destroyed, but the
Batris
was heavily damaged.
Alerted to the battle,
Starfleet
requested the
USS
Enterprise
-D
to investigate the incident. Upon arriving at the battle site, an
away team
from the
Enterprise
successfully rescued the three Klingons seconds before the
Batris
self-destructed. (
TNG
: "
Heart of Glory
")
For more information on this
studio model
, see
TNG studio models
.
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 31 speculated that the Talarian freighter design was an
Antares
-class
cargo carrier.

Batris
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Baxial
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Baxial
======================================================================

Baxial
Class:
Baxial
-type
Freighter
Affiliation:
Talaxian
Status:
Active
(
2378
)
"
…I thought she was the ugliest thing I ever saw. But she grew on me. Eventually, I couldn't imagine being without her.
"
–
Neelix
,
2376
("
Alice
")
The
Baxial
was a
Talaxian
freighter
that was in service in the late
24th century
. In the
2370s
, the
Baxial
was owned by
Neelix
. During his stay aboard the
USS
Voyager
, the
Baxial
was stored in the
starship
's
shuttlebay
. (
VOY
: "
Alice
")
When he first saw it, Neelix thought the
Baxial
was the ugliest thing he'd ever seen. But eventually, he couldn't imagine being without it. (
VOY
: "
Alice
") A preserved
Talaxian Furfly
hung in the
engine room
of the ship for six years. Neelix later gave the fly to
B'Elanna Torres
as a
good luck charm
, after the construction of the
quantum slipstream drive
on the USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Timeless
")
In
2371
, the
Baxial
was sorting through wreckage in a
debris
field near
Ocampa
when
Voyager
approached it, asking for information on the
Caretaker
's
array
. After successfully rescuing their crew members,
Captain
Janeway
had Neelix's shuttle refueled, and prepared to depart, but Neelix and newly freed
Kes
requested to remain aboard, which led to the
Baxial
being stored in
Voyager
's shuttlebay. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
")
Neelix on the bridge of the
Baxial
Shortly after Neelix decided to stay aboard
Voyager
, the ship encountered a
nucleogenic cloud being
, which the crew decided to explore.
Voyager
accidentally damaged the cloud after it left and Janeway decided to return and help it heal. Neelix demanded that the captain not return to it, as he believed it was too dangerous, or to drop him and Kes off and pick them up later. Janeway bluntly stated that there wasn't time to prep
Baxial
for launch, and he'd have to sit tight. (
VOY
: "
The Cloud
")
In
2373
, Neelix and a
security team
broke into an
Akritirian
prison
using the
Baxial
. Janeway opted to use the
Baxial
for this task because Akritirian patrols would have attacked
Voyager
if it had entered Akritirian space. (
VOY
: "
The Chute
")
In
2376
, when
Seven of Nine
was researching the possibility of a conspiracy aboard
Voyager
, she asked Neelix for his ship's
sensor
logs
from the time period around when
Voyager
was trapped in the
Delta Quadrant
. Included among the logs were records of 52 vessels, including a
Cardassian
ship
, being brought to the Delta Quadrant. (
VOY
: "
The Voyager Conspiracy
")
In
2377
, Neelix and
Commander
Chakotay
used the
Baxial
to get through a
Quarren
security perimeter, in search of
Voyager
's crew, who'd had their memories erased. (
VOY
: "
Workforce
", "
Workforce, Part II
")
Upon discovery of a
Talaxian asteroid colony
which was being bullied by a
mining
team in
2378
, Neelix used the
Baxial
to protect another
Talaxian ship
, which was planting
force field
emitters in the
asteroid
's surface. Following Neelix's decision to stay at the Talaxian colony, he piloted his shuttle there. (
VOY
: "
Homestead
")
Appendices
Appearances
VOY
:
"
Caretaker
" (interior only)
"
The Chute
" (first appearance)
"
Workforce
"
"
Workforce, Part II
"
"
Homestead
"
References
VOY
:
"
The Cloud
"
"
Alice
"
"
The Voyager Conspiracy
"
Background information
In the first draft script for "Caretaker", the
Baxial
was described as "a strange, small alien pod poking about amid the
flotsam
, like a scavenger combing through a
trash can
." The vessel was scripted to have a "cramped and messy cabin [...] filled with an eclectic assortment of junk."
The
studio model
of the
Baxial
was later seen as a background ship in episodes of
Joss Whedon
's series
Firefly
.
The vessel may have been based on
Jaheel's ship
from
DS9
: "
Babel
", as per
Ex Astris Scientia
.

Baxial
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

A Benkaran shuttle
A
Benkaran shuttle
was a
starship
operated by the
Benkarans
in the
Delta Quadrant
.
In
2377
a Benkaran shuttle attacked the
USS
Voyager
to liberate the Benkaran prisoners including
Joleg
and
Egrid
but was damaged by
Voyager
and had to retreat.
Neelix
, who read the security protocols of
warden
Yediq
, told Joleg later that the ship was able to detect the
Voyager
after he sent a message to Joleg's
brother
. (
