")

======================================================================
PAGE: Benthan fighter ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Benthan_fighter_ship
======================================================================

Benthan fighter ships intercepting the coaxial warp ship
The
Benthan fighter ships
were
starships
used by the
Benthan Guard
. They were equipped with
phasers
and
tractor beams
.
In
2374
,
Steth's ship
was intercepted by two
Benthan
fighter ships in the
Kotaba Expanse
, who were looking for
Steth
for theft of the vessel. They were driven off by
another starship
piloted by
Daelen
, who was in reality, the real
Steth
. (
VOY
: "
Vis à Vis
")
Appendices
Background information
These vessels were described in the script as "
two distinctly unfriendly-looking fighter-ships.
"
The creation of the scene in which Paris, in Steth's body, is confronted by
Daelen's ship
and a pair of Benthan fighter ships utilized
CGI models
designed by the visual effects company
Digital Muse
. Ron Moore said of the vessels, "
We weren't going to see them much, just [in] a couple of shots [...] I would get sketches from Muse and pick a direction that we'd want to go, make whatever changes we felt necessary. That's what we did for both [ship classes]. We wanted to play this size thing a little bit, with the biggest one being Daelen's ship.
" The size differential helped make it conceivable that the arrival of Daelen's ship would intimidate the Benthan crafts into immediately departing, relieving the visual effects artists of having to create a big battle. (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 30, No. 9/10, p. 90)
Apocrypha
These vessels were also playable in
Star Trek Online
's
Delta Rising
expansion as a prize from the Delta Expedition Lock Box, dubbed the Benthan assault cruiser (
β
). It also appears among Benthan NPCs. The Benthans also possess frigates and cruisers, respectively shrunken and enlarged versions of the patrol ship. For unexplained reasons, the
STO
version of the ship flies with the bow and stern reversed from the TV version.

======================================================================
PAGE: Benzite ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Benzite_ship
======================================================================

A
Benzite ship
was a type of
starship
in the
24th century
. One of the
regulations
observed aboard these ships, contrary to that aboard
Federation
starships, was that no
officer
would report an out of the ordinary occurrence until he had a full analysis and resolution.
Ensign
Mendon
was well versed in the protocols aboard Benzite ships before he served aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
as a member of the
Officer Exchange Program
in
2365
. (
TNG
: "
A Matter Of Honor
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Beta-matrix compositor
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Beta-matrix_compositor
======================================================================

The
beta-matrix compositor
was a component of
Cardassian
plasma distribution manifolds
, which could not be
replicated
.
In
2373
,
Deep Space 9
's plasma distribution manifold needed to be repaired, but the beta-matrix compositor could not be replaced by
Starfleet
.
Chief
Miles O'Brien
then led a
salvage team
to the abandoned
space station
Empok Nor
. (
DS9
: "
Empok Nor
")
See also
Theta-matrix compositor

======================================================================
PAGE: Betazoid gift box
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Betazoid_gift_box
======================================================================

A Betazoid gift box
An opened gift box
"
Blech, a Betazoid gift box. Things creep me out.
"
–
Beckett Mariner
,
2381
("
In the Cradle of Vexilon
")
The
Betazoid gift box
was an
artifact
originating from the
planet
Betazed
that was designed to be given as a "
bonding gift
" to those who were to be
married
. The gift box resembled a silver
chest
, except that it had a seemingly living,
humanoid
-like
face
imprinted on its front. This face was capable of
speech
and movement, and could thus be used to convey messages to the recipient.
In
2364
,
Counselor
Deanna Troi
received a Betazoid gift box from
Steven
and
Victoria Miller
as a
wedding
gift, as she had been bonded to their
son
,
Wyatt
. When the box was first
transported
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, it appeared to be in a sleeping state. But when Troi entered the
transporter room
and spoke, the box "awakened" and informed Troi that her
mother
,
Lwaxana Troi
, and the Millers would soon arrive and that her wedding
day
was near. With its message delivered, the gift box went back to "sleep". (
TNG
: "
Haven
")
Kerner Hauze
had a Betazoid gift box in his collection at the time of his death in
2381
. (
LD
: "
Kayshon, His Eyes Open
") Later that year,
Lieutenant jgs
Beckett Mariner
,
Sam Rutherford
, and
D'Vana Tendi
discovered another gift box of this type within the
anomaly storage room
aboard the
USS
Cerritos
. This particular gift box, however, had picked up the speech patterns of
Doctor
T'Ana
and (presumably) others it had encountered, and regularly uttered random and unconnected phrases, including strings of
profanity
and complete
gibberish
, as a result. (
LD
: "
In the Cradle of Vexilon
")
Appendices
Background information
In "Haven", the "face on gift-box" (or simply "face", as it was described in the
script
) was portrayed by
Armin Shimerman
, who went on to appear as, among others,
Quark
on
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
. In "In the Cradle of Vexilon", the face was voiced by an
unknown actor
.
Shimerman later wrote a scene with Quark selling a gift box to a
Bolian
in his novel
The 34th Rule
. The Bolian comments that the face on the box looks like Quark, but then decides he must be imagining things.
In a
deleted scene
, it was revealed that Betazoid gift boxes evidently had a variety of "faces" to choose from as Victoria Miller noted that her
husband
had chosen the "ugliest face imaginable", but Steven defended his decision, believing it to be a face that was "full of character."
External link
Betazoid gift box
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Billups tube
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Billups_tube
======================================================================

Billups tubes
The
Billups tubes
were an improved model of the older
Tucker tubes
. This was due to the fact that the Billups tubes had a third
tube
, whereas the Tucker tubes only had one or two.
This
device
was built by
Ensign
Livik
of the
USS
Cerritos
, which made his
superior officer
,
Andy Billups
, quite impressed. Livik even
named
the device after Billups, both in an attempt to gain his favor, and to one-up Ensign
Sam Rutherford
. (
LD
: "
I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Biogenic weapon
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Biogenic_weapon
======================================================================

Biogenic weapons in liquid form
Biogenic weapons
,
bioweapons
, or
biogenic devices
were a classification of
weapon
that caused little or no damage to
starships
or infrastructure, but instead were designed to
biogenically
interfere with
lifeforms
, most commonly to attack
people
. This category included
genetically-engineered
viruses
,
poisonous
chemical compounds
, and other similar
materials
. Examples of biogenic weapons included
cobalt diselenide
, which was deadly to
Cardassians
, and
trilithium resin
, which was deadly to
Humans
. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
")
Bio-mimetic gel
could be used to make biogenic weapons when combined with retroviral
vaccines
. (
DS9
: "
In the Pale Moonlight
";
TNG
: "
Preemptive Strike
")
History
One of the options pursued by the
Xindi
for the destruction of Humanity was a deadly
bio-weapon
, which they attempted to unleash on
Earth
in the year
2004
. (
ENT
: "
Rajiin
", "
Carpenter Street
")
The
Harvesters
, developed by the
T'Lani
and the
Kellerun
, were
nanobiogenic
weapons. (
DS9
: "
Armageddon Game
")
In
2373
, while in the employ of
Hagath
and
Gaila
,
Quark
claimed he'd arranged to procure a quantity of
Varaxian LM-7
, an experimental biogenic weapon that could wipe out millions of people within the space of a week. (
DS9
: "
Business as Usual
")
The
Dominion
was known to use biogenic weapons as well. One example is the
Teplan blight
, a virus that afflicted the people of the
Teplan system
when they resisted the
Jem'Hadar
. (
DS9
: "
The Quickening
", "
Inter Arma Enim Silent Leges
")
The
Maquis
gained possession of biogenic weapons after capturing the cargo of several
freighters
.
Michael Eddington
used them in attacks on Cardassian planets in the
Demilitarized Zone
until he was stopped by
Benjamin Sisko
. Several months later, Sisko was concerned the remaining Maquis biogenic weapons could have been placed on
cloaked
missiles. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
", "
Blaze of Glory
")
Thalaron radiation
had biogenic properties that made it useful as a weapon. The
Reman
warbird
Scimitar
was designed to emit thalaron radiation as a biogenic weapon capable of destroying all life on a
class M
planet
. (
Star Trek Nemesis
)
The
Federation
generally disapproved of the use of such weapons. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
")
In
The Voyager Encounter
holoprogram
,
The Doctor
designed biogenic weapons for the
warship
Voyager
to use against the
Kyrians
. His bioweapons resulted in eight million casualties during the
Great War
. (
VOY
: "
Living Witness
")
In
2377
, the
Lokirrim
accused the crew of the
Delta Flyer
of manufacturing
viral weapons
, after discovering
biomatter
onboard that The Doctor had been studying. (
VOY
: "
Body and Soul
")
According to the script for "Blaze of Glory", the pronunciation for biogenic was "bi-oh-GEN-ik".
[1]
See also
Biogenic agents
Biogenic charge
Biological weapon
Metagenic weapon
Mutagenic retrovirus
Nano-biogenic weapon
Transgenic weapon

======================================================================
PAGE: Biological weapon
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Biological_weapon
======================================================================

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
A
biological weapon
or
bio-agent
was a
weapon
that operates by spreading a
pathogen
such as a
virus
or a
bacterium
to the intended targets. This weapon is generally either lethal or at least physiologically devastating to those afflicted.
Genetic engineering
was not infrequently used in the creation of such weapons.
Nanotechnology
was also known to be used on occasion. A sub-category, bio-chemical weapons, combined elements, usually artificially, of both biological and chemical weapons. One example of such weaponry was advanced pathogenic
toxins
, with another being synthetic
prion
-based agents.
Malcolm Reed
told
General
Gosis
a
lie
during the
interrogation
when he said he and
Captain
Jonathan Archer
were
genetically enhanced
and their
immune systems
were resistent to
chemical
and biological weapons. (
ENT
: "
The Communicator
")
On
Earth
, the use of biological weapons was outlawed (in theory at least) by the
Geneva Protocol of 1928
and
2155
. (
DIS
: "
Context Is for Kings
")
In
2268
,
Leonard McCoy
, while studying an infection on
Omega IV
, found that it resembled the bacteriological warfare experiments waged on Earth in the
1990s
. (
TOS
: "
The Omega Glory
")
The
Tarellian
civilization was wiped out by biological weapons during a
civil war
, leaving a small number of
plague
-carrying survivors who escaped, typically
infecting
the populations of other
planets
after establishing contact. (
TNG
: "
Haven
")
Metagenic weapons
were a biological weapon capable of destroying all
DNA
it encountered, as well as
planetary
ecosystems
. In
2369
,
Jean-Luc Picard
,
Beverly Crusher
, and
Worf
were sent to
Celtris III
by
Starfleet Intelligence
to locate and destroy this biological weapon the
Cardassians
were supposedly developing. (
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part I
")
The
Dominion
at times used biological weapons to make examples of populations that resisted their rule, including the
Teplans
, afflicted with
the blight
, and the inhabitants of
Boranis III
. (
DS9
: "
The Quickening
", "
Inter Arma Enim Silent Leges
")
In
2373
, upon initially seeing a
Species 8472 bio-ship
,
Commander
Chakotay
speculated that it was either a
space-dwelling organism
or biological weapon. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
In a
holoprogram
titled
The Voyager Encounter
, the
Emergency Medical Android
had examined the
Kyrian
genome
and determined they would be vulnerable to any number of biological weapons. The Emergency Medical Android later established a
datalink
between his
neural net
and the
phaser array
of the
warship Voyager
to reconfigure the
phaser beam
to carry a bio-agent into the
atmosphere
of the
Kyrian and Vaskan homeworld
for the targeting of
Kyrian cities
. This attack caused 3,000 Kyrian
deaths
; however, the
fatality rate
would be 300,000 in one
hour
as the bio-agent was still dispersing through the atmosphere. The
death toll
could've been much higher had there not already been an
evacuation
taking place which resulted in thousands of lives being saved. (
VOY
: "
Living Witness
")
In
2375
,
Species 8472
accused the
USS
Voyager
of having created biological weapons and infecting their
species
. In addition they accused
Voyager
off
allying
with the
Borg
. (
VOY
: "
In the Flesh
")
The
Brunali
created a
spaceborne virus
that they then used to infect the Borg via children they deliberately allowed to be
assimilated
.
Icheb
was one such child, who (unknowingly) successfully transferred the virus to the
Borg cube
that assimilated him, killing all of the
crew
except for those
Borg drones
still in their
maturation chambers
. (
VOY
: "
Collective
", "
Child's Play
")
See also
Biogenic weapon
Harvester
Nanite
External link
Biological weapon
at
Wikipedia

.

disruptor snares

Kazon-Ogla
training
moon
of
Tarok
by
2372
. (

: "

")

Bollards (right)
A
bollard
was a
metal
post used to block
traffic
.
In
2022
, a
Toronto
alley
La'An Noonien-Singh
and
James T. Kirk
were standing in had yellow bollards in it. (
SNW
: "
Tomorrow and Tomorrow and Tomorrow
")
In
2385
,
silver
bollards lined a passage under the awning of
School shuttle stop
E01
. (
ST
: "
Children of Mars
")

at
Wikipedia

Spock Prime

death
from a Book. (

Diagnostic wrap
and the
Quarantine

======================================================================
PAGE: Bothan starships
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bothan_starships
======================================================================

The
Bothan starships
were illusory vessels created by a telepathic member of the
Botha
during his encounter with
USS
Voyager
in
2372
. Only one of the ships actually existed, the other two being figments of the crew's imagination caused by the
Bothan
's
psionic field
. (
VOY
: "
Persistence of Vision
")
Bothan starship, type 1
Bothan starship, type 2
Bothan starship, type 3
Background information
For information about the history and design of the "type 1"
studio model
, please see:
TNG studio models
; for the "type 2" model, please see:
VOY studio models
; and for the "type 3" mode, please see:
Kazon fighter
.
The appearance of what was otherwise seen as a
Kazon raider
may be an example of
Neelix
's comment stating: "
There are lots of rumors about ships entering Bothan space never to be heard from again.
" In addition, "
my sources on
Mithren
say that they have lost a number of vessels,
" also suggests that one of these designs formerly belonged to the Mithren.

A
brazier
was a type of
heater
.
In
2374
,
Sirella
criticized
Jadzia Dax
because, among other things, "
the placement of the braziers on their
pedestals
is sloppy.
" Jadzia replied that they were heavy, to which Sirella noted that a
Klingon
woman
wouldn't complain. Dax then said that a Klingon woman wouldn't have to repeat the ceremonial act three times. Sirella then explained that a Klingon woman would have gotten it right the first time. (
DS9
: "
You Are Cordially Invited
")

Devore Imperium

for viewing and changing

engineer

's

and

======================================================================
PAGE: Bridge simulator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bridge_simulator
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
A
bridge simulator
was a device used at
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
for training
cadets
in
navigation
and
starship
handling. The bridge mock-up was based on then-current bridge designs.
Simulations
were monitored from a room adjacent to the simulator. Exercises were programmed into the simulator computer by personnel assigned to the Academy, and the cadets were put through their paces using these exercises. One of the exercises performed in the simulator was the
Kobayashi Maru
scenario
. Simulations were monitored by technicians and observed by
officers
from a room adjacent to the simulator. After an exercise, the cadets' performance was reviewed in a
briefing room
. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
;
Star Trek
)
The simulator was rigged with pyrotechnics in order to realistically simulate battle conditions. As a result of the pyrotechnics, the simulator frequently required maintenance.
Smoking
was prohibited in the simulator. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
With the advent of
holographic technology
, physical simulators fell out of use as similar conditions could be reproduced on
holodecks
. (
VOY
: "
Learning Curve
",
PRO
: "
Kobayashi
")
Simulators
USS
Trainer
Mark III bridge simulator
Mark IV bridge simulator
Mark X bridge simulator

======================================================================
PAGE: Briori ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Briori_ship
======================================================================

The
Briori ship
was a type of
starship
used by the
Briori
in the
20th century
. According to
John Evansville
, their ships were not unlike
USS
Voyager
.
In
1937
, this ship transported three hundred
abducted
Humans
from
Earth
to a
planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
. When the colony was discovered in
2371
,
Voyager
's
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
had hoped to find the Briori ship and "
possibly use it to get back to Earth a little faster,
" whereupon she learned that during the
revolt
against their Briori masters, this ship was utterly destroyed. (
VOY
: "
The 37's
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Brunali transport vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Brunali_transport_vessel
======================================================================

A Brunali transport vessel
The
Brunali transport vessel
was a class of
starship
in use by the
Brunali
until at least
2376
.
In
2375
, this type of vessel was used to deliver
Icheb
to the
Borg
in the hopes the
pathogen
he carried would destroy them once
assimilated
.
A year later, after learning Icheb had been rescued by the crew of
USS
Voyager
, Icheb's mother,
Yifay
, infected him again, taking him in a ship of this class in an attempt to have him re-assimilated by Borg. The plan ultimately failed when
Voyager
rescued Icheb. (
VOY
: "
Collective
", "
Child's Play
")
For more information on the
studio model
used, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Buffer
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Buffer
======================================================================

A buffer
A
buffer
was a component of
Bynar
technology
.
The device, worn at waist level, was used for the storage and retrieval of information. The buffers were programmed for
binary code
. (
TNG
: "
11001001
")
The script for "11001001" describes the Bynar buffers as "belt-carried oblong cases roughly 4" × 6" × 2" deep, with CHASER LIGHTS."
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Bug (defect)
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bug_(defect)
======================================================================

For the disambiguation page, please see
Bug
.
A
bug
was a
malfunction
causing a complex piece of
technology
, typically a
computer
, to behave differently from how it was envisioned to behave. One notable computer bug was the
Y2K bug
. (
VOY
: "
11:59
")
After a
disastrous
test of the
NX-Alpha
, a
warp five engine
test vehicle in
2143
,
Jonathan Archer
defended
NX Program
by saying that as they were developing a new engine, it was bound to have a few bugs to work out. (
ENT
: "
First Flight
")
In
2267
,
Lieutenant
Carolyn Palamas
delivered a
Pollux V
report
which detailed a strange lack of
intelligent life
on the
planet
. The entire
Beta Geminorum system
had been almost the same and the Pollux V findings bugged the
percentages
. (
TOS
: "
Who Mourns for Adonais?
")
In
2372
,
Quark
tried to introduce holographic
waiters
to his bar, but found that there were significant bugs to work out. (
DS9
: "
Bar Association
")
The development of the
Long-term Medical Holographic program
circa
2373
required bugs to be worked out. (
DS9
: "
Doctor Bashir, I Presume
")
After learning that
Soji Asha
, an
android
, had
dreams
,
Narissa
dismissed it as a bug. (
PIC
: "
The Impossible Box
")
See also
Glitch
Gremlin
External links
Bug (engineering)
at
Wikipedia
Hardware bug
at
Wikipedia
Software bug
at
Wikipedia

. (

's

. (

: "

")

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Burr
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Burr
======================================================================

A
burr
was a rough spot on a
metal
object.
In
3189
, attempting to reassure
Sylvia Tilly
as she sat in the
captain's chair
of
USS
Discovery
,
Michael Burnham
told her, "
Listen, there is a metal burr under the left
armrest
of the captain's chair. We had one in the same place on the
Shenzhou
. Construction
glitch
at the
San Francisco Fleet Yards
. Whenever
Georgiou
got into a tricky situation, and she felt like all the different choices were mind-numbing, she would press on it with her
thumb
. To kind of stay in the moment, you know? And the first time she made me
acting captain
, when I sat down I immediately felt for it. But all that was there was this little shiny spot where she had rubbed that burr into a dent. And I've seen
Saru
touch the one here, on
Discovery
. So... burr, dent, shiny spot... whatever, it's there for you. You belong in that chair, Tilly.
" (
DIS
: "
Su'Kal
")
External link
Burr (edge)
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Bynar ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bynar_ship
======================================================================

Bynar ship
A
Bynar ship
was a type of
starship
operated by the
Bynars
during the early
2380s
.
A ship of this class was stolen in
Bynar space
by
Nova One
in
2381
. The
USS
Cerritos
was ordered to investigate the incident because it was the closest ship to the scene. Meanwhile,
Brad Boimler
returned to
Earth
, where he eventually acquired surveillance footage from one of
AGIMUS
'
murder drones
, revealing that the ship was not destroyed, but rather taken under tow by the mysterious ship. (
LD
: "
A Few Badgeys More
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Bypass
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Bypass
======================================================================

Bypass
was an alternate
route
or
method
of performing a
task
, used when the usual
procedure
was not
possible
.
In
August
2151
,
T'Pol
asked
Shaw
aboard the
Earth Cargo Service
freighter
ECS
Fortunate
why the
power
of the
internal sensors
was rerouted to the
weapons system
. Shaw
replied
that the
Fortunate
was in a battle with the
Nausicaans
. When T'Pol told him she would remove this bypass as the freighter was no longer in a battle
situation
, Shaw told her to leave that the way it was. (
ENT
: "
Fortunate Son
")

bypass displacer

used by the

in the
24th century
.

Starfleet
salvage team sent to
Empok Nor
in

.

listed their necessity as "would be nice". (
DS9
: "
Empok Nor
")

Byzatium transport
was a type of
transport
utilized during the late

.
Six Byzatium transports were scheduled to dock at
Deep Space 9
shortly before
stardate 48467
. As a result,
Kira Nerys

cargo bays
3 and 4. (

: "

")

StarTrek.com
, the Byzatium were a space-faring species.

======================================================================
PAGE: C-57-D
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/C-57-D
======================================================================

C-57-D
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
C-57-D
was a
starship
that was registered for
Federation
travel in the late
24th century
.
In
2370
, the
captain
of this ship was
J.J. Adams
. The
starship
's point of departure was
Lunar Receiving
. The starship arrived at
Deep Space 9
on
stardate
47562.0. The C-57-D was listed on the
space station
's
arrival roster
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
production art
)
This starship was only mentioned in writing.
The ship and its captain were an
in-joke
reference to the United Planets Cruiser
C-57D
and its commander John J. Adams from the classic
1956
science fiction
movie
Forbidden Planet
.

PAGE: Caatati vessel (large)

Caatati vessel
Owner:
Caatati
Active:
24th century
Speed:
Warp-capable
For the smaller type of Caatati vessel, please see
Caatati vessel (small)
.
A
Caatati vessel
was a class of
starship
that was operated by the
Caatati
refugees
following their
assimilation
by the
Borg
in
2373
. In all thirty Caatati ships, both small and large, transported the last remaining Caatati refugees from the aftermath of the destruction of
their homeworld
, including several vessels of this larger type.
At least two of these larger vessels were included in the Caatati
armada
that tracked
Voyager
down delivering both
Voyager
's
warp core
and an ultimatum for more
food
and provisions. (
VOY
: "
Day of Honor
")

PAGE: Caatati vessel (small)
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Caatati_vessel_(small)

Caatati vessel
Owner:
Caatati
Active:
24th century
Crew complement:
approx. 88
Speed:
Warp-capable
For the larger type of Caatati vessel, please see
Caatati vessel (large)
.
A
Caatati vessel
was a class of
starship
that was operated by the
Caatati
refugees
following their
assimilation
by the
Borg
in
2373
. In all thirty Caatati ships, both small and large, transported the last remaining Caatati refugees from the aftermath of the destruction of
their homeworld
, including several vessels of this smaller type.
A number of small Caatati vessels were among the
armada
encountered by
USS
Voyager
in
2374
. They were capable of carrying a crew complement of eighty-eight, and were found to be far inferior to
Voyager
, but in numbers posed something of a threat to the
Federation
starship. (
VOY
: "
Day of Honor
")
This vessel design, specifically
Rahmin
's ship, was described in the script as being "
a small alien ship which has seen better days. It's a real junker.
"
For more information on the
studio model
used, see
VOY studio models
.

's

. (

Class:

Affiliation:

Status:

The

was a
Kressari

24th century
.
In
2370
, while carrying sensitive herbaceous
DNA
materials, the
Calondon
docked at
Deep Space 9
. The ship's
cargo
was thoroughly searched by DS9 personnel for six hours, which made the
freighter
's
captain
,
Zef'No
, threaten a formal protest by the Kressari government. The ship was then allowed to leave, but
Odo
had secretly stowed away onboard. The Calondon then met with the
Cardassians
and received
weapons
from them to transport those to the
Alliance for Global Unity
on
Bajor
, with the transfer being observed by Odo. (
DS9
: "
The Circle
")

studio model

TNG studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Cargo pod
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cargo_pod
======================================================================

A
cargo pod
was a detachable
container
that held
cargo
aboard a
starship
.
The
USS
Defiant
was equipped with cargo pods. In
2373
,
Captain
Sisko
ordered that cargo pods with two hundred
kilograms
of
trilithium resin
each be attached to
quantum torpedoes
. He later fired the torpedoes into the
atmosphere
of
Solosos III
, rendering the
planet
uninhabitable to
Humans
for fifty
years
. (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
")
See also
Cargo bay
Cargo container

.

grammar school

Daniel Byrd
was on it, in revenge for his teasing. (

: "
Juggernaut
")

to separate matter from a
stellar phenomenon
that the

Voyager
went through. (

")

" was sold off on the

on eBay.

Hikaru Sulu using a chemical sprayer
A
chemical sprayer
was a type of
device
used to apply
chemicals
in the form of a
mist
, such as
weed spray
.
During a rescue mission on
Phylos
in
2269
, the
landing party
consisting of
James T. Kirk
,
Hikaru Sulu
, and
Leonard McCoy
, came armed with chemical sprayers containing McCoy's home-brewed version of
his great-great granddaddy
's weed spray, which they used to neutralize attacking
swoopers
and aid in their rescue of
Spock
. (
TAS
: "
The Infinite Vulcan
")

Sprayer
at
Wikipedia

PAGE: Chemical weapon

Alternate timeline
(covers information from an
alternate timeline
)
A
chemical weapon
was a type of
weapon
that made use of
toxins
and/or other dangerous
chemical
substances
. A sub-category was bio-chemical weapons, which combined elements of both chemical and
biological
weapons.
The
Confederation Corps
used chemical weapons, among others, against its foes. (
PIC
: "
Penance
")
See also
Biogenic weapon

Chemical weapon
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Cheron scout vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cheron_scout_vessel
======================================================================

A
Cheron scout vessel
was a type of durable long-service
starship
utilized by
Commissioner
Bele
, the
Chief Officer
of the
Commission on Political Traitors
on
planet
Cheron
.
The ship was used to pursue escapees throughout
the galaxy
. These ships would last some 50,000 years of active service. They were equipped with a
transporter
capable of beaming through
shields
. The ship was sheathed in special materials giving it an invisibility screen similar to the the
2260s
Romulan
cloaking device
. The craft was visually invisible, but its location, configuration and size could be determined with
sensor
scans. The ship's configuration was not similar to anything encountered by the
Federation
before
2268
. The power source and weaponry of the craft could not be determined by sensor scans. The size of the craft allowed
Commander
Spock
to determine however it to be a craft large enough for a crew of one or two and designated to be a rapid scout vessel built for speed rather than combat. It was at least fast enough to collide with a
Constitution
-class
starship attempting to evade it.
These scout vessels were designed to disintegrate as a means of
auto-destruct
, killing any occupant on-board. This self-destruction was automatic when the ship's advanced qualities were completely drained. Bele's ship disintegrated upon his arrival to the
USS
Enterprise
in 2268. (
TOS
: "
Let That Be Your Last Battlefield
")
Bele's scout ship was conveniently invisible, a clever way for the show to save money on visual effects. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 40)

======================================================================
PAGE: Chroniton generator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Chroniton_generator
======================================================================

A
chroniton generator
was a device which could produce a form of
radiation
with
temporal
properties.
In
2374
, the crew of
Deep Space 9
developed a plan whereby a chroniton generator would be utilized to force a
Prophet
and a
Pah-wraith
out of the bodies of
Major
Kira
and
Jake Sisko
, respectively, in order to save the station from being destroyed in a battle between the two.
Captain
Sisko
, Jake's father, overruled the plan, believing the the battle between the two, referred to as "the
Reckoning
" in
Bajoran
mythology, must be allowed to play out. As the battle was taking place, however,
Kai
Winn
, in an attempt to subvert Sisko's role as
Emissary of the Prophets
, covertly activated the generator, putting a stop to the battle. (
DS9
: "
The Reckoning
")
See also
Chroniton

======================================================================
PAGE: Cigarette machine
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cigarette_machine
======================================================================

A
cigarette machine
was a type of
vending machine
which dispensed
cigarettes
. Activating the machine typically required either
coins
or paper
currency
.
In
1957
, the
Pine Tree Bar and Grill
in
Carbon Creek
,
Pennsylvania
on
Earth
had a cigarette machine. (
ENT
: "
Carbon Creek
")
A cigarette machine was included in a
holodeck
recreation of
New Orleans
'
Bourbon Street Barn
set in
1958
. (
TNG
: "
11001001
")
External link
Cigarette machine
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Circulating pump
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Circulating_pump
======================================================================

A circulating pump for a
PXK pergium reactor
A
circulating pump
or
pump mechanism
was a retardation mechanism used in
PXK pergium reactors
which prevented the reactor from going supercritical.
In
2267
, the
silicon-based lifeform
known as a
Horta
stole
the main circulating pump from the reactor used to power
life support
on the
Janus VI pergium mining colony
, forcing
USS
Enterprise
Chief Engineer
Montgomery Scott
to construct a makeshift replacement pump. Unfortunately, Scott noted, the replacement pump would not last long, and it was critical that
Captain
Kirk
find the pump that the Horta had stolen. Eventually,
Spock
was successful in making contact with the Horta via
mind meld
, and the pump was returned. (
TOS
: "
The Devil in the Dark
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Class 1 transport
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Class_1_transport
======================================================================

A
class 1 transport
was a type of unarmed
spacecraft
used by the
Brunali
in the late
24th century
. This spacecraft, at a minimum, could support one lifeform.
Icheb
was captured in a vessel of this type in
Grid 649
by the
Borg
. He was subsequently
assimilated
into the
Borg Collective
. (
VOY
: "
Child's Play
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Cleponji
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cleponji
======================================================================

"
That ship belongs in a museum. I'm afraid we're a little late; that call for help was probably initiated over a thousand years ago.
"
-
Picard
, upon viewing the
Cleponji
Cleponji
Class:
Promellian battle cruiser
Affiliation:
Promellian
Status:
Destroyed
(
2366
)
The
Cleponji
was a
Promellian battle cruiser
that was in service in the
14th century
. It was
commanded
by
Captain
Galek Sar
.
Following the destruction of the
planet
Orelious IX
, the
Cleponji
fell victim to a
booby trap
of several thousand
aceton assimilators
planted by their enemy, the
Menthars
, concealed in the rubble surrounding the ship.
While in this booby trap, the
Cleponji
was stripped of all
propulsion
and
weapons
systems from the
energy
drain caused by the assimilators. This left the Promellians unable to move or fight. The ship continued to be lashed with lethal
radiation
from the assimilators, eventually causing the
deaths
of its
crew
, which succumbed to
radiation poisoning
. The ship remained in the planet's
debris field
, preserved in near perfect condition, for the next millennium.
The
Cleponji
being destroyed by the
Enterprise
In
2366
, the
Cleponji
was discovered by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
while the latter was exploring the remains of Orelious IX. The
Enterprise
crew explored the derelict ship, and later arranged to have it towed to the
museum
at the
Astral V Annex
. It was later discovered that the
Enterprise
-D was also trapped in the same booby trap that the
Cleponji
had fallen victim to. Following the
Enterprise
-D's narrow escape from the assimilators, the
Cleponji
and surrounding
asteroids
were destroyed to prevent any further entrapment. (
TNG
: "
Booby Trap
")
According to the script for this episode,
[1]
the pronunciation for
Cleponji
was "Klep-ON-gee".
Personnel
See
:
Cleponji
personnel
See also
Captain's log,
Cleponji

Class:

-type
Affiliation:
Clickets
Status:

)
The

.

LD
: "
Veritas
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Clockwork
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Clockwork
======================================================================

Clockwork
refers to the inner workings of
mechanical
timepieces
such as clocks or
pocket watches
. It could also describe another organism of similar reliability.
In
2268
, when
Spock
suggested that if the
landing party
disbelieved in the
illusions
of the
Melkotian planet
, it could not harm them,
Leonard McCoy
replied, "
We (
humans
) don't have that clockwork ticker in our head like you do
" (i.e. can't just turn off their feelings.) (
TOS
: "
Spectre of the Gun
")
In
2365
,
Jean-Luc Picard
described the
universe
as having "clockwork perfection". (
TNG
: "
Where Silence Has Lease
")
In
2366
,
Paul Stubbs
noted that over and over again, the intense
gravitational
pull of a little
neutron star
, inside an
unnamed
binary star system
in the
Kavis Alpha sector
, sucked up
star material
from its neighboring
red giant
which built up on the surface until it
exploded
every 196
years
like clockwork. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")
External link
Clockwork
at
Wikipedia

A
close range
was a short
distance
between two
objects
.
When, in
2364
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
was studying the
Delos
sun
at close range, the
star
's intense
magnetic fields
had a disruptive effect on the
electrical systems
. This resulted in communications interruptions and the temporary loss of other systems. (
TNG
: "
Symbiosis
")

Quark using a code sequencer

was a
piece
of
technology
used as a form of
lock
for
cargo
containers
.
In
2373
,
Quark
claimed that he needed to retrieve a
descrambler
because the code sequencer on a container of
Varaxian LM-7
was not working properly, making him unable to open the container. (
DS9
: "
Business as Usual
")

Stardate

had been commissioned 1,550
days

runabouts

statue

. (

======================================================================
PAGE: Conduit
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Conduit
======================================================================

A plasma injector conduit
A
conduit
was a medium to transfer
energy
between two points. They were often built into
walls
of habitat areas.
Prior to the creation of the
command center
aboard
Enterprise
NX-01
, the room was a
storage bay
for conduit housings. (
ENT
: "
The Xindi
")
When
Geordi La Forge
and
Ro Laren
cut a micro-hole into the bulkhead above
Ten Forward
, he was concerned about hitting conduit 227, but he managed to get by without rupturing the conduit. (
TNG
: "
Power Play
")
A conduit that had been used to transfer warp power to a
sensor array
was emitting a
subspace
particle stream after it had been tampered with by
solanogen-based lifeforms
. (
TNG
: "
Schisms
")
On
Cardassian
space stations
, conduits are accompanied by
Jefferies tube
-like crawlspaces, which run throughout the station. These crawl spaces are lined with
duranium
alloy, which prevents them from being scanned by
sensors
. This once allowed
Major
Kira Nerys
to fool a
Cardassian
warship
into thinking
Deep Space 9
had 5,000
photon torpedoes
when in fact it only had six. (
DS9
: "
Emissary
") It also allowed a group of
Starfleet
officers to hide from the
Bajoran Militia
during a siege of the station in an attempted
coup d'etat
by the
Alliance for Global Unity
. (
DS9
: "
The Siege
")
Miles O'Brien
and
Benjamin Sisko
once crawled their way through a conduit to disable the
self-destruct
program that threatened the station as part of the
counter-insurgency program
they had accidentally activated. (
DS9
: "
Civil Defense
")
While trapped inside his mind, which he visualized as DS9,
Julian Bashir
attempted to use the conduits to reach
Ops
. However, the
telepathic
attack he had suffered caused him to continually re-emerge on the
Promenade
. (
DS9
: "
Distant Voices
")
Quark
once decided to assemble an all-
Ferengi
team to rescue his mother
Ishka
from the
Dominion
. He had to find his brother
Rom
, who was on duty at the time and working in one of the station's conduits. After informing Rom of the situation, Quark got lost and found upon opening the hatch at the end of a conduit that he had reached
Captain
Sisko
's office. (
DS9
: "
The Magnificent Ferengi
")
When turning the
captain's dining room
into a
galley
on the
USS
Voyager
,
Neelix
had to re-route the conduits in the
mess hall
for the job. (
VOY
: "
Phage
")
Among their
organic technology
,
Species 8472 bio-ships
carried
electrodynamic fluid
through their
organic
conduits. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
A
plasma injector
conduit was among the items
Tau
and his
pirates
stole from the
Voyager
in
2374
.
Tuvok
identified it after the
Leonardo da Vinci
hologram
purchased it from an
alien
. (
VOY
: "
Concerning Flight
")
See also
Access conduit
Plasma conduit
Polaric conduit

======================================================================

A construction module being fitted with thrusters.
A
construction module
was a
tool
used by the
Federation
that allowed construction or implementation of various objects by mechanical means without the need of personnel near by.
In
2367
, a
garbage scow
was caught in the
gravitational forces
of
Gamelan V
, prompting
Geordi La Forge
to develop the idea of using construction modules to place
thrusters
on the scow and thereby remove it from orbit. (
TNG
: "
Final Mission
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Containment chamber
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Containment_chamber
======================================================================

A
containment chamber
was a device for isolating an object from its surroundings using a solid barrier, in contrast to a
containment field
, which used an
energy barrier
.
In
2370
,
Miles O'Brien
set up a containment chamber to hold the
protouniverse
Jadzia Dax
and
Arjin
brought back from the
Gamma Quadrant
. (
DS9
: "
Playing God
")
In
2373
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
planned to convert
storage bay
3 on the
USS
Voyager
into a containment chamber for
sirillium
, a highly combustible
energy
source discovered in a
class 17 nebula
in the
Delta Quadrant
. (
VOY
: "
Flashback
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Containment module
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Containment_module
======================================================================

A
containment module
was a modular device for isolating material from its surroundings. Each had atmospheric control, including
temperature
,
radiation
,
light
,
gravity
,
humidity
, plus
electrical spectrums
, as well as supporting a protective
stasis field
.
In
2365
, newly-promoted
Chief Engineer
Geordi La Forge
built his own array of  five hundred and twelve containment modules to hold dangerous specimens of
plasma plague
. (
TNG
: "
The Child
")
512 of these small units...
...fit into this large unit.

======================================================================
PAGE: Control bracelet
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Control_bracelet
======================================================================

An Eymorg control bracelet
A
control bracelet
was a
device
worn upon the
wrist
of
Eymorg
inhabitants
of
Sigma Draconis VI
. It had several functions, such as the ability to control the operation of a
pain band
, and to render an individual
unconscious
. It was so powerful that it could affect the entire
crew
of a ship.
The Eymorg leader
Kara
used this device to render the entire crew of the
USS
Enterprise
unconscious when she sought to steal
Commander
Spock
's
brain
in
2268
. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Control panel
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Control_panel
======================================================================

A
control panel
,
control interface
, or simply
interface
, was a means by which a person operating a particular piece of
equipment
could instruct the
device
or system as to what they
desired
, and the item being controlled could respond accordingly. It was
possible
to combine elements of one or more of each type within an overall control system structure as alternative means of accessing the controls.
Control interfaces were commonly found on
starships
and
space stations
as part of
console stations
and were critical to how
crewmembers
interact with
computer
systems like
LCARS
and
TCARS
.
A control interface could also refer to parts of the physical circuitry found in
computers
and other machines.
Exocomps
had a control interface in their internal circuitry that relayed commands from a control
PADD
. (
TNG
: "
The Quality of Life
")
Data
told
Geordi La Forge
to try a bypass of the
shield
control interface in an attempt to override the effects of the
Iconian software transmission
. (
TNG
: "
Contagion
")
There were five major types of control interface available to most
spacefaring
species
: physical, audio, touch sensitive, motion sensitive, and neural.
Physical
A
duotronic
-based control interface
A physical control interface utilized physical buttons, switches, dials, and levers for data input purposes, and relied on physical indicators such as gauges, status lights, and visual screens/monitors for response purposes. Manipulation of a physical control was often accompanied by a visual or audio cue to indicate that the input had been accepted by the interface.
A major use for physical controls, even after the introduction of more "advanced" systems, was the operation of small equipment requiring only relatively simple commands, such as personal
weapons
,
tricorders
, and
communicators
. (
Star Trek: The Original Series
;
Star Trek: The Next Generation
)
Interfaces of this type were the primary ones utilized by
Starfleet
in the
22nd century
, and continued to be prevalent in the
Federation
Starfleet
well into the late
23rd century
. (
Star Trek: Enterprise
;
Star Trek: The Original Series
)
A control panel to the left of the door
Some people, such as
Tom Paris
, felt that physical control interfaces were superior to other forms of interface, particularly in flight control operations. The belief was that the operator had a better "feel" for the responsiveness of the system. (
VOY
: "
Extreme Risk
")
The large,
round
door
leading to the secret
listening post
from inside the
reliquary
in the
catacombs
beneath the
monastery
at
P'Jem
had a small control panel with
Vulcan
writings
which could be used to open the door. (
ENT
: "
The Andorian Incident
")
Audio
Audio control interfaces utilized spoken words or other transmitted sounds as both the primary means of data input and systems response, though response via other means could be requested by the operator. Twenty-third century computers made frequent use of audio interfaces, as did personal communicators, a trend which continued into the
24th century
. (
Star Trek: The Original Series
;
Star Trek: The Next Generation
)
Touch-sensitive
A touch-sensitive control interface in 2375
Touch-sensitive interfaces typically consisted of featureless panels with no physical instruments to manipulate at all. The entire surface could be controlled by computer to display a layout of marked areas which served the same function when the surface detected contact from the user.
They were considered more "flexible" than physical interfaces, as the same physical unit could be rapidly reconfigured into whatever form a user might have required by calling up the appropriate control template.
Starfleet began deploying touch sensitive controls to vessels sometime prior to
2256
, however, by
2265
, they fell out of mainstream use. Starfleet began to to experiment with touch-sensitive interfaces again in the mid-
2280s
by incorporating them into the
bridge
of the
prototype
USS
Excelsior
. (
Star Trek: Discovery
,
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
In the
alternate reality
, Starfleet incorporated touchscreen interfaces by
2258
.
(
Star Trek
)
J.J. Abrams
commented
here
on giving the alternate
USS
Enterprise
touchscreen panels that "
Given what our computer interfaces are like now, its preposterous to assume that hundreds of years from now there won't be some version of holographic screens.
"
Motion-sensitive
Lieutenant
Ducane
at temporal navigation
Motion-sensitive interfaces were generally used to control simple mechanisms, such as pocket doors and lights. Some such interfaces could also be found on the bridge of the
USS
Enterprise
in the mid-23rd century. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
", "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
")
In at least one
alternate timeline
, Starfleet began to use so-called "holographic" control interfaces on board their ships. Little is known about them, but given the 3D nature of a hologram, some sort of motion/position sensitive operating mechanism seems likely.
(
DS9
: "
The Visitor
")
The
temporal
navigation console
aboard the
29th century
timeship
USS
Relativity
utilized a combination of touch-sensitive and and motion-sensitive controls. An
officer
touched the screen for inputs and then motioned their hand to confirm those inputs. (
VOY
: "
Relativity
")
Neural
La Forge wearing a VISOR
Neural interfaces directly transmitted commands from and information into the brain of the operator, thus bypassing the need to use comparatively inefficient physical manipulation of controls to complete a task.
In the 23rd century, Commander
Spock
's brain was stolen by an alien race and connected to a neural interface to coordinate the life support systems of their habitat. A more primitive form of interface was used to maintain and control his body until the brain could be restored to it. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")
Geordi La Forge used a
VISOR
to compensate for his blindness that contained
Neural interfaces
. He also participated in an experiment using a neural-interface-controlled remote
probe
. (
TNG
: "
Interface
")
Neural interfaces were frequently used in clandestine/
criminal
activities. (
DS9
: "
Honor Among Thieves
")
Alice
had a
neurogenic interface
. (
VOY
: "
Alice
")
See also
Atmospheric control panel
Computer control panel
Flight control interface
Impulse system control panel
Panel J14-Baker
Shield control interface
Temperature control panel
Warp control panel

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
Coolant
was a
substance
, usually
liquid
or
gas
, used for lowering the
temperature
of a system or a piece of a
machine
.
In
2266
, the
intermix chamber
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
had
primary manifolds
which conducted coolant. That year, as the
Enterprise
successfully broke free of the
tractor beam
of an
alien
pilot vessel
, the
intermix chamber
superheated
with the  coolant temperature rising to two thousand
degrees
above maximum. (
TOS
: "
The Corbomite Maneuver
")

at
Wikipedia

The

starship

Vulcan
ships" of the time. Their
maximum warp
speed was rumored to be
warp 7
. (

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Corvallen freighter
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Corvallen_freighter
======================================================================

An
Antares
-class Corvallen freighter
Corvallen freighters
were types of
warp-capable
freighters
utilized by
Corvallen
mercenaries during the
24th century
. One type they used was the old-style
Antares
-class freighter
.
In
2369
, the
IRW
Khazara
was scheduled to rendezvous with a Corvallen freighter in the
Kaleb sector
to transfer cargo that would further be transported to the
Federation
planet
Draken IV
. The
Khazara
would later destroy the freighter, forcing it to find an alternate means of transferring its cargo to Federation space. (
TNG
: "
Face Of The Enemy
")
A Corvallen freighter visited
Deep Space 9
in
2375
. Upon its departure,
Laas
shadowed the freighter to mask his escape. (
DS9
: "
Chimera
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Cosmic Duchess
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cosmic_Duchess
======================================================================

Cosmic Duchess
Class:
Cosmic Duchess
-type
Status:
Active
(
2382
)
The
Cosmic Duchess
was an interstellar
cruise ship
in operation during the late
24th century
.
The size of a small
moon
, the vessel offered numerous amenities such as
buffets
,
casinos
(including one with
Dixon Hill
slot machines
), 240 24-hour
spas
, an indoor
water park
, an underwater
dry park
, and a
mountain
with
ski
slopes. The interior was illuminated by artificial
sunlight
that could automatically detect when a guest was sufficiently
tanned
.
Latinum Elite
Guests of the
resort
were informed by the front desk of any
Starfleet
teams arriving to capture them.
In
2382
, the
Cosmic Duchess
was plagued by what appeared to be a rogue
nanite cluster
. The
USS
Cerritos
was assigned to capture it; an
away team
attempted to lure the cluster, named "
Glumpus
" by
Lieutenant jg
Sam Rutherford
, into a
trap
with an
energy cell
. However, they inadvertently caused it to rampage and grow to a massive size. The nanites were ultimately neutralized when the team detected and disrupted its control signal with
vibe tubes
. The source of the signal was found to be the
USS
Endeavour
, a
microscopic
Federation
starship
from a
parallel universe
.
Meanwhile,
Commander
Jack Ransom
and
Lieutenant Commander
Andy Billups
, along with Lieutenant jg
Brad Boimler
, embarked on an undercover mission to locate the
AWOL
Admiral
Milius
. They were captured by Milius's
acolytes
and taken to
AWOL Falls
, but they abandoned him after he became too aggravated for their liking. Milius then agreed to return to duty and assist in returning the
Endeavour
to its original universe. (
LD
: "
The Best Exotic Nanite Hotel
")
Attractions
Restaurants
The Risa Bar
Ski slopes
Cowards Gulch
Experts Demise
Water park
Lazy river
AWOL Falls
Temple of Dread
Personnel
See
:
Cosmic Duchess
personnel

======================================================================
PAGE: Cravic ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cravic_ship
======================================================================

Cravic ship
Owner:
Cravic
Operator:
Automated Personnel Units
Active:
23rd century
-
24th century
Armament:
Quantum resonance charges
disruptors
Defenses:
Subspace defense field
The
Cravic ship
was a type of
starship
initially utilized by the
Cravic
, nearly identical to
Pralor vessels
.
Tan in color, their design consisted of a central hub from which five arms extended in a star-like formation. These vessels were presumably armed with the same
weapon systems
and capabilities as their Pralor counterparts.
They were crewed by
Automated Personnel Units
and used in the
war
between the Pralor and the Cravic. However, when the war ended and the Pralor and Cravic attempted to terminate the units, the units killed both
species
. The units used the Pralor and Cravic ships to continue the war for at least another fifty years.
In
2372
, one of these vessels, commanded by
Automated Commander 122
, engaged a
Pralor vessel
, commanded by
Automated Unit 6263
, and was capable of breaking through the Pralor vessel's
subspace
defense field
. (
VOY
: "
Prototype
")

's questions for
Hikaru Sulu

Enterprise
". (

======================================================================
PAGE: Cybernetic implant
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cybernetic_implant
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Warning!
This article contains information regarding
Star Trek: Scouts
, and thus may contain
spoilers
.
Zeph, a Human with extensive cybernetic implants
Rutherford with his
oculus
A
cybernetic implant
, also called
cybernetic device
,
artificial implant
, or
body modification
, was any piece of
technology
designed to be
implanted
within a living
organism
and function seamlessly alongside, or in place of, their biological systems. A
lifeform
modified with mechanical components was known as a
cyborg
, short for
cybernetic organism
.
The
Borg
were heavily augmented with cybernetic implants. (
TNG
: "
Q Who
")
Mecha dysmorphia
was a psychological condition that created a compulsion to replace one's body parts with technology. (
Star Trek: Section 31
)
History
In
2365
, prior to the rule of
Lieutenant Commander
Data
's
sentience
,
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
considered
Starfleet
's interests in
Commander
Bruce Maddox
's attempt to disassemble and analyze the
Soong-type android
in hopes of duplicating the
android
technology
. Data, however, argued against the idea, citing, "
Lieutenant
La Forge
's
eyes
[in terms of his
VISOR
] are far superior to
Human
biological eyes. True? Then why are not all Human
officers
required to have their eyes replaced with cybernetic implants?
" (
TNG
: "
The Measure Of A Man
")
In an
alternate
2390
,
Chakotay
and
Harry Kim
reactivated
The Doctor
in order to determine the
time of death
of the frozen
Seven of Nine
, specifically, they wanted him to access her "
chronometric node
and pinpoint the exact moment her cybernetic implants disengaged from her
organic systems
.
"
(
VOY
: "
Timeless
")
In the
2380s
,
Ensign
Sam Rutherford
received a cybernetic implant a few
weeks
before Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
arrived to serve aboard the
USS
Cerritos
. The implant was referred to as an
oculus
. (
LD
: "
Second Contact
", "
Fissure Quest
")
Implant display view
In
2380
, Rutherford used his implant to come up with an optimal combat strategy to defeat Lieutenant
Shaxs
'
Combat Simulation Smorgasborg
program. (
LD
: "
Envoys
")
The
Vulcan
Sprocket
was fitted with a bionic arm at a fairly young age. The arm was primarily
silver
in
color
, and featured a
Starfleet insignia
. It could increase considerably in length, via a feature called "
reacher mode
", and could function as a blower when in "
air mode
". (
SCO
: "
Asteroid Blasters, Number 2
", "

======================================================================
PAGE: Cybernetics conference
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cybernetics_conference
======================================================================

A
cybernetics conference
was a
symposium
at which
cybernetics
was discussed.
In
2366
,
Data
attended a cybernetics conference, after which he began to spend an inordinate amount of time in a locked
lab
on
USS
Enterprise
-D
. This piqued
Geordi La Forge
's curiosity at the
android
's uncharacteristic behavior.
Data later revealed to La Forge and
Deanna Troi
that he had begun work on
Lal
, his offspring, at the conference after learning of "
a new
submicron matrix transfer
technology
introduced at the conference which I discovered could be used to lay down complex
neural net
pathways.
" (
TNG
: "
The Offspring
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Cymatic transmutation
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cymatic_transmutation
======================================================================

Cymatic transmutation
, also called
acoustic terra-moulding
, was the process of using variously pitched
sound waves
to shape
matter
.
The
Cymari
used cymatic transmutation, powered by arrays of
remalite crystals
, to manipulate the
sand
on their
homeworld
into complex structures. These structures would become unstable if even one of the crystals were removed. (
PRO
: "
First Con-tact
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Cytoplasmic lifeform vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Cytoplasmic_lifeform_vessel
======================================================================

The
cytoplasmic lifeform vessel
was a type of
ship
operated by
cytoplasmic lifeforms
native to an area of the
Delta Quadrant
. It was equipped with a
weapon
capable of draining an enemy ship's energy reserves.
In
2375
, one such vessel attacked the
USS
Voyager
on the mistaken belief that the crew had
kidnapped
one of their kind. In actual fact, it had attached itself to
Lieutenant
B'Elanna Torres
and only after intense surgery did the ship's
EMH
managed to remove the creature and have it
transported
back to its ship. As a result, the ship withdrew. (
VOY
: "
Nothing Human
")
The model was re-used to portray the Zenuvian starship in
Jonathan Frakes
'
Star Trek
spoof
Star Patrol!
in
2000
.
The cytoplasmic lifeform vessel
A vessel attacking the
USS
Voyager

D-756
Class:
Tellarite merchant ship
Status:
Active
(
2250s
)
D-756
was a
Tellarite merchant ship
owned by
bounty hunter
Tevrin Krit
in the late
2250s
.
Sometime after
Harcourt Fenton Mudd
's departure from the
USS
Discovery
in
2256
, Krit's ship rendezvoused with a female bounty hunter to purchase Mudd from her. He then set a course for the
USS
De Milo
to deliver Mudd for a
Starfleet
payout, unaware that the Mudd in his custody was actually an
android
duplicate
sold to him by the real Mudd in disguise. (
ST
: "
The Escape Artist
")

D53
was a component found on
California
-class

.

buggy
parts had a tendency to spark and cause
plasma fires
. (

")

======================================================================
PAGE: DMA controller
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/DMA_controller
======================================================================

The first DMA controller
The
DMA controller
was a device created by
Species 10-C
that generated and controlled the
Dark Matter Anomaly
. Within the anomaly, it was located in a
subspace rift
containing high concentrations of
galactic barrier
particles
, surrounded by a region of relative calm. The device was composed of a
silicon
-
ellanium
alloy
, the same rare material also used to construct Species 10-C's
Dyson rings
and the housing of the
hyperfield
's power source. (
DIS
: "
Rubicon
", "
Rosetta
", "
Coming Home
")
After the DMA first manifested in
3190
, the existence of a technological device at its heart was revealed when the anomaly unexpectedly vanished into it. The device also disappeared shortly after, leaving a temporary
subspace rupture
.
Federation
scientist
Ruon Tarka
conjectured that the controller contained a
wormhole
, allowing it and the DMA to jump across
space
. Tarka then proposed to create a miniature working model of the controller aboard
USS
Discovery
. Though
Captain
Saru
terminated the experiment when it threatened
Discovery
, enough data was gathered to verify Tarka's
theory
. (
DIS
: "
The Examples
")
Tarka determined that the controller would require an
energy source
equivalent to a
hypergiant
star to function. Coveting this power for his own purposes, Tarka designed what was effectively an
isolytic weapon
capable of generating a
cascading subspace burst
that could sever the controller from its power source, collapsing the DMA while protecting the power source within a
null space
pocket. Tarka advocated for the deployment of his weapon at the
Multilateral DMA Strategy Assembly
, but the delegates elected to pursue a diplomatic strategy instead. (
DIS
: "
The Examples
", "
...But to Connect
", "
Rubicon
")
Undeterred, Tarka enlisted the aid of
Cleveland Booker
to destroy the DMA themselves. Despite the best efforts of
Discovery
to stop them, Tarka managed to beam his weapon into the controller. The DMA was destroyed, but Tarka discovered that the controller had actually been powered remotely from Species 10-C's hyperfield outside the
galaxy
. (
DIS
: "
Rubicon
")
Species 10-C subsequently deployed a second DMA with another controller, which was the same size as the first but considerably more powerful. (
DIS
: "
The Galactic Barrier
")
After being convinced to dismantle the hyperfield and stop the DMA, the 10-C used the DMA controller's wormhole to return the
USS
Discovery
to Earth before shutting the DMA down once and for all. (
DIS
: "
Coming Home
")

======================================================================
PAGE: DNA sequencer
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/DNA_sequencer
======================================================================

A
DNA sequencer
was a
device
which determined the order of
DNA
proteins.
When infected with an
alien
virus
forcing
Kathryn Janeway
and
Chakotay
to live on
New Earth
, Janeway collected several supplies, including
Petri dishes
, a
protein analyzer
, and DNA sequencers to continue her
research
in finding a
cure
. (
VOY
: "
Resolutions
")
External link
DNA sequencer
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Dauntless class
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dauntless_class
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Dauntless
class
Owner:
Federation
Operator:
Starfleet
Active:
2380s
Decks:
8
Speed:
Warp
/
Quantum slipstream
capable
Armament:
Phaser cannons
Micro
/
Photon
/
Quantum
Torpedo launchers
Defenses:
Deflector shields
For the alien vessel that inspired this class, please see
Dauntless type
.
The
Dauntless
-class
was a
class
of
Federation
starship
operated during the late
24th century
.
Origins
The
Dauntless
had a similar design to the fake
USS
Dauntless
, which was also designated as a
Dauntless
-class starship and was altered to appear
Federation
in design during the 2370s by
Arturis
, a member of
Species 116
.
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
and the crew of the
USS
Voyager
first encountered the original
Dauntless
in the
Delta Quadrant
in late
2374
. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
Given Janeway's order for the entire
Voyager
crew to thoroughly study the ship, they had enough data to
reverse engineer
its systems. This was demonstrated in
2375
when they were able to recreate their own marginally successful attempt at the alien
Dauntless
'
slipstream drive
. (
VOY
: "
Timeless
")
Technical data
The class was equipped with four
phaser cannons
. The main phaser cannon was located on Deck E, with two forward phaser cannons on Decks B and C and a rear phaser cannon on Deck E. The ship was, also, equipped with a
torpedo launcher
, located on Deck F, which could fire standard
photon torpedoes
or
microtorpedoes
. On Deck G, there were vertical torpedo launchers which were used for firing
quantum torpedoes
. It was protected by
deflector shields
. (
PRO
: "
Crossroads
", "
Masquerade
", "
Mindwalk
", "
Supernova, Part 1
")
The size of the
Dauntless
compared to the size of the
Protostar
Externally, the Federation's
Dauntless
design retained several design cues from the original
Dauntless
, including a streamlined, arrowhead shaped
primary hull
, a near organic transition into the
secondary hull
and underslung
warp nacelles
. With the new design, the secondary hull and warp nacelles were lengthened compared to the original and the secondary hull appeared to house auxiliary
impulse engines
. It also lacked the "hump" the original had in its transition from the nape of the ship's dorsal section. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
") This class was far larger in size than a
Protostar
-class
vessel. (
PRO
: "
Crossroads
")
Based on the
master systems display
graphics inside the
Dauntless
shown throughout
PRO
: "
Mindwalk
", the ship's primary hull is eight decks in height, and has a significantly different aft section than the alien ship upon which it is based.
Interior design
Unlike the alien version of this class, the Federation's version possessed holodecks and replicators. (
PRO
: "
Asylum
", "
Let Sleeping Borg Lie
")
Main sickbay
Bridge
Captain's ready room
Corridor
Transporter room

Background information
According to
Aaron Waltke
, the
Dauntless
was a Starfleet reproduction of Arturis' vessel built from detailed scan data gathered by USS
Voyager
. It, too, is also equipped with a limited form of quantum slipstream drive.
[1]
In a
log
by Janeway on the "
Star Trek
Logs" Instagram account, the project leader in designing the
Dauntless
was
B'Elanna Torres
, who also suggested the name.
[2]

======================================================================
PAGE: Dauntless type
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dauntless_type
======================================================================

Dauntless
type
Owner:
Species 116
Active:
2370s
Speed:
Quantum slipstream
capable
For the experimental Starfleet ship of the same name, please see
Dauntless class
.
The
Dauntless
-type
was a starship built and operated by
Species 116
during the late
24th century
. In
2374
, a ship of this type was disguised as a "
Dauntless
-class" experimental
Starfleet
vessel, sent to the
Delta Quadrant
to bring the crew of the USS
Voyager
home sooner, however, was created with the nefarious intent to trap the crew for eventual
assimilation
at the hands of the
Borg
. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
History
In mid-
2374
, while still stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, the
USS
Voyager
received a transmission from
Starfleet Command
via the ancient
Hirogen communications network
. Although the message contained a number of unencoded personal messages, there was also an encrypted file that the
Voyager
crew was unable to open. (
VOY
: "
Hunters
")
On Stardate 51978,
Voyager
encountered an alien named
Arturis
who had a gift for translating languages, both verbal and visual. He easily decrypted the mysterious message, which gave a set of coordinates near their current location. Upon arrival, the
Voyager
crew discovered the USS
Dauntless
, a supposed experimental vessel constructed by
Starfleet
for the purpose of bringing the
Voyager
crew home. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
Technical data
Physical arrangement
Real bridge without the use of particle synthesis
Although created by
alien
technology
, this ship was made to resemble
Federation
starships on the interior and exterior by the means of
particle synthesis
. The darker alien interior was modified to a more familiar Federation color palette, and
LCARS
interfaces. The exterior of the vessel maintained a typical Starfleet layout, which included a clear
Primary hull
, and
Secondary hull
layout, with two
nacelles
on each side of the secondary hull. The outside hull was also modified with familiar Federation livery and the ship's name and registry. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
Interior design
The bridge disguised in Starfleet livery
By Starfleet standards, the original
Dauntless
was considered to be relatively minimalist, possessing only one
transporter
and no
replicators
,
holodecks
, or
shuttlecraft
. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
Quantum slipstream
Quantum slipstream core
The only known example of this type was equipped with a
quantum slipstream drive
that allowed it to go faster than the standard Starfleet
warp
scale at the time.
According to the ship's data, it could travel sixty thousand
light years
in three
months
, and in practice, demonstrated the ability to travel over fifteen light years in only a short time, a distance that otherwise took the
USS
Voyager
two days to cover at
high warp
. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
Starfleet reverse engineering
By
2375
, the crew of the USS
Voyager
had been able to partially reverse engineer and create their own quantum slipstream drive, with the help of
Borg
modifications for use on
Voyager
. This technology proved to be unreliable, and was later disassembled, but not before shaving ten years off their journey home. (
VOY
: "
Timeless
")
USS
Dauntless
NCC-80816
Later in the
2380s
,
Starfleet
reverse engineered the technology more properly and went on to build and commission the
Dauntless
-class
USS
Dauntless
. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
")
Alternate timelines
The
Dauntless
-type in the 26th century
In a
possible future
, a ship of this type was involved in the pivotal
Battle of Procyon V
in the
26th century
, alongside the
USS
Enterprise
-J and other allied starships. This timeline was negated with the destruction of the
Delphic Expanse
, and the
Sphere-Builders
by the
NX-01
Enterprise
in
2154
.
(
ENT
: "
Azati Prime
")
Ships in class
Named
Name
Registry
Notes
Status
Date
"
USS
Dauntless
"
"
NX
-01-A"
Real name, if any, of this starship is unknown
Assimilated
2374
Appendices
Appearances
ENT
:
"
Azati Prime
"
VOY
:
"
Hope and Fear
" (
first appearance
)
Background information
Studio model
A foam core mock-up
concept model
of the bridge of the
Dauntless
was sold in the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
of 9 March 2007 as
Lot#2034
(X)
. The model measuring 22"×36"×7.5" sold for US$876.
See
Dauntless
-class model
.
Apocrypha
Though not originally a Starfleet design, ships matching the configuration of the
Dauntless
appear in the
25th century
timeline of
Star Trek Online
as the
Dauntless
-class "Experimental Science Vessel", featuring a special device that mimics the effects of particle synthesis to distract enemies and confuse their targeting.
