USS
Dauntless
(NX-01-A)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Decommissioning
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Decommissioning
======================================================================

Decommissioning
was the act of taking a
ship
,
device
, or
computer program
out of service. A
starship
that was decommissioned could be said to be put in
mothballs
. Decommissioned vessels were often stored at
surplus depots
.
Starships
The
Enterprise
NX-01
was decommissioned in
2161
, shortly before the signing the
Charter of the
United Federation of Planets
on
Earth
. (
ENT
: "
These Are The Voyages...
")
Starfleet decommissioned its
Daedalus
-class
starships in
2196
. (
TNG
: "
Power Play
")
In
2285
, the
USS
Enterprise
was due to be decommissioned due to age and battle damage, although the vessel was ultimately
hijacked
by
Admiral
James T. Kirk
. Kirk later
self-destructed
the vessel over
Genesis
to kill a
Klingon
boarding party
. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
Following the
Khitomer Conference
in
2293
, which formalized peaceful relations between the Federation and the
Klingon Empire
,
Starfleet
ordered the
USS
Enterprise
-A
to return to
Spacedock One
for decommissioning. (
Star Trek VI: The Undiscovered Country
)
The starship
T'Pau
was decommissioned in
2364
and was later sent to
Surplus Depot Z15
at
Qualor II
, where it was registered on
stardate
41334.2 and stationed at Section-18-Gamma-12. At that time, it was stripped of materials, armaments and sensors, its
navigational deflector array
being routed to the holding vessel
USS
Tripoli
. During this, a file was created for this ship and stored in the
Qualor II Depot Z15 Inventory Database
. By
2368
, other decommissioned starships at Z15 included the
USS
Alka-Celsior
,
B-24-CLN
, two
Miranda
-class
starships
, and a
K't'inga
-class
cruiser
. (
TNG
: "
Unification I
", "
Unification II
")
In
2381
, the
California
-class
was set to be decommissioned in favor of the
Texas
-class
automated starship
following the disastrous showing of
Project Swing By
and the
Exposé
Starfleet's Shame
. However, this was rescinded following the destruction of all three Texas-class ships and the death of their creator and sponsor,
Vice Admiral
Les Buenamigo
. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")
Following the return of the
USS
Voyager
in
2378
, the ship was decommissioned and taken to a
facility
in the
Portelo system
to be converted into a
museum ship
. (
LD
: "
Twovix
") It was later stored in the
Fleet Museum
over
Athan Prime
along with a number of other decommissioned and preserved ships. (
PRO
: "
The Bounty
") In
2384
,
Voyager
was replaced by the
Lamarr
-class
USS
Voyager
-A
. (
PRO
: "
Supernova, Part 2
", "
Into the Breach, Part II
")
In
2385
, following the
Attack on Mars
and Starfleet's new isolationist policy, Starfleet was going to decommission the new
Protostar
-class
as it was determined that its unique
protostar drive
was only useful for exploration. However,
Vice Admiral
Kathryn Janeway
intervened and got the new
USS
Prodigy
turned into a
training ship
in a pilot program under her command instead. (
PRO
: "
Ouroboros, Part II
")
In
2401
, the
USS
Enterprise
-F
was scheduled for early decommissioning which occurred following the destruction of the
Borg
. (
PIC
: "
The Next Generation
", "
The Last Generation
")
A number of decommissioned starships were kept on display in the
Fleet Museum
by 2401 including the
Enterprise
NX-01, the
Enterprise
-A, the
USS
Excelsior
,
USS
Defiant
and
Voyager
. (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
In
2402
, having been pressed back into service to fight the Borg, the rebuilt
USS
Enterprise
-D
was decommissioned and returned to the Fleet Museum shortly before the launch of the
USS
Enterprise
-G
. (
PIC
: "
The Last Generation
")
In
an alternate future
around
2390
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
would have been decommissioned had it not been chosen as a command by
Admiral
William T. Riker
.
(
TNG
: "
All Good Things...
")
Non-starships
The
lateral vector transporter
was considered obsolete by both Starfleet and the
Vulcans
by
2249
and had been largely phased out by that year. Some ships, such as the
USS
Shenzhou
and
USS
Hiawatha
, continued to use them regardless. (
DIS
: "
Battle at the Binary Stars
", "
Brother
")
Prior to
2377
,
Lewis Zimmerman
tried to have
EMH
Mark Is decommissioned, but much to his dismay, Starfleet reassigned them to
waste transfer barges
instead. (
VOY
: "
Life Line
")
See also
Commissioning
Decommission protocol

======================================================================
PAGE: Dekendi transport
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dekendi_transport
======================================================================

Dekendi transport
Affiliation:
Dekendi
Type:
Transport
Active:
2152
A
Dekendi transport
was a type of
transport
-ship utilized by the
Dekendi
during the mid-
22nd century
.
One of these vessels transported
Feezal Phlox
from the surface of
Dekendi III
to the
orbiting
Enterprise
in
2152
. Another ship of this type, having a
grey
color
, was parked on the planet's
surface
. (
ENT
: "
Stigma
")
In the final draft script of "Stigma", this type of vessel was described simply as "a small, alien
shuttlecraft
."
[1]
For more information on this
studio model
, see
ENT studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Delta Rana warship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Delta_Rana_warship
======================================================================

Delta Rana warship
Class:
Warship
Affiliation:
Husnock
Status:
Exposed
(
2366
)
The
Delta Rana warship
was an
illusory starship
created by the
Douwd
who had taken the form of
Kevin Uxbridge
. He used the illusion as a form of
planetary defense system
for
Rana IV
against the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. It was noted to have five times the mass of the
Enterprise
. (
TNG
: "
The Survivors
")
History
First appearance
In
2366
, when the USS
Enterprise
-D was investigating the destruction of the
Federation
colony
on Rana IV, Kevin created this illusory warship in
orbit
of Rana IV in an attempt to force the
Enterprise
to leave the
planet
, and his home. This class of warship wasn't listed in the Federation vehicle classification index, but at the time it was assumed to be the same
Husnock warship
which had previously attacked and obliterated the Rana colony.
The warship firing streams of positrons and antiprotons.
Initially this illusory vessel fired jacketed streams of
positrons
and
antiprotons
, an equivalent firepower of 40
megawatts
, which caused no damage to the
Enterprise
. The
Enterprise
fired a warning shot at the ship, causing it to retreat from the
system
. The
Enterprise
attempted to pursue but was unable to close the gap between the two vessels, even after increasing velocity to
Warp 9.37
. (
TNG
: "
The Survivors
")
Second appearance
The
Enterprise
returns fire
Kevin made the ship reappear, some time after the
Enterprise
had returned to Rana IV. During its second appearance, the warship looked to the crew of the
Enterprise
to be much larger and much "meaner."
Enterprise
attempted to hail the ship to warn it to stay away from the planet.
In response, the ship fired four hundred
gigawatts
of
particle energy
at the
Enterprise
, causing only superficial damage to the ship, but effectively taking down its
shields
. A second shot took out the rest of the
Enterprise
's shields and caused thermal damage to the
hull
. Ship
sensors
, however, were able to determine that the warship was in possession of enormous energy reserves, capable of striking with far more powerful bursts.
The warship holds its position above Rana IV after its second encounter with the
Enterprise
Following the second attack, the ship moved into orbit of Rana IV and assumed a position over the Uxbridge residence. The
Enterprise
fired a full
phaser
and
photon torpedo
barrage at the warship, but caused no damage to it. It was then determined that the warship's defenses were able to absorb any incoming weapons. The warship returned fire, taking out the
Enterprise
's shields and weapon systems control, as well as causing some internal damage to the ship and non-fatal injuries to sixty-six members of the crew. (
TNG
: "
The Survivors
")
Third appearance
When the
Enterprise
returned for a third time,
Picard
told Kevin that the
Enterprise
would stay in orbit to protect Kevin as long as the threat from the warship remained. Kevin had the warship appear for a third time. This time, the
Enterprise
kept its distance and allowed the warship to once again enter orbit over Rana IV - at which time it destroyed the Uxbridge residence. Following the attack, the
Enterprise
fired a single photon torpedo at the warship, destroying it.
Picard had determined that the warship was directly serving the needs of Kevin Uxbridge to bait and later chase the
Enterprise
away from the planet. Kevin Uxbridge confirmed the assumption, and indicated that he had tried the same diversionary tactics on the Husnock warship, also to no avail. (
TNG
: "
The Survivors
")
Background information
The script notes for the episode indicate that the ship created by "Kevin" was a recreation of the
Husnock warship
that destroyed the colony. The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 199 as well as the
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 3rd ed., p. 103 also support the supposition that this vessel was intended to be a Husnock ship.
The deflector shields of the ship had a similar segmented visual effect as the
Borg drone
shields of this era as they were depicted in
TNG
: "
Q Who
" for example.
Studio model
This vessel was designed and built by
Tony Meininger
. According to the
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
, 3rd ed., p. 103, "
The Husnock warship, built by Tony Meininger, was one of the first not designed by a member of the TNG art staff. 'Usually there just isn't time to put into designing them if they're just a 'ship-of-the-week' that's going to be re-dressed later anyway,' said
Rick Sternbach
, adding that decisions on re-use are made by the alternating TNG visual-effects teams headed by
Dan Curry
and
Rob Legato
and their model contractors.
" It was Meininger's first involvement with the
Star Trek
franchise (and his only one for
TNG
, albeit as an outside contractor), before moving on to become the main supplier of
studio models
for televised
Star Trek
starting with
DS9
: "
Emissary
"
In this episode, the model of the ship was modified slightly for the second appearance: a third weapon spike was added to the underside of the ship, so the ship did look "meaner" the second time.

A
device
was an
object
invented
for a particular
purpose
. Although not a
machine
, a device could be a machine
component
performing a
task
.
In
2024
, a
sign
outside the
Los Angeles Police Department Headquarters
noted that all unauthorized electronic devices would be considered
contraband
and
confiscated
. (
PIC
: "
Watcher
")
In
2266
,
James T. Kirk
asked Spock if an
alien
cube
was a
vessel
, to which the answer was some type of device. Later, Kirk described the fictional
corbomite
as a device to Balok. (
TOS
: "
The Corbomite Maneuver
")
In
2364
,
Data
, in the
conference room
; described an
automobile
as an "
ancient
Earth
device, used primarily for
transportation
." (
TNG
: "
The Big Goodbye
")
Armus
referred to Data or "
Tin Man
" as he called him; as a device, rather than a
living being
. (
TNG
: "
Skin Of Evil
")

at

PAGE: Devore warship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes

Devore warship
Affiliation:
Devore Imperium
Type:
Warship
Active:
2370s
Defenses:
Refractive shielding
The
Devore warship
was a type of
warship
used by the
Devore Imperium
to patrol their territory in the
Delta Quadrant
. They sometimes operated in
squadrons
.
Tactically, a single Devore warship was comparable to a
Federation
Intrepid
-class
starship
. The vessel was equipped with
refractive shielding
, which rendered it difficult to detect on
sensors
.
While passing through
Devore
space in
2375
, the
USS
Voyager
was stopped for inspection by several pairs of Devore warships. The inspections were to search for the presence of
telepaths
. (
VOY
: "
Counterpoint
")
These vessels were identifed in the
script
as "massive alien warships, highly advanced and bristling with weapons," and later as "mighty Devore Warships".
For information on the
CGI
model of the Devore warship, see
here
.
External link
Devore warship
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Diagnosis
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Diagnosis
======================================================================

Diagnosis
was the process of identifying an
illness
,
malfunction
, or other problem by examining the
symptoms
of a
person
or
object
. Practiced in
medicine
, it was typical for a
medical doctor
to perform a diagnosis on a
patient
, but one could perform a
self-diagnosis
. Alternatively, it was typical for an
engineer
to perform a
diagnostic
on
computers
or other
technologies
, but an
artificial lifeform
could perform its own
self-diagnostic
.
In
2268
,
Lieutenant Commander
Spock
had been attempting to
decipher
obelisk
symbols
for fifty-eight
days
, having hardly
eaten
or
slept
in
weeks
.
Doctor
Leonard McCoy
believed that if he didn't let up that he'd collapse. After Spock assured him that
Vulcans
can go without sleep for weeks, McCoy noted that his Vulcan
metabolism
was so low it could hardly be
measured
. McCoy diagnosed Spock with
exhaustion
, brought on by
overwork
and
guilt
, and
prescribed
rest
. (
TOS
: "
The Paradise Syndrome
")
That same year, Doctor McCoy initially diagnosed
orphaned
children
with
lacunar amnesia
after they displayed no indications of
grief
or
fear
in response to their
parents
committing
mass suicide
on
Triacus
. (
TOS
: "
And the Children Shall Lead
")
In most
Betazoids
, their
telepathic
gifts developed at
adolescence
, however,
Tam Elbrun
was
born
with his telepathic abilities switched on. Most Betazoids born that way never led a normal life as the
noise
of other peoples'
thoughts
and
feelings
were overwhelming, incomprehensible, and painful, especially to a child. Early diagnosis and
special training
helped Tam adjust, but some problems remained. (
TNG
: "
Tin Man
")
In
2375
, after
Seven of Nine
ran a complete self-diagnostic and concluded she had
Albright-Salzman syndrome
,
The Doctor
stated that she instead had a mild case of
sensory aphasia
and warned her about the
danger
of self-diagnosis; patients always assumed the worst. (
VOY
: "
Relativity
")
In
2377
, the
Quarren
Doctor
Kadan
made false diagnoses and manipulated the
memory engrams
of
kidnapped
crewmembers
from the
USS
Voyager
so that they could be used for the Quarren
workforce
. (
VOY
: "
Workforce
", "
Workforce, Part II
")
See also
Biobed
Diagnostic analysis
Diagnostic wrap
Exploratory surgery
Prognosis
Tricardial diagnostic
Troubleshoot
External links
Diagnosis
at
Wikipedia
Diagnosis (artificial intelligence)
at
Wikipedia
Medical diagnosis
at
Wikipedia
Self-diagnosis
at
Wikipedia
Misdiagnosis
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Dial
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dial
======================================================================

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's

. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
Body function panel
A
dial
was a type of
device
.
On the
body function panels
used aboard
Constitution
-class
starships
in the
2260s
, there was one dial for each of the six
health
functions being measured. Each dial was a fixed scale with a moving pointer.
In
2265
, tapping into his growing
extrasensory perception
powers,
Gary Mitchell
manipulated the dials on the panel monitoring him, so that they would show different measurements. When Gary put himself into a
death
state for twenty-two
seconds
, all dials showed him as "dead" with the pointers at the bottom of the scales. When he revived himself, the pointers returned to normal. (
TOS
: "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
")
In the final revised draft script for "Where No Man Has Gone Before", after saying that
psychiatry
was not an exact
science
, Gary Mitchell remarked how nice it would be if there was a dial measuring the level of sanity in a
patient
.
[1]
External link
Dial
at
Wikipedia

diatonal amplifier
was a
device
that amplified
sound
.

, as part of a

,
The Doctor
planned to
sing
to
Miral Paris
while she was still in
B'Elanna Torres
's
womb
, using a diatonal amplifier to transmit his voice. He believed it would help Miral's
mathematical
abilities. (
VOY
: "
Lineage
")

cloaking technology
.

online in

,

Tucker
told

")

's
light
.

bluffed
AGIMUS
by connecting him to the dimmer switch rather than the

' controls, as he had claimed to have done. (

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Directional sonic generator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Directional_sonic_generator
======================================================================

O'Brien holding a directional sonic generator
A
directional sonic generator
was a
device
capable of producing a wide
range
of
sound
frequencies
.
When
Cardassian voles
were damaging systems on board
Deep Space 9
in
2370
,
O'Brien
planned to adjust a directional sonic generator to produce a frequency that would be uncomfortable to vole's
ears
and drive them out of hiding, but decided against it as it would take too long.
When
Quark
complained about the vole problem, O'Brien demonstrated the directional sonic generator to him, but it greatly hurt the
Ferengi
's highly sensitive
ears
, to the point that he briefly lost his
hearing
. (
DS9
: "
Playing God
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Display compass
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Display_compass
======================================================================

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
A
display compass
was a device on a
thruster suit
that enabled a user to alter their
trajectory
by
degrees
.
In
2259
of the
alternate reality
,
Hikaru Sulu
advised an off-course
James T. Kirk
to correct his course by 37.243°. When Kirk's display went dark,
Spock
informed Kirk that it would be
mathematically
impossible to hit his target (the
door
of
USS
Vengeance
) without his display. Kirk then criticized Spock's poor
bedside manner
.
Khan Noonien Singh
, whose display continued to function, then advised Kirk how to adjust his course.
(
Star Trek Into Darkness
)

disruptor snare

training
moon

======================================================================
PAGE: Door
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Door
======================================================================

A
door
was a moving structure that was used to allow or block off
access
between spaces. They were also critical
components
of
airlocks
. They were in use by numerous
civilizations
and there was tremendous variety. In size, they varied from designed to accommodate
humanoid
movement to doors blocking
starships
from exiting
spacedocks
.
The doors at the
Vulcan
monastery
at
P'Jem
, which was built around
3,000 years
ago, were made of wood. The entrance door was a double-sided large door. Upon the arrival of the
landing party
from
Enterprise
NX-01
in
June
2151
, the door featured several scratches and damage. The other doors inside the monastery were also made out of wood except one hidden single circular door found in the
reliquary
which was made of
metal
and defined the entrance to the secret
listening post
. (
ENT
: "
The Andorian Incident
")
This door previously appeared as the door to the
Klingon High Council Chamber
in the pilot episode "
Broken Bow
". (
"The Andorian Incident",
text commentary
,
ENT Season 1 DVD
special feature
)
Traditional Romulan houses always had a false front door that was never used, called a "
shaipouin
", so that people instead had to go around the back. (
PIC
: "
The End is the Beginning
")
Emergency instructions on a door aboard the
Fortunate
Earth
research modules
were equipped with large circular doors which reminded the
scientists
to follow the
contamination protocol
. (
ENT
: "
Regeneration
") Similar, emergency instructions were also placed on the doors aboard the
Earth Cargo Service
freighter
ECS
Fortunate
. (
ENT
: "
Fortunate Son
")
A Starfleet door label
Starfleet
doors normally had labels indicating the location of the
room
, the
deck
and the room
number
. (
TNG
: "
Encounter at Farpoint
", "
The Outrageous Okona
", "
Chain Of Command, Part II
", "
Genesis
" et al.)
Groppler
Zorn
of the
Bandi
had the entrance of his
office
equipped with a door made out of
wood
. The door was destroyed by
Commander
William T. Riker
and
Data
when they forced the entry. (
TNG
: "
Encounter at Farpoint
")
Captain
Picard
almost fell out of a
turbolift
when suddenly the door was missing due to his thoughts. (
TNG
: "
Where No One Has Gone Before
")
The doors to the
airlocks
of
Starbase 74
were large round doors. (
TNG
: "
11001001
")
A broken door
In late
2364
,
Admiral
Gregory Quinn
threw
Geordi La Forge
through the door of his
guest quarters
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
and the door crashed. (
TNG
: "
Conspiracy
")
When
Worf
and
Data
beamed
aboard the
space module
and Worf tried to pass a door but was unable because it didn't open for him. Data gave Worf a hint and pushed the door latch. (
TNG
: "
The Neutral Zone
")
The
Dremans
use a door that vanishes with the wave of a
hand
. (
TNG
: "
Pen Pals
")
Doors aboard
Starfleet
vessels and facilities were typically designed to open upon sensing the presence of an individual or individuals approaching them and then close again once they were out of range. In
2366
, when Captain Picard brought
Nuria
aboard the
Enterprise
-D in attempt to prove to her that he was not a
god
known as the
Overseer
, or "the Picard," she marveled at this function of the vessel's doors. She stated that even the walls obeyed his command, but he stated that the doors merely worked differently from the ones she knew. (
TNG
: "
Who Watches The Watchers
") In
2367
, Captain Picard attempted this with the doors of his
quarters
upon the unexpected appearance of
Ardra
, who was attempting to seduce him. The doors, however, failed to open and he pressed a button on a
control interface
next to them, also to no effect. Ardra then stated that it was just the two of them. (
TNG
: "
Devil's Due
")
Ensign
Brad Boimler
, having returned to the
USS
Cerritos
in
2381
, found that the computer refused to recognize him and stubbornly went into a
Jefferies tube
to reach the bridge when doors wouldn't open for him. (
LD
: "
We'll Always Have Tom Paris
")
To provide
privacy
, doors of personal quarters aboard Starfleet vessels and facilities were designed to open only for those to whom they had been assigned. Others could be admitted only if granted permission by the occupants or if those already inside chose to open them. This, however, could be overridden in cases of emergency. (
TNG
: "
Allegiance
")
In
2369
, Commander William T. Riker was trapped on
Tilonus IV
and subjected to a procedure to attempt to extract strategic information from his mind. As a defense mechanism, his mind created a
delusion
in which he found himself shifting between being on the
Enterprise
-D and as a patient in the
Tilonus Institute for Mental Disorders
. During one instance in which he believed himself to be in this insitute, one of the guards explained his
headache
as being due to him struggling with an
attendant
and hitting his head on a door. (
TNG
: "
Frame of Mind
")
In
2371
,
Major
Kira Nerys
discovered a door blocking her path when trying to locate a
power source
on the
Founders' homeworld
, one composed of a metal her
tricorder
could not identify.
Odo
found this odd, as
his people
had no need for doors, as they preferred not to take
humanoid
form. The two wondered who could be using it. Upon further investigation, they discovered that it possessed a locking mechanism designed apparently not to keep others out but to keep whatever was inside in. Odo then used his
shapeshifting
abilities to disable the lock. (
DS9
: "
The Search, Part II
")
In
2375
,
Neelix
was granted permission to
replicate
locking mechanisms for the doors of the
cabinets
in the
mess hall
of the
USS
Voyager
due to the repeated raids of an individual known only as the "
Midnight Snacker
." Ultimately, however, they proved unnecessary as the individual in question was soon afterwards identified as a
sleepwalking
Seven of Nine
, who was experiencing a form of
multiple personality disorder
due to proximity to a
Borg
vinculum
. (
VOY
: "
Infinite Regress
")
On the
Akaali homeworld
and
Makull's homeworld
, the doors had to be opened by hand. (
ENT
: "
Civilization
";
VOY
: "
Time and Again
")
In early
2401
Jack Crusher
repeatedly
hallucinated
a door at the end of a hallway. (
PIC
: "
Seventeen Seconds
", et al.)

Background information
Although the turbolift doors were easily operated by a members of the on set special effects department crew using a pulley (similar to common drapery pulleys), there were occasional accidents. In
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, the victims of such incidents included
William Shatner
and
Majel Barrett
, both of whom were caught between doors that were closing too quickly. At times, the doors were also extraneously noisy. (
The Making of Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, pp. 160-161 & 183)
Destroying the
Enterprise
bridge for
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
proved difficult because of the turbolift doors on the set, as they had been made from fiberglass. "
And you don't blow up fiberglass with people nearby,
" noted special effects supervisor
Bob Dawson
. He solved this issue by reconstructing the doors with balsa wood. Dawson also fitted the explosives required for the destruction behind the doors. (
The Making of the Trek Films
, 3rd ed., p. 56;
Trek: The Unauthorized Story of the Movies
, p. 97)
According to
Bill Wistrom
,
Douglas Grindstaff
created the sound of the doors in
Star Trek: The Original Series
by squeeking with his tennis shoe. ("Select Historical Data Year Six - Timeless Sound Effects",
TNG Season 6 DVD
special feature)
In "
The First Duty
",
Wesley Crusher
's
quarters
at
Starfleet Academy
have an old-fashioned door with a handle. In a commentary for the episode available on
TNG Season 5 Blu-ray
, episode writers
Ronald D. Moore
and
Naren Shankar
relate that
Rick Berman
freaked upon seeing this door, stating that there were no doors like this in the 24th century. A beeping sound effect was added in post to make the door more futuristic, though it was only used once. Additionally, an internal backstory was created that this was an ancient wing of the academy that hadn't been renovated.
External link
Door
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Dorian
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dorian
======================================================================

Dorian
Class:
Transport ship
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
For the similarly named
Human
civilian, please see
Doran
.
The
Dorian
was an unarmed
24th century
transport ship
. In
2369
, it was under the command of
Captain
Talmadge
.
In that year, the
Dorian
was on a mission to transport
Ambassador
Ves Alkar
to the
planet
Rekag-Seronia
. En route, the
Dorian
was flanked by two
Rekag battle cruisers
who caused damage to the
transport
's
subspace emitter
and
shields
.
Dorian
was assisted by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, which managed to deter the
Rekag
ships and would continue the
Dorian
's mission to Rekag-Seronia. (
TNG
: "
Man Of The People
")
According to
Captains' Logs: The Unauthorized Complete Trek Voyages
(pp. 257-258), this ship was named after
The Picture of Dorian Gray
, which bore similarities to the episode's story.
For more information on this
studio model
, please see
TNG studio models
.

Dramen patrol ship
Affiliation:
Dramen
government
Type:
Patrol ship
Active:
23rd century
Crew complement:
1
Speed:
Warp capable
The
Dramen patrol ship
was a small one-man spacecraft designed for
patrol
duty in the
Dramen star system
. This type of craft was slightly larger than a
Federation
shuttlecraft
.
In
2270
,
Demos
pursued the
USS
Enterprise
in a
patrol ship
, hoping to stop
James T. Kirk
from investigating
Dramia II
. The commander discovered that the
Enterprise
had "haphazardly" left the
hangar doors
open, allowing the patrol ship to enter, seemingly "unnoticed".
In reality, it was part of a plot to lure him aboard so that he could be arrested as a
stowaway
and thereby no longer interfere with Kirk's investigation. (
TAS
: "
Albatross
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Dream Alien ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Dream_Alien_ship
======================================================================

Visions and illusions
(covers information from a
vision
or
illusion
)
Dream Alien ship
Owner:
Dream Aliens
Type:
Starship
Active:
2374
Armament:
Directed energy weapons
Defenses:
Shields
The
Dream Alien ship
was a type of
starship
encountered in the
communal dream
experienced by the
USS
Voyager
crew in
2374
.
In the encounter, the initial Dream Alien ship attacked
Voyager
, it made multiple passes, forcing
Voyager
to reinforce
shields
with
auxiliary power
, while themselves unable to damage the Dream Alien ship, as their
phasers
were unable to penetrate its shields. As
Voyager
was about to launch
photon torpedoes
, it lost shields and was subjected to a localized
dampening field
, which caused a loss of power to the
torpedo launchers
. As their dampening field drained power from the remaining ship systems – engines, weapons, shields – compounded by the arrival of three more of these vessels,
Voyager
was forced to surrender, before the Dream Aliens began boarding their ship. (
VOY
: "
Waking Moments
")
Given the circumstances of how this vessel was encountered, it presumably did not exist in the so-called "
waking world
".
For more information on the
studio model
used, see
VOY studio models
.

PAGE: Drone
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Drone

For the
VOY
episode with a similar title, please see "
Drone
".
For the ant worker, please see
drone (biology)
.
For the Borg, please see
Borg drone
.
A
drone
was an unmanned
vehicle
controlled
remotely
or by
artificial intelligence
. They existed in a wide variety of sizes, configurations, and functions, ranging from hand-held
toys
to
starships
.
This article or section needs citations
This page or section does not adequately
cite its sources
, and therefore is
in need of attention
.
If you can provide references from
valid resource material
, feel free to
edit
it to add a citation or correct the data.
(
2025
)

Ba'ul drone
, a small, flying device used by the Ba'ul
Borg hull drone
, a type of insect-like device used to repair Borg cubes

, automated Federation freighters in the 24th century

, a type of Federation sensor drone used for intelligence-gathering purposes

DOT-7
DOT-23

, a
Minosian
combat drone designed to attack ground personnel and destroy starships

, a type of drone used for emergencies aboard ships in the late 32nd century

, a type of small, flying device used at Utopia Planitia Shipyards in 2385 and the Starfleet Archive Museum in 2399

, a type of small, flying device used at the Nightbox in Stardust City in 2399
Hover-cam
Hunhau drone
, a type of small, flying device used at the salvage yard on Hunhau in the late 32nd century
Hunter probe
Interface probe
Kid
Mark VII phaser sphere array drone
Medical drone
, a type of small flying device used for medical care aboard ships in the late 32nd century
Mercantile drone
, a type of small flying device used by the exchanges in the late 32nd century

, a type of drone controlled by AGIMUS
Orbital killer drone
Remote communications drone
, a type of recorder marker
Romulan drone
, a type of small flying device used by Romulans on the Artifact in 2399

======================================================================
PAGE: Drookmani ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Drookmani_ship
======================================================================

Drookmani ship
Owner:
Drookmani
Type:
Salvage
ship
Active:
2380s
Crew complement:
6
Armament:
None
The
Drookmani ship
was a type of
starship
used by
Drookmani
scavengers for their
salvage
operations during the late
24th century
.
History
Drookmani ship
In
2380
, a Drookmani ship was engaged in a heated dispute over abandoned
Starfleet
cargo
with the
USS
Cerritos
.
Captain
Carol Freeman
insisted on a diplomatic solution rather than firing on the Drookmani, who used their
tractor beam
to launch
debris
at the
Cerritos
. The conflict ended incidentally when an
animate isolinear core
ejected from the
Cerritos
disabled the Drookmani ship. (
LD
: "
Terminal Provocations
")
Later that year, a Drookmani ship arrived to salvage the
debris field
left from the
Battle of Kalla
.
In
2381
, the
exocomp
Peanut Hamper
secretly invited a Drookmani ship to
Areolus
to take the native
Areore
's
ancient ships
from beneath the surface, as part of a scheme to redeem herself to
Starfleet
by saving the Areore from the Drookmani. Peanut Hamper infiltrated the Drookmani ship and overloaded their
engines
, destroying it, though the Drookmani crew were able to
beam
to a nearby Areore ship. (
LD
: "
A Mathematically Perfect Redemption
")
Technical data
Tug section
The Drookmani ship consisted of a series of
cargo modules
attached to a crewed forward command section or
tug
. The command section could detach and operate independently, and contained a
shuttlecraft
in a ventral bay. The ship was unarmed but was equipped with two powerful
tractor beams
, one on the bow and one beneath the cargo modules, which pulled materials directly into a shredder. It had a crew complement of at least six. (
LD
: "
Terminal Provocations
", "
A Mathematically Perfect Redemption
")

======================================================================
PAGE: EM emitter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/EM_emitter
======================================================================

Reed and a security officer fire EM emitters
An
EM emitter
was a device used for the focused projection of
electromagnetic radiation
. They were used by
Starfleet
in the
22nd century
.
In about
2152
, EM emitters were typically hand-held devices, each of which had a long handle. When several members of the
Enterprise
NX-01
crew were captured by a
symbiotic lifeform
,
Malcolm Reed
and a security team initially attempted to use these EM emitters to weaken the creature, as
Doctor
Phlox
had determined that the creature was highly
photosensitive
.
In the final draft script of "
Vox Sola
", these EM emitters are described as "small, high-tech devices" that emitted "small beams of glowing energy."
Reed later jerry rigged some disc-shaped EM emitters that he programmed to set up a
force field
. These emitters each had a blue light that flashed on and off when they were ready for use, activated by a small button on the device, and could thereafter be operated by
remote control
. They were initially tested in the
Armory
aboard
Enterprise
, where their configuration was meanwhile displayed on a group of three screens. Four of these emitters were subsequently set up by Reed and
another officer
in a
cargo bay
where the creature was. Upon activating the jerry-rigged force field, Emitter B required slight adjustment, but Reed managed to correct it and the force field then held for as long as it was required. (
ENT
: "
Vox Sola
")
These EM emitters are not referred to as such on screen (despite a reference to "Emitter B"), though they were called "EM emitters" in the final draft script of "Vox Sola". Also in that script, a note read, "
They resemble the
targeting holo-emitter
we saw in "
Sleeping Dogs
".
" The circular EM emitters were operated from a console in the script, though, rather than by remote control.
In
2153
,
Commander
Tucker
considered using portable EM emitters to fire sustained bursts of
delta radiation
at
Xindi-Reptilian
weapons in order to disable them, but Phlox warned him that the amount of delta radiation necessary to do that would also be extremely hazardous to
humanoid
life.
Subsequently, Tucker used four of Reed's force field emitters while testing a
Xindi
biorifle
, believing they would contain the blast debris. However, they were ultimately not necessary, as the
rifle
wouldn't fire. (
ENT
: "
The Shipment
")

PAGE: Ekina
CATEGORIES: Starships

======================================================================

Ekina
Class:
Cargo ship
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
The
Ekina
was a "
cargo ship
", caught in a
plasma storm
near
Deep Space 9
in
2370
. It sent a
distress call
to the station, stating that their engines had been severely damaged, and would, subsequently, be
tractored
into the station's outer
docking ring
. In reality, however, the
Ekina
was a ship used by
Verad
to hijack Deep Space 9 in order to steal the
Dax symbiont
from
Jadzia Dax
. (
DS9
: "
Invasive Procedures
")
According to the script for this episode, the pronunciation for Ekina was "eh-KEY-nah".
[1]
For more information on this
studio model
, see
TNG studio models
.

An
electromagnetic collector
was a device that accumulated
electromagnetic energy
.
In
2368
,
Doctor
Kila Marr
compared the
Crystalline Entity
to an electromagnetic collector as it needed a lot of power to keep going and so stripped every form of
life
from the worlds it encountered and converted it into
energy
. (
TNG
: "
Silicon Avatar
")

Ellison
Class:
Transport
Status:
Active
(
2396
)
For additional meanings of "Ellison", please see
Ellison
.
The
Ellison
was a late
24th century
transport
.
On
May 12
,
2396
, the
Ellison
departed
Earth
for the
Beta Quadrant
. She was carrying
passengers
.
Soji Asha
claimed to be one of those passengers, but
Narek
claimed his research showed her name was not on the
passenger manifest
that day. (

This transport was only mentioned in dialogue.

co-producers
Akiva Goldsman
and
Michael Chabon
confirmed in a video made for
WIRED
magazine that the
Ellison
was named for
Harlan Ellison
.

The
emitter matrix
was a component of a
disruptor
.

,

arms dealer
Kovin
claimed he found a way to stabilize
thoron
-based disruptors by polarizing their emitter matrices. When
Seven of Nine
accused him of shooting her during this procedre, Kovin responded that he must have tuned the
induction frequency
too high, which knocked out Seven. (
VOY
: "
Retrospect
")

would-be

======================================================================
PAGE: Engineering notebook
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Engineering_notebook
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
An
engineering
notebook
was a collection of notes on engineering
data
.
In
2375
, during his last visit with
Moogie
,
Rom
brought home a
Starfleet
PADD
that he was using as an engineering notebook. While getting up in the
night
to find
something
to
eat
,
Zek
found the PADD, which contained a
schematic
for a
multidimensional transporter device
, which
Chief
Miles O'Brien
had given Rom to study. It was this schematic that gave the
Grand Nagus
the idea to travel to the mirror universe in search of new
business
opportunities for the
Ferengi
.
Zek explained all this to Rom and
Quark
when they were
detained
in the mirror universe. (
DS9
: "
The Emperor's New Cloak
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Enhancement module
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Enhancement_module
======================================================================

An enhancement module
An
enhancement module
was a piece of portable
computer
technology
that could be used to provide large amounts of active memory for a
tech cube
. Together, these two pieces made an
optronic data core
.
In
2369
, with the help of
Reginald Barclay
, the
holographic
James Moriarty
and
Countess
Regina Barthalomew
were stored in a
tech cube
equipped with an enhancement module to keep their
holodeck program
running continuously. The module had enough active memory to provide the characters a lifetime of experiences. (
TNG
: "
Ship In A Bottle
")
Tech cube prop inserted into module prop
The box-like enhancement module was represented via a prop designed by
Makeup & Effects Laboratories
. Prop fabrication specialist
Don Coleman
found the idea of the device appealing. "
The box was a vacuform pull with some pieces glued on the outside,
" explained Coleman. (
Star Trek: The Next Generation 365
, p. 289)

======================================================================
PAGE: Enolian patrol ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Enolian_patrol_ship
======================================================================

Enolian patrol ship
Affiliation:
Enolian Guard
Type:
Patrol ship
Active:
2150s
Armament:
Particle weapons
Enolian patrol ships
were a type of heavily armed
patrol ships
utilized by the
Enolian Guard
for patrol duty during the mid-
22nd century
.
In
2152
, a vessel of this class intercepted
Shuttlepod 1
as it was leaving
orbit
of
Keto-Enol
. The patrol ship discovered apparent contraband aboard the
shuttlepod
and apprehended the pod's occupants,
Jonathan Archer
and
Trip Tucker
.
Later, after it was discovered that Archer and Tucker were taken captive accidentally, the Enolian official who assisted them in locating the
Enolian transport
they were on suggested using patrol ships to intercept the transport, as they were several hours closer to it than
Enterprise
was.
Two of the patrol ships approached the transport and nearly disabled it before they were lured in and disabled themselves. (
ENT
: "
Canamar
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Enolian prison transport
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Enolian_prison_transport
======================================================================

Enolian prison transport
Affiliation:
Enolian Guard
Type:
Prison transport
Active:
2150s
Crew complement:
3
Armament:
At least one
particle weapon
The
Enolian prison transport
was a type of
prison transport
used by the
Enolian
Guard
to move groups of people to
prison
colonies
from
Keto-Enol
, as
crime
was rampant in their
system
.
Technical data
Enolian prison transports possessed a long, spindly frame with at least one docking hatch on the port side. They had two
impulse engines
, arranged vertically. The class was crewed by an average of three Guard
officers
, consisting of a
pilot
and two
security officers
. The class was heavily shielded to block
transporters
.
Although lightly armed with
particle weapons
, the class was not designed for combat and would not last long against two
Enolian patrol ships
.
History
In
2152
, an Enolian prison transport was carrying
Kuroda Lor-ehn
,
Zoumas
,
Starfleet
's
Captain
Archer
and
Commander
Tucker
, as well as a dozen other prisoners, to
Canamar
. Partway through the trip, Kuroda and a
Nausicaan
overtook the Enolian Guard officers and commandeered the ship. The Nausicaan knocked out the pilot, so they had to use Archer to fly the ship to their new heading – the trading
outpost
Tamaal
.
On the way to Tamaal, two
Enolian patrol ships
caught up with it. They damaged the transport, but Archer devised a way of releasing and detonating
warp plasma
to disable the patrol ships. They were then able to rendezvous with an
Enolian shuttle
, but a Starfleet crew was aboard. The prisoners evacuated in time, except for Kuroda, who insisted on staying and trying to take the ship out of its
decaying orbit
. However, he was unsuccessful, and he died when the ship burned up in the
planet
's
atmosphere
. (
ENT
: "
Canamar
")
Background info
In the script of "Canamar", the Enolian transport was described as "
A medium-sized vessel – one third the size of [an
NX-class
starship
], at most. It appears rugged and space-worn, its surface pitted and scarred from years of service.
" The Enolian transport's cockpit was scripted as "a cramped flight deck, as rough and worn as the ship's exterior," with "small viewports," only one seat, controls at the
helm
("which look nothing like those" aboard an NX-class vessel), and "a wall-mounted rack" for storing a
shotgun
-weapon. Access between the cockpit and the "main cabin" was via "a reinforced cockpit hatch". The cabin itself was referred to as "a dingy, claustrophobic compartment, not much more than a grimy
cargo hold
." The script additionally referred to the cabin as including two rows of metal benches, with a "central aisle" dividing both rows, though there are clearly more rows than that in the final edit of the episode.
The cockpit of the prison ship was a reuse of the
Class 2 shuttle
from
Star Trek: Voyager
.
The transport's destruction, in a fiery explosion, was scripted to be shown on screen, though does not appear in the final edit of the installment. According to the
reference book
Beyond the Final Frontier
(p. 378), the effects footage showing the craft's destruction was edited out shortly after
space shuttle orbiter
STS-107
disastrously disintegrated during reentry into Earth's atmosphere.}}

======================================================================
PAGE: Entharan starship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Entharan_starship
======================================================================

Forward view of Kovin's ship
Cockpit
Kovin's ship
was a
vessel
utilized by the
Entharan
arms merchant
named
Kovin
. The vessel was equipped with various offensive and defensive armaments, including
photon pulse
weapons, and
photonic pulse
emitters.
Kovin used his ship to try and escape the
USS
Voyager
in
2374
. While he was able to nearly disable
Voyager
, albeit while Janeway was refusing to fire, he was eventually destroyed himself when he shunted all his power to his
photonic emitters
, causing an overload. (
VOY
: "
Retrospect
")
For more information on this
studio model
, please see
DS9 studio models
.
In addition, the cockpit set was previously used as the
Aeon
's cockpit seen in
VOY
: "
Future's End
", "
Future's End, Part II
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Epidermal mold
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Epidermal_mold
======================================================================

Torso and facial molds
A
epidermal mold
was a piece of molding
technology
used in the process of forming the outer
skin
, or epidermis, of a
Soong-type
android
. The components of the mold include the
head
, arms, legs, and torso. The mold had the additional function of smoothing the texture of the epidermis.
When
Data
placed an epidermal mold over his face in
2364
and it fit,
Geordi La Forge
said, "
It's you, Data.
" (
TNG
: "
Datalore
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Equipment bag
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Equipment_bag
======================================================================

An
equipment bag
was a type of
bag
in which
tools
were carried.
In
2378
,
Tom Paris
wished to give
Yun
a
toy
he carried in his equipment bag, but was dissuaded by
Verin
, who worried it might be a
weapon
. (
VOY
: "
Friendship One
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Ergonomics
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ergonomics
======================================================================

Ergonomics
was the study of designing an
object
to suit the
user
.
Paul Stamets
remarked that the ergonomics of the
intermix pods
made no sense after they had been moved. (
DIS
: "
Scavengers
")
External link
Ergonomics
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Erstwhile
CATEGORIES: Starships

Erstwhile
Class:
Class 9 cargo vessel
Owner:
Thadiun Okona
Affiliation:
Coalition of Madena
Status:
Active
(
2384
)
The
Erstwhile
was an interplanetary
Class 9
cargo vessel
owned and operated by
Captain
Thadiun Okona
in the mid-
24th century
.
In
2365
, the
Erstwhile
experienced a
burnout
in its
guidance system
while traversing between the twin
planets
of the
Omega Sagitta system
. When the ship was intercepted by the
Federation
starship
,
USS
Enterprise
-D
, the
Erstwhile
was traveling at .02 c of
impulse
on a
course
of 232.2 mark 2. The crew of the
Enterprise
offered Okona assistance with his repairs. (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
")
In
2384
, Okona took the
Erstwhile
to
Denaxi Depot
with a load of
Ferengi
thought makers
, only to be
arrested
by the
Xindi-Reptilian
authorities for
smuggling
illegal
contraband
as soon as he landed. By that time, it had been retrofitted to be capable of speeds up to warp 4. Okona eventually escaped on the
USS
Protostar
, leaving the
Erstwhile
parked at Denaxi Depot. (
PRO
: "
Crossroads
")
For more information on the
studio model
, see
TNG studio models
.
External link
Erstwhile
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Etanian starship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Etanian_starship
======================================================================

An Etanian starship
The
Etanian starship
was a class of
starship
in use by the
Etanians
in the late-
24th century
.
Armament and Specifications
The Etanian starships are large and formidable
warship
's that were armed and equipped with
directed energy weapons
and possessed powerful
shields
, and had a crew complement of over 2,000.
History
In
2373
, the
USS
Voyager
encountered a ship of this class commanded by
Goth
, who was firing deadly asteroids at the planet Nezu in order for his people's military occupation forces to invade the planet. When Goth refused to withdraw peacefully, it is when both his personal Etanian starship and the USS Voyager engaged in a tense space battle with one another. Goth's Etanian starship was ultimately defeated using tactical data provided by the
Nezu
doctor
,
Vatm
, courtesy of
Tuvok
. The Etanian vessel then withdrew after losing the advantage. (
VOY
: "
Rise
")
Star Trek: Shipyards - The Borg and Delta Quadrant: Akritirian to Krenim
(p. 7) indicates the
CG assets
for this ship have been lost, resulting in it being left out of the publication: "The folders from the VFX team are empty, so this one vessel remains a mystery."

======================================================================
PAGE: External lighting
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/External_lighting
======================================================================

A
Dodge Ram
's activated external lighting
External lighting
referred to the exterior
lighting devices
of
land vehicles
and
space vehicles
.
Starships
,
shuttles
, and other
auxiliary craft
were equipped with such lighting. (
Star Trek: The Motion Picture
, etc.)
Automobiles
and
motorcycles
had external lighting known as headlights, that allowed the
driver
a clear view of the
roadway
ahead of them at
night
. Once in
Detroit
in the year
2004
, when
T'Pol
and
Archer
were
stealing
a
Dodge Ram
, T'Pol told Archer, "
I don't believe you have activated our external lighting.
" (
ENT
: "
Carpenter Street
")
Hovercars
and
aircars
were likewise equipped with such
technology
. When
Michael Burnham
was getting a
pardon
from the
President of the United Federation of Planets
in
Paris
, an aircar used its headlights to
illuminate
the
Eiffel Tower
. (
DIS
: "
Will You Take My Hand?
")
External links
Lighting
at
Wikipedia
Automotive lighting
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Extraction pump
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Extraction_pump
======================================================================

An extraction pump
An
extraction pump
was a large
mechanism
utilized for
retrieving
deuterium
from beneath a
planet
's surface to be utilized as
fuel
aboard
starships
.
In
2152
, the crew of
Enterprise
NX-01
discovered a deuterium
mining
colony
which utilized a series of extraction pumps. Due to repeated vandalism and assaults by the crew of a
Klingon
vessel, the colony was in an extremely impoverished state, including damaged and disabled extraction pumps, which
Enterprise
's
chief engineer
,
Charles Tucker
, helped to repair. Later, in an attempt to deceive the
Klingon crew
,
Captain
Archer
had the entire
colony
relocated approximately fifty
meters
to the south, including the pumps, in order to lure the Klingons into a trap. (
ENT
: "
Marauders
")
See also
Deuterium pump

======================================================================
PAGE: Eymorg starship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Eymorg_starship
======================================================================

Eymorg starship
Affiliation:
Eymorg
Active:
23rd century
Crew complement:
One
The
Eymorg starship
was a highly advanced
starship
operated by the
Eymorg
of
Sigma Draconis VI
during the mid-
23rd century
.
This vessel was constructed from a unique
technology
, with an origin potentially dating back as far as
8000 BC
. It was capable of high velocities, as it was driven by an
ion propulsion
drive. The vessel also possessed
transporter
capabilities and could be operated by a crew of one.
In
2268
, the
USS
Enterprise
encountered an unidentified Eymorg starship outside of the
Sigma Draconis system
. The ship, according to
Montgomery Scott
, was unlike anything he had seen, stating that its owners "could teach us a thing or two." The vessel, piloted by
Kara
, leader of the Eymorgs, was sent to the
Enterprise
to retrieve a brain to serve their new
Controller
.
After
stunning
the
Enterprise
's crew, Kara removed
Spock
's brain and left the
Enterprise
aboard her vessel, whose
ion trail
was later used by the
Enterprise
's
sensors
to track its final destination. (
TOS
: "
Spock's Brain
")
Background information
Original Eymorg starship (profile)
Original Eymorg starship (forward)
The revised final draft for the script of "Spock's Brain" described the vessel as "
Long, slender, needlelike, alien, almost glowing in the blackness of space.
"
For the
2007
remastered version of "Spock's Brain", a new computer-generated version of the ion-powered spacecraft was rendered by
CBS Digital
. The new ship featured a completely overhauled designed which abandoned the now antiquated notion of a rocket-shaped vessel.
The CBS description of the model stated:
"
In the episode "Spock's Brain" the ion propulsion spacecraft spotted by the Enterprise in 2268 certainly impressed Scotty with its design and technology. The comparison between the original and the remastered version provides two totally different looks, with styles defined more by the contemporary technology. The original is a standard rocket-shaped configuration. At the time, "spaceships" tended to fall into a one of two classic looks, the other being the flying saucer.The new, remastered version of the ion propulsion craft is smaller and more utilitarian, reflecting its single-person occupancy, and it ditches the unnecessary missile/rocket configuration. The design also reflects more modern-day ion propulsion prototypes.
"
[1]

PAGE: Fabrication device

A
fabrication device
was a form of
technology
used by the
Malurians
that was something akin to a
replicator
.
According to the Malurian
Garos
, the
antimatter reactor
in the
basement
of his
curio shop
powered a fabrication device which allowed him to produce
food
and
clothing
.
Captain
Jonathan Archer
later thought that such a reactor had "
an awful lot of power for a fabrication device,
" with which
Trip Tucker
agreed, "
He could probably feed and clothe half the
continent
with it.
"
Ultimately, this
lie
exposed Garos to Captain Archer, who later found the secret
mining
operation center under the
shop
. (
ENT
: "
Civilization
")

at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

sensor

PAGE: Fenris Ranger vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes

Fenris Ranger vessel
Owner:
Fenris Rangers
Active:
Late
24th
-Early
25th centuries
Crew complement:
1
Speed:
Warp-capable
Armament:
Directed energy weapons
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Fenris Ranger vessel
was a
class
of
warp-capable vessel
utilized by the
Fenris Rangers
during the late
24th
and early
25th centuries
. They were armed with
directed energy weapons
and flown with a single
pilot
. They had the capacity to carry
cargo
, but did not have
transporter
capabilities and were thus required to dock with other vessels in order to board them.
Seven of Nine
operated
such a vessel
when she was a member of the Rangers in
2399
. (
PIC
: "
Absolute Candor
")
Ships of the class
Unnamed Fenris Ranger vessels
Appendices
See also
Corsair
Appearances
PIC
:
"
Absolute Candor
" (
Season 1
)
"
Disengage
" (
Season 3
)
Background information
Issue #4
According to Issue #4 of the
Star Trek Universe: The Official Starships Collection
, Seven of Nine's ship was launched in the 24th century. This ship was 16.76
meters
in
length
.
The same issue also confirmed that the ship had been designed by Production Illustrator
John Eaves
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Fesarius
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fesarius
======================================================================

Fesarius
Affiliation:
First Federation
Status:
Active
(
2266
)
For star system, please see
Fesarius system
.
The
Fesarius
was a
starship
built and operated by the
First Federation
, encountered in the mid-
23rd century
. It was described as the
flagship
by its
captain
and only
crewmember
,
Commander
Balok
.
The
Fesarius
initiated
first contact
between the
Federation
and the First Federation in
2266
, when it was encountered by the Federation starship
USS
Enterprise
. (
TOS
: "
The Corbomite Maneuver
")
Technical details and capabilities
The
Fesarius
and the
Enterprise
at 5000 meters
The
Fesarius
was very large and spherical, with the surface made up of smaller, dome-like features, arranged in a distinct pattern based on triangles. The ship was able to dispatch at least one small craft, described as a "
pilot vessel
", and could be controlled by just one crewman using a very simple-looking interface.
The
mass
of the
Fesarius
was beyond the ability of 23rd century
Starfleet
instruments to register. Commander
Spock
estimated the vessel to be "a
mile
in
diameter
." However, the screen shots showing both ships indicate that the
Fesarius
clearly is much larger than Spock's estimate, because one mile would be approximately 5.5 times as long as the 947-foot
Enterprise.
Even at a
distance
of five thousand
meters
, the
Fesarius
still filled the entirety of the
Enterprise
viewscreen at normal magnification.
The vessel was able to scan the entirety of the USS
Enterprise
in a few
seconds
, in sufficient detail to then override and shut down some of its systems, a level of control that was described as "extremely sophisticated" and "brilliant." (
TOS
: "
The Corbomite Maneuver
")
Appendices
Background information
In the final draft
script
of "The Corbomite Maneuver" (dated
3 May
1966
), the
Fesarius
was described thus; "
No appraisal is possible, for this
object
[…] is completely alien in appearance. It is in the shape of a round cluster of
grapes
(or
fish
eggs
or
lead shot
or a
honeycomb
), but it looks very functional [....] Some of its cell-like sections pulse with an inner light.
" In the second revised draft of the script (dated
20 May
1966
), this description was simplified, with the
Fesarius
said to be "in the shape of a round cluster of balls." Also, the ship wasn't referred to as seeming "very functional", and the words "some of" were omitted. Both scripts characterized the vessel as a "colossus" that dwarfed the
Enterprise
. The final draft elaborated that the
Fesarius
' "cell-like sections" were to be "vari-colored" and the same script further stated, "
Not only is it formidable in size, but it is also visually impressive, looking all-powerful and frighteningly superior.
"
Neither the
Fesarius
nor any other
vessel
of the
First Federation
has been seen since "The Corbomite Maneuver".
Based on
Star Trek: Star Charts
(p. 36), and the
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Stellar Cartography", p. 10; "Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), one possible origin for the name of the ship was the Fesarius
system
. This was the capital system of the First Federation. The
primary
was a
K-class star
. This system was located in the
Alpha Quadrant
.
Studio models
Original studio model…
…and its CGI descendant
The CGI-
Fesarius
and
Enterprise
in close-up
The
Fesarius
miniature
was designed by
Matt Jefferies
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 269) The construction of the model has been attributed to
Wah Chang
, as per
Desilu
's purchase orders to him – currently archived at the
UCLA
.
[1]
According to
Star Trek History
, the model was a big ball endowed with "
a pair of dimmers to make the model ship pulsate with power. The model was covered with sliced ping-pong balls.
"
However, the
visual effects
involved in depicting the
Fesarius
kept "The Corbomite Maneuver" from airing as the first episode of
Star Trek
.
Jerry Sohl
, the writer of "The Corbomite Maneuver", explained, "
They were having such a difficult time with the special effects for the starship
Fesarius
… such an enormous ship… and this held them up for so long that it became the tenth episode to be shown.
" (
The Star Trek Interview Book
, p. 128) The effects sequences were filmed at the
Howard Anderson Company
in the period
3 June
1966
through
31 October
of that year, coming eventually in at US$17,317 – or 10% of the total budget and US$4,000 above the allowance for the effects. (
These Are the Voyages: TOS Season One
, 1st ed, pp. 126-127) The delays the company experienced during the production of these effects necessitated Associate Producer
Robert H. Justman
and Post-Production Supervisor
Edward K. Milkis
to hire virtually every other Hollywood effects house in existence at the time in order to alleviate the pressure on Anderson and to keep production of the
Original Series
on its tight schedules. (
Inside Star Trek: The Real Story
, 1997, p. 262)
When it came time to construct the
Fesarius
as a
CGI
model in 2006 for the
remastered
counterpart of the episode, the visual effects artists at
CBS Digital
tried to stay as close to the original as they could while also adding a level of detail that VFX Line Producer
Michael Okuda
stated "probably should have been there at the beginning," thus showing, though not overtly, an internal infrastructure beneath each of the ship's many domes. ("Spacelift: Transporting
Trek
into the 21st Century",
TOS Season 1 Blu-ray
special features)
Apocrypha
According to
Star Trek Maps
(Chart B and D), the
Fesarius
was a patrol ship in the largely uninhabited frontier of the First Federation. The ship was an automated craft designed to respond and intercept at
multi-warp speeds
any hostile force encountered by the defense outpost buoy network. The
Fesarius
was possibly named after the
homeworld
Fesarius and its namesake primary and star system.
In the
William Shatner
novel
Dark Victory
, this is a class of ship which is destroyed to prevent them from being captured by the
mirror universe
Kirk
.
Further reading
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
, issue 146, 2019
External links
Fesarius
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Field diverter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Field_diverter
======================================================================

A
field diverter
was a piece of
technology
used to divert
baryon
particles during a
baryon sweep
.
In
2369
, a group of
terrorists
posing as baryon sweep
engineers
from
Arkaria Base
used a field diverter installed in
engineering
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
to remain aboard the ship, which had been otherwise evacuated in advance of a sweep. After the terrorists captured
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, they brought him to engineering. During his escape from the terrorists, Picard destroyed the field diverter, forcing the group to head toward
Ten Forward
, the last place on the ship that would be affected by the sweep. (
TNG
: "
Starship Mine
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Filter
CATEGORIES: Science, Technology

======================================================================

A
filter
was any
device
,
mechanism
, agent or
procedure
that could be used to separate or remove an unwanted agent or desired byproduct from a product.
Types of filters
Air filtering system
Barystatic filter
Biofilter
Carbon dioxide filter
Carbon filter
Deuterium filter
Filter band
/
filter bandpass
Encoding filter
Narrowband filter
Theta band filter
Wideband filter protocol
Filter mask
Holodeck biofilter
Impulse response filters
L-90 enhanced resolution filter
Osmotic filter
Quantum filter
Perceptual filter
Plasmatic filter
Warp plasma filter
Things with filtration abilities
DNA
Environmental controls
/
Environmental control system
Intake manifolds
Sensors
Things that could be filtered out
Communication
distortions (
TOS
: "
The Immunity Syndrome
")
"
Human
factors
" (
TOS
: "
Journey to Babel
")
Sedatives
(
DS9
: "
The Adversary
")
Sub-harmonic distortions
See also
Filter bracket

through a

's

. It was a particularly hazardous area.

through a procedure that required him to enter the generator itself.

. (

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Fraddock's transport ship type
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Fraddock's_transport_ship_type
======================================================================

Fraddock's transport ship
Class:
Transport ship
Owner:
Fraddock
Status:
Active
(
2151
)
Fraddock's transport ship
was
a type
of small
22nd century
transport ship
operated by
Captain
Fraddock
.
In
September
2151
, Fraddock's transport ship ferried a group of
pilgrims
to the
Great Plume of Agosoria
as part of a
spiritual
encounter.
On 9 September, Fraddock's transport was contacted by the
Earth
Starfleet
starship
Enterprise
, which requested to make
contact
and make
cultural exchanges
. Fraddock agreed, docked his ship with
Enterprise
and offloaded a number of the pilgrims.
Fraddock's transport later escorted
Enterprise
along the fringe of the Plume, nearly missing an energy discharge that struck the Earth ship. (
ENT
: "
Cold Front
")
This vessel was designed by
John Eaves
, with the
studio model
built by
Pierre Drolet
.
[1]
It was scripted to be "small, weather-worn" and actually "tiny."
The interior of the vessel was depicted as a one-wall set, built on
Paramount Stage 18
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Free Spirit
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Free_Spirit
======================================================================

Free Spirit
Class:
Free Spirit
-type
Owner:
Petra Aberdeen
Status:
Active
(
2381
)
The
Free Spirit
was a small
starship
owned and operated by
Petra Aberdeen
, an
independent archaeologist
and former
Starfleet
officer
.
After recovering the
Grand Nagus' staff
from the
Museum of Antiquities
on
Tulgana IV
in
2381
, Aberdeen contacted
Ensign
Beckett Mariner
from the
Free Spirit
. (
LD
: "
Reflections
")
Later that year, having
resigned
from Starfleet, Mariner joined Aberdeen on her ship for adventures as her
number one
. (
LD
: "
Trusted Sources
")
The
Free Spirit
later took part in a battle against the renegade
USS
Aledo
, alongside an
armada
of
California
-class
ships. Following the battle, Mariner left the
Free Spirit
and returned to Starfleet. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")
The ship's name can be seen written in
cursive
on the
port
side of its
cockpit
.

.

.

A
fuel bin
or
power bin
was a storage receptacle for
fuel
.
In the
23rd century
, the
Delta-Vega Station
was equipped with fuel bins. In
2265
, while establishing a holding cell for
Gary Mitchell
at the station,
Lieutenant
Lee Kelso
was worried that if the bypass wasn't managed properly, the bins might explode. Later,
Captain
James T. Kirk
asked Kelso if the fuel bins could be set for self-destruction. He said that it was possible with the self-destruct switch being located in the control room. (
TOS
: "
Where No Man Has Gone Before
")

A
fuel synthesizer
was a
device
which was used to
synthesize
fuel
.
In
2268
,
Harry Mudd
fraudulently
sold the
Denebians
all the
rights
to a
Vulcan
fuel synthesizer, after which the Denebians informed the Vulcans of the incident. Upon learning that they had been duped, Mudd was
arrested
. (
TOS
: "
I, Mudd
")

.

======================================================================
PAGE: Galador freighter
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Galador_freighter
======================================================================

Galador freighters
were
vessels
utilized by the
Galadorans
during the
24th century
.
A Galador
freighter
operating on a
trade route
between
Galador II
and
Farius Prime
visited
starbase
Deep Space 9
in mid-
2370
. It was one of two vessels to depart the
station
following the disappearance of
Gul
Dukat
; the other
ship
was a
Bardeezan merchant ship
. Following the freighter's departure, it was on a course for Farius Prime, on a bearing of 216'177, when it went out of DS9's
sensor
range.
After some investigating, it was later determined that the Galadorans didn't have any ships in the
Bajor sector
and that the ship that had departed the station had a forged
registry
, and was actually operated by the
Maquis
. Subsequently, a
Klingon freighter
reported that it had identified the
warp signature
of the Maquis ship two
light years
from the
Demilitarized Zone
.
It was finally discovered by
Starfleet
on the surface of a
Class M
asteroid
in the
Badlands
. (
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
")

were a class of
freighter
operated by the

during the late

.

in late
2370
, carrying, among other things, commodities intended for
Elim Garak
. The ship, scheduled for arrival at 07:00, had still not arrived at the
space station
by
lunchtime
. (
DS9
: "
The Wire
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Gallough
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gallough
======================================================================

The
Gallough
was a
freighter
similar to the
Federation
Antares
-class
, in service during the
23rd century
. It had been modified to carry passengers.
In
2257
, the
Gallough
was transporting
refugees
from
Meslah V
, among them
Machalka
and her daughter
Oriana
, to the
Betteen colonies
. The ship disappeared en route; an extensive search uncovered no traces of its fate, and its 91 passengers and crew were listed as missing.
Two years later
, Oriana was found and rescued by the
USS
Peregrine
, revealing that the
Gallough
had fallen victim to the
Gorn
. (
SNW
: "
All Those Who Wander
"
display graphic
)

Gallough
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Gamma pulse ignition device
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Gamma_pulse_ignition_device
======================================================================

The
gamma pulse ignition device
was a technological device that emitted
gamma radiation
for igniting
explosives
.
Some of these devices were portable. If one of these devices was not being used in a high-
radiation
environment, a number of them would use
Walkerite 342
. An example of a gamma pulse ignition device was the
Type 12 Hillebrand detonator
. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")

PAGE: Gav'Nor

Gav'Nor
Status:
Destroyed
(
3069
)
The
Gav'Nor
was a
31st century
starship
destroyed by
the Burn
circa
3069
.
In
3189
,
Michael Burnham
obtained the
black box
of the
Gav'Nor
for her research into the Burn; its data was displayed with other
evidence
on a
holographic
note board in her
quarters
aboard the
USS
Discovery
. It was determined that the destruction of the
Gav'Nor
occurred one one-millionth of a
millisecond
after the
USS
Yelchin
, located a thousand
light years
away, indicating that the event was not simultaneous. (
DIS
: "
Forget Me Not
", "
Unification III
")
Mario Moreira
noted in
TRR
: "
There Is A Tide...
" that the
Gav'Nor
was a
Cardassian
ship, operated while the Cardassians were members of the
United Federation of Planets
.

PAGE: Gavarian ship

A
Gavarian ship
was a type of
starship
utilized during the mid-
22nd century
.
A
Ferengi starship
, commanded by the
Ferengi
merchant
Ulis
, once raided a Gavarian starship. Later in
2151
aboard
Enterprise
NX-01
,
Grish
thought that the enclosed
biobed
scanner had a similar design to the Gavarian ship's
vault
. (
ENT
: "
Acquisition
")
According to
Star Trek: Star Charts
("United Federation of Planets I") and
Stellar Cartography: The Starfleet Reference Library
("Federation Historical Highlights, 2161-2385"), there was a
star system
named Gavaria (
Sigma Pegasi
) in the
Alpha Quadrant
. This was a
trinary
system with a pair of
F-class stars
and a M-class star. In the late
24th century
, it was an independent system.

.
