PAGE: Krios Battlecruiser
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Krios_Battlecruiser
======================================================================

Krios Battlecruiser
Affiliation:
Kriosian
Type:
Battle cruiser
Active:
22nd century
The
Krios Battlecruiser
was a type of
battle cruiser
used by the
Kriosians
during the mid-
22nd century
. Along with protecting
Krios
space, the Krios battlecruiser was also assigned the duty of protecting the
First Monarch
and the Kriosian royal family, which was known across hundreds of
worlds
.
In
September
2152
, a Krios battlecruiser rendezvoused with
Enterprise
NX-01
to receive and transport First Monarch
Kaitaama
. (
ENT
: "
Precious Cargo
")
In the final draft script for "Precious Cargo", this battle cruiser was consistently called an "Imperial Vessel" (apart from in a
captain's log
entry made in both the script and the actual episode). The script also described the craft as "slightly smaller" than the
NX-class
Enterprise
.
The Krios Battlecruiser was evidently a re-use of the
CGI
for the
Akritirian patrol ship
, and even still had the emblem of the
Akritirians
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Kukulkan's ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Kukulkan's_ship
======================================================================

Kukulkan's ship
Owner:
Kukulkan
Active:
2270
Crew complement:
1
Speed:
At least
warp 3
Kukulkan's ship
was a highly advanced
starship
controlled by the winged serpent creature
Kukulkan
.
History
Kulkulkan's ship was encountered by the
USS
Enterprise
in
2270
, while investigating the alien probe that had visited and scanned several
Federation
homeworlds
. During the encounter, several members of the
Enterprise
crew were beamed aboard the vessel.
While on board the ship, the crew of the
Enterprise
were given the chance to solve the riddle of
Kukulkan's city
, which Humanity on
Earth
had failed to solve.
After an attack by the
Enterprise
,
Captain
Kirk
released a
Capellan power-cat
. Between the
Enterprise
and the cat enough damage was caused to the ship to cause Kukulkan to lose control of it.
After Kirk explained that Humans were not like these other creatures and that they didn't need him, Kukulkan understood and allowed them to leave, with his ship returning in the direction from which it came.  (
TAS
: "
How Sharper Than a Serpent's Tooth
")
Technical information
Kukulkan's ship, with the image of Kukulkan projected
Kukulkan's ship side view with the image of Kukulkan projected
Extremely unorthodox in design, it had a
hull
composed of
crystalline ceramic
, and was surrounded by an immense
energy field
that could project the visage of Kukulkan. The energy field also made the ship impenetrable to Federation
sensors
.
Much was explained in the complexity of this design in the episode's script:
Kukulkan's ship [..] is a highly advanced spacecraft. This forward view almost has the appearance of a
demon
, with
command module
as its face, the curving prow as its head, and the propulsion wings as its
horns
.
The ship is a slender cylindrical vehicle, with tapering winglike
propulsion units
only vaguely like those of the
Enterprise
. There is a flattened and sloping command module as its prow; it houses a round, glassy, onyx-shaded globe. Emphasis is on sleekness and engineering sophistication (no portholes, please.)
[On main viewing screen] a ghostly image of Kukulkan's face superimposes itself over the forward view of his ship, which remains visible under image: the prow becomes a
snake
's head, grinning and gigantic, with
jaws
agape, huge
fangs
, and a collar of rainbow
feathers
. The propulsion wings are closed within image of actual feathered wings. (This is stylized after actual
Mayan
depiction, please.)
Now we see the advanced spacecraft shrouded inside the ghostly serpent image which conforms to the smaller graceful lines of the ship.  Prow is within the head of the image, a fanged
dragon
's head with a collar of many-colored feathers; the slim hull matches the transparent serpentine body that is covered with multicolored scales; propulsion units near stern are embodies inside wings.
In all, the ship was twice the size of a
Federation
Constitution
-class
starship.
Like
the probe
sent from this ship, it too, presumably, had a highly advanced
propulsion system
, capable of achieving
at least
warp 3
.
It was also equipped with a powerful probe beam, and
transporters
that could penetrate
deflector shields
. Offensive capabilities of the ship included the ability to create a
force globe
to restrict the movement of its target, which could instantaneously bring a vessel travelling at warp 1 to a dead stop. All of which was controlled by Kukulkan via
telepathy
. (
TAS
: "
How Sharper Than a Serpent's Tooth
")
Interior design
Undefined area
This area of the ship was where
Kukulkan's city
appeared.
According to the script was a "
gray featureless plain which is a vast Daliesque, arena-like area that will be setting for the city Kukulkan will use to test Kirk and others.
"
Life room
The life room contained
Kukulkan's zoo
, and
the machinery
to create virtual worlds in the minds of other cognizant beings.  (
TAS
: "
How Sharper Than a Serpent's Tooth
")
According to the script, the life room "
is very large, low-ceilinged, stretching off almost out
of sight. No walls or corners are visible. The room is colored a dark, deep purple, heavily shadowed. Room has many levels and platforms which seem to come out of nowhere. Suspended throughout the room are several transparent containers, some round, some square, others in unusual curved shapes. On the floor are more of the same; several highly stylized transparent cages are also visible, solid glassy substance, not with bars."
External link
Kukulkan's starship
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Kyrian fighter ship

A
Kyrian fighter ship
was a type of
attack fighter
used by the
Kyrians
in the
2370s
.
In
2374
, three Kyrian fighters attacked the
USS
Voyager
during trade negotiations with the
Vaskans
. They
beamed
aboard a raiding party, led by
Tedran
, to steal
technology
. After Tedran was killed during a hostage situation, nine Kyrian ships attacked
Voyager
, compelling its retreat.
In the
holoprogram
The
Voyager
Encounter
, created in the
31st century
, eight Kyrian fighters attacked the
warship
Voyager
after
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
made a deal with Vaskan
Ambassador
Daleth
to attack the Kyrians. (
VOY
: "
Living Witness
")
This type of ship was only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Kzinti police vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes

Kzinti police vessel
Affiliation:
Kzinti government
Type:
Police vessel
Active:
23rd century
Speed:
Warp capable
The
Kzinti police vessel
was a type of
starship
utilized by the
Kzinti
. Following the
Kzinti Wars
, and in accordance to the
Treaty of Sirius
, Kzinti
police
vessels
were the only type of military craft the Kzinti were allowed to possess.
In
2269
, a police vessel known as the
Traitor's Claw
was
stolen
by the
Chuft-Captain
and his crew, where it was used in the attempted theft of a
Slaver stasis box
. (
TAS
: "
The Slaver Weapon
")
Background information
Conceptual design of a
22nd century
Kzinti starship
It was disclosed that if
Star Trek: Enterprise
had been renewed for a fifth season,
Manny Coto
wanted to do a Kzinti episode. Talk about the episode progressed to the point that a "rough rendering" of a Kzinti starship was produced for possible use. This rendering was similar to the ship designs for Kzinti warships in the alternate
Star Fleet Universe
.

Lt. Januzzi's LEAL helmet
A
LEAL helmet
was a
device
worn around the head, imprinted with an L.E.A.L
insignia
.
In
2256
,
USS
Shenzhou
communications officer
Troy Januzzi
wore a silver LEAL unit. (
DIS
: "
The Vulcan Hello
", "
Battle at the Binary Stars
")
In
2258
, a
USS
Enterprise
science officer
wore a white LEAL unit during the battle with
Control
. (
DIS
: "
Such Sweet Sorrow
", "
Such Sweet Sorrow, Part 2
")
The LEAL helmet was conceived of by
Glenn Hetrick
and
Neville Page
, inspired by Lobot from
Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back
. (
Star Trek: The Art of Glenn Hetrick's Alchemy Studios
, p. 44)

======================================================================
PAGE: Lambda field generator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lambda_field_generator
======================================================================

A schematic of a lambda field generator
A
lambda field generator
was a
field generator
which produced lambda waves.
In the
United Federation of Planets
, these generators were available from
Starfleet Materiel Supply Command
, and operational specifications on these generators were detailed in the
Starfleet material supply command database
.
These generators could be used in conjunction with a
Krieger wave converter
to produce
Krieger waves
.
Doctor
Nel Apgar
developed this procedure in the
2360s
, using a 23
megawatt
low frequency lambda field generator for his experiments. The generator was located on the surface of
Tanuga IV
, while the converter was located on the
space station
in
orbit
of the
planet
. The converter required a minimum of five thousand
kilometers
for the lambda field to produce parallel rays of
light
.
When the
USS
Enterprise
-D
visited the Tanuga IV research station in
2366
, Dr. Apgar started the lambda field generator (and therefore created Krieger waves) in an attempt to kill
Commander
William T. Riker
. However, he instead destroyed the station and killed himself in the process. After the explosion, the generator continued to recharge and fire every five hours, twenty minutes and three seconds. When the
Enterprise
used Apgar's designs to create a
facsimile
of the converter on the
holodeck
, to determine what happened, more Krieger waves were inadvertently generated and hit random parts of the
ship
, based on its relative location to the lambda field generator. (
TNG
: "
A Matter of Perspective
")
Components
(Diagram A)
Duotronic
transtator
matrix with
isolinear
Kreitzman junction box
Field sensor points with thermal equalizing emitters and nonintegral Barredo stabilizers.
High frequency
Lichfield input
servos
with parametric Molnar coils and quantum
multitronic
pulse counters. Dante
particle
lepton
compensated accelerometer for null subspace compression.
Legato fringe core.
Isolinear Hutzel field
oscillation
servo
(Diagram B)
Primary isotanium grid with integral duotronic Payne matrix and secondary loop
Graf emission plate (12)
Bruno coils (3)
Lee resonance gap, rated for at least 120
mw
/second under normal Greenburg pressures
Cryogenic
helium
supply spheres for Garza subspace core
See also
Field generator

======================================================================
PAGE: Laser detonator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Laser_detonator
======================================================================

A
laser detonator
was a technological device that emitted an energetic
gamma
pulse. It was used to ignite
explosive
materials
, such as
mooride polyronite B
, which induced small-scale
fusion reactions
.
In
2367
, some of these devices were stored aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. (
TNG-R
: "
Night Terrors
")
This detonator was only mentioned in writing.

A
laser fusion initiator
was a subsystem of
Cardassian
fusion reactors
which provided the energy input needed to bootstrap their
laser-induced fusion
reaction processes.
According to
Gul
Dukat
in
2371
,
Deep Space 9
's reactor could be shut down by disabling the initiator. (
DS9
: "
Civil Defense
")

A Ledosian patrol ship docked
The
Ledosian patrol ship
was a type of
starship
class in use by the
Ledosians
in the late-
24th century
.
In
2378
, one such ship disabled the
USS
Voyager
's
transporters
in order to stop them from beaming up Ledosian science teams and re-establishing an energy barrier which protected a primitive civilization on the planet
Ledos
.
Captain
Janeway
then ordered
Lieutenant
Tom Paris
to proceed with the mission in the
Delta Flyer
. He was ultimately successful, though the Ledosian patrol ship did also manage to disable the
Flyer
's transporters. (
VOY
: "
Natural Law
")
For more information on the studio model, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Life creation technology
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Life_creation_technology
======================================================================

Life creation technnology
was used by the
Progenitors
. It was a technology similar to, but much more sophisticated than, the
Genesis Device
. It could design and create life, but not restore it. The Progenitors had worried about doing too much with the technology which was why they had only chosen to seed life, and they had not created the technology, but merely found it. The Progenitor made
Captain
Burnham
the new steward of the technology and gave Burnham the choice of what was the best thing to do with it, showing her the four and a half billion years of events that had led to everyone in the present day being alive.
Ultimately, Burnham chose to have
USS
Discovery
throw the portal into the
event horizon
of one of the
black holes
, trusting that what the Progenitors created for them was enough and that the species responsible for creating the technology – if they still existed – would have the ability to recreate it should they wish to do so.
Doctor
Kovich
later reported that the
Red Directive
had officially been closed, everything classified, and no one would ever know anything about the Progenitors' technology. (
DIS
: "
Life, Itself
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Lighting panel
CATEGORIES: Technology

======================================================================

A lighting panel
A
lighting panel
was a small
technological
device
which had the capability of
illuminating
an entire
room
.
In
2267
,
Captain
Kirk
and
Spock
observed
a lighting panel in use on the
planet
Beta III
, one of the few
relics
left over from a time before the planet's
civilization
became enslaved by the computer intelligence
Landru
. (
TOS
: "
The Return of the Archons
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Lightning rod
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lightning_rod
======================================================================

A
lightning rod
was a piece of
metal
designed as a
conductor
to attract and direct
lightning
into the ground. This would prevent damage to structures or individuals.
An effort in
2368
to remove dust and
volcanic
ash from
Penthara IV
by attracting it to the
USS
Enterprise
-D
and discharging it harmlessly into
space
was likened to turning the
Enterprise
into a lightning rod. (
TNG
: "
A Matter Of Time
")
In
2377
, when
USS
Voyager
was brought back into
temporal
sync following a
chronokinetic surge
that had shattered it into 37 different timeframes,
Commander
Chakotay
had
Lieutenant
B'Elanna Torres
reroute power
main power
to the
deflector dish
and setting the
polarity
to a specific
frequency
, stating "
Have you ever heard of a lightning rod? In about three seconds, we're going to need one.
" (
VOY
: "
Shattered
")
External link
Lightning rod
at
Wikipedia

line amp

in the

.

metaphasic project

: "

set. This graphic was put up for auction in 2018.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Liquid data chain
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Liquid_data_chain
======================================================================

Quark inspects a vial of liquid data chains in 2370
A
liquid data chain
was a type of information device that came in
liquid
form.
In
2370
,
Quark
helped the
Klingon
mercenary
Yeto
gain access to
Deep Space 9
during a
plasma storm
in order to purchase a case of liquid data chains. The sale, however, was a ploy and the Klingon was actually part of a team hired by
Verad
to steal the
Dax
symbiont
. Despite Quark's help, he was taken prisoner along with the rest of the
station
's
senior staff
. (
DS9
: "
Invasive Procedures
")
The script for "
Invasive Procedures
" describes Quark's liquid data chains as "
...small vials of liquid
[in]
a padded case.
"
[1]
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 2nd ed., p. 274 describes them as a "fluidic information storage medium".

======================================================================
PAGE: Living construct
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Living_construct
======================================================================

The last living construct
"
Just as
the Federation
ignited
our
civil war
, when the
Protostar
comes into contact with a
Starfleet
vessel, our weapon will release a signal that will corrupt their systems, turning their ships against each other until the entire alliance is reduced to nothing!
"
–
The Diviner
,
2384
("
A Moral Star, Part 2
")
The
living construct
, or simply the
construct
, was a
weapon
created by the
Vau N'Akat
, designed to turn enemy
computer systems
against themselves.
History
Original timeline
In the
25th century
, when the
USS
Protostar
emerged from an unstable
wormhole
over
Solum
, the Vau N'Akat captured the ship and installed their last surviving construct aboard, planning to send it
back in time
to destroy the
Federation
and thereby avert the fateful
first contact
between their peoples.
Captain
Chakotay
and
Commander
Adreek-Hu
managed to escape captivity but were unable to disarm the weapon or board the ship to escape. As a last resort, Chakotay launched the ship unmanned back through the wormhole before the Vau N'Akat could enact their plan. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
", "
Preludes
")
In
2384
,
the Diviner
told his
daughter
Gwyn
about the construct and its purpose. However, Gwyn lost her
memory
of the conversation after seeing a
reflection
of
Zero
without their
containment suit
. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
")
The crew of the
Protostar
later visited the Federation
relay station
CR-721
to seek
asylum
. When the station's sole crew member,
Lieutenant jg
Barniss Frex
, attempted to
download
the
Protostar
's
logs
, the construct activated and caused the station to destroy itself. As the
Protostar
crew fled the doomed station, Gwyn remembered what her
father
had told her. (
PRO
: "
Asylum
")
The crew subsequently located the construct in a hidden subdeck below the
bridge
, but could not disable it. Thus, they decided to access the
vinculum
of a
dormant
Borg cube
to seek any information that could help them. The
Borg Collective
analyzed the weapon and indicated that it could not be removed or deactivated. They then attempted to
assimilate
the
Protostar
so they could use the construct themselves, but the
Protostar
was able to escape. (
PRO
: "
Let Sleeping Borg Lie
")
Meanwhile, Frex informed
Vice Admiral
Kathryn Janeway
of the presence of an unknown weapon aboard the ship. Unfortunately, a rogue faction of the
Romulan
Tal Shiar
were also made aware of the weapon, who attempted to steal the
Protostar
and its dangerous technology for themselves while it was stranded in the
Romulan Neutral Zone
. It was only by the narrow intervention of Dal and the
Protostar
crew that this was averted. (
PRO
: "
Crossroads
", "
Masquerade
")
With no way to deal with the construct directly, the
Protostar
crew had to flee from Starfleet. However, when they chose to give up on contacting the Federation altogether, a hidden
Vau N'Akat
subroutine
in
Hologram Janeway
's coding allowed the living construct to intervene and forced Hologram Janeway to trap the
Protostar
crew in the
holodeck
and take the ship herself, passing the
USS
Dauntless
into the heart of
Federation space
. (
PRO
: "
Masquerade
", "
Ghost in the Machine
")
When Zero and Dal attempted to telepathically warn Janeway of the Living Construct's exact nature, their psychic connection accidentally swapped Dal and Admiral Janeway's
neural patterns
. During this time, Admiral Janeway learned the full truth of the weapon and the predicament aboard the
Protostar.
Because Hologram Janeway was a non-essential system, Admiral Janeway was able to remove the Construct's corruption from her holographic counterpart before she returned to her own body. (
PRO
: "
Mindwalk
")
The construct transmitting its signal across the fleet
Unfortunately, the Vindicator, in her guise as
Trill
Ensign
Asencia
, was able to activate the construct when she contacted the
Dauntless
. The construct was able to spread its corruption to
Dauntless
and other Starfleet ships that arrived in response to its distress signal. Despite assistance from the Federation's allies, such as the
Klingons
,
Ferengi
and
Gorn
, the construct continued to corrupt Starfleet's vessels as they
destroyed themselves and their allies
. Realizing the situation would otherwise end with Starfleet's annihilation and seeing no other way to remove the construct, the
Protostar
's crew elected to destroy their ship. The ship, under the command of Hologram Janeway, was destroyed along with the construct, ending the signal as there was no longer a source. (
PRO
: "
Supernova, Part 1
", "
Supernova, Part 2
")
Altered timeline
In an altered timeline,
Chakotay
and
Adreek-Hu
escaped with the
Protostar
instead of sending it back alone, resulting in the ship never reaching Tars Lamora. (
PRO
: "
Who Saves the Saviors
") In this timeline, the ship landed on
Ysida
in
2374
after the
proto-core
and
antimatter
were ejected to ensure that it could never fly again and thus the living construct wouldn't pose a threat to the Federation. (
PRO
: "
Last Flight of the Protostar, Part I
")
After finding the
Protostar
on Ysida, the crew revealed to Chakotay the events with the living construct in their timeline, that the Federation was aware of it, and the living construct was no longer a threat if the
Protostar
were to be launched. (
PRO
: "
Last Flight of the Protostar, Part I
")
After the
Protostar
rendezvoused with the
USS
Voyager
-A
, Starfleet was able to render the living construct ineffective against their ships by using an
antivirus upgrade
developed with Starfleet's previous experience fighting the weapon and the chance to study it while the device was inactive. (
PRO
: "
Ascension, Part I
")
The
Protostar
was later sent back in time to Tars Lamora with the weapon onboard in order to repair the broken timeline. (
PRO
: "
Ouroboros, Part II
")
Writer
Aaron Waltke
posited that the Borg assimilation of Zero, combined with Zero's knowledge of the device, would lead to the development of the Borg transmitter that was utilized during the
Frontier Day
incident in 2401.
Technical data
The living construct was located in a sub-
deck
accessible via a hidden hatch on the
bridge
, which was marked with a symbol of
Solum
not visible to the naked
eye
. When another
Starfleet
vessel contacted the
Protostar
, the weapon would transmit a signal that would corrupt the
computer
of the other vessel and turn its systems against itself, causing its destruction. The construct behaved as though it were
alive
and could change shape to defend itself from threats. Given the near-insurmountable difficulty in removing a construct, the only reliable recourse for any ship with one installed was to
self-destruct
, especially if it was already corrupting vessels. (
PRO
: "
A Moral Star, Part 2
", "
Let Sleeping Borg Lie
", "
Supernova, Part 2
")
Starfleet was later able to develop an
antivirus upgrade
to render the living construct harmless to their ships after the battle it instigated and a chance to study it on the
Protostar
from an altered timeline. (
PRO
: "
Ascension, Part I
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Locking clamp
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Locking_clamp
======================================================================

The locking clamps of Soran's launcher engaged
Locking clamps
were physical mechanisms meant to keep a
missile
or other projectile in place.
In
2371
, while attempting to
sabotage
Tolian Soran
's
solar probe
-launcher,
Jean-Luc Picard
engaged the locking clamps, preventing the probe from being fired into the
Veridian
sun. By the time Soran realized what Picard had done, the countdown to the launch had already completed and Soran was killed in the resultant explosion. (
Star Trek Generations
)

======================================================================
PAGE: Lokirrim patrol ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lokirrim_patrol_ship
======================================================================

Lokirrim patrol ship
Affiliation:
Lokirrim
Type:
Patrol ship
Active:
2370s
Armament:
Phasers
,
photonic disruptor
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Lokirrim patrol ship
was a type of medium-sized
starship
used by the
Lokirrim
to patrol their territory. The ship was equipped with a
tractor beam
and a
photonic disruptor
, which could be used to remotely decompile
holograms
. It was tactically outmatched by the
Intrepid
-class
starship.
In
2377
, a Lokirrim patrol ship commanded by
Ranek
attacked the
Delta Flyer
after detecting
The Doctor
, whom they mistook for a
photonic insurgent
. The crew of the
Flyer
were detained aboard the ship until they were freed by the
USS
Voyager
. (
VOY
: "
Body and Soul
")
The Lokirrim patrol ship and the
Lokirrim warship
were both modifications of the
Akritirian patrol ship
. For more information on this
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lokirrim warship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lokirrim_warship
======================================================================

Lokirrim warship
Affiliation:
Lokirrim
Type:
Warship
Active:
2370s
Armament:
Phasers
,
photonic disruptor
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Lokirrim warship
was a class of medium-sized
starship
utilized by the
Lokirrim
in their conflict with
photonic insurgents
. The ship was equipped with
phasers
and a
photonic disruptor
that could remotely decompile
holograms
, though it was no match for a
Federation
Intrepid
-class
starship.
In
2377
, a Lokirrim warship attacked
USS
Voyager
after detecting holographic activity on its
holodeck
.
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
was able to broker a truce, though she was forced to disable the ship after learning that a
Lokirrim patrol ship
had detained the
Delta Flyer
. (
VOY
: "
Body and Soul
")
The Lokirrim warship and the Lokirrim patrol ship were both modifications of the
Akritirian patrol ship
. For more information on this
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Lutani scout ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lutani_scout_ship
======================================================================

Lutani scout ship, lateral view
The
Lutani scout ship
was a type of one-person
scout ship
employed by the
Lutani
during the mid-
23rd century
. It was armed with
directed energy weapons
.
In
2261
,
Doctor
Gyud
attempted to stop the deployment of a
weaponized
Jikaru
from a Lutani scout ship. When she fired on the
lifeform
, the Jikaru responded by producing a massive
radiation
burst that disabled her ship. The Jikaru then tore Gyud's vessel apart with her appendages. (
SNW
: "
What Is Starfleet?
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Lutani warship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lutani_warship
======================================================================

Lutani warship, forward view
The
Lutani warship
was a type of
military
vessel
employed by the
Lutani
during the mid-
23rd century
.
In
2161
, a Lutani warship commanded by
Doctor
Trunn Voor
intercepted the
USS
Enterprise
, which had run into complications on its
mission
to transport a
weaponized
Jikaru
to
Lutani VII
. Accusing
Starfleet
of wanting the Jikaru for themselves, Voor prepared to attack, which provoked the Jikaru into releasing a massive
radiation
burst that disabled his ship. (
SNW
: "
What Is Starfleet?
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Luvetric pulse
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Luvetric_pulse
======================================================================

A
luvetric pulse
was a type of wide-area blast a personnel
phaser
could be modified to emit.
In
2369
, the crew of
USS
Enterprise
-D
were looking for
Data
and
Crosis
, a
Borg drone
, on a
planet
with naturally high
EM interference
in its
magnetosphere
. They had a 20
kilometer
radius
search zone and the interference made it impossible to use
sensors
to locate Data.
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
suggested they could modify a phaser to send out a luvetric pulse. Such a pulse would have created a resonance fluctuation in Data's
power cells
. They could have then pinpointed the fluctuation with a
tricorder
.
Geordi La Forge
then pointed out that to be effective the pulse would have to be so powerful that it would destroy Data's
positronic net
in the process. (
TNG
: "
Descent
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Lysian destroyer
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Lysian_destroyer
======================================================================

Lysian destroyer
Owner:
Lysian Alliance
Operator:
Lysian
military
Type:
Destroyer
Active:
24th century
Crew complement:
53
Armament:
Disruptors
Defenses:
Deflector shields
Lysian destroyers
were short-range
attack vessels
, utilized by the
Lysian Alliance
during the late
24th century
, that had a standard crew complement of fifty-three.
Lysian destroyers employed
disruptor
-style weapons, that had a capacity of 2.1
megajoules
, and were protected by minimal
shields
. These warships were "
greatly outclassed
" by the
Federation
's
Galaxy
-class
starships
, as their level of weapons
technology
was "over 100 years behind" that which was employed by the Federation during the
2360s
.
In
2368
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
encountered and destroyed a Lysian destroyer after being tricked by a
Satarran
ploy that intentionally brought the ship and the Federation into the war between the two powers. After making contact with the Lysians,
Enterprise
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
conveyed the Federation's "
deepest regrets over the tragedy of their lost ship and
crew
.
" (
TNG
: "
Conundrum
")
For more information on this design and history of this model, please see:
Studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Machine
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Machine
======================================================================

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
Data: A Machine
A
machine
, also known as
machinery
,
mechanism
,
mechanical device
,
mechanical apparatus
, or
apparatus
, was, at its simplest, a mechanical
device
used for applying force. A compound machine, consisting of many simple machines, was powered by electric or mechanical force and worked at a higher
speed
.
A machine made work easier by reducing the cost, time, and individual effort and was designed to perform one or more operations. It increased the efficiency of individual effort.
As
dreams
became more important than
reality
to the
Talosians
, they lost the
skills
needed to repair the machines left behind by
their ancestors
. (
TOS
: "
The Cage
")
Although
Leonard McCoy
did say
individuals
were machines, he denied saying to
James T. Kirk
that individuals were ultimately superior to
mechanical devices
. (
TOS
: "
The Corbomite Maneuver
")
In
2267
, a
spore
infected
Elias Sandoval
described his
settlement
on the
planet
Omicron Ceti III
as having a simple
philosophy
where people returned to a less complicated life with few mechanical
things
, no
vehicles
, and no
weapons
. (
TOS
: "
This Side of Paradise
")
That same year,
Spock
stated that
Jackson Roykirk
's
dream
was to build a
perfect
thinking
machine, capable of independent
logic
. The result was the
prototype
Nomad
, the best thinking machine that could be
engineered
which was launched in the early
2000s
. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
")
The
Ba'ku
of planet
Ba'ku
believed that something was taken away from a man when a machine was created for doing the work of a man. (
Star Trek: Insurrection
)
External link
Machine
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Majalan combat cruiser
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Majalan_combat_cruiser
======================================================================

Majalan combat cruiser
Affiliation:
Prospect VII
colony
Type:
Combat cruiser
Active:
2250s
Armament:
Phasers
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Majalan combat cruiser
was a type of small
warship
fielded by the dissident
Majalan
colony
on
Prospect VII
in the
23rd century
. (
SNW
: "
Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
")
History
In
2259
, the Prospect VII colonists dispatched a combat cruiser in an attempt to save the new
First Servant
from being
sacrificed
on
Majalis
. The
USS
Enterprise
, responding to the
distress call
sent by the First Servant's
shuttle
, accidentally inflicted crippling damage on the cruiser and caused it to
crash
on a nearby
moon
. An
away team
subsequently found that the crew had evacuated and wiped their
data banks
to hide their true intentions, though a defaced
oath coin
and a
neural dampener
were recovered from the wreckage.
The colonists later sent another combat cruiser after the First Servant. With the collaboration of
Elder
Gamal
, the cruiser appeared to beam the First Servant directly off an
Enterprise
transporter pad
, before attempting to flee. The
Enterprise
locked a
tractor beam
on the cruiser, which was destroyed when it engaged its
warp drive
anyway. However, this action was in fact a feint, as Gamal had actually beamed the First Servant to a
cargo container
elsewhere on the
Enterprise
. (
SNW
: "
Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
")
Technical data
The cruiser's low
technology
level reflected the limited resources available to Prospect VII. It possessed turret-mounted
phasers
and
deflector shields
, though it posed no threat to a
Federation
Constitution
-class
starship
and could not withstand even a minimum-
powered
phaser blast from the latter. The ship was equipped with several forward-mounted
grapplers
for capturing other ships, as well as
transporters
. It stored information on removable
data chips
. (
SNW
: "
Lift Us Where Suffering Cannot Reach
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Malcorian warp ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Malcorian_warp_ship
======================================================================

Malcorian warp ship
Affiliation:
Malcorians
Type:
Warp
prototype
vessel
The
Malcorian warp ship
was the
prototype
of the
Malcorian
Space Bureau
's first
Malcorian
warp driven
starship
that was scheduled for launch in the late
2360s
. Its first planned destination according to
Malcor III
's Space
Administrator
Mirasta Yale
was to be the nearby
Garth system
.
However, an unfortunate
first contact
between the Malcorian
government
and the
crew
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in
2367
led to increased levels of
xenophobia
on behalf of the conservative minded
Chancellor
Avel Durken
. The Malcorian
space
program was consequently delayed while the
government
focused on more domestic issues. (
TNG
: "
First Contact
")
Background information
The model reused in "Tapestry"
The
display model
of the Malcorian warp ship, seen in Space Administrator Mirasta Yale's office, was one of the few
starship
designs in the
Star Trek
franchise that was conceived as such, not having been featured as an in-universe fully realized "real" starship. Scenic Art Supervisor
Mike Okuda
recalled on the design, "
My recollection is that I designed the Malcorian ship desktop model, but it is possible that it may have been Rick, who designed most of the ships and props...I think
Ed Miarecki
built it for us. Don't know what happened to it...I know I did the animated computer readouts in the lab. A big silkscreened diagram of the ship also appears on a clear acrylic display in the lab. Yes, it was specifically done for this episode, although the model did later appear as set dressing in another episode.
"
The front section of the model
Confirming that he was indeed at least the co-designer, Senior Illustrator
Rick Sternbach
added, "
I think I did a few doodles or 3D computer model roughs of this one, based amazingly vaguely on a mashup of a
Bussard ramjet
and an
Enzmann giant deuterium fuel tank starship
. I do believe Mike added the
warp nacelles
and offset them from the center framework, which may have hinted at the possibility that warp physics was similarly discovered and refined by separate cultures.
"
[1]
The other episode, Mike Okuda referred to was
Star Trek: The Next Generation
's
sixth season
episode, "
Tapestry
", where the model was in a similar function reused as set dressing in the
quarters
of recently graduated
Ensign
Jean-Luc Picard
in the by
Q
recreated reality.

======================================================================
PAGE: Malfunction
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Malfunction
======================================================================

A
malfunction
or
breakdown
was the unintended partial or total loss of or alteration of functionality in a system.
In
2366
, after a series of malfunctions on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
,
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
believed that they were dealing with a potential breakdown of the
main computer
, but according to
Lieutenant Commander
Data
, the system automatically provided for self-correction and there had not been a systems-wide
technological
failure on a
starship
in
seventy-nine years
. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")
Types of malfunctions
Control malfunction
A control malfunction was the malfunction of a
ship
's control, such as an unresponsive
helm
,
deflector shields
, or
inertia dampeners
.
In 2366, the
Enterprise
's launch of "
The Egg
"
probe
was interrupted by an instability of the ship. The
Enterprise
was unresponsive, preventing
Ensign
Wesley Crusher
from stabilizing it, and the ship's inertia dampeners became unresponsive and unable to be reset by
Geordi La Forge
. However, La Forge was able to reverse
impulse engines
, but the ship's
momentum
carried them into the path of
stellar matter
. After impact, Data reported that all systems were functioning normally and the
ship's computer
noted that no control malfunction had been recorded. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")
Environmental system malfunction
An environmental system malfunction was the malfunction of
life support systems
.
In 2366,
nanites
caused an environmental system malfunction on the
bridge
of the
Enterprise-D
which released
toxic
levels of
nitrogen oxide
. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")
Transporter malfunction
A
transporter malfunction
or transporter accident was a malfunction in a
transporter
system that could have a wide variety of effects from transporting to an unintended destination, alterations to the transported subject, and even
death
.
External link
Malfunction
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Malon export vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Malon_export_vessel
======================================================================

Malon export vessel
Affiliation:
Malon
waste export industry
Type:
Freighter
,
Garbage scow
Active:
24th century
Decks:
42
Crew complement:
~100
Cargo capacity:
4 trillion
isotons
For the smaller export vessel variant, please see
Malon export vessel, eleventh gradient
.
The
Malon export vessel
was a large
freighter
operated by the
waste export industry
of the
Malon
they were nothing more than a
garbage scow
. The waste export vessel was designed for long range haulage and dumping of
theta radioactive
antimatter waste and other mixed industrial byproducts produced by the Malon civilization.
Waste export vessels were commanded by a
waste controller
; crewmembers who work near the hazardous waste regions of the ship are known as
core laborers
. The ships had a
sickbay
with equipment and medicines to treat radiation exposure, although due to the nature of the work, almost all crew members on an export mission suffer some permanent
physiological
damage. They were also equipped with
escape pods
for use in emergencies and could also eject damaged storage tanks into space if they threatened the ship.
These Malon freighters had 42 decks and were equipped with twelve theta storage tanks, and were manned by a crew of around 100, where transported throughout the ship aboard
ascenders
. These vessels were capable to transporting over four trillion
isotons
of anti-matter waste. (
VOY
: "
Juggernaut
")
Control room
(left side of entryway)
Control room
(right side of entryway)
Control room
(opposite of entryway)
Infirmary
Corridor
Appendices
Background information
The sets for the Malon freighter featured in "Juggernaut" were a reuse of existing Borg sets with much of the display and control panels removed.
Studio model
Sternbach's design
The
CGI
studio model was designed by
Rick Sternbach
, who proceeded from his earlier design of the smaller
Malon export vessel, eleventh gradient
,
"There was a nine-tank
[sic.]
mega-tanker that appeared in a later episode, along with an escape pod from the same ship. Similar design with the backbone covering the tank clusters.
[1]
(X)
(...) The intent with the mega-tanker was that the tanks were the same size as the ones in the original tanker, making the mega-tanker quite mega. Regardless of the -appearance- of the superstructure. It certainly would be longer because of the fourth cluster of tanks (fore-aft), as well as much wider because of the now double row as seen from the top. Also taller, because of the bottom group of four tanks.
"
[2]
(X)
External link
Malon export vessel
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Malon export vessel, eleventh gradient
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Malon_export_vessel,_eleventh_gradient
======================================================================

Malon export vessel, eleventh gradient
Affiliation:
Malon
waste export industry
Type:
Freighter
,
Garbage scow
Active:
24th century
Crew complement:
9
Cargo capacity:
90 million
isotons
Armament:
Spatial charges
Defenses:
Deflector shields
You may also be looking for the larger
Malon export vessel
.
A
Malon export vessel, eleventh gradient
was a
freighter
-type vessel operated by the
waste export industry
of the
Malon
like most ships owned by the Malon it is classed as a
garbage scow
. They operated with a crew of nine.
The waste export vessel was designed for long range transport and dumping of
theta radioactive
antimatter
waste and other mixed industrial byproducts produced by the Malon civilization.
This gradient of vessel possessed three
theta storage tanks
, capable of carrying over ninety million
isotons
of contaminated waste. They were armed with
spatial charge
launchers and equipped with a
tractor beam
. (
VOY
: "
Night
", "
Extreme Risk
")
Appendices
Appearances
VOY
:
"
Night
"
"
Extreme Risk
"
Studio model
For this type of "Malon freighter," the revised final draft of the script described the following, "
A large, bulky, cargo vessel designed to carry toxic waste.
" It also described that the freighter had "
Several ports along the hull vent explosive bursts of toxic antimatter -- gas, debris, flotsam. The waste product of an advanced, spacefaring species.
"
Known then as the "toxic waste tanker" the design, finalized in
June 1998
, was a brainchild of
Rick Sternbach
who said, "
I don't have a whole lot to say about the tanker, except that I wanted it to look massive, which the large spherical tanks seem to accomplish. The spew nozzles are there, along with lots of supporting hardware like the carapace/spine, brackets, tubes, and so on. I didn't develop a real style for the shapes and surfaces until the later mega-tanker, which relied heavily, so to speak, on slightly streamlined and repeating parts with a good old retro feel. Think steam and diesel locomotives.
"
[1]
(X)
Questioned about the
Chris Foss
influences, Sternbach replied, "
Except that mine aren't covered with yellow and black stripes. Also see Foss' Leviathan design for ALIEN. A similar approach on my part; completely unintentional.
"
[2]
(X)
The model was eventually built as a
CGI model
at
Foundation Imaging
by
Brandon MacDougall
, who commented about the model, "
Toxic tanker was a lot of fun to build and texture and as always getting plans from Rick and the art department made the building a snap. Once built the ship was off to "
Q
" for the final steamer effects of green gas 'not shown in the rendering' back then it was over 1 hr for the gas to render one small frame.
"
[3]
(X)
Being one of the more fleetingly-seen ships, McDougall out of courtesy re-rendered his model for
Doug Drexler
's blog.
[4]
(X)

Malurian vessel
Affiliation:
Malurians
Type:
Warship

22nd century

Weapon platforms

Energy shields
A
Malurian vessel
or
Malurian ship
was a type of armed
starship
utilized by the
Malurians
during the mid-
22nd century
. These large vessels were equipped with multiple
weapon platforms
which could fire ten powerful charges simultaneously. They were also able to generate defensive
energy shields
.
In
2151
, one of these vessels came to the
Akaali homeworld
to pick up
veridium
ore
from the Malurian
mining
operation on the
planet
and subsequently engaged
Enterprise
NX-01
as its crew who were trying to
sabotage
their operation. After being defeated by the
Earth
ship, the Malurian vessel picked up the three-man mining crew and left the
system
. (
ENT
: "
Civilization
")
John Eaves
designed this vessel. Two sketches by him, titled "Alien Attack Vessel", were later sold off on the
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
on eBay.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Manifold
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Manifold
======================================================================

In
engineering
, a
manifold
was a design element used to distribute and manipulate (ie. split or combine) the flow of
fluid
(ie.
liquid
,
gas
,
plasma
, or
energy
) so as to introduce or remove it from a connected system. Various forms of manifolds were essential in the operation of
propulsion systems
.
Sam Rutherford
etched a note on the side of manifold on the
USS
Cerritos
model
he was constructing to get the
deflector shields
on it to work. The note read "
field distortion amplifier
88," which, while initially meant nothing to Rutherford, it was later discovered that it written there to slow his completion of the model. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
Types
Atmospheric recycling manifold
Engine manifold
Impulse manifold
Warp manifold
Exhaust manifold
Fuel manifold
Deuterium manifold
Fusion manifold
Gravimetric field displacement manifold
Injector manifold
Interspatial manifold
Manifold thrust
ODN manifold
Ontarian manifold
Plasma distribution manifold
Intake manifold
Plasma intake manifold
Tertiary subspace manifold
Tricyclic input manifold
External link
Manifold
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Manual control
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Manual_control
======================================================================

Manual control
was the operation of
technology
directly (i.e. with one's own
hands
) rather than with automatic settings and
programs
.
In
2366
, an
environmental system malfunction
on the
bridge
of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
released
toxic
levels of
nitrogen oxide
, which caused the
bridge crew
to
cough
.
Commander
William T. Riker
switched to manual control of the
air handling system
to return the
air
to normal. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")

Mark III bridge simulator
was a
bridge simulator
at
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
in the
23rd century
.
In
2285
, this simulator, located on the second level of a building, was in room #215. This simulator was listed with other simulators on a directory placard. (

, set artwork)

It's A Wrap! sale and auction

======================================================================
PAGE: Mark IV bridge simulator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mark_IV_bridge_simulator
======================================================================

Pyrotechnics in use in the simulator
The
Mark IV bridge simulator
was a
bridge simulator
at
Starfleet Academy
's
Starfleet Training Command
in the
23rd century
. This simulator, located on the second level of a building, was in room #216. The simulator was listed with other simulators on a directory placard.
In
2285
, this simulator was a mock-up of a bridge for a
Enterprise
-class
starship
for use by
Starfleet cadets
in
Starfleet Academy
training missions, such as the
Kobayashi Maru
scenario
.
Captain
Spock
had a class of cadets, under the command of
Saavik
, make use of the simulator (playing the role of the
USS
Enterprise
), joined by Starfleet officers
Leonard McCoy
,
Hikaru Sulu
,
Uhura
, and
Montgomery Scott
.
Admiral
James T. Kirk
also visited the simulator to watch the cadets' performance on the Kobayashi Maru scenario. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
)
"No smoking" sign visible
The simulator set was obviously a reuse of the
Enterprise
bridge in
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan.
The only noticeable difference is the lack of the "No Smoking" sign on the "real"
Enterprise
bridge as seen throughout the rest of the movie.
The simulator (or one very much like it) is used in the
Star Trek: Starfleet Academy
computer game, however, the ship designation and configuration changes from mission to mission.
The placard  was sold on
It's A Wrap! sale and auction
for US$31.99.
[1]
(X)

======================================================================
PAGE: Master subsurface pump
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Master_subsurface_pump
======================================================================

The
master subsurface pump
was the main
water
pump used in the
terraforming
of
Velara III
.
Geordi La Forge
called the pump "very impressive" while he and
Data
were examining its
LCARS
control panel. The two used the diagram to ascertain that the
water table
on the
planet
was what Data called a "thin ribbon between the sandy
surface
layer and the rocks
below
". Indeed, the LCARS further demonstrated that the water table had such a precise and regular depth that the
hydraulic probes
would need to be controlled with "extreme precision". (
TNG
: "
Home Soil
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Matter synthesizer
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Matter_synthesizer
======================================================================

A
matter synthesizer
was a
technology
in use in the
Federation
during the
23rd century
, which worked through
matter
synthesis.
Much like the later
replicators
, they were used to create certain day-to-day necessities for life aboard
starships
, specifically
food
and
uniforms
. Food specifically was dispensed from
food synthesizers
. (
DIS
: "
Context Is for Kings
", "
The Butcher's Knife Cares Not for the Lamb's Cry
")
When
Sylvia Tilly
was nine, she worked out how to make her matter synthesizer produce only
spumoni ice cream
by calculating the exact
protein
concentration and small particle dispersion of
emulsifiers
required. (
ST
: "
Runaway
")
Although
Star Trek: Discovery
depicts the technology very similar to how replicators were depicted, the technology was presumably not the same, since a line from
VOY
: "
Flashback
" established that replicators did not yet exist.
See also
Molecular synthesizer
Protein resequencer
External link
Matter synthesizer
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Mazarite ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mazarite_ship
======================================================================

Mazarite ship
Affiliation:
Mazarite High Council
Type:
Warship
Active:
22nd century
Speed:
Warp 5+
Armament:
Directed energy weapons
Defenses:
Deflector shields
Mazarite ships
were a type of
starship
utilized by the
Mazarite High Council
's
government
, which was highly corrupt, during the mid-
22nd century
. Because the
Mazarites
of the
2150s
were
technologically
far less advanced than the
Vulcans
of the same era, several Mazarite ships could easily be outmatched and disabled by a single
Vulcan combat cruiser
.
Mazarite ships had a
maximum speed
of at least
warp 5
. They had
deflector shield
technology, and the ability to
jam
other ships'
communications
technology.
In early
2152
, three of these ships chased
Enterprise
while that vessel was escorting
Ambassador
V'Lar
to
Vulcan
to offer testimony against the Mazarites' corrupt government. The Mazarite ships were eventually able to catch
Enterprise
, which docked with one of the Mazarite ships, as the Mazarites were insistent on taking V'Lar back to
Mazar
.
The
sensors
on the Mazarite vessels, however, were jammed by
Enterprise
long enough for the
Sh'Raan
to arrive and disable the Mazarite ships. After the Vulcan
captain
threatened to destroy the Mazarite ships if the Mazarites didn't surrender their weapons, the Mazarites complied, and the
Sh'Raan
was able to return V'Lar to Vulcan. (
ENT
: "
Fallen Hero
")
The type of weaponry equipped aboard Mazarite ships is not identified in the final version of "Fallen Hero". However, the episode's script referred to the weapons fire from one of the craft as "energy bursts."
For more information on this design and the history of this model, please see:
Studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Medusan vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Medusan_vessel
======================================================================

Medusan vessel
Affiliation:
Medusans
Active:
23rd century
–
24th century
Speed:
Warp-capable
The
Medusan vessel
was a type of
starship
in operation during the
23rd
and
24th centuries
. Sharing some commonalities with
Federation
starship design, the vessel functioned under the authority of the
Medusans
. It was capable of
landing
on a
planetary
surface. (
PRO
: "
Preludes
")
In
2268
, the
USS
Enterprise
conveyed the Medusans'
ambassador
to the
Federation
,
Kollos
, along with
Miranda Jones
, to a
rendezvous
with a Medusan vessel following an unspecified diplomatic mission, and a short diversion through the
galactic barrier
. Jones had originally intended to
mind link
with Kollos once the two had reached the Medusan vessel, which was bound for the
Medusan homeworld
; however, circumstances forced the link to be established aboard the
Enterprise
. (
TOS
: "
Is There in Truth No Beauty?
")
Ships of the class
Unnamed Medusan vessels
Appendices
Appearances
TOS-R
: "
Is There in Truth No Beauty?
"
PRO
: "
Preludes
"
Background information
The Medusan vessel went unseen in the original airing of the episode, only being referenced in dialogue. Indeed, the original ending sequence had the
Enterprise
approaching the
Medusan homeworld
. This was altered in the
2008
remastered
version of the episode, where a
CGI model
was created for the vessel. Based on information obtained from
Michael Okuda
, no
name
or
registry number
was specifically assigned to the model.
In terms of its design, Okuda added that "
the basic idea was
Dave Rossi
's suggestion. He thought that because the Medusans were
non-corporeal
and might not have developed
technology
as we know it, they might use Federation hardware, so their ships could have a distinct Federation style.
"
Issue #92
According to Issue #92 of the
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
, the Medusan vessel was a type of
transport
and
diplomatic vessel
used by the Medusans. This class was approximately 90
meters
in length, had a
maximum speed
of
warp factor
6, and a
crew
complement of 30. It was equipped with
phaser emitters
.
External link
Medusan starship
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Melthusian starship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Melthusian_starship
======================================================================

Bela Tyken
was a
Melthusian
captain
who first encountered the rare
space
anomaly
which came to be known as a
Tyken's Rift
sometime before
2367
.
When Tyken and
his crew
were trapped in the rift, his analysis determined that a massive
energy
release might overload and dislocate the anomaly. Utilizing his
cargo
of
anicium
and
yurium
, he was able to detonate the explosion and escape through the ruptured center of the rift.
Unlike a similar case experienced aboard the
starships
USS
Brattain
and
USS
Enterprise
in
2367
, there were no reports of
behavioral
changes in his crew. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")
This character was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Menos' ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Menos'_ship
======================================================================

Meno's ship
Menos' ship
was a small
transport
which the rogue
Vulcan
,
Menos
, used to
smuggle
synthetic biotoxins
. The ship was equiped with a
holographic projector
that created a false wall in the
cargo hold
. Menos stored the smuggled biotoxins in the hidden part of the ship, while the rest was full of spent
warp injector casings
, or some other legitimate cargo. (
ENT
: "
The Seventh
")
In the final draft script of "The Seventh", the cockpit of Menos' ship was described as "a cramped, one-man cockpit." Evidently, the set of the cockpit was a re-used set also used for the cockpits of
inspection pods
and the
Phoenix
. For more information on this
studio model
, see
ENT studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Merchantman
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Merchantman
======================================================================

Merchantman
Class:
Merchantman
-type
Status:
Destroyed
(
2285
)
The
Merchantman
was a small
civilian
Merchantman
-type
freighter
in service during the late
23rd century
.
In
2285
,
Klingon
operative
Valkris
hired the
Merchantman
to carry classified
Federation
data regarding the
Genesis Device
to a
Klingon Bird-of-Prey
commanded
by
Kruge
. After Kruge learned that Valkris had viewed the data, he ordered the
Merchantman
destroyed with all aboard. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
Crew complement
Merchantman
personnel
Appendices
Background information
This craft was not named on screen. The
name
"
Merchantman
" was taken from the
script
and the call sheets for
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
.
[1]
The stage directions in the revised final draft of the film's script suggested that the ship was in
Organian
space
when it was attacked and destroyed. Also in the script, the craft's exterior was described as "a dark-looking merchantman" whereas its interior was characterized as "small and grubby."
[2]
The interior of the
Merchantman
was represented with a set on
Paramount Stage 5
. (
Information from call sheets
)
For more about this model, see
studio models
.
Issue #143
According to Issue #143 of the
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
, the
Merchantman
was  approximately 150
meters
in length, had a
maximum speed
of
warp factor
5, and had a
crew
complement of 3. This ship was equipped with
phasers
.
Apocrypha
The
Starships
RPG sourcebook describes this type of ship as a Class III tanker.
In the RPG module
The Serpent Factor
, published in
Stardate
Vol 1 #1, the
Merchantman
was instead called the SS
Eileen
.
External links
Merchantman
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Monarch
class
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Merchantman
blueprints

======================================================================
PAGE: Merchantman type
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Merchantman_type
======================================================================

Merchantman
type
Owner:
Coalition of Madena speciess
Klingons
Pelians
Sheliak
Vidiian
Private ownership
Type:
Colony ship
Freighter
Scout
Active:
Late-
23rd century
–Late-
24th century
Crew complement:
2 – 78
Speed:
Warp power
; some variants are powered only by
impulse power
Armament:
Lasers
Phasers
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Merchantman
-type
of
spacecraft
was a highly modifiable, upgradeable, and versatile vessel used in the
Alpha Quadrant
,
Beta Quadrant
, and
Delta Quadrant
by
governments
,
military
, and
civilians
. All ships of this type had the same basic configuration; however, there were variations in the size of the ships, with some being considerably larger than others. These ships were either
warp powered
or
impulse powered
and were equipped with
lasers
or
phasers
. The crew compliment on aboard these ships could be as low as two and as high as 78.
The most common variants of this spacecraft type were used as
colony ships
and
transports
. These ships could ferry hundreds of
colonists
or
passengers
.
Another popular use for this type was as
freighters
, with the largest having a cargo capacity of 14
metric tons
.
Less common variants were used as
scouts
with a minimal crew.
They were first used in the
23rd century
and saw continued use into the late
24th century
. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
;
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
", "
The Ensigns of Command
", "
The Host
";
DS9
: "
The Maquis, Part I
", "
Rules of Engagement
";
VOY
: "
Phage
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Metagenic weapon
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Metagenic_weapon
======================================================================

Metagenic devices
were powerful
biological
weapons
outlawed
by most major
governments
in the
24th century
. A metagenic device was a
genetically-engineered
, airborne
virus
that destroyed all
DNA
it encountered, and was capable of destroying entire
planetary
ecosystems
within
days
.
A force armed with such a device could introduce it into a target planet's
atmosphere
to rapidly eliminate all
life
– and resistance – while leaving all infrastructure intact, and within a
month
, the metagenic virus itself would become inert, essentially leaving the planet conquered.
In
2369
,
Starfleet Intelligence
suspected the
Cardassians
of developing a method to launch dormant metagenic material on a subspace
theta-band
carrier wave
in a secret
base
under the surface of
Celtris III
. This was actually a ruse to lure
Captain
Picard
out and capture him, as he was
Starfleet
's only active member with knowledge of
theta-band devices
. (
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part I
", "
Chain Of Command, Part II
")
See also
Biogenic agents
Biogenic weapon

======================================================================
PAGE: Microfilament
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Microfilament
======================================================================

Microfilament from a circa-
2371
combadge
A
microfilament
was a component of
Federation
technology shaped like a small, extremely thin wire.
In
2374
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
and
Seven of Nine
were imprisoned aboard the
USS
Dauntless
, an
alien
ship designed to look like a
Starfleet
vessel. In the
brig
, the captain removed a microfilament from her
combadge
. She then used the component to adjust Seven's
cranial implant
, which activated several of the former
Borg
's
nanoprobes
. Seven's
bio-electric field
was altered as a result, allowing her to easily walk through the
force field
, deactivate it, and escape with Janeway. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Microfusion initiator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Microfusion_initiator
======================================================================

Microfusion initiators
were technological devices utilized by
Starfleet
in the
24th century
.
In
2372
, a group of rogue
Jem'Hadar
conducted a raid on
Deep Space 9
, stealing microfusion initiators,
photonic amplifiers
, and
EPS power stabilizers
. They intended to use the devices to restore an
Iconian gateway
which had been discovered by the
Dominion
in the
Gamma Quadrant
. (
DS9
: "
To the Death
")
In
2373
,
Miles O'Brien
was unable to find any microfusion initiators aboard a
Jem'Hadar fighter
that had
crashed
on
Torga IV
. (
DS9
: "
The Ship
")
See also
Nuclear fusion
External link
Microfusion initiator
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Microvision
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Microvision
======================================================================

Device containing microvision technology
You may also be looking for the
video game
Star Trek: Phaser Strike
produced for use with the Microvision Handheld Game System.
Microvision
was an exotic and advanced
technological component
created by
Andromedan
androids
of the
planet
Mudd
.
Norman
attempted to entice
Montgomery Scott
by showing him one of their workshops that contained a device that was hand-worked to the finest tolerances, and included such components as microvision and a
nanopulse laser
. Scott had never seen anything like in before, and described it as "absolutely fantastic." (
TOS
: "
I, Mudd
")

microwave pulse detonator
was a technological device that could be used to activate both
Molnar composite (thermal)
and
Neussite 283
. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")
This detonator was mentioned in the descriptions for these explosive compounds.

======================================================================
PAGE: Microwave pulse ignition device
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Microwave_pulse_ignition_device
======================================================================

A
microwave pulse ignition device
was a type of technological device used with
ultritium 283
and
ultritium 342
for
mining
,
terraforming
, and large scale excavations. For the mining applications, it emitted precision charge patterns. At higher power settings, it was used for activating
Pastorium liquid 342
for
plasma
flow applications and ionizing pulse generation. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Mirror machine
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mirror_machine
======================================================================

The mirror machine
The
mirror machine
was the means by which
Trelane
, the Squire of Gothos, performed most of his tricks. Trelane referred to this device as one of his "medium(s) of instrumentality".
James T. Kirk
gave the device this name when attempting to take sole responsibility for Trelane's charges against his
crew
, among them the device's destruction.
In
2267
, the
USS
Enterprise
discovered the
planet
Gothos
within a
star desert
– a region of
space
completely void of
stars
. On Gothos, they encountered Trelane, an
alien
who appeared
Human
, but who was capable of a great many superhuman feats. These included rearranging
matter
as a
replicator
does (although far faster), generating powerful
energy fields
that inhibited
sensors
and communication, instantaneous matter transmission, holding living beings in
suspension
, teaching living beings new skills instantaneously, and perhaps more. Trelane used these powers to control, entertain and torment various
crew
members.
On discovering that Trelane's
food
had no flavor, and his fire emitted no heat,
Captain
Kirk began to suspect that Trelane only knew the forms of things and not their substances. He and
Spock
further speculated that some external agency was realizing Trelane's wishes – and when he noticed Trelane never strayed far from his large wall mirror, Kirk suspected where that agency might be hidden.
The ruined mirror machine
Challenging the hapless Squire of Gothos to a
duel
, Kirk instead shot the mirror – which exploded in a shower of sparks. Most of Trelane's effects ceased operating, and as the smoke cleared, Kirk's suspicions were confirmed – the mirror concealed an intricate device that performed most of Trelane's tricks for him. With it gone, Kirk and his crew were able to escape Gothos – for a while. The mirror machine wasn't the only device at Trelane's command, and when he recaptured the
Enterprise
a short while later, his bag of tricks was once again full. Additionally, Trelane's
oasis
remained intact, suggesting the existence of another machine too large to fit inside Trelane's house.
Trelane evidently commanded the mirror machine via
telepathy
, or some other non-verbal form of communication. He never spoke his requests aloud, instead performing his extraordinary feats with at most a wave of his hand. Sophisticated as it was, Trelane was able to repair or replace it within minutes of its destruction. (
TOS
: "
The Squire of Gothos
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Mislenite freighter
CATEGORIES: Starship classes

A disabled Mislenite freighter
The
Mislenite freighter
was a type of
cargo ship
used by the
Mislenites
in the late-
24th century
.
In
2373
, a ship of this type was disabled by
Swarm vessels
and later discovered by the
USS
Voyager
. The only survivor,
Chardis
, was
transported
to
Voyager
but later
died
of his injuries. (
VOY
: "
The Swarm
")

The
Mithran
-class
was a
fighter
under development in
2374
, said to be more advanced than any other
warship
in the
quadrant
.
A lifeform
impersonating
Steth
claimed he was about to do the first test flight of this class when he got back from flying the
coaxial warp prototype
. He invited Paris to come with him and test-pilot the ship. (
VOY
: "
Vis à Vis
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Modern medicine
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Modern_medicine
======================================================================

Chekov benefiting from modern medicine (late 23rd century as opposed to late 20th)
Modern medicine
was a
term
used to
describe
the current standard of
medical
care, as opposed to
primitive
,
risky
, or radical procedures. Comparatively
primitive medicine
was sometimes referred to as having originated in the
Dark Ages
. (
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
In
2153
, when
N. Kemper
commented that those on
Enterprise
NX-01
who had not gotten their
space legs
yet still had to visit
Dr.
Phlox
,
Romero
replied, "
The wonders of modern medicine.
" (
ENT
: "
The Xindi
")
In
2371
,
The Doctor
was frustrated over his being asked to repair realtively insignificant problems, such as
runny noses
, stubbed
toes
and
pimples
, bragging, "
I am not just a doctor. I've been designed with the information from two thousand medical reference sources and the experience of forty seven individual
medical officers
. I am the embodiment of modern medicine.
" (
VOY
: "
Parallax
")
External link
Modern medicine
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Molecular phase inverter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Molecular_phase_inverter
======================================================================

Geordi examines a Romulan interphase generator, a combination of a molecular phase inverter and a cloaking device.
A
molecular phase inverter
was a device capable of altering the normal structure of
matter
, enabling it to pass through normal matter and
energy
. Such "
phased
" matter could no longer physically interact with its environment, but could interact with other, similarly-phased matter.
At one point, the
Klingon Empire
experimented with combining a molecular phase inverter with a
cloaking device
. Vessels equipped with such an
interphase generator
would theoretically be able to hide anywhere, even inside a
planet
, and would be immune to conventional weapons. The
Klingons
abandoned this research, however, due to several accidents which occurred as a result.
In
2368
, the
Romulans
attempted the same experiment, equipping one of their vessels with an
interphase
generator. This experiment ended in disaster, however, resulting in a
forced chamber explosion
in their
resonator coil
, severely damaging the ship. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
answered the Romulans'
distress call
, and assisted them in making repairs. Upon
beaming
back to the
Enterprise
, however,
Lieutenant Commander
Geordi La Forge
and
Ensign
Ro Laren
became contaminated with
chronitons
leaking from the Romulans' cloaking device, and became phased themselves, unable to interact or communicate with anyone but each other and a phased
member
of the Romulan crew. La Forge and Ro were eventually de-phased when they were subjected to a high-intensity
anyon
emission. (
TNG
: "
The Next Phase
")
See also
Phasing cloaking device
Phase inverter
External links
Molecular phase inverter
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Phase shifting
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Alternate timeline
(covers information from an
alternate timeline
)
A
molecular scanner
was a scanning device in use in an
alternate timeline
.
Linnis Paris
once admitted to
Kes
that she felt awkward around her and
Harry Kim
's
baby
,
Andrew Kim
, commenting that she felt more comfortable with a molecular scanner than a
diaper
. (
VOY
: "
Before and After
")
See also
Isomolecular scanner

PAGE: Monaveen

Monaveen
Class:
Monaveen
-type
cruiser
Owner:
Queen
Paolana
Affiliation:
Hysperians
Status:

(
2381
)
The
Monaveen
was a
Hysperian
Monaveen
-type
cruiser
used by
Queen
Paolana
.
In
2381
, when her crew was unable to repair the ship's faulty "
dragonbreath engines
", the queen contacted the
USS
Cerritos
to request assistance of his
chief engineer
, and the queen's
son
,
Andarithio Billups
.
When tasked with repairing the ship's engine, Billups enlisted the help of
Ensign
Sam Rutherford
. Rutherford was, however, intimidated with the idea, stating that the
Monaveen
, unlike the
Cerritos
, was "
a fancy cruiser stuffed with puffy
monarchs
.
" (
LD
: "
Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
")
Later the same year, members of the
Monaveen
's bridge crew later watched
Nick Locarno
's quadrant-wide subspace message on a
PADD
. (
LD
: "
Old Friends, New Planets
")

See
:
