personnel

======================================================================
PAGE: Monaveen type
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Monaveen_type
======================================================================

Monaveen
-type
Affiliation:
Hysperians
Type:
Cruiser
Active:
2380s
Decks:
7
The
Monaveen
-type
was a class of
cruiser
utilized by the
Hysperians
during the late
24th century
.
Technical specifications
While this type of ship was
warp driven
, and possessed such standard advanced technologies as
transporters
and
force fields
, it also utilized a number of antiquated technologies, such as
sconces
with open
flames
, and large
wooden
doors
that were opened manually. The
Monaveen
also had
stables
, located in the ship's ventral line, in which
horses
were kept. There was also an area for keeping a
dragon
, which was located near the ship's center.
Docking between ships was completed through the use of a torch-lit
docking corridor
. (
LD
: "
Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
")
Interior design
Bridge
The ship's bridge
The
bridge
was located at the center of the ship. It consisted of a two-level design, with two curved
staircases
that led to the
command center
. (
LD
: "
Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
")
Main engineering
The ship's main engineering
Main engineering
was centrally located on the starboard side of the ship, directly off the upper level of the bridge; it was maintained by the
chief blacksmith
, who also oversaw the ship's
royal engineers
.
It housed the ship's "
dragonbreath engines
" and other various components, such as the "
dragonsblood flame
", known as the
primary fusion reactor
to those in the
Federation
, which worked in conjunction with the ship's "
elf matrix
", otherwise known as the
subspace field matrix
. The reactor also contained the ship's
plasma regulators
and
resonators
, which were responsible for regulating the
plasma
levels. The drive itself was designed to resemble the
head
of a dragon. (
LD
: "
Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
")
Section 8
The location of Section 8 on an
MSD
Section 8 of the ship contained a
corridor
lined with suits of
knights
'
armor
(or
statues
resembling knights), and a room with a
banquet table
, comparable to a
mess hall
. (
LD
: "
Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Mondor
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Mondor
======================================================================

Mondor
Class:
Mondor
-type
Affiliation:
Pakled
Status:
Active
(
2365
)
"
Their ship is apparently quite fragile.
"
–
Data
,
2365
("
Samaritan Snare
")
The
Mondor
was a
sub-light
Pakled
ship
that was in service during the mid-
24th century
, under the
command
of
Captain
Grebnedlog
; the
ship
's
chief engineer
was his
friend
Reginod
.
Over time, the
Mondor
had been heavily modified with various systems
stolen
from other, more advanced civilizations, including
Romulan
shields
, and
Jarada
and
Klingon
systems. It also had a
replicator
, and was equipped with
photon torpedoes
.
In
2365
, the ship was located in
Rhomboid Dronegar Sector 006
where broadcast a
Mayday
signal, which was responded to by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
As a ruse, the ship appeared to have a failure in its
guidance system
, which
Enterprise
chief engineer,
Geordi La Forge
, was assigned to repair, but unbeknownst to him, he was only responding to carefully programmed and misleading signals. As such, upon the completion of the initial repairs, the ship's
power generator
then failed. While
Enterprise
Commander
William T. Riker
offered to
tow
their ship with a
tractor beam
to their nearest
starbase
, La Forge assured Riker his repairs were moments from being completed. As La Forge prepared to
beam
back to the
Enterprise
, the Pakled's engaged their shields, and effectively
kidnapped
and held La Forge
hostage
.
The Pakleds demanded he use their replicator to duplicate his
type 2 phaser
, before demanding that he help make them strong, by helping them utilize the weapons technology they already held in their possession.
In their own ruse, the crew of the
Enterprise
attempted and succeeded at retrieving La Forge before parting ways with the
Mondor
. (
TNG
: "
Samaritan Snare
")
Personnel
Mondor
personnel

, to depths up to a hundred
kilometers
. They posed no threat to an
Intrepid
-class

orbit
of "

======================================================================
PAGE: Multiaxis exercise trainer
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Multiaxis_exercise_trainer
======================================================================

The
multiaxis exercise trainer
was an
exercise
machine in the
Enterprise
gymnasium
.
In
2152
, this machine was used by
Charles Tucker III
. (
ENT
: "
Vanishing Point
")
This machine was named by the
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 4th ed., vol. 2, p. 58, which defined it as a, "
free-rotating exercise device consisting of three concentric gimbals, each rotating on a different axis, with a harness at the center. The user shifts his or her weight to cause the device to rotate, providing a full-body workout.
"
It is noted by this
reference work
, "
The device, which was a modified version of a commercially-produced exercise machine [...] is based on
NASA
's multiaxis space test inertia facility, used to train early
astronauts
. During the third season, the exercise device was modified to become part of the deadly
Xindi
weapon
that nearly destroyed
Earth
.
"
See also
Gyrosphere

======================================================================
PAGE: Multiphasic chamber
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Multiphasic_chamber
======================================================================

A nucleogenic lifeform confined within a multiphasic chamber
A
multiphasic chamber
was a device constructed by the crew of the
USS
Equinox
in
2376
for the purpose of confining
nucleogenic lifeforms
and preventing them from escaping to their native realm. Designed as a
stasis chamber
, the device operated by surrounding a lifeform with a
multiphasic force field
, which inhibited its ability to escape the normal
spacetime continuum
via
interspatial fissure
.
When the
Equinox
crew first used the device to study one of the lifeforms, they discovered that it could only survive outside of its realm for a brief period of time, and inadvertently caused its death. Upon examining the remains, they discovered that they could be converted into a source of power with which the crew could augment their
warp drive
, significantly shortening their journey back to the
Alpha Quadrant
. They subsequently modified the chamber with a
polaron grid
and a
submolecular resequencer
, for the purpose of converting the alien
cell
structures into a
crystaline
compound.
After encountering the crew of the
USS
Voyager
, the
Equinox
crew attempted to conceal their criminal experiments by flooding their
research lab
with
thermionic radiation
. When
Captain
Janeway
became suspicious, however, she sent
The Doctor
to investigate. Examining the equipment in the lab, The Doctor uncovered the
Equinox
crew's activities, and Janeway subsequently confronted Captain
Ransom
. (
VOY
: "
Equinox
")

or culture".

works

Munition
was a broad
term
that sometimes referred to
ammunition
, but also any
weapon
which used ammunition, and any piece of
military
equipment
or
device
in general.
Ultritium compound 902
was a
weapons-grade
chemical
explosive
compound used in small munitions devices and
type 43 fusion detonators
. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")
In
2373
during the
Q Civil War
,
Q
was
injured
by
Q weapons
which were
perceived
as
cannonballs
and
lead
charges
.
Kathryn Janeway
didn't
think
that a
Q
could be injured, but Q told her that she would be
surprised
what
innovative
munitions could be created by one
immortal
being who set their
mind
on
killing
another. (
VOY
: "
The Q and the Grey
")

A
mutagenic retrovirus
was a type of
biogenic weapon
, comprised of
genetically-engineered
viruses
. When used on a population, mutagenic retroviruses could cause millions of casualties in a short period of time.
In
2373
, the
Regent of Palamar
purchased a quantity of mutagenic retrovirus from
Hagath
and
Gaila
's
arms
business for use against
General
Nassuc
's
homeworld
. However,
Quark
, unwilling to have the deaths of 28 million people on his conscience, told them that their supplier had sold all his stock to the
Minnobia
for their
war
against the
Vek
. (
DS9
: "
Business as Usual
")
See also
Mutagenic pathogen
Mutagenic virus

A
nadion emitter
was a component aboard a
Federation
starship
. It was, as its
name
suggests, a type of
emitter
that produced
nadions
.
Defiant
-class
starships had multiple nadion emitters.
In
2375
, while preparing the
USS
Defiant
for launch from
space station
Deep Space 9
,
Lieutenant Commander
Worf
ordered
Ensign
Nog
to "
collimate the nadion emitters,
" to which Nog replied, "
Collimation sequence in progress.
" (
DS9
: "
The Changing Face of Evil
")
In the first draft script of "The Changing Face of Evil", Worf didn't mention the nadion emitters and instead commented to Nog that he wanted the
Defiant
ready to depart from DS9 as soon as
Captain
Sisko
arrived, and that that included the
communications array
, a statement to which Nog simply replied, "
Aye
,
sir
.
"

biogenic weapon
.

2370

and

encountered a type of nano-biogenic weapon called the
harvesters
, on a
T'Lani cruiser
. They
attacked
the individual
cells
in a
body
, resulting in a very slow and very painful
death
. O'Brien was in contact with the harvesters, and became
infected
. However, Bashir later saved his life. (
DS9
: "
Armageddon Game
")

Norman

Montgomery Scott

microvision
and a nanopulse laser. Scott had never seen anything like in before, and described it as "absolutely fantastic." (
TOS
: "
I, Mudd
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Nanotechnology
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nanotechnology
======================================================================

A graphic of nanoprobes, a type of nanotechnology
Nanotechnology
was a field of
science
and
technology
involving devices so small that the naked
eye
could not see them.
In
2153
,
scientists
of the
Arctic Archaeology Team
accessed a
medical report
from the
nanotechnology database
while they examined two
Borg drones
. (
ENT
: "
Regeneration
")
Denobulans
were known to have experimented with nanotechnology by the early
2150s
. (
ENT
: "
Regeneration
")
The
Borg Collective
was infamous for their use of nanotechnology to advance their goals, specifically
assimilating
new members and
technologies
with
nanoprobes
. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
A similar method was developed by
Control
after it went rogue in the late
2250s
, as a way of taking over flesh beings. It had previously relied on fabricated
holographic communications
, but required more freedom to operate. The
nanobots
used by Control used ferromagnetic materials. (
DIS
: "
Perpetual Infinity
", "
Through the Valley of Shadows
")
The
Federation
Starfleet
also commonly used nanotechnology, such as in
isolinear circuitry
. In
2366
,
Wesley Crusher
was experimenting with
nanites
, small robotic devices in the field of nanotechnology. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")
In
2370
,
Major
Kira Nerys
delayed
Vedek
Winn
's ship and claimed it was to inspect for nanotechnology. (
DS9
: "
The Collaborator
")
See also
Nanite
Nano-imaging camera
Nano-biogenic weapon
Nanomaterial
Nanoprobe
External link
Nanotechnology
at
Wikipedia

PAGE: Nanut
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nanut

Nanut
Class:
Freighter
Affiliation:
Tygarian
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
The
Nanut
was a
Tygarian
freighter
that was in service in the late
24th century
. Its
executive officer
in the
2370s
was
Romah Doek
.
In early
2370
, it docked at
Deep Space 9
. The
Nanut
was on its way to the
Gamma Quadrant
for a two
year
voyage there.
Li Nalas
stowed away aboard the
Nanut
, but was discovered. Li had planned to leave the ship in the Gamma Quadrant. (
DS9
: "
The Homecoming
")
According to the script for the episode, the pronunciation for Nanut was "NAH-nut".
[1]
The exterior of the
Nanut
was never seen on-screen – only a room in which Romah Doek was seen in during his brief conversation with Commander Benjamin Sisko.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nausicaan ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nausicaan_ship
======================================================================

"I've seen that hull signature enough times."
Crewman Shaw to Matthew Ryan, upon encountering the same Nausicaan ship that attacked the ECS
Fortunate
before
Nausicaan ship
Affiliation:
Nausicaan
pirates
Type:
Fighter
Active:
22nd century
Armament:
Plasma cannons
Defenses:
Shields
Nausicaan ships
were
fighters
employed by
Nausicaan
pirates
during the mid-
22nd century
for raiding
freighter
trade routes
.
Armaments and specifications
These vessels were armed with
fore
and
aft
plasma cannons
and possessed primitive
shields
. Although their
weapons
were considered formidable to a
Y-class
freighter
, Nausicaan ships were outmatched by
NX-class
starships
, whose
spatial torpedoes
were capable of piercing Nausicaan shields.
History
Only three of these vessels, operating out of a
Nausicaan cargo station
, were ever encountered. One notable Nausicaan ship relentlessly pursued and constantly attacked the
ECS
Fortunate
and raided its
cargo
for over a period of several
months
during
2151
. When the Nausicaans yet again attacked the
Fortunate
, her captain was badly injured when the Nausicaans boarded, leaving first officer
Matthew Ryan
in command. One of the Nausicaan boarders was captured, tortured, and interrogated for information by Ryan, who was intent on getting revenge against the pirates.
The
Fortunate
, after escaping from the
Starfleet
vessel
Enterprise
, later tracked down and chased the same previously-encountered Nausicaan ship all the way to their base of operations. The
Fortunate
's own weapons were unfortunately unable to penetrate the shields of the lead Nausicaan ship. The two other Nausicaan ships that had been docked with the cargo station immediately decoupled, and instantly attacked and successfully disabled the
Fortunate
's own impulse engines in order to prevent them from escaping. Fortunately, the
Enterprise
was able to offer assistance during that
year
and managed to negotiate with the Nausicaans to allow the
Fortunate
to leave after the
Enterprise
's helmsman
Travis Mayweather
successfully managed to reason with and convince Matthew Ryan to return the captive Nausicaan prisoner to his fellow Nausicaans. The Nausicaan ships, upon retrieving their comrade, immediately withdrew and allowed the
Enterprise
and the
Fortunate
to leave without any further resistance. (
ENT
: "
Fortunate Son
")
Ships commissioned
Unnamed Nausicaan ships
Background information
In the
script
for "Fortunate Son", this type of vessel was described as "small" and "fast." The
bridge
of a Nausicaan ship was described as "dark," although the final version of the room clearly featured large, colorful
wall panels
. Also, the offensive and defensive specifications of a Nausicaan ship were described thus, "
Medium yield plasma cannons. Simple (TECH) shielding,
" where the word "tech" was later to be replaced by a more technical term. In the episode's final version, this became, "
Fore and aft plasma cannons,
" and a less direct reference, immediately thereafter, to the craft having shields.
Star Trek: Enterprise
's production design team received the instruction to design the
studio model
for the small
Nausicaan attack fighter
during the show's
first season
. Even though Nausicaan starships had never before been portrayed, inspiration for the forthcoming design was taken from how Nausicaans themselves had been established as typically behaving; due to their thuggish nature as belligerent troublemakers, their vessels had to have a fittingly threatening and aggressive appearance.
Illustrator
John Eaves
began designing the Nausicaan vessel by sketching out a variety of basic shapes. "
I just threw a whole bunch of stuff together,
" he stated. These initial sketches included a design concept for a Vulcan ship which Eaves had drawn earlier. The same ship layout, with blade-like curves, gave Eaves a design direction, which he expanded upon in the other sketches. As he became increasingly more inspired, Eaves felt the look he was aiming for was somewhere "between the bottom of a shovel and a manta ray." The producers approved one of the initial illustrations as the final version, a configuration Eaves likened to a manta ray. "
On this one,
" he continued, "
they chose a ship right off the first pass [....] It was a busy show, so I didn't get to flesh it out a little bit more, but I didn't need to.
"
The only view of the bridge as a backdrop behind actor
Danny Goldring
The reason it wasn't essential for Eaves to describe all the details of the Nausicaan craft was that the
CGI
artists knew how to proceed from his rudimentary design. At
Eden FX
, Lead CG Artist
Pierre Drolet
took Eaves' illustration and digitally turned it into a three-dimensional model. Drolet incorporated some of his own ideas into the design, shading the Nausicaan ship with an earthy color scheme, and added detailing such as the forward plasma cannons. All these finishing touches helped give the ship its highly sought-after aggressive look. (
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
, issue 30, pp. 16-17)
The bridge of the Nausicaan ship was built as a backdrop set on
Paramount Stage 8
. (
Call sheet
)
Presumably, these vessels had
warp drive
and could travel faster than
warp
1.8, the
maximum speed
of the
Fortunate
, for them to be able to constantly locate, track, attack, and raid said vessel over an extended period of time. Issue 30 of
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
listed the Nausicaan fighters as having a maximum speed of warp two and a length of ninety meters.

======================================================================
PAGE: Navigation beacon
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Navigation_beacon
======================================================================

A
navigation beacon
was a component of an
Andorian escape pod
which allowed nearby
spacecraft
to easily detect it. The beacon blinked intermittently, which was visible on the craft's
hull
, while active.
In
December
2153
, a navigation beacon was used by an
Andorian
escape pod
containing
Starfleet
Captain
Jonathan Archer
. The beacon helped
Enterprise
NX-01
to find him. (
ENT
: "
Proving Ground
")
The term "navigation beacon" comes from the final draft script of "Proving Ground".

The
negaton hydrocoil
was a piece of
nanotechnology
equipment that natives of
Arret
used to construct
androids
.
In
2268
,
Thalassa
asked
Commander
Scott
to prepare them, utlizing
drawings
from
Sargon
as a reference. Although the
chief engineer
complied, he had his doubts that a device resembling a drop of
jelly
could be effective, suggesting they use
microgears
and a
pulley
instead. Thalassa felt Scott's suggestion to be inefficient in comparison. (
TOS
: "
Return to Tomorrow
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Nerve gas
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nerve_gas
======================================================================

A
nerve gas
,
nerve agent
or
neural agent
was an airborne (gaseous or aerosol) agent of chemical warfare. Usually lethal, these compounds worked by inhibiting synaptic activity. Such activity was essential for propagation of impulses from the
central nervous system
to other systems of the body; without that activity, the victim became
paralyzed
and
died
.
Prior to the
23rd century
, the
Klingons
employed a nerve gas called
theragen
. Theragan was deadly only in concentrated form. (
TOS
: "
The Tholian Web
")
In
2371
,
Samantha Wildman
and
The Doctor
found that because
Vidiian
immune systems
had been compromised by the
Phage
, they could
synthesize
a neural agent that would incapacitate them without affecting the crew of
USS
Voyager
. When complete, the agent would inhibit the Vidiian's
motor functions
without causing any permanent damage. When a
Vidiian ship
later attempted to board
Voyager
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
called down to
Sickbay
to have them release it on
deck 3
, to which The Doctor figured 50
milliliters
would suffice. (
VOY
: "
Fury
")
In
2372
, when
Quark
sought to fulfill the terms of a
contract
with
Brunt
calling for his
death
, nerve gas was one option
Elim Garak
suggested he use to cause it. However, Quark thought the nerve gas smelled bad, and he decided he wanted his death to be sudden. Ultimately, he broke the contract. (
DS9
: "
Body Parts
")
In
2373
,
The Doctor
euthanized
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
with nerve gas, in response to her having had contracted the
Vidiian
phage
, during one of the delusional realities induced by the
alien being
attempting to lure her into its
matrix
. (
VOY
: "
Coda
")
External link
Nerve agent
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Network
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Network
======================================================================

A
network
was a group of interconnected individual units, such as
computers
or
circuitry
pathways.
In
2151
, a
proximity scan
was among the
data
displayed on the display
table
in the situation room next to a
planetary surface scan
of the
tunnel
network on
Terra Nova
. (
ENT
: "
Terra Nova
")
Types of networks
Earth satellite network
Neural network
Television network

======================================================================
PAGE: Neural neutralizer
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Neural_neutralizer
======================================================================

Neural neutralizer
Neural neutralizer control panel
A
neural neutralizer
was a type of device used at the
Tantalus Penal Colony
in
2266
. It was an outgrowth of experimental
beam neutralization
technologies developed on
Earth
. The neural neutralizer was essentially a beam emitter, a round
orange
lens
that was located in a
treatment room
, above a
chair
in which the
patient
sat. A windowed
alcove
, connected to the room, housed a
control panel
, which was used for manually operating the device, and space for the
therapist
, who could view the patient through the window.
The chair was reused in
TOS
: "
Operation -- Annihilate!
" and (in altered form) "
Whom Gods Destroy
". (
The Star Trek Compendium
, 4th ed., p. 42)
Operation of the device was straightforward. One control turned the device on and off, and another selected the intensity of the beam. The therapist could communicate with the patient through a microphone. The remainder of the control panel consisted of status indicator lamps.
The neural neutralizer was somewhat effective as a means of implanting suggestions. It created in the patient a profound sense of loneliness, also described as an "emptiness". Anything said to the patient while he was in this state would become his own thoughts. The effectiveness varied according to the complexity of the suggestion, the number of times it had been repeated, the volume of the beam, the strength of the patient's will, and the degree to which the suggestion was opposed by his own desires. Long term or repeated exposure could produce profound mental illness and synaptic damage. Additionally, the "emptying" effect could be immediately lethal;
Dr.
Tristan Adams
was killed by accidental, unsupervised exposure to the beam.
In 2266, Dr. Adams began to conduct unauthorized and unethical experiments upon inmates and staff alike. When the
USS
Enterprise
visited the Tantalus colony in that year, Adams' assistant
Simon Van Gelder
had been made severely ill by exposure. Adams later used the device on
Kirk
, and might have injured him severely had not Dr.
Helen Noel
managed to interrupt the power long enough for
Enterprise
crewmen to
beam
down and secure the
colony
. Dr. Van Gelder subsequently dismantled and destroyed the neural neutralizer. (
TOS
: "
Dagger of the Mind
")
In early drafts of "Dagger of the Mind", a machine containing colored lights was used to
hypnotize
the patient in a lengthy scene. This developed into the neural neutralizer established in the final version of the episode. (
The Star Trek Compendium
, 4th ed., p. 41)
In the final draft script of "Dagger of the Mind", the neural neutralizer was also once called a "neural potentiator device".
See also
Engramatic purge
Memory wipe
Mind scanner
Psycho-tricorder
Rehabilitation chair
Romulan mind probe

======================================================================
PAGE: Neural servo
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Neural_servo
======================================================================

A neural servo
A
neural servo
was a
device
, sometimes inserted subdermally in
humanoids
, in order to excite neural pathways. These devices were often used to control or
punish
individuals.
In
2268
, the
instrument of obedience
was used by the
Oracle
of
Yonada
to punish sacrilegious or contumely behavior. It appeared to operate in a manner similar to a neural servo. (
TOS
: "
For the World is Hollow and I Have Touched the Sky
")
In
2370
,
Arctus Baran
used neural servos, which were implanted in each member of his crew, as a means of maintaining
obedience
. (
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
", "
Gambit, Part II
")
Other examples of such devices were the various
agonizers
used by a number of species.

======================================================================
PAGE: Neuro-depolarizing device
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Neuro-depolarizing_device
======================================================================

A
neuro-depolarizing device
was a
suicide
device used by covert operatives in
Section 31
in the
24th century
to prevent others from extracting information from their mind. The neuro-depolarizer was implanted into the operative's brain and could be activated by thought while the operative was restrained in a
force field
. Upon activation, it killed the operative immediately, although quick medical intervention could allow
higher brain function
to continue for up to an hour, though the memory pathways would be scrambled. The device prevented the use of
Romulan mind probes
, requiring someone to go into the mind of the operative to find information.
In
2375
,
Luther Sloan
of Section 31 activated a neuro-depolarizing device in an attempt to prevent
Doctor
Julian Bashir
from obtaining a cure for the
morphogenic virus
designed by Section 31. (
DS9
: "
Extreme Measures
")
See also
Suicide gland
Termination implant

======================================================================
PAGE: Neurocine gas
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Neurocine_gas
======================================================================

Neurocine gas
Neurocine gas
was a
toxic
gaseous
compound used by the
Cardassians
. It was
lethal
to both
Bajorans
and
Humans
.
During the Cardassian
Occupation of Bajor
, this gas was used as a method of riot control against the Bajoran workers on
Terok Nor
.
In
2371
, when a
counter-insurgency program
was accidentally activated by the
Starfleet
crew of the
space station
Deep Space 9
, the ore processing center was flooded with neurocine gas in an attempt to control a worker riot. Later on, the program threatened to flood the entire
habitat ring
with the gas, potentially killing hundreds of innocent civilians. (
DS9
: "
Civil Defense
")
The public explanation for
Section 31
's recruitment of
William Boimler
into the organization was that he had died in a neurocine gas leak aboard the
USS
Titan
. (
LD
: "
Crisis Point 2: Paradoxus
")
See also
Neurazine gas
Neurozine

PAGE: Next generation
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Next_generation
======================================================================

For the genealogical descriptor, please see
generation
.
For the television series, please see
Star Trek: The Next Generation
.
Next generation
was a
term
used to
describe
the newest iteration of a
technology
.
Even when the
warp five engine
was newly introduced and the only one in
Starfleet
was being used by
Enterprise
NX-01
, warp five engines were
expected
to be installed in the next generation of
freighters
. (
ENT
: "
Fortunate Son
")
In
2375
,
Kathryn Janeway
described the
quantum slipstream drive
"the next generation of instellar
propulsion
. (
VOY
: "
Timeless
")
(
DIS
: "
Kobayashi Maru
")
(
DIS
: "
The Examples
")
(
DIS
: "
...But to Connect
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Nieser cage
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nieser_cage
======================================================================

A
Nieser cage
was a piece of
technology
related to
telepathy
.
While evaluating some
Devore scanners
Haz Mazaro
had for sale on the
Karma Barge
in
3190
,
Doctor
Ruon Tarka
complained that they could not "
pick out a
Betazoid
standing all alone in a Nieser cage.
" (
DIS
: "
All In
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Night Alien ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Night_Alien_ship
======================================================================

Three Night Alien ships
The
Night Alien ship
was a type of
starship
operated by the
Night Aliens
of the Delta Quadrant. Thousands of these ships resided in the region of space dubbed "
the Void
" by the
USS
Voyager
crew.
The ships were equipped with
cloaking devices
that produced a
photonic
surge upon deactivation. They also had
transporters
and could generate a
dampening field
to drain the power from other ships. They were equipped with powerful
directed energy weapons
and
shields
, but were outgunned by
Malon export vessels
.
In
2375
,
Voyager
was temporarily disabled by three Night Alien ships while traversing the Void.
Tuvok
illuminated the ships by modifying a
photon torpedo
into a
warp flare
. The ships nearly overwhelmed
Voyager
before they were driven off by a Malon export vessel commanded by
Emck
.
After
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
made peace with the Night Aliens, several of their ships supported
Voyager
's attempt to destroy the
spatial vortex
Emck had been using to dump
antimatter waste
in the Void. The Night Alien ships weakened Emck's vessel enough for
Voyager
to destroy it. (
VOY
: "
Night
")
The revised final draft of the script stated the following regarding the "Night Ships": "
The vessels are smaller than
Voyager
... barely visible, glowing with incandescence... encrusted with alien projections... threatening.
" The weapons fire from these vessels was described as a "acrackling blast of energy."
The "Night Ship" was designed by
Dan Curry
, with
CGI
model created by
Brandon MacDougall
at
Foundation Imaging
. His original concepts, dated
12 June
1998
and
29 June
1998
, were inspired by deep sea creatures that also existed in darkness, perhaps organically grown with technological armor attached. (
Star Trek: The Artistry of Dan Curry
, p. 60;
Star Trek Voyager: A Celebration
, p. 213)

======================================================================
PAGE: Nightingale (medical transport)
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nightingale_(medical_transport)
======================================================================

Nightingale
AKA: Medical Transport 136
Class:
Medical transport
Owner:
Kraylor
Operator:
Brell
Harry Kim
Status:
Active
(
2377
)
For the
Wallenberg
-class  transport, please see
Nightingale
.
The
Nightingale
was the name given by
Ensign
Harry Kim
to the
Kraylor
starship
Medical Transport 136
, previously under the command of
Captain
Brell
. The
Nightingale
differed from other
Kraylor medical transports
in that it was used on a secret mission by a research team to transport a
cloaking device
developed for their fleet.
The
crew
of the
USS
Voyager
first came across this vessel in early
2377
when the
Delta Flyer
responded to a
distress call
from the
commander
of the Kraylor vessel. Responding to the signal, Kim discovered the Kraylor were at
war
with the
Annari Empire
, and were under attack by
Annari warships
. Kim tried to negotiate with the
Annari
, but was forced to disable their vessels when he found himself under attack.
After
beaming
onto the Medical Transport 136, Kim and
Neelix
found the vessel in complete disarray – about half the crew were dead, including all but one of the
bridge officers
. Kim offered to help with repairs of the vessel, along with Neelix and
Seven of Nine
.
After the vessel had been repaired,
Loken
, head of the research team, begged Kim to
captain
the vessel back to their
homeworld
, telling him that the
vaccine
they have been working to develop must reach there. Kim was reluctant, not wanting to violate the
Prime Directive
, but finding out that the
planet
where
Voyager
had landed was en route, agreed to take them that far at least.
Nightingale
's captain's ready room
Captain
Janeway
was eventually convinced to take the Kraylor's side in the matter, and permitted Kim to take command of the medical transport, which he promptly renamed the
Nightingale
. As Kim himself admitted, he named this vessel for
Florence Nightingale
, whom he described as being known for aiding wounded soldiers on the battlefield – an appropriate name for this vessel, which he believed was delivering vaccines to their homeworld. However, Janeway asked Seven of Nine to go along on the journey. Kim was excited at the chance of having his "first real command."
The
cloak
began to fail a short time into the journey due to a fluctuation in the propulsion
power
systems. Repairs were made, yet Kim dismissed Seven's recommendations to run a full diagnostic, reminding her Janeway wanted them back before
Voyager
finished repairs. A short time later, as predicted, the primary generator failed, as did the cloak. Half a dozen Annari vessels moved into attack range, and a Kraylor researcher named
Dayla
volunteered to try and bring the cloak back online, receiving instructions from Kim, and she and Loken together figured out how to bring the cloak back online by taking the
warp core
off-line. Unfortunately for Dayla,
life support
failed in the
engineering
area, and she died.
Confronting Loken, Kim discovered the
Nightingale
's
cloak
was actually a
prototype
, what Loken's team, including Dayla, had really been working on at a secret
base
. The Annari knew about the prototype, and didn't want it to arrive at the
Kraylor homeworld
, by setting up a
blockade
around their planet. Kim realized that he was in the middle of a breach of the Prime Directive, and ordered the
Nightingale
to reverse course, but was relieved of command. Seven of Nine pointed out to Kim that if he truly cared about the crew, he needed to help the Kraylor home, or they may not survive.
At the Kraylor homeworld, a new series of
scanning
pulses illuminated cloaked vessels. Having been detected, surrounded and outgunned by Annari vessels, Kim took command, promising to help them, to which Loken agreed. Realizing the situation, Kim appeared to offer a conditional surrender with the Annari; however, if they didn't allow the
scientists
to safely evacuate to the planet, Kim threatened to destroy the
Nightingale
. Loken was furious about Kim's apparent betrayal, but was assured he had a plan to save the vessel, but if he failed, "...at least the scientist who designed the cloak will be alive to create another one." Once under the grip of the Annari
tractor beam
, Kim reversed the
Nightingale
's
shield polarity
, and used the momentum to outrun their pursuers to the defense perimeter.
Harry Kim's final entry in the
Nightingale
's
log
noted that his actions led to cloaking mechanisms being added to the Kraylor fleet. (
VOY
: "
Nightingale
")
For more information on the
studio model
, see
DS9 studio models
.

PAGE: Nihydron warship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nihydron_warship
======================================================================

Alternate timeline
(covers information from an
alternate timeline
)
Forward view
Aft view
A
Nihydron warship
was a type of
warship
utilized by the
Nihydron
species
during the late
24th century
.
In
an
alternate
2374
, three Nihydron warships and two
Mawasi cruisers
aided the
USS
Voyager
in the destruction of the
Krenim weapon ship
. In the end, however, two of these vessels were destroyed in the process, when they were hit by the ship's temporal weapon.
(
VOY
: "
Year of Hell, Part II
")
For more information on this
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nintendo Switch
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nintendo_Switch
======================================================================

Nintendo Switch
The
Nintendo Switch
was a
video game
console
from early
21st century
Earth
.
The
Zibalian
collector
Kerner Hauze
had one of these
devices
in his collection in
2381
. (
LD
: "
Kayshon, His Eyes Open
")
Several
Star Trek
games are available to play on the
Nintendo
Switch.
External link
Nintendo Switch
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Nokaro
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nokaro
======================================================================

Nokaro
Class:
Colony ship
Status:
Destroyed
(
2336
)
The
Nokaro
was a
colony ship
carrying nearly three thousand people, mostly families.
In
2336
, the ship was consumed by a giant
bioplasmic organism
, which fooled the crew into believing that they were headed for a habitable
planet
.
Qatai
's family was among those lost, leading him to dedicate his life to destroying the creature. (
VOY
: "
Bliss
")
This starship was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nova One
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nova_One
======================================================================

Nova One
Class:
Nova One
-type
Owner:
Nick Locarno
Affiliation:
Nova Fleet
Status:
Destroyed
(
2381
)
"
Uh, nice ship. Very... white. And... nothing else.
"
–
Lieutenant jg
Beckett Mariner
,
2381
("
Old Friends, New Planets
")
The
Nova One
was a small independent
starship
designed and operated by
Nick Locarno
during the early
2380s
.
History
Throughout
2381
, the ship was, from the perspective of
Starfleet
, responsible for the destruction of several non-Federation ships, including the
Klingon
Bird-of-Prey
the
IKS
Che'Ta'
, a
Romulan starship
, an
Orion ship
, a
Ferengi starship
, a
Bynar ship
, and several
Cardassian ships
. This was so believed due to the existence of
debris
at the location of each encounter. (
LD
: "
Twovix
", "
I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee
", "
Something Borrowed, Something Green
", "
Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
", "
A Few Badgeys More
")
The
Betazed government
's
intelligence agency
, the
BIA
, was the first to acquire grainy footage of the ship, which they later shared with
Captain
Carol Freeman
of the
USS
Cerritos
. (
LD
: "
Empathological Fallacies
")
Meanwhile, the attacks later affected
Ferengi
trade routes
, prompting
Grand Nagus
Rom
to initiate membership into the
Federation
. (
LD
: "
Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
")
Later the same year, one of
AGIMUS
'
murder drones
acquired footage of the capture of the Bynar ship. The footage was later acquired by
Lieutenants
Brad Boimler
and
D'Vana Tendi
and shared with
Starfleet
after AGIMUS had a change of heart in his actions. The footage revealed that the Bynar's ship wasn't destroyed, but towed away. At the same time, the
Cerritos
was sent into
Bynar space
to investigate the loss of the Bynar ship.
Badgey
exploited the incident and the
Cerritos
' resulting presence to get revenge on
Sam Rutherford
. (
LD
: "
A Few Badgeys More
")
After this
Starfleet Intelligence
learned that the ship was not only targeting non-Federation ships, but turning to ex-Starfleet personnel, prompting Starfleet to locate and recover a number these such former officers, with the
Cerritos
tasked with recovering Locarno from
New Axton
. On New Axton, Locarno's hangar facility was eventually discovered, alongside clear evidence of his direct involvement in the thefts:
schematics
of the mysterious ship.
Simultaneously, an
away team
of
Cerritos
lower deckers
– Lieutenants Boimler, Tendi,
T'Lyn
, and
Beckett Mariner
– were sent to
Sherbal V
to repair a
weather satellite
. Captain Freeman, unaware that the stolen ships' command crews were being marooned on the planet, had arranged this mission to keep an increasingly reckless Lieutenant Mariner out of harm's way; she was not aware that she was in fact sending Mariner into the lion's den. After they had discovered that the satellite had been tampered with, they departed, and were immediately attacked by the IKS
Che'Ta'
.
Fortuitously, they
beamed
to the
planet
's
surface
, where they discovered several crews from the ships that were attacked by the mysterious ship that Starfleet was investigating. On the planet's surface, Mariner was able to unite the captured crews against their attacker, but was promptly beamed aboard the mysterious ship, where she later discovered its
pilot
, former
Nova Squadron
leader, Nick Locarno. (
LD
: "
The Inner Fight
")
Aboard
Nova One
, Mariner was taken to the
Detrion system
where she was introduced to
Nova Fleet
. She further accompanied Locarno to the ship's bridge, where she was unwillingly involved in his plot. From the bridge, Locarno announced Nova Fleet's existence to the
Alpha Quadrant
, and eventually invited her to speak, expecting her to incite Starfleet lower deckers to rebel. She instead spoke out against him, confiscated the stolen black market
Genesis Device
that Locarno had in his possession, and escaped the
Nova One
by
commandeering
the
USS
Passaro
.
Soon after, the
Nova One
joined the pursuit of Mariner's stolen ship and went into a
level 7 ion storm
to retrieve the confiscated Genesis Device. After inflicting damage to Mariner's ship, Locarno boarded it and unsuccessfully attempted to disarm the device that Mariner had activated. Seconds later, the device detonated and drew in all the surrounding matter, including the
Nova One
, to recombine it into the newly formed planet,
Locarno
. (
LD
: "
Old Friends, New Planets
")
Appendices
Appearances
LD
:
"
Twovix
" (
Season 4
)
"
I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee
"
"
Something Borrowed, Something Green
"
"
Empathological Fallacies
"
(image on
PADD
)
"
Parth Ferengi's Heart Place
"
"
A Few Badgeys More
"
"
The Inner Fight
"
"
Old Friends, New Planets
"

joined the pursuit of Mariner's stolen ship and went into a

to retrieve the confiscated Genesis Device. After inflicting damage to Mariner's ship, Locarno boarded it and unsuccessfully attempted to disarm the device that Mariner had activated. Seconds later, the device detonated and drew in all the surrounding matter, including the
Nova One
, to recombine it into the newly formed planet,
Locarno
. (
LD
: "
Old Friends, New Planets
")

Numiri patrol ship
Affiliation:
Numiri
Armament:
Directed energy weapons
Defenses:
Regenerative shields
The
Numiri patrol ship
was a small, standard vessel of the
Numiri
fleet. They were armed with
directed-energy weapons
comparable to an
Intrepid
-class
starship. They were also equipped with
regenerative shields
and
tractor beams
.
The
USS
Voyager
fought and defeated two Numiri patrol vessels in
2371
, thanks to a
Maquis
trick previously used by
Commander
Chakotay
against two
Starfleet
runabouts
. (
VOY
: "
Ex Post Facto
")
For information on this
studio model
, see
TNG studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Nuu'Bari mining vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nuu'Bari_mining_vessel
======================================================================

A Nuu'Bari mining vessel
The
Nuu'Bari mining vessel
was a
warp-capable
24th century
starship
operated by the
Nuu'Bari
.
In
2377
, such a vessel had contact with the sentient
photonic lifeform
Iden
and his ship. Iden wanted to free the
holograms
which worked as laborers on this vessel. After he transported them onto his ship he destroyed the
mining vessel
by firing
photon torpedoes
on its
warp core
. (
VOY
: "
Flesh and Blood
")
This vessel was described in the
script
as "a small
MINING SHUTTLE
," "much smaller" than the
Holograms' ship
.
The model was a modified version of
Tony Meininger
's original creation. The model appeared in several episodes of
Star Trek: The Next Generation
,
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
,
Star Trek: Voyager
, and
Star Trek: Enterprise
; see also
TNG studio models
.

PAGE: Nygean prison ship

A Nygean prison ship (2377)
A
Nygean prison ship
was a
starship
operated by the
Nygean Detention Force
to transport
prisoners
from one
planet
to another, sometimes for execution. Nygean prison ships were capable of
warp
8.2 and equipped with
escape pods
.
In
2377
, such a
prison ship
under the command of
warden
Yediq
suffered a ruptured
conduit
, flooding it with
plasma radiation
. Luckily, the
crew
was rescued by the
USS
Voyager
, which was nearby when they issued a
distress call
. Its (surviving) complement was comprised of three
Nygean
guards, three Nygean prisoners, and five
Benkaran
prisoners. (
VOY
: "
Repentance
")
For more information on this
CGI model
design, please see
VOY studio models
.

24th century
.

USS

in "
the Void
" in

. It was one of the 29 vessels that had managed to survive the Void, later escaping. (
VOY
: "
The Void
")

studio model

======================================================================
PAGE: Nyrian biosphere vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Nyrian_biosphere_vessel
======================================================================

Diagram of the biosphere vessel
The
Nyrian biosphere vessel
, also described as a
habitat vessel
, was a vast
starship
utilized by the
Nyrians
of the
Delta Quadrant
. It contained at least four decks, and corridors containing control terminals, and at least one more extensive control center (or what appeared like it).
The ship was equipped with a
translocator
and acted as the
prison
in which the Nyrians might keep the
crews
of ships,
space stations
, and
colonies
that they had acquired.
The ship contained 94 habitats, speculated to possibly be
holographic
in nature, containing thousands of prisoners. The habitats appeared to be tailored to make the stay of the prisoners as comfortable as possible. Habitats could be entered from the main body of the ship via hidden portals. These portals also linked one habitat to another.
The habitats were individually named, including
Argala
,
Acacia
,
Bourget
,
Bowsers
,
Calentar
,
Federation
,
Niacin
,
Porcion
,
Quitar
,
Tanatuva
, and
Zooabud
. Monitoring programs, incorporating
biosensors
, were deployed in each habitat.
The crew of the
USS
Voyager
found themselves imprisoned on the ship in late
2373
, only to quickly escape and take control. After translocating several high ranking Nyrians into the Argala habitat, which was very cold by their species norms, they managed to arrange for all prisoners to be taken home. They also planned to disable the translocator. (
VOY
: "
Displaced
")
Habitat: Bourget
Habitat: Federation
Habitat: Tanatuva
Habitat: Zooabud
Unknown habitat
Unknown habitat

======================================================================
PAGE: Oculus
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Oculus
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Rutherford with his oculus
"
It robbed me of my memories, and it was used to cover up deadly crimes… but it's so cool! I'm still finding submenus I had no idea were in there!
"
–
Sam Rutherford
,
2381
("
The Stars At Night
")
The
oculus
was a supposedly
Vulcan
cybernetic implant
that
Sam Rutherford
received after being severely injured in an accident during his time at
Starfleet Academy
. Then-
Lieutenant Commander
Les Buenamigo
had the
cybernetics
installed on the left side of his skull -- programmed to make him falsely believe he'd chosen to receive it to assist with engineering -- and all memories of the "program" and the officer involved were erased on Buenamigo's orders; as a side effect of this neural reprogramming, he developed a new, more strait-laced and carefree personality which carried forward for most of his career thereafter. (
LD
: "
Second Contact
", "
First First Contact
", "
Reflections
", "
The Stars At Night
")
The implant would occasionally act up due to Rutherford's original personality trying to reassert itself. (
LD
: "
Reflections
")
In
2380
, Rutherford's original oculus was destroyed when
Lieutenant
Shaxs
ripped it from his face and left it behind on a
Pakled Clumpship
, in order to save Rutherford from
Badgey
's attempt to kill him. The remnants of it survived the explosion and were later recovered along with Badgey by the
Drookmani
. However, as a result of the loss of his implant, Rutherford lost his long-term memory. (
LD
: "
No Small Parts
", "
The Stars At Night
", "
A Few Badgeys More
")
The destroyed oculus was subsequently replaced with a new identical device. (
LD
: "
Strange Energies
")
In
2381
, Rutherford's implant acted up again. This time, it caused him to experience the moment of his accident as a recurring nightmare. When
D'Vana Tendi
tried to erase the memories from his implant's cache, it caused his older, more aggressive personality to reassert itself, explaining that the brain was too complex for the memory wipe to have actually worked, instead leaving Cadet Rutherford locked away. Rutherford's many glitches and strange emotional quirks were a result of his previous personality trying to reassert itself. The two agreed to a race to decide who would control their body with Cadet Rutherford reconstructing the
Sampaguita
while Ensign Rutherford reconstructed the
Delta Flyer
with his closest friends serving as his crew. Ensign Rutherford won, and Cadet Rutherford vanished for good so that the person he had become could live on. Finally accepting that Starfleet had made him a better person, Cadet Rutherford urged his newer self to hold onto the friends that he had made. As a result, Rutherford was able to remember at least some parts of his past, most notably the fact that his implant was installed to cover up illegal activities, rather than voluntarily. However, Rutherford was unable to remember who was behind it. (
LD
: "
Reflections
")
After discovering the truth behind the
Texas
-class
automated starships
, Rutherford regained his memory of the fact that now-
Vice Admiral
Les Buenamigo was responsible for him having the oculus. Despite this, he chose to continue using the oculus following an attack caused by the vessels' emotional instability because he found it too useful to give up. (
LD
: "
The Stars At Night
")
In an alternate reality, by
2382
,
Rutherford
had become mostly
cybernetic
, rather than only having an oculus implant.
(
LD
: "
Dos Cerritos
")
While on a break in 2382, Rutherford spent the morning trying to connect his oculus to the systems of the
USS
Cerritos
without any success. The oculus suddenly shot a plasma beam that nearly hit
Brad Boimler
despite the fact that it was not supposed to be able to shoot plasma. (
LD
: "
Fissure Quest
")
After his oculus caused problems during the effort to stop a destructive
soliton wave
, Rutherford gave it up for good even after the crisis was over, instead becoming fully organic or replacing it with a standard
ocular implant
. Afterwards, he noted that the implant came with a
dopamine
inhibitor that was interfering with his ability to make romantic connections with people, causing  Rutherford's feelings for Tendi to become obvious for the first time with it gone. (
LD
: "
The New Next Generation
")

subspace

transporter

shields
of
Federation
Intrepid
-class
starships. (

Warhead
")

PAGE: Optronic integrator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Optronic_integrator

An optronic integrator
An
optronic integrator
was a piece of
optronic circuitry
used to facilitate connections in
security
and
communications
relays aboard a
starship
or
space station
.
In
2373
, a
Pah-wraith
inhabiting the body of
Keiko O'Brien
demanded that
Miles O'Brien
reconfigure an optronic integrator on
Level 5
of
Deep Space 9
on her behalf. When Miles informed his staff he would be working on the integrator,
Rom
initially wanted to help, because he had an affinity for work involving
phase shifting
reconfigurations. After O'Brien completed the work, the pah-wraith informed him that the request was only test to be sure that it could trust him and that the "real work" would begin the next day. (
DS9
: "
The Assignment
")

PAGE: Optronic relay
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Optronic_relay
======================================================================

A 24th century optronic relay
An
optronic relay
was a piece of technology that came in many different sizes and was in use since at least as early as the
22nd century
.
A
Kantare
ship stranded on a desolate
planet
was equipped with optronic relays that were starting to malfunction.
Trip Tucker
helped repair the relays. (
ENT
: "
Oasis
")
When
Dukat
wanted to turn on the
holofilters
on his
Klingon Bird-of-Prey
as the ship was flying to
Ty'Gokor
, the holofilters didn't work since the optronic relays were fused. (
DS9
: "
Apocalypse Rising
")
A short time later, an optronic relay broke again, this time in the
device
Worf
used to make
Quark
look like an accomplished
bat'leth
fighter
when he was courting
Grilka
. (
DS9
: "
Looking for par'Mach in All the Wrong Places
")
When it seemed that
Morn
had died in early
2374
,
Chief
O'Brien
kept Morn's seat warm and repaired an optronic relay on the counter of
Quark's
. (
DS9
: "
Who Mourns for Morn?
")
When
Kellin
and
Ezri Dax
were trying to figure out a way to modify a
tricorder
so that it could detect the
Houdinis
, Ezri suggested cross-linking the optronic and
isodyne relays
. (
DS9
: "
The Siege of AR-558
")

opti-cable
•
optronic coupler
•
optronic data core
•
optronic data stream
•
optronic emitter
•
optronic integrator
•
optronic pathway
•

•

at

, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Ord'mirit mining vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ord'mirit_mining_vessel
======================================================================

An Ord'mirit mining vessel
The
Ord'mirit mining vessel
was a type of
ship
in use during the late-
24th century
.
In
2375
, a
biomimetic copy
of the
USS
Voyager
encountered a vessel of this type while in
orbit
of a
class Y planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
. The
commander
of the ship claimed their presence was in violation of the
Ord'mirit Mining Treaty
. Although the duplicate
Kathryn Janeway
attempted to explain the situation, the ship simply reiterated its earlier demands and fired again. While
Voyager
could have completely destroyed the opposing ship with a polaron blast that would have destabilized its warp core, Janeway rejected that offer despite
Voyager
's current dire condition, not wanting to destroy a ship that was only attacking them due to a misunderstanding. (
VOY
: "
Course: Oblivion
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Organic technology
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Organic_technology
======================================================================

Organic technology
was the result of
science
and
engineering
for the creation and usage of
tools
, crafts, and sometimes
weapons
with
biological
components. Like any other
technology
, it affected a
species
' ability to control and adapt to its environment.
Organic
materials
used in the construction of organic technology could include, living
organisms
, organic compounds, or the products of decay from a recently living organism.
Examples of organic technology include:
Xindi-Reptilian
employment of
biorifles
that used organic
power cells
called
techno-larvae
. (
ENT
: "
The Shipment
")
Organic circuitry
was a component utilized by a
31st century
Earth vessel
. (
ENT
: "
Future Tense
")
Spore drive
was an organic
propulsion system
Starfleet
experimented with during the
2240s
and
2250s
. (
DIS
: "
Context Is for Kings
")
The
Borg
by their very nature seemed to possess a variety of organic technology. As
cybernetic lifeforms
and their goal was to enhance their biological and technological distinctiveness through the
assimilation
of other lifeforms and their technology. The Borg
cybernetic implants
were either
surgically
attached to the
body
or grown internally by
nanoprobes
that have been injected in the
bloodstream
. (
TNG
: "
The Best of Both Worlds
";
Star Trek: First Contact
)
Bio-neural gel packs
were a form of biological
computer technology
used by Starfleet, first developed circa
2370
. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
")
Species 8472
were very advanced, and had a unique
genetic
makeup that they shared with their
bio-ships
: living
organic-based vessels
capable of
warp speed
. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
As of
2373
, the
Breen
, among others, were also known by Starfleet to have used organic-based vessels. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
See also
Biotechnology

======================================================================
PAGE: Orion multi-key
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Orion_multi-key
======================================================================

Orion multi-key
Orion multi-key with prongs extended
The
Orion multi-key
was a traditional
tool
of
Orion
pirates
. A short truncheon with a heavy head bearing four extendable prongs, it had a variety of functions including
weapon
,
lock pick
, and
bottle opener
.
Ensign
Mesk
obtained a "
religious exemption
" to wear an Orion multi-key with his
Starfleet uniform
, though he did not actually know how to use it due to being raised by
Humans
in
Ohio
. In
2381
, Ensign
D'Vana Tendi
took his multi-key to stop a
Karemma ship
from entering the
Bajoran wormhole
, for which she used it to pry open a wall panel, bludgeon a
Karemma
guard, extract the guard's
latinum
tooth
, override a
door
control, and place said tooth to
magnetically
decouple
the
propulsion
controls. (
LD
: "
Hear All, Trust Nothing
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Ornaran freighter
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ornaran_freighter
======================================================================

Ornaran freighter
Affiliation:
Ornaran
Type:
Interplanetary
freighter
Active:
24th century
Speed:
Sublight
An
Ornaran freighter
was a type of small
impulse ship
that was employed by the
Ornarans
. These
freighters
were equipped with
transporters
.
These freighters were considered to be very old by
2364
. By this year, all three were in a state of disrepair which affected their ability to be operable. One of these freighters, the
Sanction
, was lost above
Brekka
when the crew weren't able to repair the
magnetic coil
in time to prevent the ship from disintegrating in the planet's
atmosphere
. (
TNG
: "
Symbiosis
")
See also
Antares
-class
For more information on this
studio model
, see
TNG studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Osaarian merchant ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Osaarian_merchant_ship
======================================================================

Osaarian merchant ship
Affiliation:
Osaarian merchant fleet
Active:
22nd century
Armament:
Particle beam
emitters
An
Osaarian merchant ship
was a type of
starship
utilized as part of the
Osaarian merchant fleet
during the mid-
22nd century
.
This class of vessel was heavily armed, possessing at least two forward
particle beam
emitters. The
hull
of this class of vessel was constructed of
polyduranium
.
Prior to
2153
, two
Osaarian
merchant ships entered the
Delphic Expanse
, looking for new
trade routes
. While in the Expanse, the ships were hit by a
distortion wave
, causing the crews to decide to return home. One of the ships was destroyed trying to return through the
thermobaric cloud
perimeter. The second ship remained in the Expanse, and the crew was forced to turn to
piracy
to survive. (
ENT
: "
Anomaly (ENT)
")
When the Osaarian merchant ship was introduced in the final draft script of "Anomaly", it was referred to as "an ominous-looking MARAUDER VESSEL". From that point on, the script commonly referred to this type of craft as a "Marauder Vessel". In the final draft script of "
Extinction
", the vehicle was likewise referred to as "the marauder ship", though vessels of this configuration are called "merchant ships" on screen in "Anomaly". The script of that installment additionally referred to the weaponry on the Osaarian ships as
disruptors
and stated that one of these vessels would be defeated by
phase cannons
and three
photon torpedoes
, though the craft is actually shown to be foiled by only two
photonic torpedoes
in the final edit of the episode.
The Osaarian merchant ship was designed by
John Eaves
, based on an upside-down mini shovel he kept on his desk. Early sketches described it as a "Maurader Attack Vessel" [sic] and a "
Ktarian
maurader" [sic]. The
CGI model
of the craft was rendered by
Pierre Drolet
.
[1]

: "

======================================================================
PAGE: Override
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Override
======================================================================

This article or section is incomplete
This page lacks essential detail, and is therefore
in need of attention
.
Information regarding expansion requirements may be found on the article's
talk page
. Feel free to
edit
this page to assist with this expansion.
An
override
,
manual override
, or
mutual override
was an
automated system
, such as a
computer
where
manual control
was given to a
person
.
In
2366
, a
control malfunction
led to the
USS
Enterprise
-D
drifting into the path of
stellar matter
. With thirty
seconds
until impact,
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
ordered
manual override on
deflector shields
. Later,
ship
sensors
indicated the approach of a
Borg
vessel, but shields were not responding and manual override was jammed. Even later,
Commander
William T. Riker
switched to manual control of the
air handling system
after an
environmental system malfunction
released
nitrogen oxide
onto the
bridge
. (
TNG
: "
Evolution
")
External link
Manual override
at
Wikipedia

magazine

Earth

.

Alexandria Books
in

. (

")

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Pakled (ship)
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Pakled_(ship)
======================================================================

Pakled
Class:
Clumpship
Operator:
Pakleds
Affiliation:
Dorg
Status:
Active
(
2381
)
The
Pakled
was a
Pakled
Clumpship
in service during the late
24th century
. (
LD
: "
wej Duj
")
On
StarTrek.com
, this ship was identified as the
PSS
Pakled
. "
PSS
" presumably stands for "
P
akled
S
pace
S
hip
", which was how
Rebner
introduced the vessel to
Dorg
.
Crew
Pakled
personnel

======================================================================
PAGE: Pallet
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Pallet
======================================================================

A stack of pallets in an alley
Pallets
A
pallet
was a
wooden stand
on which
items
were stacked for
shipping
.
In an
alternate
1944
, there was a stack of pallets in an
alleyway
where
Alicia Travers
,
Carmine
and his
gangsters
were having a
shoot-out
against some
Nazis
. There was another pallet sitting fairly close to that stack as well, that had a couple of
cardboard boxes
on top of it.
(
ENT
: "
Storm Front, Part II
")
In
2022
, a
Toronto
alley
La'An Noonien-Singh
and
James T. Kirk
were standing in was littered with broken pallets. (
SNW
: "
Tomorrow and Tomorrow and Tomorrow
")
In
2382
, when
capitalism
ended on
Targalus IX
; all of their
money
and
gold
no longer had any
monetary value
, and as such, some of it was turned over to the
USS Cerritos
. Some of the
gold nuggets
were on pallets in the
cargo bay
. (
LD
: "
Shades of Green
")
See also
Sensor pallet
External link
Pallet
at
Wikipedia

panel

electronics
and

controls.

,

warp engine
from a panel in
junction
42-A. (

: "
The Catwalk
")

Computer control panel
Control panel
Entrance panel
