======================================================================
PAGE: Series
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Series
======================================================================

A
series
was a set of similar items created in succession.
While on
planet
Mudd
,
Harcourt Fenton Mudd
created several series
of androids
, though
Norman
was unique. (
TOS
: "
I, Mudd
")
According to
Spock
, the
space
probe
Nomad
was the last of its series. (
TOS
: "
The Changeling
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Serosian vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Serosian_vessel
======================================================================

A Serosian vessel
The
Serosian vessel
was a type of
warp-capable
starship
used by the
Serosians
during the late-
24th century
. They operated with a crew of six and were usually equipped with
isomorphs
; holographic programs used for ship maintenance. 59.2 percent of the vessel's power reserves were dedicated to its
life support systems
.
In
2374
,
USS
Voyager
encountered one such vessel after responding to a
distress call
sent out by the isomorph,
Dejaren
. (
VOY
: "
Revulsion
")
For other appearances of this
studio model
, please see:
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Servocircuit
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Servocircuit
======================================================================

A
servocircuit
was a component that
Data
once added to his
phaser
in order to get it to work in the environment of
Tau Cygna V
. The circuit compensated for the fact that the
planet
's naturally high levels of
hyperonic radiation
randomised the beam of a phaser. According to the
android
, it accomplished this by "continuously recolumnating the output". (
TNG
: "
The Ensigns of Command
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Sheliak colony ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sheliak_colony_ship
======================================================================

Sheliak colony ship
Owner:
Sheliak Corporate
Type:
Colony ship
Active:
2366
Speed:
Warp capable
A
Sheliak colony ship
was a type of
starship
used by the reclusive
Sheliak
species
for the
transport
of "the membership", which was their term for "
colonists
".
In
2366
one such ship was about to arive at
Tau Cygna V
, leading the Sheliak to request the removal of a
Human
colony
on
Tau Cygna V
. This represented the first contact between the
Federation
and Sheliak in
111 years
. The
USS
Enterprise
-D
intercepted the ship. Asking for a consultation under the
Treaty of Armens
,
Jean-Luc Picard
and
Deanna Troi
boarded the ship to discuss with the
commander
about getting more time. Viewing Picard's response to its refusal as an emotional outburst, Picard and Troi were beamed back to the
Enterprise
. The commander finally relented when Picard used the treaty to delay them, and it waited three weeks to continue to Tau Cygna V. (
TNG
: "
The Ensigns of Command
")
The colony ship was identified as a Sheliak
warship
in the script.
[1]
The colony ship was a modified version of the
Merchantman
model first used in
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
. (
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 441) The most obvious modifications were two long protuberances and the distinctive blue coloring. For more information on history of this studio model, please see
Studio models
.
The interior of the ship was a re-dress of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
battle bridge
. ("Star Trek Encyclopedia", 3rd ed., p. 441)

======================================================================
PAGE: Shepherd ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Shepherd_ship
======================================================================

Shepherd ship
Affiliation:
Shepherds
Type:
Escort
Active:
2250s
Armament:
Torpedo launchers
The
Shepherd ship
was a type of
starship
used by the
Shepherd
species
in the mid-
23rd century
to protect ancient
comets
that they venerated as
Arbiters of Life
. These vessels were substantially larger and more technologically advanced than a
Federation
Constitution
-class
starship, endowed with superior
speed
and
firepower
. They were equipped with several forward
torpedo launchers
and a
tractor beam
.
When the
USS
Enterprise
attempted to prevent the comet
C/2260-Quentin
from impacting
Persephone III
in
2259
, a Shepherd ship identifying itself as the
escort
of the Arbiter
M'hanit
interceded and threatened to destroy them if they interfered with the comet any further. As they were tactically outmatched by the Shepherd ship, the
Enterprise
crew implemented a plan to make it seem as though
M'hanit
diverted to a new course on its own. (
SNW
: "
Children of the Comet
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Sherval Das
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sherval_Das
======================================================================

Sherval Das
Class:
Transport
Affiliation:
Valerian
Status:
Active
(
2369
)
The
Sherval Das
was a
Valerian transport
ship
in operation in the
24th century
.
In
2369
, the
Sherval Das
docked at
Deep Space 9
for maintenance.
Major
Kira Nerys
believed that the
starship
was carrying
dolamide
explosives
intended for use in
Cardassian
weapons
because the ship had previously visited
Ultima Thule
,
Fahleena III
, and
Mariah IV
,
planets
on the former route used in dolamide shipping during the
Occupation of Bajor
. (
DS9
: "
Dramatis Personae
")
Personnel
See
:
Sherval Das
personnel
According to the script for the episode, the pronunciation for Sherval Das was "shur-VAL das".
[1]
For more information on this design and history of this model, please see:
Studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Shield-tunneling technology
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Shield-tunneling_technology
======================================================================

Breen dreadnaught using shield-tunneling technology
Shield-tunneling technology
was a
weapon
utilized by the
Breen Imperium
in the
32nd century
. It consisted of an
energy beam
that automatically matched the
shield frequency
of its target via
harmonic resonance
, creating a localized disruption that allowed
transporters
to penetrate.
In
3191
,
Primarch
Ruhn
's
Breen dreadnaught
used shield-tunneling technology to insert
soldiers
into the
Eternal Gallery and Archive
through its shields. This was the first time that the technology had been witnessed by the
Federation
, though
Commander
Rayner
knew of it from his previous experience with the Breen. Commanders
Paul Stamets
and
Jett Reno
, and
Ensign
Adira Tal
, devised a means to temporarily close the tunnel by using a concentrated
positron beam
from the
USS
Discovery
to fool the system into believing that Archive's shield frequency had changed. (
DIS
: "
Labyrinths
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Ship class
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ship_class
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
For ships of a common mission-type, please see
Starship classification
.
A formation of four
Constitution
-class starships, 2268
A
ship class
was a shared construction design of
spacecraft
or
starship
operated by an entity.
Vast research and testing efforts went into perfecting a new ship class before construction, such as the
Galaxy Class Starship Development Project
by
Starfleet
or the development process of the
D7 class
by the
Klingon Empire
. (
TNG
: "
Booby Trap
",
DIS
: "
Point of Light
")
Starfleet's primary hub of ship class research and development was
Utopia Planitia Fleet Yards
on
Mars
. (
TNG
: "
Booby Trap
",
DS9
: "
The Search, Part I
")
In
2365
,
Geordi La Forge
implemented a change to the design of the
dilithium crystal
orientation aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. Upon viewing the modification, one of the ship's designers,
Leah Brahms
, observed that his modification was due to be introduced in the next class starship. (
TNG
: "
Galaxy's Child
")
Starfleet often continued the
Earth
tradition of naming the
first starship of a class
after the class (such as the
USS
Excelsior
, the first
Excelsior
-class
starship launched) or the
USS
Prometheus
(the first
Prometheus
-class
starship). These vessels often bore the
registry
prefix
NX
. Starfleet also commonly continued the tradition of listing a starship's class on its
dedication plaque
. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
;
VOY
: "
Message in a Bottle
")
The term
sister ship
was often used to describe vessels of shared lineage. (
ENT
: "
The Expanse
", et al.) 
By
2267
, at least twelve
Constitution
-class
ships had been commissioned by Starfleet. (
TOS
: "
Tomorrow is Yesterday
")
Some
Klingon
ship classes used a "D" prefix before a number (i.e.
D4 class
,
D5 class
, D7 class, or
D12 class
.) (
ENT
: "
Judgment
";
Star Trek Into Darkness
)
In the late
24th century
, a
Galaxy
-class
starship was often the point of comparison for ship classes. The
Duras sisters
admitted that
their Bird-of-Prey
was "no match" for a
Galaxy
-class starship. In
2371
,
Admiral
Patterson
described the
Intrepid
-class
as "
not as big as a
Galaxy
-class, but she's quick and smart...
" (
Star Trek Generations
,
VOY
: "
Relativity
").
Appendices
See also
Starship classes
Starship classes
(alternate reality)
Background information
Shared ship classes have had the added benefit to
Star Trek
of reducing production costs and production time when a new ship is needed, especially in the age of
studio models
.
The
Star Trek: The Next Generation Technical Manual
portrays a
twenty-year
design and construction process of
Galaxy
-class ships, although "
Booby Trap
" and "
Eye of the Beholder
" seem to suggest that this timeline was much faster. The
Defiant
-class
, launched in
2371
, was said to be developed in response to the
Borg
, which first presented themselves as a threat in
2365
/
2366
.
External link
Ship class
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Short circuit
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Short_circuit
======================================================================

A
short circuit
was a
flaw
in an
electrical system
causing
malfunction
and
damage
.
In
2267
,
Montgomery Scott
attributed the shorting of a
circuit
on the
SS
Botany Bay
to
dust
. (
TOS
: "
Space Seed
")
External link
Short circuit
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Shrike (ship)
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Shrike_(ship)
======================================================================

Shrike
Affiliation:
Vadic
,
rogue Changelings
Type:
Warship
Active:
2401
Armament:
40
isolytic burst
warheads
88
plasma torpedoes
236
photon torpedoes
18
antimatter missiles
20
pulse wave torpedoes
directed energy weapons
portal weapon
Defenses:
Deflector shields
"
...that's
not a ship, it's a
guillotine
.
"
–
William T. Riker
,
2401
("
Disengage
")
The
Shrike
was a formidable
starship
commanded by the
Changeling
Vadic
. The ship was
named
after
a small bird
from
Earth
. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
History
Until its encounter with the
USS
Titan
-A
in
2401
, little was known about the
Shrike
. According to
Seven of Nine
, there were
rumors
among the
Fenris Rangers
which noted "
A ship, similar in design, flying no
flag
but boasting every
weapon
known to
Starfleet
and then some.
" (
PIC
: "
Disengage
")
After the death of
Vadic
and her crew, the
Shrike
was destroyed by the
Titan
with five
photon torpedos
. (
PIC
: "
Surrender
")
Technical data
Tactical systems
The
Shrike
locks a tractor beam on the
Eleos XII
The
Shrike
was heavily armed with weapons both known and unknown to Starfleet. During the ship's encounter with the
Titan
-A in 2401, it was armed with forty
isolytic burst
warheads
, eighty-eight
plasma torpedoes
, two hundred thirty-six
photon torpedoes
, eighteen
antimatter missiles
, twenty
pulse wave torpedoes
, and
directed energy weapons
at the tips of its four forward prongs. In addition, the ship's bay was fitted with a
portal weapon
, based on
quantum tunneling
technology
stolen from
Daystrom Station
.
Mura
also noted the ship had "thirty series five…" but was interrupted by
Shaw
.
The ship was also equipped with a powerful
tractor beam
that could be reversed and utilize
anti-gravitational polarity phasing
to throw another spacecraft with substantial force. (
PIC
: "
Disengage
", "
Seventeen Seconds
")
Appendices
Appearances
PIC
:
"
The Next Generation
"
"
Disengage
"
"
Seventeen Seconds
"
"
The Bounty
"
"
Dominion
"
"
Surrender
"
Background information
The
Shrike
was designed by
Doug Drexler
,
John Eaves
, and
Darek Zabrocki
, and was heavily featured in the
The Ready Room
episode "
Disengage
".
[1]
According to production art, the
Shrike
is 2,162 feet (659 meters) in length.
[2]
The
bridge
set of the
Shrike
was built within the cargo bay of the
SS
La Sirena
. (
TRR
: "
Disengage
")

PAGE: Sikarian vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes

======================================================================

A Sikarian vessel
The interior of the vessel
The
Sikarian vessel
was a small
starship
used by the
Sikarians
for interstellar travel.
In
2371
,
Gathorel Labin
used such a vessel to send a
distress call
to the
USS
Voyager
in the
Delta Quadrant
. After assuring
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
that he was not in distress, rather he sensed her and her crew were, he used his vessel to guide
Voyager
to
Sikaris
. (
VOY
: "
Prime Factors
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Silicon Valley
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Silicon_Valley
======================================================================

Silicon Valley
was a
region
of
California
known for
technology
.
In
1992
,
Henry Starling
received a "
Man of the Year
"
award
from the
Partners in Science
in Silicon Valley, California. (
VOY
: "
Future's End
")

Silicon Valley
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Skagaran starship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Skagaran_starship
======================================================================

Skagaran starship
Affiliation:
Skagarans
Active:
19th century
The
Skagaran starship
was a class of
warp-capable
ship
in use by the
Skagarans
during the
19th century
.
On such ship was used to transport a group of
Humans
from
Earth
to a
Skagaran colony
for use as
slaves
in its construction. After a violent uprising, the Humans eventually overthrew the Skagarans, becoming the dominant
species
on the
planet
.
Skag town
was a settlement built by the Skagarans from the remains of their ship. (
ENT
: "
North Star
")
In the final draft script of "North Star", the Skagaran ship was described as "large". Its wreckage was scripted to be "skeletal remains".

======================================================================
PAGE: Skrreean ships
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Skrreean_ships
======================================================================

The
vessels used by the
Skrreea
were of various designs.
The Skrreea were
farmers
who had been used as laborers and servants for centuries. When they finally were freed sometime before
2370
, they gathered any ships they could get their hands on to search for the "
Eye of the Universe
" that would lead them to the legendary home world of the Skrreea,
Kentanna
. Three million Skrreea were carried by these ships through the
Bajoran wormhole
when
Haneek
discovered it in
2370
.
The ship transporting Haneek and her family suffered from a
plasma leak
in the
engine core
, causing it to overheat. Likewise, the ship's
life support system
was "barely" functioning. Once the four passengers were beamed aboard, the ship was docked and diagnosed. Following the arrival of Haneek at
Deep Space 9
, the station dispatched its
runabouts
to the Gamma Quadrant to make contact with additional ships. Once these ships were brought through the wormhole, the passengers were confined to their ships, due to the station's comparably minimal capacity. (
DS9
: "
Sanctuary
")
Haneek's ship was described in the script as "a ramshackle alien vessel". Later, the script described "Traffic is congested in a way we've never seen: every docking port has a Skrreean ship anchored to it, including the upper and lower pylons… six more ships are lined up just beyond the station, awaiting their turn…"
Information about the
studio model
can be found
here
,
here
, and
here
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Slot machine
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Slot_machine
======================================================================

A slot machine
A
slot machine
was a
gambling
device
often found in
casinos
.
Users
of the device would
deposit
their
wager
, in an amount of
money
requested
by the device, into it and then pull a
lever
located on the side. The machine would then display random and independent images on its front, and if those images formed various combinations, the user would be considered a winner and receive an amount of money proportional to the odds of the image sequence appearing. As with most forms of gambling, the odds were not in the user's favor.
Slot machines were described in the novel
Hotel Royale
, and were seen in a recreation of the Hotel, created for the benefit of
Stephen G. Richey
by an alien race. (
TNG
: "
The Royale
")
Vic's lounge
, within the
Vic Fontaine
holoprogram
, also had slot machines.
Kasidy Yates
used one of them while trying to distract to the
security
guard
guarding the
count room
during a scheme to
rob
the casino. (
DS9
: "
It's Only a Paper Moon
", "
Badda-Bing, Badda-Bang
")
A slot machine was present on
Starbase 80
in the late
24th century
. (
LD
: "
Starbase 80?!
")
The
Star Trek
slot machines
can be found in many US casinos.
External links
Slot machine
at
Wikipedia
Slot Machine
at the
Online Casino wiki

======================================================================
PAGE: Snowplow
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Snowplow

A snowplow
A
snowplow
was a
device
used on
Earth
which was mounted on
land vehicles
to allow them to collect and remove
snow
and
ice
.
In the year
2000
, when
Shannon O'Donnel
arrived in the
town
of
Portage Creek
the
streets
of the
city
were freshly snow plowed, so as to allow
traffic
to flow better. (
VOY
: "
11:59
")
In
2153
, a
wheeled
vehicle
using a snowplow cleared the area in the
base camp
of the
A-6 excavation team
in the
Arctic Circle
on Earth. (
ENT
: "
Regeneration
")
External link
Snowplow
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Soil reclamator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Soil_reclamator
======================================================================

Soil reclamators
were devices developed by the
Bajoran Agricultural Ministry
and used to remove
poison
and other
toxins
from
soil
in order to allow
farmers
to grow crops and make the land more hospitable.
In
2371
,
Winn Adami
, who was both the
kai
and the acting
first minister
of
Bajor
, wanted to use a number of soil reclamators to cleanse key areas of Bajor for agriculture, to grow crops for export. However,
Shakaar Edon
and two other members of the
Shakaar resistance cell
had been promised a longer use of the reclamators and were unwilling to give them up, resulting in a province-wide
arrest
warrant
being issued by the
Bajoran civil police
. (
DS9
: "
Shakaar
")
External link
Soil reclamator
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Solar collector
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Solar_collector
======================================================================

A
solar collector
was a large
device
made of
orange
material which could be used to collect solar energy for power.
During the
USS
Protostar
's ten years
marooned
on
Ysida
,
Captain
Chakotay
used solar collectors to recharge the ship's emergency power cells, having ejected the
Protostar
's
antimatter
and
proto-core
. However, the solar collectors lost efficiency each year. (
PRO
: "
Last Flight of the Protostar, Part II
")
When the
Protostar
was turned into a
sailing ship
, the solar collectors served the dual purpose of
sails
and a power source. However, while circling the maelstrom containing the
deuterium
gas that the ship needed, the solar collectors were torn off by the wind, leaving the
Protostar
with limited power remaining.
Hologram Janeway
shut herself down and redirected all of the remaining power to the ship's
Bussard collectors
as a result. (
PRO
: "
Last Flight of the Protostar, Part II
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Soong Method
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Soong_Method
======================================================================

An incomplete golem
The
Soong Method
was the process of creating a
golem
, a
synthetic
organic
android
that functioned near-identically to a biological
lifeform
. It was named after
Doctor
Altan Soong
, who created the technology in the late
24th century
.
In
2399
, the Soong Method was used to transfer the mind of
retired
Starfleet
Admiral
Jean-Luc Picard
into a new body, without the brain defect that had caused his Human body's death. After this initial success, the process was attempted several more times, though the success rate was so low that it was eventually all but abandoned. (
PIC
: "
Et in Arcadia Ego, Part 2
";
DIS
: "
Anomaly (DIS)
")
In
3190
, this process was used to create a new body for
Gray Tal
and ultimately
resurrect
him once Gray's consciousness was unjoined from
Tal
. (
DIS
: "
Anomaly (DIS)
", "
Choose to Live
")
See also
Synaptic fusion

Name The Winner
's sound effects man on
892-IV
Sound effects
were
sounds
created by a variety of techniques for the purpose of augmenting a
television program
or
movie
.
In
2268
, the
Empire TV
television program
Name the Winner
made use of sound effects. A man sitting behind the
camera
at a sound effects
booth
was
responsible
for providing them. (
TOS
: "
Bread and Circuses
")

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Space elevator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Space_elevator
======================================================================

Orbital station of the Noble Isle space elevator
Base of a Nezu orbital tether
A
space elevator
,
orbital lift
, or
orbital tether
was a cable that connected a
planet
's surface to a
space station
in
orbit
. A
mag-lev carriage
was a vehicle commonly used for moving along the tether.
Neelix
spent two years working on one-tenth scale prototype models of orbital tethers on
Rinax
.
A
Nezu
colony planet
in the
Delta Quadrant
had an orbital tether over three hundred
kilometers
long. Moving from the ground all the way to the station took twelve hours. When the planet came under bombardment by
Etanian
asteroids
in
2373
,
Tuvok
,
Neelix
, and several
Nezu
escaped the surface using the tether, rising above the interference in the
ionosphere
so that
USS
Voyager
could rescue them. (
VOY
: "
Rise
")
An orbital tether was located on
Koralis III
, in the
Koralis system
, but it had been abandoned by
2375
. (
DS9
: "
Chimera
")
Due to their
planet
Dulaine
's
thermosphere
blocking
transporters
, the
Dulainians
built a system of orbital lifts to enable transport to and from the surface. In
2381
, the
USS
Cerritos
was assigned to repair one of the lifts and host the stranded travelers in the interim. (
LD
: "
The Least Dangerous Game
")
The only way to access the surface of
Noble Isle
was a space elevator, which served as the planet's
starport
.
Thadiun Okona
described the technology as "a bit archaic", but necessary as the planet's
atmosphere
was otherwise impassable due to
ion storms
. In
2384
, the
USS
Protostar
docked there so as to conduct repairs on its
protostar drive
. (
PRO
: "
Masquerade
")
External link
Space elevator
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Space fun helmet
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Space_fun_helmet
======================================================================

A space fun helmet
Another space fun helmet
A
space
fun
helmet
was a type of
headgear
that was kept as something of collectible value during the late
24th century
.
Ensign
Beckett Mariner
had stashed a space fun helmet on the
USS
Cerritos
, along with many other
contraband
items. When the
ship
was being
boarded
by
Pakleds
in
2380
, she armed the
crew
with her contraband, with
Lieutenant Commander
Andy Billups
picking up the space fun helmet. (
LD
: "
No Small Parts
")
There was a
Collector's Guild
recruitment booth
on
Tulgana IV
in
2381
that had a space fun helmet on display along with
Data
-shaped
bubble bath
bottles
, a
Gorn
doll
and many other
collectibles
. (
LD
: "
Reflections
")
The original "Space Fun Helmet"
In the real world, the Space Fun Helmet was a
children's toy
released in
1976
made by Enco Industries. It came with a decal sticker sheet with the names "
Kirk
", "
Spock
", "
McCoy
", and "
Scott
", and the words "CREW", "Property of USS ENTERPRISE" and "Return to
Ward Room
." listed on the back of the helmet. Other than these details, the Space Fun Helmet had absolutely nothing to do with
Star Trek
whatsoever – that is, until its "official" appearance
forty-four
years
later, in
Star Trek: Lower Decks
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Space technology
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Space_technology
======================================================================

Space technology
was a level of
technological advancement
that allowed a
species
to enter
orbit
, achieve
spaceflight
, and reach out into and
explore
space
. More specifically this included those technologies used for creating and operating
artificial satellites
,
marker beacons
,
probes
,
spacecraft
, and
space stations
.
During the mid-
23rd century
,
Doctor
Richard Daystrom
's advances in
multitronics
, from the
M-1
through
M-4
, had purportedly culminated with the design of his
M-5 multitronic unit
in
2267
. According to
Commodore
Bob Wesley
, the M-5's ability to solve "
a series of routine
research
and
contact
problems, [...] plus
navigational maneuvers
and the
war games problem
,
" would ultimately realize a new "
revolution
in space technology as great as
warp drive
.
" (
TOS
: "
The Ultimate Computer
")
According to
Jean-Luc Picard
, the decision for the
Federation
to establish
first contact
was determined when a
society
reached a level of technology that indicated they were clearly about to initiate warp travel. As was the case of the
Malcorians
of
Malcor III
in
2367
. However, according to the Malcorian
Minister
Krola
, many of his people believed that the very same threshold was an indicator that they had perhaps "gone far enough," emphasizing how "
all these new ideas, new technology, and now space travel [..] confuses [and] frightens them.
"  (
TNG
: "
First Contact
")
In
2372
,
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
noted that, an unspecified number of
years
beforehand,
Starfleet
used a technology to assist deep space travel by keeping the
body
in
stasis
and providing an artificial
environment
, or
mental
landscape
, to keep the
mind
active and alert. (
VOY
: "
The Thaw
")
Related technologies
Aviation technology
Slipstream technology
Warp technology
External link
Space technology
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Spacematic
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Spacematic
======================================================================

Spacematic
Class:
Scout ship
Owner:
Cyrano Jones
Status:
Active
(
2268
)
The
Spacematic
was a one-man
scout ship
that was in service in the mid-
23rd century
. This ship was owned by
Cyrano Jones
, and was larger than a
Class F shuttlecraft
.
For seven years, from
2261
to
2268
, Cyrano Jones used the
Spacematic
for
asteroid
prospecting
and
trading
. Less than four
months
prior to
stardate
4523, the
Spacematic
was within the
Klingon Empire
's
sphere of influence
. The ship's
logs
were later accessed by
Arne Darvin
as part of an effort to divert attention from himself and
framing
Jones as a Klingon
spy
. The vessel visited
Space Station K-7
in 2268. (
TOS
: "
The Trouble with Tribbles
";
DS9
: "
Trials and Tribble-ations
")
Jones was later found piloting a
Federation scout ship
when he was rescued by the
USS
Enterprise
the
following year
. (
TAS
: "
More Tribbles, More Troubles
")
Background information
The
Spacematic
's type and activities were described in "The Trouble with Tribbles".
This ship's name was labeled on a
miniature studio model
seen in "Trials and Tribble-ations". This ship is presumed to be the same as Jones' scout ship.
[1]
The model was built by
Jason Kaufman
.
Greg Jein
commented: [Jones’ ship is] “not based on anything - it's just parts that Jason had in his toy bag”. (
The Magic of Tribbles: The Making of Trials and Tribble-ations
, p. 43)
Footage of the
Spacematic
can be seen in the
DS9 Season 5 DVD
's special features that look at the making of "Trials and Tribble-ations".
External link
Spacematic
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Spaceworthy
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Spaceworthy
======================================================================

A
spacecraft
that was
spaceworthy
was capable of
spaceflight
without malfunctioning.
In
2371
,
Miles O'Brien
examined the
specifications
Benjamin Sisko
brought from
Bajor
for a
Bajoran lightship
and felt they were not spaceworthy. He doubted such a ship could have traveled from Bajor to
Cardassia
. Sisko explained that he didn't want to
sail
for
years
, just build one and prove its spaceworthiness. O'Brien countered that he could do so with a
computer model
.
Later,
Jadzia Dax
questioned why Sisko made such a detailed
replica
, as it wasn't necessary to do so in order to test the the ship's spaceworthiness. Sisko agreed, but he still wanted everything to look right. (
DS9
: "
Explorers
")
In
2376
, though she still had a ways to go,
Tom Paris
assured
B'Elanna Torres
that he could make the ship
Alice
spaceworthy. (
VOY
: "
Alice
")
See also
Seaworthy

======================================================================
PAGE: Species 10026 vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Species_10026_vessel
======================================================================

A severely damaged Species 10026 vessel
Species 10026 vessels
were advanced
starships
utilized by
Species 10026
during the latter half of the
24th century
. These vessels were armed with
modulating phaser pulses
, which was considered by the
Borg
to be the vessel's tactical strength, as they were capable of penetrating Borg
deflector shielding
. The tactical weakness of these vessels was their lack of maneuverability.
Thirty-nine of these vessels engaged the two
Borg cubes
and
Borg Queen's ship
that attempted to
assimilate
the
Species 10026 homeworld
in
2375
. The Borg were able to adapt to these ship's weapons by
triaxillating
their shield geometry to absorb the phaser pulses, neutralizing the Species 10026 vessel's threat. (
VOY
: "
Dark Frontier
")
For more information on this
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Species 116 sentry vessel

URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Species_116_sentry_vessel
======================================================================

A
sentry vessel
was a type of
spacecraft
used by a species in the
Delta Quadrant
which the
Borg
knew as
Species 116
. These vessels were posted at specific locations to prevent the passage of the Borg into the territory of Species 116.
In
2374
,
Arturis
, a member of Species 116, told
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
and
Seven of Nine
that the sentry vessels belonging to his people had provided little defense when the Borg finally managed to invade his
homeworld
. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")

Species 6339 starships
The
Species 6339 starship

starship
utilized by
Species 6339
.
This class of vessel was equipped with twenty-two
phaser cannons
on
aft
section alone and was capable of overpowering an
Intrepid
-class
starship. (
VOY
: "
Infinite Regress
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Species 8472 bio-ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Species_8472_bio-ship
======================================================================

Species 8472 bio-ship
Owner:
Species 8472
Active:
2370s
Crew complement:
1
Armament:
1
directed energy weapon
Bio-ship
redirects here; for the general vessel type, please see
organic-based vessel
.
The
Species 8472 bio-ship
was a type of
organic-based vessel
used by
Species 8472
in large numbers during their
invasion
of the
Milky Way Galaxy
in
2373
. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
History
In
2373
, the
Borg
provoked a
war
with Species 8472 by invading
fluidic space
. Species 8472 defeated the Borg and launched their own counter-invasion of the Milky Way, sending hundreds of bio-ships through a string of
quantum singularities
in a region of space later nicknamed the "
Northwest Passage
".
Near the end of that year, the
USS
Voyager
discovered a single regenerating bio-ship amongst the debris of 15
Borg cubes
. The ship fired on
Voyager
, knocking it off-course before
Voyager
escaped. Many more bio-ships were detected as
Voyager
traveled deeper into Borg space. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
After forging an alliance with the Borg, the
Voyager
crew developed
nanoprobe
-based
bio-molecular warheads
effective against Species 8472.
Seven of Nine
then took
Voyager
into fluidic space to force a confrontation;
Voyager
successfully destroyed four bio-ships within fluidic space, and thirteen more after it returned to normal space. Faced with this weapon, the remaining bio-ships retreated to fluidic space en masse. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion, Part II
")
One damaged bio-ship was left behind in the
Delta Quadrant
. It was chased by a
Hirogen
hunting party for six months and eventually abandoned by its pilot above an
asteroid
. (
VOY
: "
Prey
")
Technical data
Bio-ship console
A bio-ship was composed of
genetically
similar organic material to Species 8472 and thus shared their resistance to
technology
and weakness to modified nanoprobes. The ship was highly resistant to conventional weaponry and could regenerate itself if damaged. The
hull
was also impervious to
sensors
and
tractor beams
. It also had energy shields. Each bio-ship was piloted by a single individual.
Bio-ships were armed with a single forward-mounted
directed energy weapon
powerful enough to destroy Borg cubes with a few shots. Eight bio-ships, directing their firepower into an
energy focusing ship
, were capable of destroying a
planet
. Equipped with an
antimatter
-based
warp
propulsion system
, the ship could operate both in its native medium of fluidic space and in the
vacuum
of normal space, and open quantum singularities to pass between the two. When in flight, the three fins on the bio-ship's rear section were extended.
Internally, the bio-ship had structures resembling flesh and bones. There was a network of
organic conduits
that carried
electrodynamic fluid
, possibly as an
energy
source, as well as a
binary matrix
laced with
neuropeptides
that may have functioned as a
computer core
. The bio-ship could dock to another vessel by "dissolving" through to its interior. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
", "
Scorpion, Part II
")
When first encountered by
USS
Voyager
in
2373
, it was identified as a
biomass
, and was impervious to
Federation
technology. It did not register as a
starship
on
sensors
, however, it did register as
organic
bioreadings
, but the sensors could not penetrate the
hull
. Furthermore, a
transporter lock
could not be established on the ship, nor did the
tractor beam
have an effect on it. It was first suggested by
Chakotay
of possibly being a
space-dwelling organism
or a
biological weapon
. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
Bio-ship interior supports
Bio-ship corridor (damaged)
Organic conduits
Binary matrix
Ships of the class
See:
Unnamed Species 8472 bio-ships
Appendices
Background information
The final draft of "
Scorpion
" gave the following description of this type of ship during the initial long-range viewing, "
…a small, organic-looking
BIO-MASS
on the [Borg Cube's] hull. It's bulbous and ashen. Strange.
" As they magnified the screen, "
We can now see what looks like the body of a "
squid
" attached to the hull…no
tentacle
…but it definitely looks like an organism of some kind.
"
The Species 8472 bio-ship concept art was designed a by
Steve Burg
and completed on
13 April
1997
. The design was inspired by "sea creatures", so noted in the script, and should be capable of latching on to a Borg ship and look good flying in formation. (
Star Trek Voyager: A Celebration
, p. 206) It was created as a
CGI
model built by
Foundation Imaging
's senior supervising visual effects animator,
Adam Lebowitz
. Their two forms of weaponry were devised by Visual Effects Supervisor
Ronald B. Moore
and Visual Effects Coordinator
Cheryl Gluckstern
. (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 29, No. 6/7, p. 95)
The bio-ship was featured in the
menu screen
of the
VOY Season 6 DVD
. However, Species 8472 does not appear in the
sixth season
of
Voyager
.
Apocrypha
In
Star Trek Online
, this ship is identified as a
Nicor
-class
cruiser
.
External link
Nicor
class
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Species 8472 energy focusing ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Species_8472_energy_focusing_ship
======================================================================

Lateral view of Species 8472 energy focusing ship
The
Species 8472 energy focusing ship
was a type of
organic
starship
used by
Species 8472
. Similar in configuration to the more numerous
bio-ship
, it was also capable of crossing between normal space and
fluidic space
using
quantum singularities
.
The energy-focusing ship could utilize the
energy weapon
discharges of eight other bio-ships to fire a powerful beam capable of destroying a
planet
. During Species 8472's invasion of the
Milky Way Galaxy
in
2374
, bio-ships and energy focusing ships were used to systematically annihilate
Borg
planets. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
", "
Scorpion, Part II
") During their
war
against the Borg, hundreds of planets were destroyed in this manner. (
VOY
: "
In the Flesh
")
Energy-focusing ship working in tandem with bio-ships
Close-up of energy-focusing ship
The energy-focusing ship's beam reaches
a planet
The planet's crust begins to split open
The plant's crust breaks apart
The planet's crust breaks open, destroying it
The planet's remains scatter through space
In
2375
, the energy focusing ship was displayed on "
Valerie Archer
"'s
desktop monitor
. (
VOY
: "
In the Flesh
")
Appendices
Background information
Dialogue in
VOY
: "
Scorpion
" did not distinguish between the energy focusing ship and the more common bio-ship, simply referring to them both as "bio-ships".
The manner in which this ship fires – multiple beams of energy converging into one large planet-destroying beam – bears a striking resemblance to that of the
Death Star
from the
Star Wars
films. When creating the weapons effect for
Star Trek: Voyager
however, Visual Effects Supervisor
Ronald B. Moore
was interested in doing something slightly different than the Death Star, finding the solution of the vessels converging and then cumulatively focusing their firepower to be "more interesting." (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 29, No. 6/7, p. 95)
While
Steve Burg
was designing the Species 8472 bio-ships, the producers notified him about the scenes demonstrating swarms of the ships. As a result, the bio-ships had to look good in formation. (
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
issue 43, p. 12)
After Moore and Visual Effects Coordinator
Cheryl Gluckstern
devised the converged beams, they were animated by
Greg Rainoff
at
Digital Magic
, via Harry animation. (
Cinefantastique
, Vol. 29, No. 6/7, p. 95)
Apocrypha
In
Star Trek: Armada II
, this type of vessel is classified as a 'frigate', and it is capable of using its energy-focusing ability while grouped with anywhere from four to eight other ships.
In
Star Trek Online
, this vessel is identified by name as the
Dactylus
-class, a variant of the
Nicor
-class
bioship.
External link
Dactylus
class
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Sphere-Builder vessels
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Sphere-Builder_vessels
======================================================================

Sphere-Builder vessel, type 1
Sphere-Builder vessel, type 2
Sphere-Builder vessels
were armed starships used by the
Sphere-Builders
while they attempted to invade the
Milky Way Galaxy
in a
possible alternate
26th century
, part of their arsenal that also included the
Delphic Expanse spheres
. In the pivotal
Battle of Procyon V
, three different vessel designs were employed by the Sphere-Builders, with none of them being able to survive counterattacks from
Federation
forces. (
ENT
: "
Azati Prime
")
In the final draft script of "Azati Prime", the second Sphere-Builder ship was described thus; "
Its bizarre hull configuration bears the same odd striations as the Spheres.
"
For information on previous uses of the first
studio model
-type, see:
Devore warship
; for information on previous uses of the second and third studio model-types, see:
Vissian starship
;
Kreetassan ship
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Spotlight
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Spotlight
======================================================================

A
spotlight
was a type of
lamp
used to illuminate
performers
and
athletes
. One who focused spotlights on others was called a
spotlight operator
.
In
2373
,
Quark
ordered the
computer
on
Deep Space 9
to turn on a spotlight in
Quark's Bar
. (
DS9
: "
Rapture
")
In
2376
, the
tsunkatse
arena
on
Penk's starship
was lit by spotlights. (
VOY
: "
Tsunkatse
")
Metaphorically
, to be in the spotlight was to be the subject of attention. In
2155
,
T'Pol
and
Trip Tucker
suggested that
Jonathan Archer
and
Enterprise
NX-01
should have received some of the attention being given to
Nathan Samuels
as the
Coalition of Planets
was taking shape. Archer declined, saying, "
Samuels likes the spotlight, and he's welcome to it.
" (
ENT
: "
Demons
")
In
2374
, following
Odo
's
singing
performance,
Vic Fontaine
asked the
changeling
how he liked being in the spotlight. Odo replied it was "
very bright
". When asked to elaborate, he said he supposed the lights in his eyes kept him from seeing the
audience
and being
nervous
. (
DS9
: "
His Way
")
External link
Spotlight
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Srivani vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Srivani_vessel
======================================================================

Two Srivani vessels leaving
Voyager
The
Srivani vessel
was a type of small
craft
used by the
Srivani
in the late-
24th century
. It was equipped with
stealth
technology
to render it invisible and intangible at a
phase variance
of 0.15.
In
2374
, two such vessels were docked with the
USS
Voyager
while the
crew
was unknowingly put through several
medical
experiments
. When
Voyager
began to approach a
binary
pulsar
, the two ships evacuated but one was destroyed. (
VOY
: "
Scientific Method
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Stabilizer beam
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Stabilizer_beam
======================================================================

Discovery
stabilizer beams orient the ship in a Klingon cavern
Stabilizer beams
were a type of
tractor beam
used by
ships
to maintain flight in an enclosed space like a
cavern
.
When the
USS
Discovery
used their
spore drive
to jump directly into an underground cavern on
Qo'noS
in
2257
,
Keyla Detmer
used the ship's stabilizer beams to compensate for the
planet
's
gravitational pull
and level the ship. (
DIS
: "
Will You Take My Hand?
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Standby
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Standby
======================================================================

Standby
was a
status
of readiness maintained by
technology
and/or
personnel
, indicating that it was available for use at a
moment
's notice.
In
2151
,
Malcolm Reed
said, "
Have the
launch bay
put
Shuttlepod 2
on standby
". (
ENT
: "
The Andorian Incident
")
See also
Standby alert
Standby crew

======================================================================
PAGE: Starship
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Starship
======================================================================

Silhouettes of several Earth and Federation starships
A
starship
was any manned
spacecraft
that was capable of viable
interstellar travel
, i.e. any manned spacecraft that could transport and support a
crew
across interstellar distances. Most starships accomplished this via the employment of some form of
faster-than-light
spacecraft
propulsion
technology
, such as
warp drive
.
Many times, starships were often
personified
as
female
by their crew. (
TOS
: "
Charlie X
",
etc.
) This is because that historically sailors, who were predominantly male, often developed a close bond with their ships, viewing them as a source of protection and sustenance during long voyages. This led to associating the ship with the nurturing and protective roles traditionally attributed to
women
.
In the
Federation
during the
22nd
and
23rd centuries
, spacecraft, such as those of the
Starship-class
, were identified almost exclusively as vessels operated by
Starfleet
, or as stated by
R.M. Merik
, "
a very special vessel and crew.
" (
TOS
: "
Bread and Circuses
")
Within the Federation, starships were viewed as having the role of protector within the civilian community, as starship
captains
were given a certain level of authority in the region they patrolled. (
TOS
: "
Mudd's Women
", "
The Conscience of the King
", "
The Trouble with Tribbles
")
Design was also a factor in defining a starship. The configuration of a vessel, as identified by
sensor
scans, could be used in identifying a starship. (
TOS
: "
The Doomsday Machine
")
Likewise, a starship could be identified on sensors by its
power
utilization curve, as a vessel utilizing 100% power could skew sensor readings. (
TOS
: "
Journey to Babel
")
Generally limited to the roles of exploration and defense, starships did not generally
transport
cargo
, unless in the case of an emergency, where they could take on any role. (
TOS
: "
The Cloud Minders
")
While discussing his service history with
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
, Captain
Montgomery Scott
noted that in his lifetime he had "served aboard
eleven ships
," including "
freighters
,
cruisers
, [and] starships." (
TNG
: "
Relics
")
By the mid-
24th century
, though, the exclusivity of the term "starship" had essentially fallen out of use, and was redefined as a term interchangeable with "
spaceship
" and "spacecraft". (
Star Trek: The Next Generation
,
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
,
Star Trek: Voyager
)
When the
Ferengi Marauder
Kreechta
was about to target the
USS
Enterprise
-D
, after interfering with the
battle simulation
Operation Lovely Angel
, the Ferengi ship's
sensor codes
were overridden and it was fooled to believe there was "another Federation ship" closing. When it was further revealed that the Federation ship was in fact "a starship," the Ferengi feared that they had been outmaneuvered and quickly retreated. (
TNG
: "
Peak Performance
")
Appendices
See also
Galactic starship design
Starship classification
List of classifications
List of starships
List of starship classes
Background information
In
The Original Series
, the term "starship" was seemingly synonymous with
Starship-class
, if not any reference made to any
other
Starfleet vessels. In at least two instances, the term was applied to two other non-
Constitution
-class vessels, namely the "old
Class J starship
" in "
The Menagerie, Part I
", and the
D7 class
in "
Elaan of Troyius
". Contrarily, at least two other alien ships were specifically said not to be starships, the
Orion scout ship
in "
Journey to Babel
", and the
Orion vessel
in "
The Pirates of Orion
".
External links
Starship
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works
Starship
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: State of the art
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/State_of_the_art
======================================================================

State of the art
or
cutting edge
referred to very modern
technology
.
In
2365
, the
Galaxy
-class
starship
was considered state of the art as the most sophisticated piece of machinery ever built. (
TNG
: "
Contagion
")
USS
Voyager
was considered state of the art upon being placed in service in
2371
. (
VOY
: "
Caretaker
")
External link
State of the art
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Status
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Status
======================================================================

A
status
was one's standing when compared with that of others or one's previous
situation
.
After the
incident
with
Professor
James Moriarty
aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-D
in early
2365
, two
engineering
officers
checked the status of
magnetic fuel lines
. (
TNG
: "
Elementary, Dear Data
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Storage
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Storage
======================================================================

Storage
was the putting away of something for later use.
In
2364
,
Jean-Luc Picard
told
Data
to theorize by asking, "
What would be the purpose of such an encasement?
", to which Data answered, "
Typically, the purpose of such an enclosure is for storage.
" (
TNG
: "
The Arsenal of Freedom
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Storage cell
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Storage_cell
======================================================================

A
storage cell
was a component of
Federation
starships
'
bussard ramscoops
. The storage cells were
responsible
for holding whatever interstellar material that had been collected by the ramscoops.
In
2375
,
Commander
Riker
ordered the ramscoops of the
USS
Enterprise
-E
utilized to collect
metreon gas
in the area of space known as "the
Briar Patch
," while attempting to elude two pursuing
Son'a battle cruisers
. Subsequent to the storage cells reaching maximum capacity, Riker ordered the ramscoops released, leaving a cloud of the gas between the
Enterprise
and the
Son'a
vessels, which ignited upon being fired into by one of the pursuing ships, in a tactic that
Lieutenant Commander
La Forge
dubbed "the
Riker Maneuver
." (
Star Trek: Insurrection
)

======================================================================
PAGE: Stress test
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Stress_test
======================================================================

A
stress test
was a test that determined how much strain a
starship
could withstand.
In
2365
,
Geordi La Forge
noted that a stress test indicated
Thadiun Okona
often pushed his ship the
Erstwhile
beyond what its design suggested it was capable of. (
TNG
: "
The Outrageous Okona
")
External link
Stress test
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Subdermal implant
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Subdermal_implant
======================================================================

A
subdermal implant
was a device that was
implanted
under the
skin
. They could have a variety of purposes.
Kuroda Lor-ehn
had subdermal implants installed on his wrists, designed to depolarize
triburnium
alloys
. This enabled him to escape his restraints on an
Enolian transport
in
2152
. (
ENT
: "
Canamar
")
During the
Occupation of Bajor
, members of the
Bajoran Resistance
had subdermal implants composed of
tritonium
isotopes
. In the event of capture, the implants could be activated to leave a trail that could be followed. In
2372
,
Major
Kira Nerys
used such a trail to find the survivors of the
Ravinok
. (
DS9
: "
Indiscretion
")
See also
Subdermal bioprobe
Subdermal transponder
Subdermal transmitter
External link
Subdermal implant
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Submolecular resequencer
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Submolecular_resequencer
======================================================================

A
submolecular resequencer
was a
matter
-conversion technology capable of altering an
organic
compound into a
crystalline
compound.
The
multiphasic chamber
constructed by the crew of the
USS
Equinox
in
2376
contained a submolecular resequencer and a
polaron grid
, for use in converting the remains of
nucleogenic lifeforms
into a source of power with which to augment their
warp engines
. (
VOY
: "
Equinox
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Subnucleonic device
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Subnucleonic_device
======================================================================

A
subnucleonic device
was a device of unknown design that could be used to damage
ore processors
.
In
2365
, a subnucleonic device was used to damage an ore processor on Level 21 of
Terok Nor
, taking it out of operation for two weeks.
Kira Nerys
of the
Bajoran Underground
claimed responsibility to causing the
sabotage
, but admitted to
Odo
in
2370
that her colleague actually damaged the processor, as her responsibility was to find
Vaatrik
's list of
Bajoran
conspirators – an action that ultimately resulted in Kira
murdering
Vaatrik. (
DS9
: "
Necessary Evil
")
It is unclear what a subnucleonic device looks like or how it functions. Kira claimed she could describe the device to Odo, but was never asked to.
See also
Subnucleonic beam
Subnucleonic radiation
Subnucleonic reaction

======================================================================
PAGE: Subspace flux isolator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Subspace_flux_isolator
======================================================================

Alternate timeline
(covers information from an
alternate timeline
)
A subspace flux isolator with an attached interphasic compensator (top)
A
subspace flux isolator
was a device invented by
Jake Sisko
in an
alternate timeline
. It was used to open up a path into
subspace
.
In
2422
, an aged Jake used this device aboard the
USS
Defiant
in an attempt to retrieve
his father
, who had been trapped in subspace
fifty years previously
. Timing his use of the isolator to coincide with a
subspace inversion
of the
Bajoran wormhole
, the device worked, but instead of pulling his father out of subspace, it drew him into a
subspace fragment
where he was able to interact with his father, due to a subspace link which had been created between them at the time of the initial accident. Jake was soon returned to normal space however, his attempt to rescue his father failing. The timeline in which these events occurred was negated thirty-eight years later when Jake committed suicide, thereby severing the link between them and sending his father
back in time
to the moment of the initial accident, where he was able to change history by avoiding the energy surge which pulled him into subspace in the first place. (
DS9
: "
The Visitor
")
While this was not stated in the episode, the device presumably had something to do with
subspace flux
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Subspace isolation field
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Subspace_isolation_field
======================================================================

The activation of the subspace force fields
A
subspace isolation field
(or
subspace force field
) was a localized
force field
that contained its own pocket of
subspace
.
In
2368
, most of the command crew of the
USS
Enterprise
-D
were able to use a subspace force field, in conjuction with a
phase discriminator
, to phase-shift themselves into the time continuum of the
Devidians
,
aliens
who were using a
synchronic distortion
with a positive displacement of .004 percent to conceal themselves. (
TNG
: "
Time's Arrow
")
The next year
,
Captain
Picard
suggested the use of subspace force fields to beam on board the
Enterprise
, which was trapped in a
temporal fragment
.
Geordi La Forge
agreed but noted that they needed an extremely sensitive phase discriminator for the plan to succeed. On the advice of
Lieutenant Commander
Data
, they used the
emergency transporter armbands
, which contained a sufficiently powerful discriminator. La Forge modified several armbands for use by the
away team
, enabling them to exist and interact in the fragment without being integrated into it's timeframe, essentially creating an artificial pocket of time around each member of the away team. (
TNG
: "
Timescape
")
See also
Life support belt
Subspace bubble

======================================================================
PAGE: Subspace modulator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Subspace_modulator
======================================================================

A
subspace modulator
was a piece of
subspace
technology
.
In
2372
,
Admiral
Leyton
ordered
Lieutenant
Arriaga
to secretly attach a subspace modulator to the
wormhole relay station
. The modulator caused the
Bajoran wormhole
to open at random, making it appear as though a
cloaked
Dominion
fleet could be traveling through it. This deception was part of Leyton's plan to impose
Starfleet
martial law over
Earth
to address the
Changeling
threat. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
")

======================================================================

Subytt freighters
were a type of
freighter
employed by the
Subytts
during the late
24th century
.
In late
2369
/early
2370
,
Quark
passed on to
Odo
information on a Subytt freighter that was
smuggling
defective
isolinear rods
to
Bajor
. Odo conducted an investigation of the freighter, and ultimately arrested the
first officer and two of the crew
. (
DS9
: "
The Homecoming
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Summoning beacon
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Summoning_beacon
======================================================================

An active summoning beacon
A
summoning beacon
was a
device
used by the
Ankari
to
summon
their "spirits of good fortune",
nucleogenic lifeforms
from another
dimension
.
After being stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
in
2371
, the
USS
Equinox
received supplies from the Ankari and witnessed the summoning beacon being used. The
Equinox
crew purchased their own beacon to summon the lifeforms for study, to accidentally discover that the beings could be used to enhance their
warp drive
.
In early
2376
, after the
Equinox
escaped the
USS
Voyager
, the
Voyager
crew contacted the Ankari for assistance. In
Voyager
's
cargo bay
, an Ankari used the summoning beacon and acted as an intermediary between
Kathryn Janeway
and the aliens. (
VOY
: "
Equinox
", "
Equinox, Part II
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Superconductor magnet
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Superconductor_magnet
======================================================================

A superconductor magnet held by Wesley Crusher
A
superconductor magnet
was an extremely powerful
magnet
.
In
2365
, the
allasomorph
Salia
correctly identified a superconductor magnet that
Wesley Crusher
was carrying as he walked past her on the
USS
Enterprise
-D
. She warned Crusher to be careful with the device as it could, "
rip the
iron
right out of your
blood
cells
.
" (
TNG
: "
The Dauphin
")
The superconductor prop was later reused as the
spatial trajector matrix
in
VOY
: "
Prime Factors
".

======================================================================
PAGE: Swarm species vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Swarm_species_vessel
======================================================================

Swarm species vessel
For the Ancient Ones' vessel, please see
Swarm ship
.
The
Swarm species vessel
was a vessel used by the
Swarm species
to protect their space from foreign vessels.
The
USS
Voyager
encountered numerous Swarm vessels in
2373
, while attempting to cross their space. Initially, a single vessel was detected leaving a freighter belonging to a
Mislenite
named
Chardis
. This vessel disengaged
Voyager
's
stealth shield modifications
and alerted other Swarm vessels to the presence of
Voyager
.
Rather than attacking directly with weaponry, the tactic of Swarm vessels against larger vessels was to literally 'swarm' a ship, simultaneously draining its energy and partially crushing it, disabling vital systems. Members of their species would attempt to
transport
on board the vessel under attack.
The ships were
shuttle
-sized, with a weakness being their connectivity with other Swarm vessels. If a
feedback pulse
was fired at Swarm vessels, other nearby vessels would also be damaged. (
VOY
: "
The Swarm
")
The Swarm ships had to be designed from scratch by the effects team of
Star Trek: Voyager
. Mitch Suskin described the vessels as "little trilobyte ships." He continued by recalling, "
Rick Sternbach
[senior illustrator] provided us with the basic design, and Ron Thornton carried it forward.
" (
Star Trek Monthly
issue 30
, p. 104)

======================================================================
PAGE: Switch
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Switch
======================================================================

A
switch
was a
device
used for turning something on or off.
In
2268
, as instructed by
Captain
James T. Kirk
to find a local
radio station
, contact the
USS
Enterprise
, and have themselves beamed aboard,
Spock
and
Leonard McCoy
flipped a few switches so that  the
Enterprise
could answer. (
TOS
: "
A Piece of the Action
")
External link
Switch
at
Wikipedia

in speed, firepower and defenses.

Harvester
weapon
in

. (

stub
.

expanding it
.

script
, the

, 3rd ed., p. 489, and
StarTrek.com

weapons
systems included
torpedoes
.

2373

when it was infected by a
macrovirus
, though
Captain
Kathryn Janeway
managed to convince them to cease their attack as a cure had been found in time. (

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Takret starship

Takret starship
Affiliation:
Takrets
Active:
22nd century
Speed:
Warp 7
The
Takret starship
was a type of
warp-capable
vessel
used by the
Takret
in the
22nd century
. It was probably capable of at least
warp 7
.
The starship that docked and boarded
Enterprise
NX-01
in
2152
had three
impulse engines
on its back, and two nacelles coming out of its front. A roll bar also protruded from its top, as well as what appears to be two
Bussard collectors
.
It was much smaller than
Enterprise
, and the
Enterprise
crew far outnumbered them. (
ENT
: "
The Catwalk
")
No physical description of this craft was written in the final draft script of "The Catwalk".

======================================================================
PAGE: Tamarian deep space cruiser
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tamarian_deep_space_cruiser
======================================================================

Tamarian deep space cruiser
Affiliation:
Tamarian
Type:
Cruiser
Active:
24th century
Armament:
Phasers
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Tamarian deep space cruiser
was an advanced
starship
-
cruiser
design utilized by the
Tamarians
in the
24th century
.
History
Between
2268
and
2368
, Tamarian starships had encountered Federation vessels seven times. Each meeting was without incident, but formal relations were never established because
communication
was not possible. The last encounter occurred on
stardate
35056.4 with the
SS
Shiku Maru
.
In 2368, a
Tamarian deep space cruiser
arrived at
El-Adrel IV
where they began transmitting a
subspace
signal toward
Federation space
. Three
weeks
later they were approached by the Federation starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
After a failed attempt at communication, the
captains
of both
starships
were beamed to the surface of El-Adrel IV, whereafter, the Tamarians projected their particle scattering field around the planet. The
Enterprise
-D was finally able to disable the polarity coil generator of the Tamarian starship before the two ships exchanged phaser fire.
In the brief exchange between the two vessels, the Tamarian ship inflicted damage that resulted in the loss of forty-eight percent of the
Enterprise
-D's
starboard
shields and a complete loss of its forward shields. In the
Enterprise
-D's retaliatory attack, it caused no damage to the Tamarian vessel. A second volley from the Tamarians completely disabled the shields of the
Enterprise
-D. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
Specifications
Information on this class was sparse. Tamarian deep space cruisers were armed with
phasers
and were comparable in size, but superior in power, to that of a
Federation
Galaxy
-class
starship.
Tamarian
technology
allowed Tamarian starships to create a
particle scattering field
in the
ionosphere
of a
planet
. By projecting a
particle sustaining beam
into the upper
atmosphere
of said planet, they could effectively inhibit
transporter
and
communication
technology to or from the planet's surface. The field was created with a
polarity coil generator
, which was located on Tamarian starships in a heavily
shielded
section, aft of the
warp drive
. (
TNG
: "
Darmok
")
Interior design
Interior,
bridge
Appendices
Background information
The name "Tamarian deep space cruiser" appeared on an
okudagram
appearing at the beginning of the episode. According to the script, the Tamarian starship, was described as "formidable looking," a "mysterious, powerful-looking vessel," and "slightly more robust" than the
Enterprise
-D.
The scenes on the Tamarian deep cruiser bridge were filmed on
Paramount Stage 9
on Thursday,
25 July
1991
.
For information about the history and design of this model, please see:
Studio models
. The
Star Trek Customizable Card Game
gives its name as
Tama
and calls it a
Darmok
class ship.
A visual effects model of this ship was auctioned off on the
40 Years of Star Trek: The Collection
at Christie's.
[1]
(X)
External link
Darmok
-class
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

" was the
Federation
spacecraft ID
for the
Tamarian deep space cruiser
commanded by

.

, this vessel arrived at
El-Adrel IV
where they began transmitting a
subspace
signal toward
Federation space
. Three
weeks
later they were approached by the Federation starship
USS
Enterprise
-D
.
After a failed attempt at communication, the
captains
of both
starships
were beamed to the surface of El-Adrel IV, where after, the Tamarians projected their particle scattering field around the planet. The
Enterprise
-D was finally able to disable the
polarity coil generator
of the Tamarian vessel before the two ships exchanged
phaser
fire.
In the brief exchange between the two vessels, the Tamarian ship inflicted damage that resulted in the loss of forty-eight percent of the
Enterprise
-D's
starboard
shields and a complete loss of its forward shields. In the
Enterprise
-D's retaliatory attack, it caused no damage to the Tamarian vessel. A second volley from the Tamarians completely disabled the shields of the
Enterprise
-D. (

")

okudagram
appearing in
the episode.

at

======================================================================
PAGE: Tandaran patrol ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tandaran_patrol_ship
======================================================================

Tandaran patrol ship
Operator:
Tandarans
Type:
Patrol ship
Active:
22nd century
Armament:
Pulse cannons
Tandaran patrol ships
were a type of
patrol ship
utilized by the
Tandarans
during the mid-
22nd century
.
Enterprise
was attacked by two vessels of this class in circa
2151
, while attempting to rescue
Jonathan Archer
and
Travis Mayweather
from Tandaran
Detention Complex 26
. Despite being outnumbered,
Enterprise
,
under the command
of
Sub-Commander
T'Pol
, was able to inflict enough damage to these vessels to force them to land on the
planet
's surface for repairs. (
ENT
: "
Detained
")
In the final draft script of "Detained",
Grat
instead referred to these vessels as "interceptors" (although they were, elsewhere in the teleplay, referred to as "patrol ships"). They were not physically described in the script but Grat additionally spoke about them being capable of
warp drive
. Though the two ships immediately open fire on
Enterprise
in the episode, they were scripted as giving
Enterprise
a final warning via
audio
; T'Pol responded that the patrol ships were outmatched by
Enterprise
. Ironically, during the ensuing battle, the patrol ships were more persistent than they are in the final version of the installment, one of the pair even attacking
Shuttlepod 2
as it descended. The other patrol ship continued its offensive against
Enterprise
but was ignored by the
starship
, which opted to target the engines of the lead patrol ship, causing it to explode.
