PAGE: Tandaran transport
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tandaran_transport
======================================================================

The
Tandaran transport
was a type of
transport
vessel in use by the
Tandarans
in the mid-
22nd century
.
One such ship visited
Detention Complex 26
every sixty days. (
ENT
: "
Detained
")
In ultimately omitted dialogue from the final draft script of "Detained",
Malcolm Reed
mentioned a Tandaran transport, while disguised as a
Suliban
and questioned by Tandaran
Major
Klev
; Reed lied that he had arrived "on the last transport," in an attempt to explain why he looked unfamiliar to Klev.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tank
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tank
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
Ekosian tanks in a newsreel.
German Jagdtigers in front of the White House.
A Panzer IV tank targeted by a
Na'kuhl soldier
...
...and tanks shown in the reset time stream.
A Sherman tank operating in the
mirror universe
For the type of tank which contains liquid, please see
tank (liquid)
.
A
tank
was a type of fighting
land vehicle
, typically fully tracked. Consisting of heavy
armor
and a large
cannon
, a tank was often a critical part of a land battle.
The moniker "tank" originated from the World War I British code name for the vehicle, the liquid-containing
tank
.
The
Germans
were known for their Panzer (German for "armor") tank divisions during
World War II
. (
VOY
: "
The Killing Game
")
In an
alternate
World War II
where the Germans occupied the Eastern
United States of America
,
Alicia Travers
mentioned to
Captain
Jonathan Archer
in
1944
, that people had fled
Manhattan
,
New York City
, as tanks rolled up
Flatbush Avenue
, eventually spreading into
Manhattan
. A
modulating plasma pulse
brought to that time from the future by the
Na'kuhl
, was capable of destroying a
Panzer IV
tank. Additionally, two German
Jagdtigers
were seen parked on the lawn in front of the
White House
.
(
ENT
: "
Storm Front
")
Several
Renault FT
tanks in action during
World War I
were shown in the
time stream
as it reset, as were a group of
Chinese
tanks advancing on a protesting person on
Tiananmen Square
. (
ENT
: "
Storm Front, Part II
")
The Jagdtigers scene is an entirely
CGI
effect created at
Eden FX
, whereas the tanks in the reset time stream was footage, taken from a
Tiananmen Square protests of 1989
newsreel, of the more modern
Chinese
made
Type 59 tanks
.
On the
planet
Ekos
, the
Ekosian Wehrmacht
also used tanks for warfare against the
Zeons
, who were the victims of prejudice on that world, since they were
colonists
from the planet
Zeon
. (
TOS
: "
Patterns of Force
")
In the Ekosian newsreel (actually a historical newsreel,
showing
the advance through either Belgium or northern France during the May 1940 Blitzkrieg campaign in the West), the third tank featured is the historical
Panzer I
, whereas the foreground two are its derivative, the
SdKfz 265 Panzerbefehlswagen
, both German-made and used in the pre-, and early stages of World War II.
In the
mirror universe
, at least three different types of tanks were used on
Earth
during that
planet
's
20th century
.
(
ENT
: "
In A Mirror, Darkly
"
opening credits
)
The mirror universe tanks were, in order of appearance, the World War I-era
French
-made Renault FT, the US made
M4 Sherman
, also used in World War II and beyond, and ultimately the even more modern
USSR
-made
T-62 main battle tank
.
In
2381
, in the
holonovel
called
Crisis Point II: Paradoxus
, there was a
chase sequence
in which the
USS Cerritos
crew
were
driving
gravcycles
in pursuit of
Romulans
that were driving some sort of
hovertank
. (
LD
: "
Crisis Point 2: Paradoxus
")
External link
Tank
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Tantalus field
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tantalus_field
======================================================================

Mirror universe
(
coexisting on another dimensional plane
)
A typical Tantalus field control panel, with victim on screen
"
In my cabin is a device that will make
you
invincible.
"
-
Captain
James T. Kirk
, "
Mirror, Mirror
"
The
Tantalus field
was a
23rd century
device found in the
mirror universe
, which could be used to monitor and eliminate enemies from existence with the touch of a button.
History
Tantalus field control panel
Captain
James T. Kirk
owed his success against a number of enemies to the Tantalus field, and the
unknown alien scientist
whose lab he plundered for it. He used it to assassinate Captain
Christopher Pike
.
In the novel
Dark Victory
, the "alien scientist" in question is
Balok
; however, in
The Greater Good
, a short story from the
Shards and Shadows
anthology, the "alien" is actually
Simon Van Gelder
. In
The Sorrows of Empire
, Grand Admiral
Garth of Izar
mentions that it was "probably something he (Kirk) found in Doctor
Adams
's house of horrors on the
Tantalus Colony
".
The field was apparently a secret from everyone except
Marlena Moreau
, and to others it seemed that Kirk's enemies had a tendency to "simply disappear." The field was used by Moreau to save the
prime Kirk
when he was trapped in the mirror universe. Before leaving the ISS
Enterprise
, the prime Kirk attempted to convince
Spock
to reform the
Terran Empire
before it collapsed as a result of its own brutality. When Spock expressed doubts about his ability to survive if he attempted dissent from imperial policy, Kirk revealed the existence of the Tantalus field to him, telling him it would make him invincible. Kirk apparently hoped that Spock could survive long enough to change the Empire for the better. (
TOS
: "
Mirror, Mirror
")
Another of these devices was installed in
Emperor
Philippa Georgiou
's quarters, where she used it to monitor the actions of
Michael Burnham
.
(
citation needed
•
edit
)
(
DIS
: "
Terra Firma, Part 2
")
Appendices
Background information
In a story planned for an unrealized
William Shatner
guest appearance on
Star Trek: Enterprise
, it would have been revealed that the Tantalus field did not kill its victims, but transported them to a different time. In the story, it would have been revealed that, sometime after the events of "
Mirror, Mirror
", Spock had used the device on Kirk, depositing him on a remote planet in the 2120s, where he would have later been discovered by the crew of the
Enterprise
NX-01
in the 2150s, shortly before the "mirror universe" was to have diverged from the standard
Trek
universe as an
alternate timeline
.
[1]
(X)
The device was never seen or mentioned in any of the other "mirror" episodes in
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
or
Star Trek: Enterprise
. An identical control-panel device was seen in the background behind
Jane Wyatt
as Spock's mother
Amanda Grayson
in "
Journey to Babel
", and probably not meant to depict the Tantalus Field as seen in "
Mirror, Mirror
".
Apocrypha
In the novel
Dark Mirror
by
Diane Duane
,
Jean-Luc Picard
looks up Mirror
Spock
's history, and finds that he managed, briefly, to become a very powerful and influential figure in the Empire, largely because his enemies had a way of "disappearing" when they became too threatening.
In
The Sorrows of Empire
, Spock takes possession of the device after he slays Captain Kirk. Spock was able to use it with more frightening efficiency, as he was able to wipe out the entire crew of a Klingon battle cruiser
and
a Romulan Bird-of-Prey within a year of taking control of it. These incidents – plus the "disappearance" of
Uhura
for plotting a mutiny against Spock – lead to rumors that Spock is unleashing some previously-unknown Vulcan psionic power. The Tantalus field's power fuels a rapid rise through the imperial ranks that, by 2277, culminates with his becoming Emperor of the Terran Empire. In 2293, after announcing the foundation of a Terran Republic, Spock – knowing the Republic would be short-lived – destroys the device with a phaser to prevent another tyrant from taking possession of it.
A version of the Tantalus field appears in the prime universe in
Star Trek Online
. A player ship equipped with all four pieces of the "Delta Alliance Assault" set (shield, deflector, impulse engine, and either a singularity core for Romulan ships or a warp core for all others) can deploy a "Prototype Tantalus Field", which immediately destroys any targetable torpedoes (heavy plasma torpedoes for instance), mines, or fighter craft in a radius around the player's ship.
External link
Tantalus field
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Tarellian starship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tarellian_starship
======================================================================

Tarellian starship
Owner:
Tarella
Operator:
Tarellians
Type:
Transport
Active:
2360s
Speed:
Warp capable
Tarellian starships
(also referred to as
Tarellian plague ships
) were
vessels
used as late as the
2360s
to carry
Tarellian
refugees, following the
Tarellian Civil War
. These ships were equipped with
warp drive
and
transporter
technology.
Nearly every
star system
that the vessels entered either turned them away or destroyed them, through fear of spreading the
Tarellian plague
. By the early
2360s
, there was only one of these ships known to still exist.
In
2364
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
encountered the last surviving vessel, and its sparse crew. On board the
Enterprise
-D at the time,
Wyatt Miller
joined the last of the Tarellian crew, hoping that he would, some
day
, find a cure for their
plague
. (
TNG
: "
Haven
")
Background information
According to the script, this vessel was described as "
a ship of strange, unearthly design. Its center is a shiny globe. Dozens of spike-like structures jut out of it, tumbling end over end as the vessel moves along. Though clearly alien, there is a pleasing aesthetic quality to the ship, which gives off a soft, violet glow.
" Furthermore, according to the script, this ship was built at a base located three
miles
below sea-level.
For more information on this design and history of this model, please see:
Studio models
.

The Taresian starship defends the USS

The

.
A ship of this type defended the
USS
Voyager
against three
Nasari
Nerada
-type
starships
that attacked them while in
orbit
of the
planet
Taresia
. (
VOY
: "
Favorite Son
")
This vessel was identified by
Lyris
as "the Taresian starship", and was later described by
Chakotay
as a "patrol ship."
For more information on this
studio model
, see
DS9 studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Targ scoop
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Targ_scoop
======================================================================

A
targ scoop
was a device used on the front of
Klingon
ground assault vehicles
to prevent collisions with
targ
herds
. By emitting a high-
frequency
tone, the
animals
were dispersed away from the path of the vehicle.
In
2371
,
Lieutenant
B'Elanna Torres
used the idea of the targ scoop when modifying the
main deflector
of the
USS
Voyager
to emit an
inverted magnetic pulse
, in order to disperse a pursuing flock of
space dwelling life forms
. (
VOY
: "
Elogium
")

======================================================================

Target practice
was the
goal
of firing at
targets
to hone one's
skills
.
In
2151
, during target practice,
Hoshi Sato
's
hit-to-miss ratio
with a
phase pistol
was below fifty percent, something
Malcolm Reed
noted. (
ENT
: "
Sleeping Dogs
")
External link
Target practice
at
Wikipedia

2

Tarkalean freighters
were
starships
utilized by the
Tarkaleans
during the
22nd century
. They had two
decks
and carried at least twenty-two
crewmembers
.
In
2153
,
Enterprise
NX-01
received an
automated
distress call
requesting immediate assistance from a Tarkalean freighter that was under attack by the
Borg
-
assimilated
Arctic One
. Two survivors were rescued; the rest were killed or assimilated in the attack. (
ENT
: "
Regeneration
")
The crew complement is inferred from the number of people aboard
Arctic One
(twenty-nine, including nine
Humans
) plus the two rescued by
Enterprise
.

studio model
, please see
ENT studio models
.

. She would later describe it as

that she had encountered a similar device on a Tarkalean vessel. (

")

======================================================================
PAGE: Technology Future
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Technology_Future
======================================================================

Henry Starling on the cover of Technology Future.
Technology Future
was a
magazine
published in the late
20th century
on
Earth
.
Henry Starling
was featured on the cover of at least one issue of Technology Future. The cover of said issue stated "Starling does it again! - Inside the
Hyperpro 2000
".
That issue also featured articles about a breakthrough in chips (
optical
next?) and stated that "
Newton
works!" (
VOY
: "
Future's End
")
The
Star Trek Encyclopedia
, 3rd ed., p. 500 entry for the magazine showed an alternative cover. This cover featured the same (or very similar) photo, and the same head articles, but the other articles advertised on the front page were different. They were "
Chronowerx
triumph", "
Mac
is back!", and "Heuristic Algorithms in Urbana". The article also specified that it was the December
1995
issue.

cruiser

Onizuka
, was able to be transported from
Risa
to the

-D
by a Teldarian cruiser when the
Enterprise
was ordered to the
Kriosian system
. La Forge would have had to stay much longer on Risa had the Teldarian ship not picked him up. (
TNG
: "
The Mind's Eye
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Temporal compensator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Temporal_compensator
======================================================================

A temporal compensator
A
temporal compensator
was a device invented in
2376
by
a species
native to
Gotana
, a unique planet in the
Delta Quadrant
possessed of a
tachyon
core
, for use in overcoming the natural
temporal
differential
between the
planet
and the surrounding
space
.
Due to the powerful
temporal field
produced by the planet's tachyon core, for every 1.03
seconds
which passed in normal
space
, nearly a day was experienced on the planet. When two
astronauts
boarded the
USS
Voyager
, which had become trapped in the surrounding
tachyon field
, they found that their
physiologies
had a difficult time adjusting to the differential, and
Terrina
, the
commander
of the mission, died as a result.
Gotana-Retz
, the
pilot
, subsequently returned to the surface and, after approximately one-and-a-half years, returned with the compensator, which allowed him to exist in
Voyager
's time frame without actually leaving his own, but only for a few minutes.
After assisting the
Voyager
crew in leaving
orbit
of the planet, Gotana said his goodbyes, and watched
Voyager
leave from the surface, an old man. (
VOY
: "
Blink of an Eye
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Temporal setting
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Temporal_setting
======================================================================

Hologram Janeway's temporal settings.
Temporal settings
were systems used to track and monitor the passage of
time
on a
starship
.
In
2383
,
Hologram Janeway
's temporal settings on the
USS
Protostar
were disrupted by an approaching
tachyon storm
. When the ship was fractured into several parallel pockets of
oscillating
time moving at different speeds, Hologram Janeway was able to move between them by adjusting her temporal settings, aiding the crew in fixing the
anomaly
by passing information and
schematics
between them. (
PRO
: "
Time Amok
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Terellian cargo freighter
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Terellian_cargo_freighter
======================================================================

Terellian cargo freighter
Owner:
Terellian
Type:
Cargo
freighter
Active:
24th century
A
Terellian cargo freighter
was a type of
cargo
freighter
utilized by the
Terellians
during the
24th century
. To the trained eye, they could be readily identified by their interior design. The cargo module utilized rechargeable
power cells
to function. They also carried a large supply of
microfusion flares
.
Many years prior to
2370
, a Terrellian freighter
crash landed
with a single
Human
survivor named
Anna
. After seven years stranded on the planet, she was rescued.
Some time later, several years prior to 2370, the
Iyaarans
of
Iyar
discovered the remains of one of these freighters on a
class M planet
, containing Anna's
logs
, giving them a glimpse into Human culture.
In 2370, the freighter was part of a simulation in which the Iyaaran
Ambassador
Voval
explored the concept of
love
with
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
by appearing as Anna. According the ruse, the ship crashed on
Stardate 40812
, seven years prior. (
TNG
: "
Liaisons
")
Width of the cargo area
Power cell placement in bulkhead
Background information
According to the
script
, this set was described as a "cargo freighter". Its interior incorporated "
[..] a long, tunnel-like room, with a single door along one side. A few half-charred consoles can be seen...and several
cargo containers
, which are nothing more than shapes in the dim light. The room is
CANTED
slightly, as if it's been in a crash.
"
Preliminary sketch
Concept art
for this vessel was designed by
Rick Sternbach
with preliminary sketches drawn in
July 1993
, where it was described as a "Crashed Cargo Vessel".
Of note, it was indicated that the forward section was a "Smashed Impulse Towship (Possibly less damaged than indicated, most confined to nose.)" The forward section was connected to "Cargo Modules" said to "reflect set wall design (see blueprints)." Other components pointed out included the freighter's "Structural Spine" and "Broken Warp Pods". (
Star Trek: The Next Generation - The Continuing Mission
, p. 197)

======================================================================
PAGE: Termination implant
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Termination_implant
======================================================================

The last stages of the termination implant
The
termination implant
was a device attached to the
brain stem
of
Vorta
operatives of the
Dominion
. It was triggered by hand with a
gesture
that applied pressure with the index finger behind the right earlobe and the
thumb
under the
jaw
. When the device was activated (voluntarily) by the owner, it caused near-instant, painful death (though the
Founders
told the Vorta that death would be painless, this was proven not entirely true). Its purpose was to prevent Vorta – and the strategic information they possessed – from falling into enemy hands.
During his attempt to
defect
to the
Federation
in
2375
,
Weyoun 6
activated his termination implant in order to force the
Jem'Hadar
away from the
USS
Rio Grande
, the
runabout
he was aboard with
Odo
. (
DS9
: "
Treachery, Faith and the Great River
")
Presumably
Yelgrun
had a termination implant as well, but for some reason decided not to activate it. It was known that
Keevan
chose not to commit suicide.
See also
Neuro-depolarizing device
Suicide gland

======================================================================
PAGE: Terrellian ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Terrellian_ship
======================================================================

Irina's ship and the
Delta Flyer
in
Voyager
's
hangar
Irina's ship
was a
Terrellian
ship which took part in the first
Antarian Trans-stellar Rally
in
2377
. It was co-piloted by
Irina
and
Joxom
. The ship was more maneuverable than the
Delta Flyer
, though it still lost to the
Flyer
in an impromptu race.
Overloading the ship's
power transformer
could result in
nyocene gas
leaking into the cabin. Repairing it could require a
veridium injector
. The ship's power and propulsion systems also utilized
EPS relays
,
impulse manifolds
,
reactor coils
, and
injector ports
.
During the Antarian Trans-stellar Rally, a
phase inverter
interfaced with the ship's
shield generators
caused Joxom's console to explode, leading
Harry Kim
to take over as Irina's co-pilot. Near the end of the race, Kim's console exploded, having been
sabotaged
by Irina who was revealed to be the
terrorist
that had caused Joxom's "accident". Shortly afterward, Kim discovered she had also sabotaged the
fuel converter
she had provided the
Delta Flyer
. (
VOY
: "
Drive
")
Ship interior
Piloting controls
For more information on the
studio model
, see
VOY studio models
.

The
Tesnian starship
was a type of
ship

. They were equipped with
escape pods

crew
complement of 34.

2151

sensor array
and
navigational systems
malfunctioned during an attempted docking with
Enterprise
NX-01
. Fortunately, the crew managed to get to their
escape pods
in time. (
ENT
: "
Shuttlepod One
")

tether
was a
rope
or
cable

movement
.

,

ceiling
of the
Columbia
NX-02
's
launch bay
. (

: "
Divergence
")

Tether
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Tether coupling
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tether_coupling
======================================================================

A
tether coupling
was a device that held a
mag-lev carriage
in position on an
orbital tether
, using
magnetic fields
. If the couplings failed, the carriage would fall to the ground. (
VOY
: "
Rise
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Tezra
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tezra
======================================================================

Tezra
Class:
Freighter
Affiliation:
Tellarite
Status:
Destroyed
(
2140s
)
Tezra
was a
Tellarite freighter
owned and operated by
Tellarite
Captain
Skalaar
and his brother and
first officer
Gaavrin
, operating under the authority of the
Tellarite Mining Consortium
in the
22nd century
.
Tezra
was the first
ship
of her class, and the fastest ever built, as Skalaar
retrofitted
her
engines
himself.
Tezra
was able to haul one million
metric tons
of
cargo
at a maximum speed of
Warp 4.5
, something even the most powerful
Earth
vessels could not match.
Tezra
was seized by the
Klingon Empire
after Skalaar crossed into
Klingon
territory while hauling
firesalt
, believing he could shave a few days off his run by cutting across an insignificant
sector
. The ship and its cargo were
confiscated
, something Gaavrin never forgave his brother for.
In ultimately omitted dialogue from the final draft script of "
Bounty
", these events were described as having taken place "several years" prior to
2153
.
Believing
Tezra
impounded, Skalaar turned to
bounty hunting
in order to pay the
impound fee
. It was not until his last mission, in 2153, that it was revealed by the still bitter Gaavrin that
Tezra
had been scrapped for its parts by the Klingons several years earlier, ending Skalaar's dream of redeeming himself. (
ENT
: "
Bounty
")
This ship was only mentioned in dialogue.

======================================================================
PAGE: Thalaron generator
CATEGORIES: Technology, Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Thalaron_generator
======================================================================

The thalaron generator on board the
Scimitar
A
thalaron generator
was a device designed to emit a
cascading biogenic pulse
with which to destroy all
organic
matter
in the target area.
Shinzon
's co-conspirator,
Senator
Tal'aura
, utilized a small, hand-held thalaron generator in
2379
to assassinate the
Romulan Senate
, in order to facilitate Shinzon's appropriation of the
Praetor
position. Later, Shinzon planned to use the
Reman
Warbird
Scimitar
, which itself was, essentially, a massive thalaron generator, to destroy all life on
Earth
, thereby crippling the
Federation
. Shinzon was stopped by the crew of the
USS
Enterprise
, who were able to destroy the
Scimitar
before he was able to utilize the weapon. (
Star Trek Nemesis
)
In
2401
, a thalaron generator was stored on
Daystrom Station
. (
PIC
: "
The Bounty
")
According to the non-
canon
short story "Twilight's Wrath" in
Tales of the Dominion War
, the thalaron generator had been developed by the
Tal Shiar
during the
Dominion War
and had been stolen by Shinzon.
See also
Romulan technology
Thalaron radiation
External link
Thalaron generator
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

PAGE: Thasian ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Thasian_ship
======================================================================

Thasian ship
Affiliation:
Thasians
Active:
23rd century
Speed:
Warp capable
The
Thasian ship
was a
space
-faring vessel utilized by the
non-corporeal
Thasians
. Apparently
energy
-based, the ship took on the appearance of an indistinct, green mass of light.
In
2266
, this ship intercepted the
starship
USS
Enterprise
to retrieve
Charlie Evans
, a young man whom the Thasians had given
psychokinetic
abilities which they themselves also possessed. The Thasian ship was scheduled to take him back to their
homeworld
of
Thasus
, due to Charlie and his powers having proved dangerous to others. (
TOS
: "
Charlie X
")
The original effect used for the ship
In the final draft script of "Charlie X", the Thasian ship was described as "a transparent blob [...] ...
Ectoplasmic
.
"

======================================================================
PAGE: The Arm of Hephaestus
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/The_Arm_of_Hephaestus
======================================================================

The Arm of Hephaestus
The Arm of Hephaestus
was a
humanoid
automaton
seen to be worked on by
Leonardo da Vinci
in the
Leonardo da Vinci's workshop
holoprogram
. It was named after
Hephaestus
,
god
of the
forge
.
It was designed to aid
blacksmiths
by swinging a
hammer
,. He used
goose grease
to improve its
clockwork
mechanism. (
VOY
: "
Scorpion
")
Leonardo is in reality indeed known to have worked on
a robot
, although not this one.

======================================================================
PAGE: Thermal pulse ignition charge
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Thermal_pulse_ignition_charge
======================================================================

A
thermal pulse ignition charge
or
thermal pulse ignition device
was a type of technological device. This charge was used with
Evora oxide
for precision-charged patterns of destruction for demolition work. At higher power settings, this charge was used for activating
sternbachium
for chemical laser ignition applications and
gamma
pulse generation. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")
Thermal pulse is a term used in nucleonics which is defined as "the radiant power versus time pulse from a nuclear weapon detonation."
[1]
This device was mentioned in the graphics created for the remastered episode.

Two Regressives are executed by thermal sweep
A
thermal sweep
was a
device
or technique that killed by extreme
heat
.
In the early
24th century
, the
Enarans
used thermal sweeps to enact the secret
genocide
of thousands of
Regressives
. (
VOY
: "
Remember
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Thermionic converter
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Thermionic_converter
======================================================================

A
thermionic converter
was a device used in the power distribution facility on
Quarra
. They operated on a rotating
frequency
and so had to be closely monitored.
In
2377
,
Chakotay
, while investigating the apparent
brainwashing
of the
USS
Voyager
crew on Quarra in the guise of
Amal Kotay
, was assigned to monitor the thermionic converters. He was the replacement for an expert in thermionic conversion, although he was unaware that the expert was
Tuvok
, who had left for "health reasons." Chakotay's level 5 aptitude in thermionic conversion made him a suitable substitute, and was apparently substantially better than his skill in
microkinetics
, rated at a level 3. (
VOY
: "
Workforce
")
External link
Thermionic converter
at
Wikipedia

was a

Fourier analysis
.

,

Rain Robinson

spectral analysis
, impressing Robinson with his
knowledge
of
astrophysics
. (

")

PAGE: Theta class (starship)

Theta
class
Affiliation:
Miradorn
Type:
Raider
Active:
24th century
Speed:
Warp 5+
Armament:
Photon torpedoes
,
plasma charges
Defenses:
Deflector shields
The
Theta
-class
raider
was a type of
starship
used by the
Miradorn
during the
2360s
and
70s
.
The
Theta
-class raider was equipped with
deflector shields
and armed with a dual forward
photon torpedo
bank and two wing mounted cannons for launching concentrated
plasma charges
. With this weaponry it could easily outgun small ships, such as
Starfleet
Danube
-class
runabouts
. Its
maximum velocity
was capable of exceeding
warp 5
. (
DS9
: "
Vortex
")
Ships of the class
Named by commander
Name
Registry
Notes
Status
Date
Ah-Kel's raider
-
Destroyed
2369
Appendices
Background information
This
studio model
was designed by
Ricardo F. Delgado
, and was described on his
concept art
as a "raptor". Notes on the camera test model of Baran's ship indicate this ship to be "1/4 length of
Enterprise
", which suggest an approximately length of 160 meters.
[1]
In "Vortex", the runabout's
computer
identified Ah-Kel's raider as "Miradorn vessel,
Theta
-class", suggesting that the ships of this class are constructed by the Miradorn.
Baran's mercenary vessel
, later seen in
TNG
: "
Gambit, Part I
" and "
Gambit, Part II
", was not specified as a
Theta
-class ship in those episodes, but rather the
ship classification
BC 011
. However, based on its numerous similarities, unmodified
studio model
and use as a
mercenary
ship, and moderate top speeds, suggest it is also a ship of this class.
Apocrypha
A model of the Miradorn starship was released by
Galoob
as part of their
Star Trek
Micro Machines collection.
Star Trek: The Official Starships Collection
released the raider as Baran's ship.
The
CCG: Second Edition
named this type of vessel as a
Ma-Karn
-class ship.

======================================================================
PAGE: Theta storage tank
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Theta_storage_tank
======================================================================

Theta storage tanks
were the primary component of a
Malon
waste export vessel
, designed for the containment of
theta radiation
,
antimatter waste
and other industrial byproducts during transport. The tanks were moderately radiation proof, but prolonged proximity to the tanks still led to radiation exposure. In the event of a tank breach or other high radiation leak, the tanks could be jettisoned into space.
In
2375
,
Fesek
told
Chakotay
of a
legend
which claimed
poisonous
radiogenic
waste
creatures
called
Vihaar
were created and lived in these tanks, causing damage to the ship.  (
VOY
: "
Juggernaut
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Theta-band device
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Theta-band_device
======================================================================

A
theta-band device
was a type of
theta-band
subspace delivery system that could be used to create a
metagenic weapon
.
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
was one of three Starfleet captains who had extensive knowledge of theta-band devices. In
2369
, the
Cardassians
created a fake metagenic weapon in order to lure Picard into capture, based on their knowing of his extensive knowledge of theta-band devices. (
TNG
: "
Chain Of Command, Part II
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Think Tank vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Think_Tank_vessel
======================================================================

The Think Tank vessel
The
"Think Tank" vessel
was a type of
starship
operated by the "
Think Tank
", described by
Kurros
as being a "modest vessel". In reality, this vessel was incredibly advanced, as it was composed of a
neutronium
-based
alloy
that made it very resilient. The vessel spent most of its time hidden in
subspace
. Because of this, most vessels could not detect or attack it; the only indication of its presence was a brief
subspace flux
before it entered normal
space
. The vessel's offensive systems were impressive as well; the
directed energy weapons
(sixteen weapon hardpoints in all) were able to target and fire on multiple vessels at once.
Think Tank vessel under attack
They were also able to make use of complex
isomorphic projections
, similar to
holograms
. This was the main form of
communication
used by the Think Tank. The isomorphic projections were so advanced, they could even taste
food
.
Due to the diverse nature of the Think Tank, a sort of
universal translator
was necessary. The ship had a
telepathic
communications system, known as the "
dome
", which was built into the floor of the main chamber. This device not only allowed the members to communicate with each other, but also with other species, and the ship itself. (
VOY
: "
Think Tank
")

alternate timelines
)

torture device

.

,

speculated that
clamp
was a torture device. (

2401
of the
Confederation of Earth
timeline
,
Agnes Jurati
noted that their
technology
was similar to that of the
prime reality
, but with a "boy, that torture device really looks like it's gonna
hurt
kind of look." (
PIC
: "
Penance
")
See also
Torture chamber

PAGE: Tosk ship
CATEGORIES: Starship classes

======================================================================

A Tosk starship docked with Deep Space 9
The
Tosk's ship
was a type of small
spacecraft
used by the
Tosk
during the late-
24th century
. Their
engineering
systems were equipped with
arva nodes
for collecting space
matter
and converting it into usable
coladrium
fuel
. When in motion, the ship's engines also gave off detectable
ionized
L-band emissions
, identical to that of the
Hunters' ship
.
In
2369
, a single
Tosk
piloted
a ship of this type  through the
Bajoran Wormhole
from the
Gamma Quadrant
to
Deep Space 9
in the
Alpha Quadrant
. The ship was damaged after being attacked by the
Hunters
, but
Chief
Miles O'Brien
was able to repair it and assist the Tosk pilot in escaping his pursuers. (
DS9
: "
Captive Pursuit
")
Interior
The pilot's controls
The interior
According to the script notes on this design, it was described as "a small Vessel of radical design," adding that "[i]t's a single pilot vessel."
The interior of the ship was a redress of a set used for various alien ships and shuttles on
Star Trek: The Next Generation
, beginning with
Arridor's shuttle
in "
The Price
". It had most recently been used as
Berlinghoff Rasmussen
's
time-pod
in "
A Matter Of Time
".
[1]
For more information on history of the
studio model
used to represent the exterior of Tosk's ship, please see:
TNG studio models
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Tox Uthat
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tox_Uthat
======================================================================

The Tox Uthat (2366)
"
A piece of the future.
"
"
A very dangerous piece.
"
–
Vash
and
Jean-Luc Picard
,
2366
("
Captain's Holiday
")
The
Tox Uthat
was a
quantum phase inhibitor
, a device capable of halting all
nuclear fusion
within a
star
. It was described by
Ajur
as "
a
crystal
cube
that fits in the
palm
of the
hand
.
"
Invented by a
scientist
named
Kal Dano
in the
27th century
, the Uthat was almost
stolen
by two
Vorgon
criminals
, Ajur and
Boratus
. In order to prevent the Uthat from falling into the wrong hands, Dano
traveled back in time
to the
22nd century
and hid it on
Risa
.
By the
24th century
, the history of the Uthat had passed into local legend. A
Federation
archaeologist
,
Samuel Estragon
, and his assistant
Vash
took an interest in finding the Uthat. After Dr. Estragon's death in the
2360s
, Vash carried on the search, with financial backing from Estragon's some-time associate, a
Ferengi
named
Sovak
. However, Vash betrayed Sovak and traveled to Risa without him.
In
2366
, Vash found the Uthat buried in a
cave
on Risa, and subsequently threw Sovak off her trail with the assistance of
Captain
Jean-Luc Picard
. Ajur and Boratus traveled back to this point in time, having discovered from historical records that Picard would locate and destroy the Uthat. Posing as security officers, they attempted to convince Picard that they were interested in securing the Uthat for safekeeping.
However, when Vash informed him that it was two Vorgons who attempted to steal the Uthat in the first place, Picard destroyed it using
Transporter Code 14
, to prevent it from falling into the wrong hands, thus fulfilling his role in history. (
TNG
: "
Captain's Holiday
")
Appendices
See also
Nuclear inhibitor
Quantum implosion
Trilithium weapon
Apocrypha
In
Q's Guide to the Continuum
,
Q
says that the Vorgons would not have been a threat even if they had gotten their hands on the device; that in almost every possible reality where they found it, they ended up blowing themselves up with it. But then, Q does have a very strange sense of humor.
The Tox Uthat returns in the
Star Trek Online
episode "Sunrise". Players also meet its creator, Kal Dano, who came from the 27th century (his 31st century
time pod
having been supplied by
Daniels
) to save his ancestors from their star dying and wiping out their race. However, it was a plot by the
Tholians
to obtain the device, who steal it when his ship is overwhelmed. The following episode "Stormfront" has the player character and Kal Dano attempt to retrieve the Tox Uthat, only to have it used to kill a star and threaten the homeworld of the
Na'kuhl
. The two time travel to the 22nd century and bury it on Risa for Picard to fulfill his role in history.
In the
Agents of Yesterday
story line, Agent
Daniels
reveals that Picard had in fact faked the Tox Uthat's destruction by using a transporter, and joins the player in taking the device from its secure vault beneath
Starfleet Headquarters
during the
Breen attack on Earth
in 2375. The Tox Uthat is then brought to the
26th century
, where it is installed aboard the
USS
Enterprise
-J
to defeat the
Sphere-Builders
at the
Battle of Procyon V
.
External link
Tox Uthat
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

======================================================================
PAGE: Traitor's Claw
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Traitor's_Claw
======================================================================

Traitor's Claw
Class:
Police vessel
Affiliation:
Kzinti
Status:
Wrecked
(
2269
)
The
Traitor's Claw
was a
23rd century
Kzinti
police vessel
. It was officially recorded as
stolen
in
2269
, but was in fact a
privateer
in service of the
Kzinti government
, in violation of the
Treaty of Sirius
.
On
stardate
4187.3,
Chuft-Captain
and his
crew
lured the
Starfleet
shuttlecraft
Copernicus
to a small icebound world in the
Beta Lyrae system
, and took the shuttle's occupants prisoner aboard the
Traitor's Claw
. The Kzinti's goal was to regain control of a
Slaver
stasis box
and the
weapon
it contained.
Damage after explosion of the Slaver weapon
The
Traitor's Claw
was severely damaged and her crew killed when the weapon's
self-destruct
mechanism was activated in close proximity to the ship. (
TAS
: "
The Slaver Weapon
")
According to
D.C. Fontana
, the
pink
coloration of the vessel was a result of director
Hal Sutherland
being
color blind
, and thus unable to discern it as anything but a shade of
gray
.
[1]
However, storyboard artist/character designer
Bob Kline
laid the blame on color director
Irvin Kaplan
. "Pink equals Irv Kaplan," shared Kline... "Irv was in charge of ink and paint, coloring the various characters and props (and he would do it himself in his office, he would sit down with a cel and paint it). He was also referred to by many people there as the purple and green guy. You'll see it in a lot of scenes, purple and green used together – that was one of his preferences. He made dragons red, the Kzintis' costumes pink. It was all Irv Kaplan's call. He wasn't listening to anyone else when he picked colors or anything." {"Star Trek: The Official Guide to the Animated Series", p. 26.)
External link
Traitor's Claw
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

Innards of a trans-sonic drill
A
trans-sonic drill
was a type of drill used for
mining
pergium
.
In
2375
,
Yanas Tigan
asked
Miles O'Brien
if he could take a look at a malfunctioning trans-sonic drill which nobody else on her staff was able to repair. Her
son
,
Janel Tigan
protested that while O'Brien was an
engineer
, he was not a
mining engineer
; while her
daughter
Ezri Dax
protested that O'Brien needed to rest, but O'Brien assured them he felt fine.
O'Brien was able to determine that the problem was in the secondary input
transtator
, discovering that the drill required a
52-J
, but was equipped with a
52-L
. (
DS9
: "
Prodigal Daughter
")
According to the script "
The drill is a large piece of equipment about the size of a mini-van. There's a beam emitter at one end, which is used for cutting into the ore.
"

======================================================================
PAGE: Transportation device
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Transportation_device
======================================================================

A type of transportation device
A
transportation device
was any type of
technology
that was capable of
transporting
people
or
things
to another location.
In the case of
devices
capable of
teleportation
, such a transportation device could
beam
a
person
to another location seemingly instantaneously; whereas a transportation device such as a
vehicle
, was often capable of
driving
on the
ground
,
spaceflight
or other types of travel.
In
2267
, when
Apollo
wasn't allowing
Captain
James T. Kirk
and his
landing party
to beam back
to their
ship
, he told them "your
transportation device
no longer functions." (
TOS
: "
Who Mourns for Adonais?
")
In
2371
,
Ensign
Harry Kim
referred to a
spatial trajector
as a transportation device. He described it as "extremely sophisticated. It operates on the principal of folding
space
." (
VOY
: "
Prime Factors
")
That same year, Captain
Kathryn Janeway
speculated that a
photonic lattice
that the
crew
of the
USS
Voyager
encountered was some type of transportation device for
photonic lifeforms
. She assumed it was their equivalent of a
ship
. (
VOY
: "
Heroes and Demons
")
External link
Transportation device
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Tret's containment vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tret's_containment_vessel
======================================================================

Two of Tret's containment vessels and
Enterprise
in
orbit
of the
Loque'eque homeworld
.
Tret
's containment vessel
was a class of
starship
utilized by
Tret's species
to prevent any individual infected from the
Loque'eque
mutagenic virus
from leaving the
Loque'eque homeworld
and infecting neighboring
star systems
. This class of vessel was armed with dual forward
particle cannons
, and were capable of exceeding
warp 4
. During the
2150s
, a vessel of this class was
commanded
by Tret.
In
2153
, Tret's containment vessel, and one of her sister ships, intercepted
Enterprise
in
orbit
of the Loque'eque homewhere, where they discovered a Loque'eque aboard the
Human
vessel. They declared that
Enterprise
was under
quarantine
, and that it they should prepare to be boarded. The commander of
Enterprise
at the time,
Trip Tucker
refused to be boarded, but offered to Tret that he may come aboard to discuss the situation. After departing
Enterprise
, Tret returned to his vessel, which remained in orbit alongside her sister ship and the
Earth
ship.
After
Enterprise
attempted to flee the two containment vessels, they both took chase, and quickly caught
Enterprise
, after nearly disabling her
aft
hull plating
. Threatening to destroy
Enterprise
, the two ships stood down after they were offered an
antitoxin
capable of reversing the mutagen. (
ENT
: "
Extinction
")
Forward view
Profile view
Aft view
Background information
In the final draft script of "Extinction", the only physical description of the containment vessels were that they be "sizable".
The containment vessel only made one appearance and was designed by
John Eaves
. Eaves was less than enamored with the design, however, or as he has put it, "
UHHHHGGGGG!!! as in ugly, by far my least favorite of anything I have ever drawn. based on a hatchet in a tool pouch it goes to show that not all shapes can inspire a spacecraft idea. HAAAA this one is embarrassing!!!
"
[1]
Nevertheless, the design was approved in
June 2003
and was sent over to
Eden FX
, where the design was turned into a
CGI model
by
Pierre Drolet
, whose modelling did not soften Eaves' opinion. "
Even Pierre's brilliant modeling,
" Eaves remarked, "
couldn't stop the fairies with ugly sticks from beating the tar out of this one!
"
[2]

======================================================================
PAGE: Triannon vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Triannon_vessel
======================================================================

Triannon vessel
Owner:
Triannon
Operator:
D'Jamat
's faction,
Ceris
' faction
Type:
Starship
Active:
2150s
Crew complement:
23
Armament:
Minimal
The
Triannon vessel
was a type of
starship
class used by the
Triannon
species
in the mid-
22nd century
.
A ship of this type under the command of
Pri'Nam
D'Jamat
left Triannon in late
2152
, making a 6.3
light year
trek in about a year.
In
November
2153
,
Enterprise
NX-01
came to the aid of D'Jamat's vessel, when it appeared to have been disabled by the
spatial anomalies
present in the
Delphic Expanse
. In reality, the
crew
had faked their
distress call
in order to capture the
Earth
Starfleet
ship
which they intended to use to end a
religious
war
on their
homeworld
. D'Jamat used the captured
Enterprise
to battle four vessels of this type under the command of
Ceris
, and noted to Ceris it would take ten of these vessels to match
Enterprise
's fire power. After damaging Ceris' ships, with the intention of targeting their
reactors
, D'Jamat's forces were thwarted by the crew of
Enterprise
. (
ENT
: "
Chosen Realm
")
Bridge station
In the final draft script of "Chosen Realm", this type of ship was initially twice described as "small" before a fuller scene description referred to D'Jamat's ship as "a good deal smaller than
Enterprise
, space-worn and battered." The script additionally specified that the other Triannon craft were to be "identical in configuration to D'Jamat's ship."
The design of this starship was created by
John Eaves
, who inked four different concepts for this ship in early
October 2003
. One of the sketches later became the
Denobulan shuttle
seen in the episode "
The Augments
".
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Tripwire
CATEGORIES: Weapons
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tripwire
======================================================================

A
tripwire
or
trip line
was any
length
of line that could be used as part of a
booby trap
, designed to trigger an
alarm
or simply trip someone to make them
fall
.
Kirk setting a basic tripwire
After
Captain
James T. Kirk
refused to help
Jojo Krako
become the top
boss
on
Sigma Iotia II
in
2268
, Kirk was "put on ice" in the cellar of Krako's headquarters. While captive, Kirk took the
copper
wire
out of a
vacuum tube
radio
and strung it between a
desk
and a
table
to create a tripwire. He then feigned an injury that lured a
Iotian
gangster
into the room, where he tripped over the wire, before he was subsequently incapacitated by Kirk. (
TOS
: "
A Piece of the Action
")
An officer pulling a trip cord tight
After several
Gelrakian
boarding parties
came aboard the
USS
Cerritos
in
2380
, they pursued and attacked several members of the
Cerritos
' crew, including
one officer
who lured two Gelrakians into the
shuttlebay
, where they were tripped by
another officer
who pulled a large
cable
trip cord. (
LD
: "
Temporal Edict
")
External link
Tripwire
at
Wikipedia

======================================================================
PAGE: Tucker tube
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Tucker_tube
======================================================================

Reginald Barclay
with Tucker tubes in
2377
Tucker tubes
were devices, each consisting of a pair of generators connected by a transparent tube, in which
energy beams
were visible. Their power output was measured in
cochranes
, a unit of
subspace distortion
. (
LD
: "
I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee
")
Tucker tubes were already in use among
Humans
in the
22nd century
, including on
Cold Station 12
. (
ENT
: "
Cold Station 12
") In the
23rd century
they were used on
United Federation of Planets
facilities such as
Regula I
and
Starfleet
ships. (
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
;
Star Trek V: The Final Frontier
) By the
24th century
they were in common use not only in the Federation, but also among non-Federation species such as
Talarians
and
Tanugans
. (
TNG
: "
A Matter of Perspective
", "
Suddenly Human
";
VOY
: "
Inside Man
")
Tucker tubes were used by Doctor
Arik Soong
in his hideout on
Trialas IV
, as well as by his descendant
Noonien Soong
in his laboratory on
Omicron Theta
. (
ENT
: "
Cold Station 12
";
TNG
: "
Datalore
")
Tucker tubes in the USS
Cerritos
engineering in 2381
In
2381
,
Ensign
Sam Rutherford
increased the power output of the Tucker tubes on
USS
Cerritos
by seven
picocochranes
. Meanwhile, Ensign
Livik
added a third tube to create an improved version of the device, which he named "
Billups tubes
" after his superior,
Andy Billups
. (
LD
: "
I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee
")
Appendices
Background information
The device was a prop commonly seen in many movies and TV series aside from
Star Trek
. It was created by the company Modern Props, originally for
The Incredible Hulk
television series. Its catalogue number was #195-290-1 and it was described as "dual generators with rotating neon lights inside an acrylic tube; light-controlled panel with knobs and buttons".
[1]
Notable appearances include
Airplane II: The Sequel
, where it was seen on the lunar base commanded by
William Shatner
's character, Buck Murdock.
It first appeared on
Star Trek
in
Star Trek II: The Wrath of Khan
and was used multiple times in
Star Trek: The Next Generation
,
Star Trek: Voyager
and
Star Trek: Enterprise
, but was not named until
LD
: "
I Have No Bones Yet I Must Flee
". Prior to this, they were informally known as "lateral neon "blinky" units". (
Star Trek: The Next Generation Companion
(1st ed., p. 227))
The name is a reference to
Charles Tucker III
. In an interview with Screenrant, supervising director
Barry J. Kelly
mentions "references to [
Star Trek
:]
Enterprise
" and the "name-drop [of] someone about a particular engineering element."
[2]
. Based on the name and its appearances in
ENT
: "
Cold Station 12
" it is likely that the device was invented by Tucker prior to
2154
.

======================================================================
PAGE: Turei vessel
CATEGORIES: Starship classes
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Turei_vessel
======================================================================

A Turei vessel
The
Turei vessel
was a type of
starship
in use by the
Turei
in the late-
24th century
. These ships were heavily armed with
plasma charge
weapons.
A Turei vessel fires a plasma torpedo
In
2376
, a ship of this type attacked the
USS
Voyager
after it was inadvertently pulled into
Underspace
.
Voyager
managed to retreat to an ancient
Vaadwaur
planet
where it landed for repairs. During this time, several Turei ships
bombarded
the
planet
but the
radiogenic particles
in the
atmosphere
prevented their
sensors
locking onto
Voyager'
s exact position. The fleet was eventually disabled by
Voyager
after using a
Vaadwaur sentry satellite
to locate their positions in
orbit
. (
VOY
: "
Dragon's Teeth
")
For more information on the
studio model
used, see
VOY studio models
.
External link
Turei frigate
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

of the

. However, the Clickets attacked

phaser
fire between the vessels. (

: "
Veritas
")

======================================================================
PAGE: Typing
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Typing
======================================================================

Multiple realities
(covers information from several
alternate timelines
)
"
How quaint.
"
–
Montgomery Scott
,
1986
(
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
Typing on a typewriter
Typing
was the act of pressing
keys
on a
typewriter
or
computer
keyboard
to form words on a piece of
paper
or a computer screen.
In
1968
, after
Gary Seven
assured
Roberta Lincoln
she wasn't to operate a typewriter with her
fingers
, she asked if she should use her
nose
instead. She was surprised that the device typed everything she was saying and ordered it to stop. (
TOS
: "
Assignment: Earth
")
As of
2373
,
Kathryn Janeway
had never learned how to type, a fact obvious to
Chakotay
after she faltered while attempting to use
Henry Starling
's
computer
in
1996
. She explained that
Starfleet Academy
didn't require one to take a course on
Turn-of-the-Millennium Technology
.
(
VOY
: "
Future's End
")
According to
Agnes Jurati
, the
Borg Queen
could think faster than Jurati could type. (
PIC
: "
Watcher
")
External link
Typing
at
Wikipedia

Tzenkethi raider
was a type of
starship
utilized by the
Tzenkethi
during
their war against
the
Federation
.
During the conflict, the
USS
Okinawa
encountered three
raiders
, which later fled into an
asteroid belt
.
Captain
Leyton
wanted to pursue them, although his
first officer
Benjamin Sisko
was not sure. Leyton overruled them and the
Okinawa
pursued the raiders.
In
2372
, Captain Sisko told
Admiral
Leyton he was right to go after the raiders. (
DS9
: "
Paradise Lost
")

at

, the wiki for licensed

on

======================================================================
PAGE: USGS Powell
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/USGS_Powell
======================================================================

USGS
Powell
Status:
Active
(
2370
)
The
USGS
Powell
was a
24th century
starship
registered for
Federation
travel. In
2370
, the
captain
of this ship was
S. Sondheim
. The
starship
's point of departure was
Hispaniola Minor
. The starship arrived at
Deep Space 9
on
stardate
47550.5. The
USGS
Powell
was listed on the
space station
's
arrival roster
. (
DS9
: "
Whispers
",
production art
)
This starship was only mentioned in writing.
The starship was presumably named after
John Wesley Powell
, who served as the second director of the
United States Geological Survey
(USGS) from 1881 to 1894.

======================================================================
PAGE: USS Dauntless (NX-01-A)
CATEGORIES: Starships
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/USS_Dauntless_(NX-01-A)
======================================================================

"USS
Dauntless
"
Class:
Dauntless
-type
Registry:
NX-01-A
Affiliation:
Species 116
Status:
Presumed
assimilated
(
2374
)
"
Starfleet didn't send us this vessel and
you're
not here to help. Is this your ship?
"
–
Kathryn Janeway
,
2374
("
Hope and Fear
")
The
"USS
Dauntless
"
was an
alien
starship
disguised as an experimental
Federation
starship. Allegedly, the
Dauntless
was the
prototype
for its class of starship, launched on
Stardate
51472.
History
In mid-
2374
, while still stranded in the
Delta Quadrant
, the
USS
Voyager
received a transmission from
Starfleet Command
via the ancient
Hirogen communications network
. Although the message contained a number of unencoded personal messages, there was also an encrypted file that the
Voyager
crew was unable to open. (
VOY
: "
Hunters
")
On Stardate 51978,
Voyager
encountered an alien named
Arturis
who had a gift for translating languages, both verbal and visual. He easily decrypted the mysterious message, which gave a set of coordinates near their current location. Upon arrival, the
Voyager
crew discovered the
Dauntless
, which activated its
quantum slipstream drive
when the away team of
Chakotay
,
Tom Paris
, and
Tuvok
boarded it to travel fifteen
light years
in a few minutes.
The
Dauntless
MSD
Although intrigued at the drive's possibilities and its promise of a faster trip home, the
Dauntless
was relatively minimalist by other Starfleet standards, possessing only one
transporter
and no
replicators
,
holodecks
, or
shuttlecraft
. Because of the sparse facilities aboard this vessel,
Captain
Janeway
commented that
Neelix
's skills at
cooking
and morale building would be much needed. Neelix, for his part, embraced the challenge. While investigating the
Dauntless
, it soon proved to be an elaborate
trap
set by Arturis to arrange for the
Voyager
crew's
assimilation
by the
Borg
, with its resemblance to Starfleet technology the result of
particle synthesis
, an advanced form of replicator technology.
Arturis' race
had been recently assimilated by the Borg, and Arturis blamed that event on Janeway's short-lived alliance with the Borg against
Species 8472
.
He had assumed that the crew's desire to get home would prompt them to immediately take the ship and use the slipstream to try and get back to Earth, only learning after activating it that it was programmed to take them to
his homeworld
, now inside
Borg space
, but past experience and an unwillingness to abandon
Voyager
prompted the crew to carry out various tests first. After Janeway decoded enough of the original message to reveal that
Dauntless
was a trap, Arturis attempted to simply take Janeway and
Seven of Nine
back to Borg space, but
Voyager
was able to duplicate the slipstream and rescue them, leaving Arturis stranded aboard the
Dauntless
as it entered Borg space, where he was presumably assimilated. (
VOY
: "
Hope and Fear
")
The registry number seen on the hull was NX-01-A, while internal LCARS displays used the registry number NX-01A. Since both were seen on screen, both are valid. In the
script
, it was spelled out "NX Zero One-A".
External link
USS
Dauntless
(NX-01-A)
at
Memory Beta
, the wiki for licensed
Star Trek
works

. (

Alternate reality
(
split 2233
)
USS Trainer
Owner:
United Federation of Planets
Operator:
Starfleet
The
USS
Trainer
was the name given to the
bridge simulator
at
Starfleet Academy
in the mid-
23rd century
used for the
Kobayashi Maru
scenario
.
On his third trial,
Cadet
James T. Kirk
inserted a
subroutine
to cheat the test and rescue the crew of the
USS
Kobayashi Maru
from the
Klingons
in
2258
. (
Star Trek
)
The
Trainer
bridge was a reuse of the
USS
Kelvin
bridge set.
The name USS Trainer originated from the source's
script
.
[1]

======================================================================
PAGE: Ultrasonic generator
CATEGORIES: Technology
URL: https://memory-alpha.fandom.com/wiki/Ultrasonic_generator
======================================================================

An
ultrasonic generator
was a device capable of creating a
resonance frequency
by means of
acoustic energy
. A
covariant oscillator
could be used as a makeshift ultrasonic generator.
In
2371
, when the
Female Changeling
was pretending to be
Kira Nerys
and trapped in a growing
crystal
, she talked of a
theft
on
Remmil VI
where a band of
Nausicaan raiders
broke into the
central museum
by using an ultrasonic generator to create a sympathetic vibration in the webbing and shatter it. (
DS9
: "
Heart of Stone
")

"Shroomie" starship
An unidentified alien ship
In
September
2151
, this vessel of an
unknown species
destroyed the
subspace relays
Echo One
and
Echo Two
. It also attacked the
United Earth
vessel
Enterprise
NX-01
twice. The third time it attempted to do so,
Enterprise
was ready for it and, using their newly installed
phase cannons
, damaged the alien vessel to such an extent that it fled the scene at high
impulse
. It was never seen again.
As a 22nd century starship, this vessel was highly advanced in a technological regard, possessing strong
shields
and technology that prevented invasive scans from other vessels. It utilized a powerful
energy weapon
that fired green, crescent-shaped pulses. It had a crew of at least fifteen individuals, and carried at least
one shuttlecraft
. It also had the ability to emit a powerful
dampening field
, which could effectively disable an
NX-class
starship. (
ENT
: "
Silent Enemy
")
In the script for "Silent Enemy", this craft was described as "sleek and dark, so dark, in fact, that it's difficult to make out against the blackness of space." The
CGI model
of the alien ship and its shuttle was made by Brandon MacDougall. See the Drex Files.
[4]

' personal blog.

, 4th ed., vol. 1, p. 441 identified this type of vessel as a
Kovaalan starship
.

-class escort. Their owners are called the
Elachi
.

Triglobulin Harvester Species starship
Triglobulin harvester's starship
This
unidentified hostile alien
vessel
possessed high-yield
particle weapons
, heavy
deflector shielding
, a
tractor beam
and a grappling/drill used for piercing
starship
hulls
.
In
2151
, a vessel of this class
attacked and killed the crew
of an
Axanar cargo ship
, and later hooked the crew up to a
bio pump
which was used to extract
triglobulin
from their
blood
.
Triglobulin harvesters capture
Enterprise
Several days later, the hostile vessel returned and attacked
Enterprise
NX-01
, which had discovered the Axanar vessel and had sent a
distress call
from the ship.
During the encounter, the hostile vessel was more than a match against the
Earth
ship's
spatial torpedoes
. The hostile vessel quickly disabled
Enterprise
's
warp drive
and captured the small Earth ship in its tractor beam. The hostile ship proceeded to conduct a
sub-molecular bioscan
of the ship's crew, before locking onto the ship and beginning to drill into
Enterprise
's
hull
.
Before too much damage was done,
Enterprise
was rescued by an
Axanar warship
that first disabled the hostile alien's tractor beam, releasing
Enterprise
, before destroying the ship with ease;
Enterprise
also fired a
spatial torpedo
after
Malcolm Reed
had got the
targeting scanners
working properly. (
ENT
: "
Fight or Flight
")
This type of vessel wasn't identified in either the script for "Fight or Flight" nor the final version of that episode. In the script, the craft is referred to as "futuristic" and "highly advanced [...] with hull plating and conduits similar to the bio-pumps."
A schematic of this design was present in
Daniels
's
31st century
database
when Captain Archer and
T'Pol
accessed it in
2152
. (
ENT
: "
Future Tense
")
Starboard particle weapon
Grappler
Drill head
Destroyed by the Axanar
In Daniels' database
Xantoras evacuation transports
At least four
transports
were evacuating
Xantoras
in
2153
at the time of the arrival of
Enterprise
NX-01
per the request of the Xantoras military.
Transport #1
Transport #2
Transport #3
Transport #4
The fourth vessel (
right
), which was capable of
planetary landing
, was evacuating the
planet
when it suffered a rupture to its
reactor casing
while it was still in
low orbit
. The
ship's captain
requested permission to return to the surface after several of its compartments had flooded with
radiation
, causing injuries to several of the passengers and crew, including the
Antaran
Hudak
. The ship's request was denied; subsequently, assistance was rendered to the vessel by
Enterprise
. (
ENT
: "
The Breach
")
For information on transport #1, see
VOY studio models
; for transport #2, see
DS9 studio models
; and, for transport #3, see
TNG studio models
. Transport #4, the only one to be described in the final draft script of "The Breach", was scripted to be "large Alien Transport Vessel," marked with "some visible external damage." It appears to have been a new
CGI model
.

======================================================================

List of
unnamed starships
from the
23rd century
belonging to various species who originated from the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrants
.
Federation starships
See
:
Unnamed Federation starships (23rd century)
Flint's shuttle
Flint's shuttle on a ramp.
A
shuttle
could be seen on a ramp at
Flint
's
castle
on the
planet
Holberg 917G
. (
TOS
: "
Requiem for Methuselah
")
Elysia
Numerous
starships
were trapped in
Elysia
after disappearing from the
Delta Triangle
region in the many centuries leading into the
23rd century
.
Among the species who lost ships in the Triangle were the
Andorians
,
Tellarites
,
Klingons
,
Orions
,
Vulcans
,
Romulans
,
Humans
,
Phylosians
, and the
Gorn
. Among the ships lost were the
Bonaventure
, the
USS
Enterprise
, and the
IKS
Klothos
. (
TAS
: "
The Time Trap
")
Two of the vessels that appeared here were the same design as vessels that appeared at the
Vedala asteroid
rendezvous (see below), suggesting that further presence of the
Vedala
,
Em/3/Green's species
,
Sord's species
, or the
Skorr
, as well.

Another group of ships trapped in Elysia.

Klothos
in the foreground.

Vedala asteroid rendezvous
Rendezvous at the Vedala asteroid
In
2269
, four
spacecraft
and the
USS
Enterprise
converged at the
Vedala asteroid
to be briefed on their mission to the "
mad planet
".
The four unnamed starships were the ships in which
Em/3/Green
of an
insectoid species
,
Sord
of a
reptilian race
,
Tchar
of the
Skorr
species and
Lara
, a
Human
, arrived to the rendezvous. (
TAS
: "
The Jihad
")
These vessels were described in the
script
as being "
a curious constellation of spacecraft: one toroid; one cubical; one ridged disc; several cylinders of varying sizes and curvatures.
" (Editor's note: None of which actually appeared in the stated forms.)
Two of the vessels were of same design as some of the derelict ships in Elysia in "
The Time Trap
" (see above).
Ship for hire
In
2285
, this
ship
was available for hire when
Leonard McCoy
contacted its
captain
about transport to the
Mutara sector
. (
Star Trek III: The Search for Spock
)
This ship was only mentioned in dialogue.
Three smaller ships neutralized by the Whale Probe
In
2286
, these three
ships
, smaller than a
Federation
starship
, were neutralized by the
Whale Probe
. (
Star Trek IV: The Voyage Home
)
These ships were only mentioned in dialogue.

PAGE: Unnamed Alpha and Beta Quadrant starships (24th century)

======================================================================

List of
unnamed starships
that were located in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrants
during the
24th century
.
Ancient generation ship
The ancient generation ship
MSD of the ancient generation ship
This
ancient
generation ship
was discovered
adrift
, after several
centuries
, by the
Federation
in the late 24th century. The
ship's crew
was found
mummified
in disabled
cryo units
.
This vessel carried a unique
molecular fluid
capable of transforming
inorganic material
into a
living
ecosystem
, which the ship's inhabitants had presumably planned to use to
terraform
their destination.
In
2380
, on
stardate
57538.9, the
USS
Cerritos
assisted the
USS
Merced
in relocating the ship. During the relocation of the vessel, the
Merced
moved out of formation and accidentally caused a
hull breach
in the generation ship. This caused a leak of the terraforming emulsion to both the
Merced
and the
Cerritos
.
Lieutenant
Beckett Mariner
and
Captain
Carol Freeman
, working together, were able to save the
Cerritos
. However, the
Merced
was too severely damaged, and
her crew
had to be beamed aboard the generation ship to ensure their survival. (
LD
: "
Moist Vessel
")
Deep Space 9 visitors
Alien freighter 1
Alien freighter
This
alien
freighter
was seen numerous times near
Deep Space 9
. (
DS9
: "
The Way of the Warrior
")
It visited in
2372
, (
DS9
: "
Hard Time
", "
The Muse
") twice in
2373
, (
DS9
: "
For the Uniform
", "
Ties of Blood and Water
") multiple times in
2374
, (
DS9
: "
Resurrection
", "
His Way
", "
Profit and Lace
") and several times more in
2375
. (
DS9
: "
Treachery, Faith and the Great River
", "
Inter Arma Enim Silent Leges
", "
'Til Death Do Us Part
")
The
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine
opening title sequences
from the
fourth season
opener "The Way of the Warrior" onward, consisted of a combination of
motion control photography
and
CGI
elements. At the time still a time-intensive production method,
ILM
's
John Knoll
pitched in with the CGI work out of courtesy. "
I made about half a dozen little bits and pieces
[rem:
work bees
and the welders on the pylons]
for the sequence,
" he said, "
including a generic alien ship with blue glowy engines.
" (
Star Trek: Deep Space Nine Companion
, p. 335)
Betazed transport
Alien transport
This
transport
had a scheduled run from
Deep Space 9
to
Betazed
in
2372
.
Prior to its arrival,
Odo
considered checking when the next transport left DS9 for Betazed, when offering to help
Lwaxana Troi
find a quiet place to have her baby. Following this suggestion, Troi felt that she "
couldn't possibly go there. That's the first place
Jeyal
will look for me. I know him. He won't give up. Not until he finds me and gets his son back.
"
Later, when she was released from her obligation to Jeyal, Troi informed Odo that she had "wonderful news", specifically that "
there's a transport for Betazed leaving this
afternoon
. I'm going home.
" (
DS9
: "
The Muse
")
This vessel was not referenced in the script, however, its appearance shortly after Odo and Troi's final scene, does give a strong implication of the role it could presumably fulfill.
For more information on this
studio model
, please see
TNG studio models
.
Emi's family's ship
(
DS9
: "
Prophet Motive
")
Sarda's ship
(
DS9
: "
Captive Pursuit
")
Umani sector transport
This
transport
traveled from the
Umani sector
to Deep Space 9 in 2372 carrying Jeyal. (
DS9
: "
The Muse
")
This ship was only mentioned in dialogue.
Gravity well planet derelicts
These
derelicts
had previously
crash landed
on the surface of a
planet
that was surrounded by a powerful
gravity well
. (
LD
: "
Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
")
Horned alien species' ship
This
ship
, which crashed on the gravity well planet sometime prior to
2381
, belonged to a
horned humanoid
.
After
scans
of the ship revealed that its
engines
were still intact, and "could be our ticket out of here",
Brad Boimler
wondered to
AGIMUS
, "
You sure this thing can still fly? Seems pretty beat-up.
" AGIMUS assured Boimler that it could once he brought it on line. In a ruse, once they were on the ship's
bridge
, Boimler told AGIMUS that he could "sub you in as the
navigation controls
," but he "discovered" that the
access port
was locked behind a
panel
. Boimler determined that he needed an
auxiliary power source
to open it, and AGIMUS offered him use of his
battery
, which Boimler promptly used to send a
distress signal
. Meanwhile, while AGIMUS was busy scheming aloud that "
With this ship's materials, I will produce a whole
fleet
of
murder drones
!
" Boimler connected him to a
dimmer switch
, and ultimately foiled his plans while still being able to use him to effect a rescue. (
LD
: "
Where Pleasant Fountains Lie
")
Horn-alien species' ship
Forward view of bridge
Aft view of bridge
New Axton visitors
These
ships
, including a
Free Spirit
-type
vessel, visited
New Axton
in
2381
. (
LD
: "
The Inner Fight
")
Billups' ship
See
:
Billups' ship
Old derelict freighter
This
old derelict
freighter
was found
adrift
by the
Bajorans
about a
light year
from
Deep Space 9
.
According to
Quark
, who was
auctioning
off the contents discovered within, the freighter's
cargo hold
was "
crammed with valuable items of every description.
Antiques
,
paintings
,
vehicles
.
" (
DS9
: "
In the Cards
")
This freighter was only mentioned in dialogue.
Old waste vessel
Forward view
Aft view
This
old waste vessel
was a large, unoccupied
sublight
freighter
that had been drifting through space while carrying leftover radioactive waste, eventually endangering the population of
Gamelan V
in
2367
.
Upon receiving
distress calls
from the planet, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
towed the barge into
Gamelan
, narrowly avoiding exposure to the lethal doses of radiation in the process. The barge's propulsion system employed a
gaseous core fission reactor
but it appeared to have been inactive for the
last three hundred years
.
Geordi La Forge
referred to it as a "
garbage scow
." (
TNG
: "
Final Mission
")
For information on history of this studio model, please see
TNG studio models
.
Qualor II surplus depot
The
Qualor II
Surplus Depot Z15
was a
Federation
junkyard
that contained the hulks of numerous retired
starships
, along with at least
two space stations
. Among the identified vessels that were located at the depot were the
Apollo
-class
T'Pau
, located at Section 18-Gamma-12, and the
holding vessel
USS
Tripoli
, located along the outer rim.
Depot
quartermaster
Klim Dokachin
once found a fourteen foot
Caldorian eel
on board a
freighter
that had been retired here. (
TNG
: "
Unification I
", "
Unification II
")
According to the script, the vessels here were "
old, abandoned, and decommissioned vessels, stretching as far as the eye can see, a graveyard of once proud ships from throughout the Federation.
"
This junkyard included several
studio model
reuses from various
films
and
TNG
episodes, including hulk #1:
Niagara
-class
; #2:
Challenger
-class
; #3:
USS
Enterprise
study model; #4, #11, & #14:
USS
Excelsior
study models; #5:
Nebula
-class
study model; #6:
K't'inga
-class
; #8 & #10:
Miranda
-class
; #9:
Talarian freighter
; #12
Antares
-class
; #13:
Mars Defense Perimeter
ship; #15
Talarian warship
, and, #16:
Springfield
-class
. Hulk #7 was a previously unseen and unknown model, Hulk #19: Klingon K't'inga-class hall.
Hulk #1
Hulk #2
Hulk #3
USS
Excelsior
Hulk #4

Hulk #19
Smuggler's combat vessel
The smuggler ship
This
combat vessel
was encountered at the
Qualor II
depot
in
2368
.
Of unknown origin, the vessel was involved in smuggling starship components from the depot by taking the place of the holding vessel
USS
Tripoli
. This combat vessel's armaments were considerable – its
phasers
were powerful enough to reduce a
Galaxy
-class
starship
's shields by at least 15% in a single volley.
Following the discovery of the remains of the
Vulcan
vessel
T'Pau
's
navigational deflector
, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
uncovered the operation, and – disguised as an abandoned ship – lay in wait for the vessel's arrival. When it took position to receive that day's shipment of goods, the
Enterprise
powered up and attempted to make contact. The vessel responded to hails by opening fire. A low-power phaser burst in return destroyed one of its phaser arrays, and caused collateral damage in her
cargo bays
– which at the time were fully loaded, mostly with weaponry. This led to massive power fluctuations and internal explosions, finally resulting in the destruction of the entire ship. (
TNG
: "
Unification I
")
The
ex-husband
of
Amarie
was a member of the ship's crew. (
TNG
: "
Unification II
")
The script describes this vessel as being "
an ominous and impressive vessel,
" further describing it as a "
dark, huge, sinister-looking, bristling with armament, and carries absolutely no marking of any kind.
"
For more information on this
studio model
, see
TNG studio models
.
Solarion IV attacker
This
vessel
attacked the
Solarion IV colony
in
2368
. After its signal was detected by the
USS
Enterprise
-D
as it was leaving
orbit
, the vessel broadcast a message saying the
Bajora
were responsible for the
destruction
of the colony. An
investigation
by the
Enterprise
-D crew later concluded the attack was staged by the
Cardassians
. (
TNG
: "
Ensign Ro
")
This attacking vessel was only mentioned in dialogue.
Starbase 25 visitors
These
starships
visited or were docked at
Starbase 25
in
2381
. (
LD
: "
An Embarrassment Of Dooplers
")
Vessel trapped in a Tyken's Rift
An alien vessel
This
alien vessel
was trapped in a
Tyken's Rift
located in an
uncharted binary star system
in
2367
.
The ship, which appeared as a glowing mass, belonged to an
unidentified alien species
who attempted to
telepathically
contact the crews of both
USS
Brattain
, and later, the
USS
Enterprise
-D
– two
Federation
ships found trapped on the other side of the rift. The crews of the vessel and the
Enterprise
-D were eventually able to collaborate, and after near-simultaneously releasing an unknown compound that interacted with
hydrogen
released from the
Enterprise
, an
explosion
was successfully created that freed both ships from the rift. (
TNG
: "
Night Terrors
")
According to the script, this object was revealed as "
a shapeless mass... a sparkling energy field... the other ship.
"

List of
unnamed starships
that were located in the
Alpha
or
Beta Quadrants
during the
32nd century
.
Adira's rescue ship
This
ship
found an
evacuation shuttle
transporting
survivors
, including
Adira Tal
, from the ill-fated
Tal's ship
in
3188
. (
DIS
: "
Forget Me Not
")
This ship was only mentioned in dialogue.
The Colony ships
These
ships
belonged to the
mining
settlement on the
planet
known as "
The Colony
" in
3189
. Scans of these ships by the
USS
Discovery
revealed designs that were presumably
warp capable
, but they did not have
dilithium
. (
DIS
: "
Far From Home
")
Courier model ship
Cloaked courier model ship
This
ship
, identified by
Cleveland Booker
as a
courier model
, was conducting
salvage
operations at a former
Emerald Chain
work camp
under
cloak
in
3190
. (
DIS
: "
The Galactic Barrier
")
The ship used the same model as the Hunhau transport from "
Scavengers
".
Emerald Chain ships
Andorian
and
Orion
ships
of the
Emerald Chain
were discovered in the
Sigma Draconis system
in 3189 by the
USS
Cuyahoga
. The starship reported its discovery to
Federation Headquarters
. (
DIS
: "
Die Trying
")
These ships were only mentioned in dialogue.
Hunhau transport ship
Transport ship lifting off Hunhau
This
yellow
transport ship
was parked outside the
perimeter fence
at the
Hunhau
salvage yard
when dozens of
slave
laborers successfully escaped from their Emerald Chain guards. Upon taking possession of the
transport
, it was piloted by the prisoners away from the yard into
warp
flight. (
DIS
: "
Scavengers
")

alternate timelines
)
List of
unnamed
Bajoran
starships
.

-class carriers
